```py
  181 |     "if p(v700^a) then v700^a, '. ' fi "
  182 |     "v200^a "
  183 |     "if p(v200^f) then ' / ', v200^f fi "
  184 |     "if p(v101) then ' . — ', v101 fi"
  185 | )
  186 | # Full: brief plus inventory numbers, on separate lines.
  187 | DEFAULT_FULL_PFT = (
  188 |     "if p(v700^a) then 'Автор: ', v700^a fi "
  189 |     "'\nЗаглавие: ', v200^a "
  190 |     "if p(v200^f) then ' / ', v200^f fi "
  191 |     "if p(v101) then '\nЯзык: ', v101 fi "
  192 |     "if p(v920) then '\nРабочий лист: ', v920 fi "
  193 |     "if p(v910^b) then '\nИнв. №: ', v910^b+| ; | fi"
  194 | )
  195 | 
  196 | 
  197 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  198 | # Record-draft accessors. Repeatable fields are lists of instances (str|dict).
  199 | # Local copies (mirror flk/pft) so this module is self-contained — the value
  200 | # we index must agree with what the engines see.
  201 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  202 | def _instances(record, field):
  203 |     raw = record.get(field) if record else None
  204 |     if raw is None:
  205 |         return []
  206 |     if isinstance(raw, list):
  207 |         return raw
  208 |     return [raw]
  209 | 
  210 | 
  211 | def _inst_value(inst, subfield):
  212 |     """Read ``subfield`` from one instance. subfield=None => whole-field value."""
  213 |     if isinstance(inst, dict):
  214 |         if subfield is None or subfield == '':
  215 |             if inst.get(''):
  216 |                 return inst['']
  217 |             if inst.get('value'):
  218 |                 return inst['value']
  219 |             return ''.join(str(v) for v in inst.values())
  220 |         return (inst.get(subfield) or inst.get(subfield.lower())
  221 |                 or inst.get(subfield.upper()) or '')
  222 |     return inst if (subfield is None or subfield == '') else ''
  223 | 
  224 | 
  225 | def _field_values(record, field, subfield=None):
  226 |     """All non-empty instance values of ``field`` (optionally a subfield)."""
  227 |     out = []
  228 |     for inst in _instances(record, field):
  229 |         v = _inst_value(inst, subfield)
  230 |         if v:
  231 |             out.append(str(v))
  232 |     return out
  233 | 
  234 | 
  235 | def _norm(term):
```

<!-- ─── страница 55 ─── -->

```py
  236 |     """Normalize a term for matching (casefold + trim). ИРБИS inverted terms are
  237 |     matched case-insensitively; we keep the original ``term`` for display."""
  238 |     return (term or '').strip().casefold()
  239 | 
  240 | 
  241 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  242 | # Search-expression parsing. ``PREFIX=term`` (e.g. ``T=каталог``, ``A=Иванова``).
  243 | # A bare term with no recognized prefix is treated as a title (``T=``) search.
  244 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  245 | def parse_expr(expr):
  246 |     """Parse ``PREFIX=term`` -> (prefix, term). Unknown/absent prefix => ('T', expr).
  247 | 
  248 |     The prefix match is case-insensitive and tolerant of a trailing '='
  249 |     (``T=``/``t=``). A bare string with no '=' searches titles."""
  250 |     if not expr:
  251 |         return ('T', '')
  252 |     s = expr.strip()
  253 |     if '=' in s:
  254 |         pre, term = s.split('=', 1)
  255 |         pre = pre.strip().upper()
  256 |         if pre in SEARCH_PREFIXES:
  257 |             return (pre, term.strip())
  258 |     return ('T', s)
  259 | 
  260 | 
  261 | class CatalogError(Exception):
  262 |     """A catalog operation error (record not found, etc.)."""
  263 | 
  264 | 
  265 | class CatalogStore:
  266 |     """Own sqlite-backed bibliographic record store + CRUD.
  267 | 
  268 |     Parameters
  269 |     ----------
  270 |     db_path : str
  271 |         sqlite path. Use ``':memory:'`` or a temp file in tests.
  272 |     access_store : object | None
  273 |         Optional A5 vocabulary store passed to ФЛК so dictionary rules (language
  274 |         / worklist codes) can validate against the seeded vocabularies. When None,
  275 |         those rules no-op (the engine returns no violation it can't substantiate).
  276 |     brief_pft / full_pft : str | None
  277 |         Override the default brief / full display formats (PFT bodies).
  278 |     authority : object | None
  279 |         Optional :class:`access.authority.AuthorityStore` handle. When wired,
  280 |         ``save`` resolves pending authority references (a field instance carrying
  281 |         an ``_authority_ref`` subfield) through ``authority.substitute`` — filling
  282 |         ``^a/^b/^g`` + the ``^3`` link before ФЛК / index (INTEGRATION_MAP edge
  283 |         6.1). When None, save is unchanged: the client must supply ``^3`` itself.
  284 |     """
  285 | 
  286 |     def __init__(self, db_path=':memory:', access_store=None,
  287 |                  brief_pft=None, full_pft=None, authority=None):
  288 |         self.db_path = db_path
  289 |         self.access_store = access_store
  290 |         self.authority = authority
```

<!-- ─── страница 56 ─── -->

```py
  291 |         self.brief_pft = brief_pft or DEFAULT_BRIEF_PFT
  292 |         self.full_pft = full_pft or DEFAULT_FULL_PFT
  293 |         self._local = threading.local()
  294 |         self.ensure_schema()
  295 | 
  296 |     # ---- connection / schema ---- #
  297 |     def _conn(self):
  298 |         c = getattr(self._local, 'conn', None)
  299 |         if c is None:
  300 |             c = sqlite3.connect(self.db_path)
  301 |             c.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
  302 |             c.execute('PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON')
  303 |             self._local.conn = c
  304 |         return c
  305 | 
  306 |     def ensure_schema(self):
  307 |         c = self._conn()
  308 |         c.executescript(SCHEMA_SQLITE)
  309 |         c.commit()
  310 | 
  311 |     # ------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  312 |     # Validation (ФЛК A2 on save). Returns the engine result dict
  313 |     # {overallSeverity, canSave, violations:[...]}.
  314 |     # ------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  315 |     def validate(self, record, *, phase='save', current_mfn=None,
  316 |                  tenant_overrides=None):
  317 |         """Run ФЛК over ``record`` for ``phase`` (default the full ``save`` run).
  318 | 
  319 |         The A5 vocabulary store is threaded through so dictionary rules resolve
  320 |         against the seeded vocabularies; the inverted index is wired as the ФЛК
  321 |         ``dup_index`` so the (carried) duplicate-inventory rule can fire."""
  322 |         return flk.validate(
  323 |             record, phase=phase, store=self.access_store,
  324 |             current_mfn=current_mfn, dup_index=self._dup_index,
  325 |             tenant_overrides=tenant_overrides)
  326 | 
  327 |     def _dup_index(self, index, value, current_mfn):
  328 |         """ФЛК duplicate-predicate hook: does another ACTIVE record carry this
  329 |         inverted term? ``index`` is the ИРБИS index code (e.g. 'IN='); we map it
  330 |         to our internal prefix and look it up. Returns a list of other mfns."""
  331 |         prefix = (index or '').rstrip('=').upper()
  332 |         if prefix not in SEARCH_PREFIXES:
  333 |             return []
  334 |         # current_mfn is the db-scoped mfn being saved; without a db we can't
  335 |         # scope, so dedup across all dbs on the term (conservative — a warning).
  336 |         rows = self._conn().execute(
  337 |             '''SELECT r.mfn, r.db FROM record_index ri
  338 |                JOIN record r ON r.id = ri.record_id
  339 |                WHERE ri.prefix=? AND ri.term_norm=? AND r.status='active' ''',
  340 |             (prefix, _norm(value))).fetchall()
  341 |         return [r['mfn'] for r in rows if r['mfn'] != current_mfn]
  342 | 
  343 |     # ------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  344 |     # Indexing (extract inverted terms from a record).
  345 |     # ------------------------------------------------------------------- #
```

<!-- ─── страница 57 ─── -->

```py
  346 |     def index_terms(self, record):
  347 |         """Extract ``[(prefix, term), ...]`` from a record per ``INDEX_SPEC``.
  348 | 
  349 |         Public so a caller (or a reindex job) can preview what a record will be
  350 |         findable by. Duplicate (prefix, normalized-term) pairs are collapsed."""
  351 |         seen = set()
  352 |         out = []
  353 |         for prefix, field, subfield in INDEX_SPEC:
  354 |             for term in _field_values(record, field, subfield):
  355 |                 key = (prefix, _norm(term))
  356 |                 if key in seen or not _norm(term):
  357 |                     continue
  358 |                 seen.add(key)
  359 |                 out.append((prefix, term))
  360 |         return out
  361 | 
  362 |     def _reindex(self, conn, record_id, record):
  363 |         conn.execute('DELETE FROM record_index WHERE record_id=?', (record_id,))
  364 |         rows = [(record_id, prefix, term, _norm(term))
  365 |                 for prefix, term in self.index_terms(record)]
  366 |         if rows:
  367 |             conn.executemany(
  368 |                 'INSERT INTO record_index(record_id,prefix,term,term_norm) '
  369 |                 'VALUES(?,?,?,?)', rows)
  370 | 
  371 |     # ------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  372 |     # CRUD.
  373 |     # ------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  374 |     def _next_mfn(self, conn, db):
  375 |         r = conn.execute('SELECT COALESCE(MAX(mfn),0)+1 AS n FROM record WHERE db=?',
  376 |                          (db,)).fetchone()
  377 |         return r['n']
  378 | 
  379 |     def _row(self, db, mfn, include_deleted=False):
  380 |         sql = 'SELECT * FROM record WHERE db=? AND mfn=?'
  381 |         if not include_deleted:
  382 |             sql += " AND status='active'"
  383 |         r = self._conn().execute(sql, (db, mfn)).fetchone()
  384 |         return r
  385 | 
  386 |     def save(self, db, record, *, mfn=None, skip_warnings=False,
  387 |              tenant_overrides=None, authority=None):
  388 |         """Validate then upsert a record.
  389 | 
  390 |         Pipeline:
  391 |           0. (edge 6.1) if an ``authority`` handle is available (per-call arg or
  392 |              the store default), resolve any pending authority reference into the
  393 |              record IN PLACE — filling ``^a/^b/^g`` + the ``^3`` link — BEFORE
  394 |              validation/index, so the saved record + index already carry ``^3``.
  395 |           1. run ФЛК (A2) — if ANY severity-1 (непреодолимая) violation, REJECT:
  396 |              return ``{id:None, mfn:None, saved:False, violations:[...]}`` and
  397 |              touch no row.
  398 |           2. otherwise upsert (insert with a fresh sequential mfn, or update the
  399 |              existing row when ``mfn`` is given) and (re)build the inverted index.
  400 | 
```

<!-- ─── страница 58 ─── -->

```py
  401 |         ``skip_warnings`` is accepted for the warning-acknowledgement workflow:
  402 |         severity-2 violations never block a save in either case here (they're
  403 |         преодолимые), but the flag is surfaced in the result for the caller/UI.
  404 | 
  405 |         Returns ``{id, mfn, saved, violations, overallSeverity, canSave}``.
  406 |         """
  407 |         conn = self._conn()
  408 |         target_mfn = mfn
  409 | 
  410 |         # Edge 6.1: pull authority subfields + ^3 before validation/index.
  411 |         auth = authority if authority is not None else self.authority
  412 |         if auth is not None:
  413 |             self.resolve_authority_refs(record, authority=auth)
  414 | 
  415 |         existing = self._row(db, mfn, include_deleted=True) if mfn is not None else None
  416 | 
  417 |         res = self.validate(record, current_mfn=target_mfn,
  418 |                             tenant_overrides=tenant_overrides)
  419 |         violations = res['violations']
  420 |         if not res['canSave']:                         # severity-1 present -> reject
  421 |             return {
  422 |                 'id': None, 'mfn': None, 'saved': False,
  423 |                 'violations': violations,
  424 |                 'overallSeverity': res['overallSeverity'],
  425 |                 'canSave': False,
  426 |             }
  427 | 
  428 |         now = time.time()
  429 |         data_json = json.dumps(record, ensure_ascii=False)
  430 |         if existing is not None:
  431 |             conn.execute(
  432 |                 'UPDATE record SET data_json=?, status=?, updated=? WHERE id=?',
  433 |                 (data_json, 'active', now, existing['id']))
  434 |             record_id = existing['id']
  435 |             assigned_mfn = existing['mfn']
  436 |         else:
  437 |             assigned_mfn = mfn if mfn is not None else self._next_mfn(conn, db)
  438 |             cur = conn.execute(
  439 |                 'INSERT INTO record(db,mfn,data_json,status,created,updated) '
  440 |                 'VALUES(?,?,?,?,?,?)',
  441 |                 (db, assigned_mfn, data_json, 'active', now, now))
  442 |             record_id = cur.lastrowid
  443 |         self._reindex(conn, record_id, record)
  444 |         conn.commit()
  445 |         return {
  446 |             'id': record_id, 'mfn': assigned_mfn, 'saved': True,
  447 |             'violations': violations,
  448 |             'overallSeverity': res['overallSeverity'],
  449 |             'canSave': True,
  450 |             'skippedWarnings': bool(skip_warnings and violations),
  451 |         }
  452 | 
  453 |     # ------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  454 |     # Authority↔Catalog seam (INTEGRATION_MAP edge 6.1).
  455 |     #
```

<!-- ─── страница 59 ─── -->

```py
  456 |     # A field instance asks for authority substitution by carrying an
  457 |     # ``_authority_ref`` subfield = the authority record id (e.g.
  458 |     # ``{'700': [{'_authority_ref': 42}]}``). ``apply_authority`` resolves that
  459 |     # id through the authority store and fills ``^a/^b/^g/…`` + the ``^3`` link
  460 |     # via ``authority.substitute`` (the same fill-map the UI uses), then drops the
  461 |     # ref marker. ``resolve_authority_refs`` sweeps every fill-mappable field on a
  462 |     # record (called from ``save`` when an authority handle is wired).
  463 |     # ------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  464 |     def apply_authority(self, record, field, authority_id, *, instance=0,
  465 |                         authority=None):
  466 |         """Fill one field instance's subfields from an authority record + ``^3``.
  467 | 
  468 |         Resolves ``authority_id`` in the base bound to ``field`` (via the authority
  469 |         module's FILL_MAP), runs ``authority.substitute(field, rec)`` to build the
  470 |         ``{^a/^b/^g, '3': id}`` patch, and merges it into ``record[field][instance]``
  471 |         in place (mutating + returning the record). The ``^3`` link is always set
  472 |         for linkable fields (DB_AUTHORITY §5.2). Returns the merged patch too via
  473 |         the instance; raises the authority module's errors on a missing record /
  474 |         unknown field tag so the caller sees a broken ^3 reference loudly.
  475 |         """
  476 |         auth = authority if authority is not None else self.authority
  477 |         if auth is None:
  478 |             raise CatalogError(
  479 |                 'apply_authority called without an authority handle wired')
  480 |         tag = str(field)
  481 |         spec = _authority.FILL_MAP.get(tag)
  482 |         if spec is None:
  483 |             raise _authority.UnknownCatalogField(
  484 |                 'no fill-map for catalog field %r' % tag)
  485 |         rec = auth.get(spec['db'], authority_id)
  486 |         if rec is None:
  487 |             raise _authority.AuthorityNotFound(
  488 |                 'authority %r not found in %s for field %s'
  489 |                 % (authority_id, spec['db'], tag))
  490 |         patch = _authority.substitute(tag, rec)
  491 | 
  492 |         insts = record.get(field)
  493 |         if insts is None:
  494 |             insts = [{}]
  495 |             record[field] = insts
  496 |         elif not isinstance(insts, list):
  497 |             insts = [insts]
  498 |             record[field] = insts
  499 |         if instance >= len(insts):
  500 |             insts.extend({} for _ in range(instance - len(insts) + 1))
  501 |         cur = insts[instance]
  502 |         if not isinstance(cur, dict):
  503 |             cur = {'': cur} if cur else {}
  504 |             insts[instance] = cur
  505 |         cur.pop('_authority_ref', None)
  506 |         cur.update(patch)
  507 |         return record
  508 | 
  509 |     def resolve_authority_refs(self, record, *, authority=None):
  510 |         """Sweep ``record`` for pending ``_authority_ref`` markers and apply each.
```

<!-- ─── страница 60 ─── -->

```py
  511 | 
  512 |         For every fill-mappable field (700/701/710/606/607/…) whose instance
  513 |         carries ``_authority_ref``, resolve+fill it in place. Instances without the
  514 |         marker are left untouched (so an operator-supplied ``^3`` survives). Returns
  515 |         the list of ``(field, instance, authority_id)`` triples applied."""
  516 |         applied = []
  517 |         for field in list(record.keys()):
  518 |             if str(field) not in _authority.FILL_MAP:
  519 |                 continue
  520 |             insts = record[field]
  521 |             if not isinstance(insts, list):
  522 |                 insts = [insts]
  523 |             for i, inst in enumerate(insts):
  524 |                 if isinstance(inst, dict) and inst.get('_authority_ref') is not None:
  525 |                     aid = inst['_authority_ref']
  526 |                     self.apply_authority(record, field, aid, instance=i,
  527 |                                          authority=authority)
  528 |                     applied.append((str(field), i, aid))
  529 |         return applied
  530 | 
  531 |     # ------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  532 |     # Exemplar status (910^A) — the Catalog↔Circulation seam (edges 2.1/2.2).
  533 |     #
  534 |     # A bibliographic record holds its copies as repeating ``910`` fields; each
  535 |     # carries a shelfmark/inventory key (``910^b``) and an availability flag
  536 |     # (``910^A``: 0 free / 1 issued / …). Circulation flips ``910^A`` here when a
  537 |     # copy is checked out / returned, and reads it to know what is free. The item
  538 |     # is resolved by inventory number (``910^b``), the same key circulation stores
  539 |     # as its ``loan.item`` (903/910^b per the spec).
  540 |     # ------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  541 |     @staticmethod
  542 |     def _exemplar_key(inst):
  543 |         """Inventory/shelfmark key of a 910 instance (``910^b``), or '' if none."""
  544 |         if isinstance(inst, dict):
  545 |             return str(inst.get('b') or inst.get('B') or '')
  546 |         return ''
  547 | 
  548 |     def find_exemplar(self, db, item):
  549 |         """Locate the ``910`` instance keyed by inventory number ``item``.
  550 | 
  551 |         Scans active records' ``910`` fields for one whose ``910^b`` == ``item``.
  552 |         Returns ``(mfn, index, instance_dict)`` or ``None`` — the address
  553 |         circulation uses to flip ``910^A`` for that specific copy."""
  554 |         target = str(item)
  555 |         for r in self._conn().execute(
  556 |                 "SELECT mfn, data_json FROM record WHERE db=? AND status='active' "
  557 |                 'ORDER BY mfn', (db,)).fetchall():
  558 |             record = json.loads(r['data_json'])
  559 |             insts = record.get('910')
  560 |             if insts is None:
  561 |                 continue
  562 |             if not isinstance(insts, list):
  563 |                 insts = [insts]
  564 |             for i, inst in enumerate(insts):
  565 |                 if self._exemplar_key(inst) == target:
```

```py
  569 |     def exemplar_status(self, db, item):
  570 |         """Read ``910^A`` for the copy keyed by inventory ``item`` (None if absent)."""
  571 |         found = self.find_exemplar(db, item)
  572 |         if found is None:
  573 |             return None
  574 |         inst = found[2]
  575 |         if isinstance(inst, dict):
  576 |             return str(inst.get('a') or inst.get('A') or '')
  577 |         return ''
  578 | 
  579 |     def is_available(self, db, item):
  580 |         """True iff the copy keyed by ``item`` exists and its ``910^A`` is FREE.
  581 | 
  582 |         The availability read circulation uses for free-exemplar matching / hold
  583 |         placement (edge 2.2). Any non-free / unknown status ⇒ not available."""
  584 |         st = self.exemplar_status(db, item)
  585 |         return st == EXEMPLAR_FREE
  586 | 
  587 |     def set_exemplar_status(self, db, item, status):
  588 |         """Flip ``910^A`` of the copy keyed by inventory ``item`` to ``status``.
  589 | 
  590 |         The write-back that closes edges 2.1/2.2: circulation calls this on
  591 |         checkout (``status=EXEMPLAR_ISSUED``) and return (``status=EXEMPLAR_FREE``).
  592 |         Re-indexes the touched record so search stays consistent. Returns the
  593 |         ``mfn`` whose copy was flipped, or None if no such copy exists (circulation
  594 |         then degrades gracefully — it still records its own loan)."""
  595 |         found = self.find_exemplar(db, item)
  596 |         if found is None:
  597 |             return None
  598 |         mfn, idx, _inst = found
  599 |         conn = self._conn()
  600 |         r = self._row(db, mfn, include_deleted=False)
  601 |         if r is None:
  602 |             return None
  603 |         record = json.loads(r['data_json'])
  604 |         insts = record.get('910')
  605 |         if not isinstance(insts, list):
  606 |             insts = [insts]
  607 |             record['910'] = insts
  608 |         inst = insts[idx]
  609 |         if not isinstance(inst, dict):
  610 |             inst = {'b': str(item)}
  611 |             insts[idx] = inst
  612 |         inst['a'] = str(status)
  613 |         conn.execute('UPDATE record SET data_json=?, updated=? WHERE id=?',
  614 |                      (json.dumps(record, ensure_ascii=False), time.time(), r['id']))
  615 |         self._reindex(conn, r['id'], record)
  616 |         conn.commit()
  617 |         return mfn
  618 | 
  619 |     def get(self, db, mfn, include_deleted=False):
  620 |         """Return the stored record dict (the I1 draft), or None if absent."""
```

```py
  626 |     def get_meta(self, db, mfn, include_deleted=True):
  627 |         """Return the row metadata (id/mfn/status/created/updated), no data."""
  628 |         r = self._row(db, mfn, include_deleted=include_deleted)
  629 |         if r is None:
  630 |             return None
  631 |         return {'id': r['id'], 'db': r['db'], 'mfn': r['mfn'],
  632 |                 'status': r['status'], 'created': r['created'],
  633 |                 'updated': r['updated']}
  634 | 
  635 |     def delete(self, db, mfn):
  636 |         """Logical delete: flip status to 'deleted' and drop the inverted index
  637 |         (so it vanishes from search) while keeping the row + mfn. Returns True if
  638 |         a record was deleted, False if it wasn't active/found."""
  639 |         conn = self._conn()
  640 |         r = self._row(db, mfn, include_deleted=False)
  641 |         if r is None:
  642 |             return False
  643 |         conn.execute("UPDATE record SET status='deleted', updated=? WHERE id=?",
  644 |                      (time.time(), r['id']))
  645 |         conn.execute('DELETE FROM record_index WHERE record_id=?', (r['id'],))
  646 |         conn.commit()
  647 |         return True
  648 | 
  649 |     def undelete(self, db, mfn):
  650 |         """Restore a logically-deleted record and rebuild its index."""
  651 |         conn = self._conn()
  652 |         r = self._row(db, mfn, include_deleted=True)
  653 |         if r is None or r['status'] != 'deleted':
  654 |             return False
  655 |         record = json.loads(r['data_json'])
  656 |         conn.execute("UPDATE record SET status='active', updated=? WHERE id=?",
  657 |                      (time.time(), r['id']))
  658 |         self._reindex(conn, r['id'], record)
  659 |         conn.commit()
  660 |         return True
  661 | 
  662 |     # ------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  663 |     # Search.
  664 |     # ------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  665 |     def search(self, db, expr, limit=20, offset=0):
  666 |         """Search ``db`` by a ``PREFIX=term`` expression.
  667 | 
  668 |         Looks the normalized term up in ``record_index`` (only ACTIVE records are
  669 |         indexed), returns ``{total, items:[{mfn, brief, ...}]}`` with a brief
  670 |         display rendered via PFT (A1). ``limit``/``offset`` page the items; ``total``
  671 |         is the full match count.
  672 |         """
  673 |         prefix, term = parse_expr(expr)
  674 |         term_norm = _norm(term)
  675 |         conn = self._conn()
```

```py
  676 |         # total distinct matching records
  677 |         total = conn.execute(
  678 |             '''SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT r.id) AS n FROM record_index ri
  679 |                JOIN record r ON r.id = ri.record_id
  680 |                WHERE r.db=? AND r.status='active'
  681 |                  AND ri.prefix=? AND ri.term_norm=?''',
  682 |             (db, prefix, term_norm)).fetchone()['n']
  683 |         rows = conn.execute(
  684 |             '''SELECT DISTINCT r.id, r.mfn, r.data_json FROM record_index ri
  685 |                JOIN record r ON r.id = ri.record_id
  686 |                WHERE r.db=? AND r.status='active'
  687 |                  AND ri.prefix=? AND ri.term_norm=?
  688 |                ORDER BY r.mfn LIMIT ? OFFSET ?''',
  689 |             (db, prefix, term_norm, limit, offset)).fetchall()
  690 |         items = []
  691 |         for r in rows:
  692 |             record = json.loads(r['data_json'])
  693 |             items.append({
  694 |                 'mfn': r['mfn'],
  695 |                 'brief': self._render(self.brief_pft, record, r['mfn']),
  696 |             })
  697 |         return {'total': total, 'items': items, 'prefix': prefix, 'term': term}
  698 | 
  699 |     # ------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  700 |     # Display (PFT A1 render).
  701 |     # ------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  702 |     def _render(self, fmt, record, mfn):
  703 |         out = pft.eval(fmt, record, {'mfn': mfn})
  704 |         # Defensive projection: if a format yields nothing (e.g. a record with no
  705 |         # 200^a that slipped past as a soft warning), fall back to a plain
  706 |         # title/author projection so brief is never empty for a real record.
  707 |         if out:
  708 |             return out
  709 |         title = (_field_values(record, '200', 'a') or [''])[0]
  710 |         author = (_field_values(record, '700', 'a') or [''])[0]
  711 |         parts = [p for p in (author, title) if p]
  712 |         return '. '.join(parts)
  713 | 
  714 |     def brief(self, db, mfn):
  715 |         """Render the brief display of a stored record via PFT (A1), or '' if absent."""
  716 |         record = self.get(db, mfn)
  717 |         if record is None:
  718 |             return ''
  719 |         return self._render(self.brief_pft, record, mfn)
  720 | 
  721 |     def full(self, db, mfn):
  722 |         """Render the full display of a stored record via PFT (A1), or '' if absent."""
  723 |         record = self.get(db, mfn)
  724 |         if record is None:
  725 |             return ''
  726 |         out = pft.eval(self.full_pft, record, {'mfn': mfn})
  727 |         return out if out else self._render(self.brief_pft, record, mfn)
  728 | 
  729 |     # ------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  730 |     # Listing / counts (small conveniences for callers / tests).
```

```py
  731 |     # ------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  732 |     def count(self, db, include_deleted=False):
  733 |         sql = 'SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM record WHERE db=?'
  734 |         if not include_deleted:
  735 |             sql += " AND status='active'"
  736 |         return self._conn().execute(sql, (db,)).fetchone()['n']
  737 | 
  738 |     def list_mfns(self, db, include_deleted=False, limit=100, offset=0):
  739 |         sql = 'SELECT mfn FROM record WHERE db=?'
  740 |         if not include_deleted:
  741 |             sql += " AND status='active'"
  742 |         sql += ' ORDER BY mfn LIMIT ? OFFSET ?'
  743 |         return [r['mfn'] for r in self._conn().execute(
  744 |             sql, (db, limit, offset)).fetchall()]
```

### Файл: `irbis-web/backend/access/circulation.py`  · строк: 1101

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """Circulation engine — loan / hold / return / renew + business rules.
    3 | 
    4 | Gap E2, epic #188. Implements SPEC_business_circulation.md, FIRST shippable slice.
    5 | 
    6 | What this module is
    7 | -------------------
    8 | A *self-contained* circulation engine: the domain rules that decide whether a
    9 | loan / renewal / hold is **allowed**, and that **compute** fines, replacement
   10 | values and queue positions — over a small own sqlite store. It is deliberately
   11 | standalone: pure stdlib + ``sqlite3`` (dev parity with the rest of the backend,
   12 | ADR-004), no network I/O, no new pip deps.
   13 | 
   14 | The spec is emphatic that a *rule* is not an *operation*: a rule is the predicate
   15 | ``decide(ctx) -> {allow|deny|require_override, reasons[], computed?}`` that gates
   16 | the ws3 operation (create RDR.40, set ``40^F``/``40^L``/``40^U`` …). Here the two
   17 | are folded together for the slice — the engine both decides and applies to its
   18 | own store — but the *decision* is always exposed (``Decision``) so the rule layer
   19 | is testable and reusable in isolation, exactly as §0 demands.
   20 | 
   21 | The moving parts
   22 | ----------------
   23 |   1. :class:`CirculationStore` — own sqlite store with four tables:
   24 |        * ``reader(id, category, blocked)``
   25 |        * ``loan(id, reader, item, due, returned, renewals, lost_status)``
   26 |        * ``hold(id, reader, item, status, queued_at, ready_at)``
   27 |        * ``fine(id, reader, loan, amount, status, kind)``
   28 |      create-on-init; ``:memory:`` / temp file in tests.
   29 |   2. :func:`default_policy` — the per-tenant ``circ_policy`` dict, defaults
   30 |      straight from §5.3 / §7 of the spec (limits by category, ``max_prolong=5``,
   31 |      ``fine_per_day`` / ``fine_cap`` / ``overdue_grace_days``, ``lost_after_days``,
   32 |      ``hold_shelf_days`` …).
   33 |   3. :class:`CirculationEngine` — the operations: ``checkout`` (limits + debtor
   34 |      gate, ``staff_override``), ``return_item`` (clear debt marker, trigger next
   35 |      hold), ``renew`` (holds-block-renewal + renew cap), ``place_hold`` (FIFO
   36 |      position), ``accrue_fines`` (the §3.2 formula), ``mark_lost`` (candidate →
   37 |      confirm), ``queue_position``. Every gated op returns a :class:`Decision`.
   38 | 
   39 | Rule decisions enforced (per spec section)
   40 | ------------------------------------------
   41 |   * §1 holds-block-renewal — ``holds_block_renewal`` ∈ {block, threshold, allow};
```

<!-- ─── страница 65 ─── -->

```py
   42 |     default ``block``: an active hold/queue on the item denies renewal
   43 |     (``hold_exists``) unless ``staff_override`` (right ``circ.renew.override_hold``).
   44 |     Renewal cap ``max_prolong`` (default 5).
   45 |   * §2 debtor: two separate rules — reader self-service gate (a *hard* debtor is
   46 |     denied checkout/renew with ``reader_has_debt``) vs staff override
   47 |     (``circ.lend.override_debt``) which lets the librarian lend over the debt.
   48 |   * §3 fines — ``min(fine_per_day × billable_days, fine_cap)`` after
   49 |     ``overdue_grace_days``; ``fine_per_day == 0`` ⇒ fine-free; accrual is
   50 |     idempotent per ``(loan, day)``.
   51 |   * §4 lost — overdue beyond ``lost_after_days`` ⇒ ``lost_candidate`` + staff
   52 |     alert (no auto-debt); ``mark_lost(confirm=True)`` ⇒ ``lost`` + replacement
   53 |     charge ``910^E × multiplier + fee`` (fallback when price empty).
   54 |   * §5 limits — ``max_books`` / ``max_dolg_books`` by category; over-limit ⇒
   55 |     ``limit_exceeded`` unless ``staff_override``.
   56 |   * §6 hold queue — FIFO position; return hands the freed item to the queue head
   57 |     (``hold_ready`` + shelf TTL).
   58 | 
   59 | Events
   60 | ------
   61 | Operations return *event intents* (``{'event','recipient','payload'}``) for A6
   62 | (``hold_ready`` / ``fine_charged`` / ``renewal_confirmed`` / ``fine_paid`` /
   63 | ``hold_cancelled`` / ``lost_confirmed`` / ``staff_alert`` …). If an
   64 | :class:`access.notifications.NotificationQueue` is handed to the engine (the
   65 | optional ``notifications=`` handle, mirroring the ``catalog=`` seam) each intent
   66 | is also **rendered + enqueued** through A6: the event is mapped to its template,
   67 | a reader-friendly render context is built (item→title, epoch→date, price→amount,
   68 | currency default), and the notice is queued on the template's default channels
   69 | (idempotent by dedup_key — no double-send). A reader-facing event notifies the
   70 | reader; ``staff_alert`` is re-addressed to the staff recipient. Dispatch is
   71 | best-effort and fully isolated: an unknown event or any A6 error is skipped, the
   72 | circulation operation still succeeds. With no handle the engine is standalone —
   73 | intents are only returned, never dispatched (back-compat).
   74 | """
   75 | import sqlite3
   76 | import threading
   77 | import time
   78 | 
   79 | # Optional A6 integration — emit events through its idempotent enqueue when a
   80 | # queue is wired; else we just return event intents. Never a hard dependency.
   81 | try:  # pragma: no cover - depends on sibling A6 module / wiring
   82 |     from access import notifications as _notifications  # type: ignore
   83 | except Exception:  # ImportError when run in isolation
   84 |     _notifications = None
   85 | 
   86 | 
   87 | SECONDS_PER_DAY = 86400
   88 | 
   89 | 
   90 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   91 | # Policy — the per-tenant ``circ_policy`` (defaults from SPEC §5.3 / §7).
   92 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   93 | # Limit matrix keyed by reader category (50.mnu: В01–В05 adults, Д01–Д03 kids,
   94 | # STD student, GUEST guest). Values are the spec's starting defaults; a tenant
   95 | # tunes them via the low-code configurator (D1). ``_DEFAULT`` is the fallback
   96 | # profile used before 50.mnu is seeded (A5) / for an unknown category.
```

```py
  111 | 
  112 | def default_policy(tenant_id='public'):
  113 |     """Return a fresh per-tenant ``circ_policy`` dict (defaults from §5.3 / §7).
  114 | 
  115 |     A fresh deep-ish copy each call so a caller mutating one tenant's policy
  116 |     never leaks into another (isolation, AC7). Values are the spec's documented
  117 |     starting defaults — every one is meant to be tuned per-tenant.
  118 |     """
  119 |     return {
  120 |         'tenant_id': tenant_id,
  121 |         # §5 limits — by category × item kind (here: by category).
  122 |         'limits': {cat: dict(lim) for cat, lim in _LIMIT_MATRIX.items()},
  123 |         # §1 renewal.
  124 |         'renewal': {
  125 |             'holds_block_renewal': 'block',   # block | threshold | allow
  126 |             'hold_block_threshold': 0,        # threshold mode: queue len that still allows
  127 |             'strong_prolong': True,
  128 |         },
  129 |         # §2 debtor.
  130 |         'debt': {
  131 |             'overdue_grace_days': 1,          # soft window: not yet a hard debtor
  132 |             'reader_block_on_hard': True,     # hard debtor self-service gate
  133 |             'override_grant': 'circ.lend.override_debt',
  134 |         },
  135 |         # §3 fines — min(fine_per_day × billable_days, fine_cap).
  136 |         'fine': {
  137 |             'fine_per_day': {'_DEFAULT': 5.0, 'Д01': 0.0, 'Д02': 0.0, 'Д03': 0.0,
  138 |                              'rare_fund': 20.0},
  139 |             'fine_cap': 500.0,                # ceiling per loan (≤ item price, §3.2)
  140 |             'overdue_grace_days': 1,          # billing grace before accrual starts
  141 |             'currency': 'RUB',
  142 |             'pay_dbn': 'PAY',
  143 |             'waive_grant': 'circ.fine.waive',
  144 |         },
  145 |         # §4 lost.
  146 |         'lost': {
  147 |             'lost_after_days': 60,            # overdue beyond this ⇒ lost_candidate
  148 |             'multiplier': 1.0,                # replacement = price × mult + fee
  149 |             'handling_fee': 0.0,
  150 |             'default_replacement_value': 100.0,  # fallback when 910^E empty/0
  151 |             'lost_supersedes_fine': True,
```

```py
  163 | def category_limits(policy, category):
  164 |     """The limit row for ``category`` (falls back to ``_DEFAULT``)."""
  165 |     limits = policy['limits']
  166 |     return limits.get(category) or limits['_DEFAULT']
  167 | 
  168 | 
  169 | def fine_per_day(policy, category, item_kind=None):
  170 |     """Per-day fine rate for ``category`` (and optional ``item_kind`` override)."""
  171 |     table = policy['fine']['fine_per_day']
  172 |     if item_kind and item_kind in table:
  173 |         return float(table[item_kind])
  174 |     if category in table:
  175 |         return float(table[category])
  176 |     return float(table.get('_DEFAULT', 0.0))
  177 | 
  178 | 
  179 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  180 | # Decision — the rule predicate result (allow | deny | require_override).
  181 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  182 | ALLOW = 'allow'
  183 | DENY = 'deny'
  184 | REQUIRE_OVERRIDE = 'require_override'
  185 | 
  186 | 
  187 | class Decision:
  188 |     """Result of a rule: ``decision`` + machine ``reasons`` + ``computed`` data.
  189 | 
  190 |     ``ok`` is True only for :data:`ALLOW`. ``reasons`` is a list of short stable
  191 |     codes (``limit_exceeded`` / ``reader_has_debt`` / ``hold_exists`` /
  192 |     ``max_prolong_reached`` …) for the UI / notification layer. ``events`` carries
  193 |     any A6 event intents the operation produced.
  194 |     """
  195 | 
  196 |     def __init__(self, decision, reasons=None, computed=None, events=None):
  197 |         self.decision = decision
  198 |         self.reasons = list(reasons or [])
  199 |         self.computed = dict(computed or {})
  200 |         self.events = list(events or [])
  201 | 
  202 |     @property
  203 |     def ok(self):
  204 |         return self.decision == ALLOW
  205 | 
  206 |     def __repr__(self):
```

```py
  207 |         return 'Decision(%s, reasons=%r, computed=%r)' % (
  208 |             self.decision, self.reasons, self.computed)
  209 | 
  210 | 
  211 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  212 | # Store — own sqlite (reader / loan / hold / fine). create-on-init.
  213 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  214 | SCHEMA_SQLITE = """
  215 | CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS reader (
  216 |   id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  217 |   category TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '_DEFAULT',
  218 |   blocked INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0
  219 | );
  220 | CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS loan (
  221 |   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
  222 |   reader TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES reader(id),
  223 |   item TEXT NOT NULL,             -- shifr / 903
  224 |   item_kind TEXT,                 -- читальный зал / редкий фонд / NULL
  225 |   item_price REAL,                -- 910^E (replacement basis)
  226 |   due REAL NOT NULL,              -- planned return epoch (40^E)
  227 |   checked_out_at REAL NOT NULL,
  228 |   returned INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,   -- 40^F: 0='******' on-hand, 1=returned
  229 |   returned_at REAL,
  230 |   renewals INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,   -- prolong_count (MAXPROLONGCOUNT)
  231 |   lost_status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'none'  -- none | lost_candidate | lost
  232 |        CHECK (lost_status IN ('none','lost_candidate','lost'))
  233 | );
  234 | CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS loan_reader_idx ON loan(reader, returned);
  235 | CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS loan_item_idx ON loan(item, returned);
  236 | CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS hold (
  237 |   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
  238 |   reader TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES reader(id),
  239 |   item TEXT NOT NULL,
  240 |   status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'queued'   -- queued | ready | fulfilled | cancelled | expired
  241 |        CHECK (status IN ('queued','ready','fulfilled','cancelled','expired')),
  242 |   queued_at REAL NOT NULL,
  243 |   ready_at REAL
  244 | );
  245 | CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS hold_item_idx ON hold(item, status, queued_at);
  246 | CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS fine (
  247 |   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
  248 |   reader TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES reader(id),
  249 |   loan INTEGER REFERENCES loan(id),
  250 |   amount REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  251 |   status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'accrued'  -- accrued | charged | paid | waived
  252 |        CHECK (status IN ('accrued','charged','paid','waived')),
  253 |   kind TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'fine_overdue'  -- fine_overdue | lost_replacement
  254 |        CHECK (kind IN ('fine_overdue','lost_replacement')),
  255 |   accrued_through REAL,           -- last day already accrued (idempotency)
  256 |   updated_at REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT (strftime('%s','now')),
  257 |   UNIQUE(loan, kind)              -- one fine row per loan per kind (§3.4 idempotent)
  258 | );
  259 | CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS fine_reader_idx ON fine(reader, status);
  260 | """
  261 | 
```

```py
  262 | 
  263 | class CirculationStore:
  264 |     """Own sqlite store for circulation state (reader / loan / hold / fine).
  265 | 
  266 |     ``db_path=':memory:'`` (default) or a temp file for tests; create-on-init.
  267 |     Connection is thread-local (house style); ``sqlite3.Row`` rows are returned
  268 |     as plain dicts from the accessors.
  269 |     """
  270 | 
  271 |     def __init__(self, db_path=':memory:'):
  272 |         self.db_path = db_path
  273 |         self._local = threading.local()
  274 |         self.ensure_schema()
  275 | 
  276 |     def _conn(self):
  277 |         c = getattr(self._local, 'conn', None)
  278 |         if c is None:
  279 |             c = sqlite3.connect(self.db_path)
  280 |             c.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
  281 |             c.execute('PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON')
  282 |             self._local.conn = c
  283 |         return c
  284 | 
  285 |     def ensure_schema(self):
  286 |         c = self._conn()
  287 |         c.executescript(SCHEMA_SQLITE)
  288 |         c.commit()
  289 | 
  290 |     # ---- readers ---------------------------------------------------------- #
  291 |     def add_reader(self, reader_id, category='_DEFAULT', blocked=0):
  292 |         c = self._conn()
  293 |         c.execute('INSERT OR REPLACE INTO reader(id,category,blocked) VALUES(?,?,?)',
  294 |                   (reader_id, category, 1 if blocked else 0))
  295 |         c.commit()
  296 |         return self.get_reader(reader_id)
  297 | 
  298 |     def get_reader(self, reader_id):
  299 |         r = self._conn().execute(
  300 |             'SELECT * FROM reader WHERE id=?', (reader_id,)).fetchone()
  301 |         return dict(r) if r else None
  302 | 
  303 |     def set_blocked(self, reader_id, blocked):
  304 |         c = self._conn()
  305 |         c.execute('UPDATE reader SET blocked=? WHERE id=?',
  306 |                   (1 if blocked else 0, reader_id))
  307 |         c.commit()
  308 | 
  309 |     # ---- loans ------------------------------------------------------------ #
  310 |     def add_loan(self, reader_id, item, due, checked_out_at,
  311 |                  item_kind=None, item_price=None):
  312 |         c = self._conn()
  313 |         cur = c.execute(
  314 |             'INSERT INTO loan(reader,item,item_kind,item_price,due,'
  315 |             'checked_out_at) VALUES(?,?,?,?,?,?)',
  316 |             (reader_id, item, item_kind, item_price, due, checked_out_at))
```

```py
  317 |         c.commit()
  318 |         return self.get_loan(cur.lastrowid)
  319 | 
  320 |     def get_loan(self, loan_id):
  321 |         r = self._conn().execute(
  322 |             'SELECT * FROM loan WHERE id=?', (loan_id,)).fetchone()
  323 |         return dict(r) if r else None
  324 | 
  325 |     def loans_on_hand(self, reader_id):
  326 |         """RDR.40 rows still on hand (``40^F='******'``) for a reader."""
  327 |         return [dict(r) for r in self._conn().execute(
  328 |             'SELECT * FROM loan WHERE reader=? AND returned=0 AND lost_status!=? '
  329 |             'ORDER BY id', (reader_id, 'lost')).fetchall()]
  330 | 
  331 |     def count_on_hand(self, reader_id):
  332 |         """``count_on_hand`` = number of RDR.40 with ``40^F='******'`` (§5.4)."""
  333 |         return self._conn().execute(
  334 |             'SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM loan WHERE reader=? AND returned=0 '
  335 |             'AND lost_status!=?', (reader_id, 'lost')).fetchone()['n']
  336 | 
  337 |     def item_on_hand_count(self, item):
  338 |         """Number of on-hand (not returned, not lost) loans of ``item`` (910^b).
  339 | 
  340 |         The read book-provision uses to decide whether an issued copy still
  341 |         belongs to the фонд (INTEGRATION_MAP ребро 4.4/4.7): a copy on a reader's
  342 |         hands is provisioned, just in use. A confirmed-lost copy has left the фонд
  343 |         (``lost_status='lost'``) and is excluded."""
  344 |         return self._conn().execute(
  345 |             'SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM loan WHERE item=? AND returned=0 '
  346 |             'AND lost_status!=?', (item, 'lost')).fetchone()['n']
  347 | 
  348 |     def open_overdue_loans(self, today_epoch, grace_days=0):
  349 |         """On-hand loans past ``due + grace`` (basis for accrual / lost scan)."""
  350 |         cutoff = today_epoch - grace_days * SECONDS_PER_DAY
  351 |         return [dict(r) for r in self._conn().execute(
  352 |             'SELECT * FROM loan WHERE returned=0 AND lost_status!=? AND due<? '
  353 |             'ORDER BY id', ('lost', cutoff)).fetchall()]
  354 | 
  355 |     def mark_returned(self, loan_id, returned_at):
  356 |         c = self._conn()
  357 |         c.execute('UPDATE loan SET returned=1, returned_at=? WHERE id=?',
  358 |                   (returned_at, loan_id))
  359 |         c.commit()
  360 | 
  361 |     def bump_renewal(self, loan_id, new_due):
  362 |         c = self._conn()
  363 |         c.execute('UPDATE loan SET renewals=renewals+1, due=? WHERE id=?',
  364 |                   (new_due, loan_id))
  365 |         c.commit()
  366 | 
  367 |     def set_lost_status(self, loan_id, status):
  368 |         c = self._conn()
  369 |         c.execute('UPDATE loan SET lost_status=? WHERE id=?', (status, loan_id))
  370 |         c.commit()
  371 |
```

```py
  386 |     def active_holds(self, item):

  388 |         return [dict(r) for r in self._conn().execute(

  392 |     def set_hold_status(self, hold_id, status, ready_at=None):

  394 |         if ready_at is not None:
  395 |             c.execute('UPDATE hold SET status=?, ready_at=? WHERE id=?',
  396 |                       (status, ready_at, hold_id))
  397 |         else:
  398 |             c.execute('UPDATE hold SET status=? WHERE id=?', (status, hold_id))
  399 |         c.commit()

  401 |     # ---- fines ------------------------------------------------------------ #
  402 |     def upsert_fine(self, reader_id, loan_id, amount, kind='fine_overdue',
  403 |                     status='accrued', accrued_through=None):
  404 |         """Idempotent per ``(loan, kind)`` — one fine row per loan per kind."""
  405 |         c = self._conn()
  406 |         c.execute(
  407 |             'INSERT INTO fine(reader,loan,amount,kind,status,accrued_through,'
  408 |             'updated_at) VALUES(?,?,?,?,?,?,?) '
  409 |             'ON CONFLICT(loan,kind) DO UPDATE SET amount=excluded.amount, '
  410 |             'status=excluded.status, accrued_through=excluded.accrued_through, '
  411 |             'updated_at=excluded.updated_at',
  412 |             (reader_id, loan_id, amount, kind, status, accrued_through, time.time()))
  413 |         c.commit()
  414 |         return self.get_fine(loan_id, kind)
  415 | 
  416 |     def get_fine(self, loan_id, kind='fine_overdue'):
  417 |         r = self._conn().execute(
  418 |             'SELECT * FROM fine WHERE loan=? AND kind=?',
  419 |             (loan_id, kind)).fetchone()
  420 |         return dict(r) if r else None
  421 | 
  422 |     def set_fine_status(self, fine_id, status):
  423 |         c = self._conn()
  424 |         c.execute('UPDATE fine SET status=?, updated_at=? WHERE id=?',
  425 |                   (status, time.time(), fine_id))
  426 |         c.commit()
```

```py
  428 |     def outstanding_fines(self, reader_id):

  431 |             "SELECT * FROM fine WHERE reader=? AND status IN ('accrued','charged') "

  440 | 
  441 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  442 | # Debt model — §2.1: none | soft | hard.
  443 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  444 | def debt_level(store, policy, reader_id, today_epoch):
  445 |     """Classify a reader's debt (§2.1): ``none`` / ``soft`` / ``hard``.
  446 | 
  447 |     ``hard`` if any of: an overdue on-hand loan past the overdue grace; a
  448 |     confirmed-lost loan with an unpaid replacement; an unpaid fine over the money
  449 |     grace. ``soft`` = something owed but inside grace. ``none`` = clean.
  450 |     """
  451 |     grace_days = policy['debt']['overdue_grace_days']
  452 |     cutoff_hard = today_epoch - grace_days * SECONDS_PER_DAY
  453 | 
  454 |     has_soft = False
  455 |     # overdue on-hand loans
  456 |     for ln in store.loans_on_hand(reader_id):
  457 |         if ln['due'] < today_epoch:
  458 |             if ln['due'] < cutoff_hard:
  459 |                 return 'hard'
  460 |             has_soft = True
  461 |     # unpaid fines / lost replacement
  462 |     fines = store.outstanding_fines(reader_id)
  463 |     for f in fines:
  464 |         if f['kind'] == 'lost_replacement' and f['amount'] > 0:
  465 |             return 'hard'
  466 |     total = sum(f['amount'] for f in fines)
  467 |     if total > 0:
  468 |         # money grace: a token amount stays soft; here grace is the per-day rate
  469 |         # floor — any non-trivial balance is hard. Default money grace = 0 ⇒ hard.
  470 |         return 'hard'
  471 |     return 'soft' if has_soft else 'none'
  472 | 
  473 | 
  474 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  475 | # Engine — operations (each gated op returns a Decision).
  476 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  477 | class CirculationEngine:
  478 |     """Circulation operations over a :class:`CirculationStore` + ``circ_policy``.
  479 | 
  480 |     ``notifier`` (optional) is an :class:`access.notifications.NotificationQueue`;
  481 |     when present, event intents are also enqueued (idempotent). ``tenant`` scopes
```

```py
  482 |     enqueued events. All time arguments are epoch seconds — tests inject ``today``
  483 |     deterministically.
  484 |     """
  485 | 
  486 |     def __init__(self, store=None, policy=None, notifier=None, tenant='public',
  487 |                  catalog=None, catalog_db='IBIS', notifications=None,
  488 |                  staff_recipient='staff', acquisition=None):
  489 |         self.store = store or CirculationStore(':memory:')
  490 |         self.policy = policy or default_policy(tenant)
  491 |         # Optional A6 (notifications) seam — INTEGRATION_MAP edge Circulation→A6.
  492 |         # ``notifications`` is the documented handle (an
  493 |         # :class:`access.notifications.NotificationQueue`); ``notifier`` is a
  494 |         # back-compat alias for the same role. Whichever is supplied wins
  495 |         # (``notifications`` takes precedence if both are given). With no handle
  496 |         # the engine stays fully standalone — events are only *returned* as
  497 |         # intents, never dispatched (back-compat, mirrors the ``catalog=`` seam).
  498 |         self.notifier = notifications if notifications is not None else notifier
  499 |         # Staff-facing events (``staff_alert``) are addressed to a librarian, not
  500 |         # the reader; this is the recipient handle they enqueue to.
  501 |         self.staff_recipient = staff_recipient
  502 |         self.tenant = tenant
  503 |         # Optional Catalog↔Circulation seam (INTEGRATION_MAP edges 2.1/2.2). When a
  504 |         # ``catalog`` (access.catalog.CatalogStore) handle is wired, checkout flips
  505 |         # the matching exemplar's ``910^A`` 0→1 (issued) and return flips it 1→0
  506 |         # (free); availability reads come from the catalog. ``catalog_db`` is the
  507 |         # base whose exemplars this engine reflects into (default IBIS / ЭК). With
  508 |         # no handle the engine stays fully standalone (back-compat).
  509 |         self.catalog = catalog
  510 |         self.catalog_db = catalog_db
  511 |         # Optional Acquisition (КСУ) seam (INTEGRATION_MAP ребро 5.2:
  512 |         # Книговыдача→Комплектование). When an ``acquisition``
  513 |         # (access.acquisition.AcquisitionEngine) handle is wired, a confirmed loss
  514 |         # (``mark_lost(confirm=True)``) is reflected into the acquisition КСУ
  515 |         # выбытия: the lost copy is written off as выбытие (910^V/^X, статус
  516 |         # «списан»). Best-effort, optional — with no handle circulation stays fully
  517 |         # standalone (back-compat, mirrors the ``catalog=`` seam).
  518 |         self.acquisition = acquisition
  519 | 
  520 |     # ---- acquisition-driven seams (поступление → выдача · выдача → КСУ) ---- #
  521 |     def register_acquired_item(self, item, catalog_db=None):
  522 |         """Confirm a freshly-acquired copy (``item`` = 910^b) is lendable now.
  523 | 
  524 |         The Комплектование↔Книговыдача seam (called by
  525 |         :meth:`access.acquisition.AcquisitionEngine.receive` through its
  526 |         ``circulation`` handle). A received copy that has been reflected into the
  527 |         catalog as a free 910 exemplar is checkout-ready: this returns True iff the
  528 |         copy is free per the catalog (``910^A=0``). When no catalog is wired the
  529 |         copy is accepted as lendable by default (circulation keeps no inventory of
  530 |         its own — the loan only references the item shifr). Read-only: it asserts
  531 |         lendability, it does not create a loan."""
  532 |         db = catalog_db or self.catalog_db
  533 |         if self.catalog is None:
  534 |             return True  # no catalog opinion — circulation lends against the shifr
  535 |         try:
  536 |             found = self.catalog.find_exemplar(db, item)
```

```py
  539 |             return self.catalog.is_available(db, item)

  542 | 
  543 |     def item_on_hand(self, item):
  544 |         """True iff ``item`` (910^b) is on an on-hand loan (not returned, not lost).
  545 | 
  546 |         The read-only probe book-provision uses to count a checked-out copy as
  547 |         still-provisioned фонд (INTEGRATION_MAP ребро 4.4/4.7)."""

  549 | 
  550 |     # ---- event emission --------------------------------------------------- #
  551 |     # Events that are addressed to library staff (not the reader): they describe
  552 |     # an action a librarian must take (write-off candidate), so they enqueue to
  553 |     # ``staff_recipient`` and the catalog routes them to the staff channel.
  554 |     _STAFF_EVENTS = frozenset({'staff_alert'})
  555 | 
  556 |     def _notice_context(self, event, recipient, payload):
  557 |         """Map a circulation event payload to its A6 *template* render context.
  558 | 
  559 |         The notification templates (access.notifications.EventCatalog) interpolate
  560 |         reader-friendly slots — ``title`` / ``reader_name`` / ``due_date`` /
  561 |         ``amount`` / ``currency`` / ``hold_until`` … — but the circulation engine
  562 |         speaks in domain terms (``item`` shifr, ``due`` epoch, ``price`` …). This
  563 |         bridges the two so a template never renders with a literal ``{title}`` for
  564 |         want of the key. Unknown slots degrade gracefully (the renderer leaves a
  565 |         missing placeholder verbatim), but the common ones are always supplied.
  566 | 
  567 |         The reader/staff identity drives ``reader_name``; ``ref`` is carried
  568 |         through verbatim so A6's dedup key stays the circulation domain id (one
  569 |         enqueue per logical event, no double-send).
  570 |         """
  571 |         ctx = dict(payload or {})
  572 |         # reader/staff display name — the store holds no human name, so the
  573 |         # reader id is the best available label (a real directory would map it).
  574 |         ctx.setdefault('reader_name', payload.get('reader_name') or recipient)
  575 |         # item shifr stands in for the human title until a catalog title is wired.
  576 |         item = payload.get('item')
  577 |         if item is not None:
  578 |             ctx.setdefault('title', payload.get('title', item))
  579 |         # epoch fields → human dates the templates print.
  580 |         for src, dst in (('due', 'due_date'), ('hold_until', 'hold_until')):
  581 |             val = payload.get(src)
  582 |             if val is not None and dst not in ctx:
  583 |                 ctx[dst] = self._fmt_date(val)
  584 |         # lost replacement carries ``price`` as the charge basis → ``amount``.
  585 |         if 'amount' not in ctx and payload.get('price') is not None:
  586 |             ctx['amount'] = payload['price']
  587 |         # currency default so {currency} never renders verbatim on money notices.
  588 |         if 'amount' in ctx:
  589 |             ctx.setdefault('currency', self.policy['fine']['currency'])
  590 |         return ctx
  591 | 
```

```py
  592 |     @staticmethod
  593 |     def _fmt_date(value):
  594 |         """Format an epoch (or pass through a string) as YYYY-MM-DD for a notice."""
  595 |         try:
  596 |             return time.strftime('%Y-%m-%d', time.localtime(float(value)))
  597 |         except (TypeError, ValueError):
  598 |             return value
  599 | 
  600 |     def _emit(self, event, recipient, payload):
  601 |         """Build an event intent; render + enqueue it through A6 when wired.
  602 | 
  603 |         Returns the event *intent* (``{'event','recipient','payload'}``) regardless
  604 |         of dispatch — the rule layer never depends on A6 being present. When a
  605 |         notifications handle IS wired the notice is also routed to A6:
  606 | 
  607 |           * the event is mapped to its template (A6 ``EventCatalog``) and a
  608 |             reader-friendly render context is built (:meth:`_notice_context`);
  609 |           * a reader-facing event notifies the reader; ``staff_alert`` is
  610 |             re-addressed to ``staff_recipient`` (the catalog routes it to the
  611 |             staff channel);
  612 |           * ``enqueue`` resolves the template's default channels and queues a
  613 |             single notice (idempotent by dedup_key — no double-send).
  614 | 
  615 |         Robustness: an event with no matching template, or any dispatch failure,
  616 |         is logged-and-skipped — it never propagates out of the circulation
  617 |         operation (the loan/return/hold still succeeds).
  618 |         """
  619 |         intent = {'event': event, 'recipient': recipient, 'payload': dict(payload)}
  620 |         notifier = self.notifier
  621 |         if notifier is None:
  622 |             return intent  # standalone — only the intent, no dispatch (back-compat)
  623 |         try:
  624 |             # No template for this event → skip gracefully rather than letting the
  625 |             # intent fall through silently or raising out of the circulation op.
  626 |             catalog = getattr(notifier, 'catalog', None)
  627 |             if catalog is not None and not catalog.has(event):
  628 |                 return intent
  629 |             to = self.staff_recipient if event in self._STAFF_EVENTS else recipient
  630 |             ctx = self._notice_context(event, recipient, payload)
  631 |             notifier.enqueue(event, to, ctx, tenant=self.tenant)
  632 |         except Exception:
  633 |             # A6 is best-effort: a bad template / queue error must NOT break the
  634 |             # circulation operation. The intent is still returned for the caller.
  635 |             pass
  636 |         return intent
  637 | 
  638 |     # ---- catalog 910^A write-back (edges 2.1/2.2) ------------------------- #
  639 |     def _flip_catalog_status(self, item, status):
  640 |         """Reflect a loan-state change into the catalog exemplar's ``910^A``.
  641 | 
  642 |         Resolves ``item`` (903/inventory) to the matching ``910`` copy and flips
  643 |         its ``910^A`` to ``status`` (``'1'`` issued on checkout, ``'0'`` free on
  644 |         return). A no-op (returns None) when no catalog is wired or the copy isn't
  645 |         found in the catalog — the engine never depends on the catalog being
  646 |         present (back-compat) and degrades gracefully if the copy is unknown.
```

```py
  655 |     def catalog_available(self, item):
  656 |         """Is ``item`` free per the catalog's ``910^A``? (edge 2.2).
  657 | 
  658 |         Returns True/False from the catalog when wired and the copy is known;

  669 | 
  670 |     # ---- checkout (§2 debtor gate + §5 limits) ---------------------------- #
  671 |     def checkout(self, reader_id, item, today, item_kind=None, item_price=None,
  672 |                  staff_override=False, override_grant=False):
  673 |         """Lend ``item`` to ``reader_id``. Enforces limits + the debtor gate.
  674 | 
  675 |         ``staff_override`` lets a librarian lend over a deny — but only when the
  676 |         override is actually authorised (``override_grant=True``, the
  677 |         ``circ.lend.override_debt`` / ``circ.lend.override_limit`` right). An
  678 |         unauthorised override is ignored (the deny stands).
  679 | 
  680 |         Returns a :class:`Decision`; on ALLOW ``computed['loan']`` is the new loan.
  681 |         """
  682 |         reader = self.store.get_reader(reader_id)
  683 |         if reader is None:
  684 |             return Decision(DENY, ['unknown_reader'])
  685 | 
  686 |         category = reader['category']
  687 |         limits = category_limits(self.policy, category)
  688 |         reasons = []
  689 | 
  690 |         # §2 debtor self-service gate (rule A): a hard debtor is denied.
  691 |         level = debt_level(self.store, self.policy, reader_id, today)
  692 |         if level == 'hard' and self.policy['debt']['reader_block_on_hard']:
  693 |             reasons.append('reader_has_debt')
  694 | 
  695 |         # §5 limits: max_books, or max_dolg_books when in debt.
  696 |         on_hand = self.store.count_on_hand(reader_id)
  697 |         cap = limits['max_dolg_books'] if level != 'none' else limits['max_books']
  698 |         if on_hand >= cap:
  699 |             reasons.append('limit_exceeded')
  700 | 
  701 |         if reader['blocked']:
```

<!-- ─── страница 77 ─── -->

```py
  702 |             reasons.append('reader_blocked')
  703 | 
  704 |         if reasons:
  705 |             # Only the debt/limit reasons are override-able by authorised staff.
  706 |             overridable = set(reasons) <= {'reader_has_debt', 'limit_exceeded'}
  707 |             authorised = staff_override and override_grant
  708 |             if overridable and authorised:
  709 |                 pass  # rule B — staff override (would be journalled by 152-ФЗ layer)
  710 |             elif overridable and staff_override and not override_grant:
  711 |                 return Decision(DENY, reasons + ['override_unauthorised'])
  712 |             elif overridable:
  713 |                 return Decision(REQUIRE_OVERRIDE, reasons)
  714 |             else:
  715 |                 return Decision(DENY, reasons)

  717 |         due = today + limits['max_return_days'] * SECONDS_PER_DAY
  718 |         loan = self.store.add_loan(reader_id, item, due, today,
  719 |                                    item_kind=item_kind, item_price=item_price)
  720 |         # Edge 2.1: reflect the issue into the catalog exemplar (910^A 0→1).
  721 |         cat_mfn = self._flip_catalog_status(item, '1')  # EXEMPLAR_ISSUED
  722 |         computed = {'loan': loan, 'due': due, 'override': bool(reasons)}
  723 |         if cat_mfn is not None:

  725 |             computed['exemplar_status'] = '1'
  726 |         return Decision(ALLOW, [], computed)

  728 |     # ---- renew (§1 holds-block-renewal + cap) ----------------------------- #
  729 |     def renew(self, loan_id, today, staff_override=False, override_grant=False):
  730 |         """Renew a loan. Blocks on an active hold (holds-block-renewal) and on the
  731 |         renewal cap; ``staff_override`` (right ``circ.renew.override_hold``) lets a
  732 |         librarian renew past a hold but never past the cap.
  733 |         """
  734 |         loan = self.store.get_loan(loan_id)
  735 |         if loan is None:
  736 |             return Decision(DENY, ['unknown_loan'])
  737 |         if loan['returned']:
  738 |             return Decision(DENY, ['loan_closed'])
  739 | 
  740 |         reader = self.store.get_reader(loan['reader'])
  741 |         category = reader['category'] if reader else '_DEFAULT'
  742 |         limits = category_limits(self.policy, category)
  743 |         reasons = []
  744 | 
  745 |         # §1 renew cap — never override-able.
  746 |         max_prolong = limits['max_prolong']
  747 |         if loan['renewals'] >= max_prolong:
  748 |             reasons.append('max_prolong_reached')
  749 | 
  750 |         # §1.2 renewable flag (reading room / rare fund) — never override-able here.
  751 |         if limits['max_return_days'] <= 0:
  752 |             reasons.append('not_renewable')
  753 | 
  754 |         # §1.3 holds-block-renewal.
  755 |         queue = self.store.active_holds(loan['item'])
  756 |         queue_len = len(queue)
```

```py
  796 |     def return_item(self, loan_id, today):
```

```py
  849 |     # ---- place_hold (§6 FIFO queue position) ------------------------------ #
  850 |     def place_hold(self, reader_id, item, today):
  851 |         """Place a hold; returns ALLOW with ``computed['position']`` (1-based FIFO).
  852 | 
  853 |         If the item is free (no on-hand loan, no queue) the hold is created and
  854 |         immediately ``ready`` at position 1.
  855 |         """
  856 |         reader = self.store.get_reader(reader_id)
  857 |         if reader is None:
  858 |             return Decision(DENY, ['unknown_reader'])
  859 | 
  860 |         # A reader already in this item's queue keeps their place (no double-hold).
  861 |         existing = [h for h in self.store.active_holds(item)
  862 |                     if h['reader'] == reader_id]
  863 |         if existing:
  864 |             pos = self.queue_position(item, reader_id)
  865 |             return Decision(ALLOW, ['already_queued'],
  866 |                             {'hold': existing[0], 'position': pos})
```

```py
  890 |     def queue_position(self, item, reader_id):
  891 |         """1-based FIFO position of ``reader_id`` in ``item``'s queue (0 = absent).
  892 | 
  893 |         position = 1 + count(active holds queued strictly earlier) (§6.3, fifo).
  894 |         """
  895 |         queue = self.store.active_holds(item)  # already FIFO ordered
  896 |         for idx, h in enumerate(queue):
  897 |             if h['reader'] == reader_id:
  898 |                 return idx + 1
  899 |         return 0
  900 | 
  901 |     def cancel_hold(self, hold_id, today, reason='отменена читателем'):
  902 |         """Reader cancels a hold; frees the item to the next in queue (§6.5).
  903 | 
  904 |         Emits ``hold_cancelled`` (A6) to the reader whose hold was dropped.
  905 |         """
  906 |         hold = self.store.get_hold(hold_id)
  907 |         if hold is None:
  908 |             return Decision(DENY, ['unknown_hold'])
  909 |         self.store.set_hold_status(hold_id, 'cancelled')
  910 |         ev = self._emit('hold_cancelled', hold['reader'],
  911 |                         {'ref': hold_id, 'item': hold['item'], 'reason': reason})
  912 |         return Decision(ALLOW, [], {'cancelled': hold_id}, [ev])
  913 | 
  914 |     # ---- accrue_fines (§3.2 formula) -------------------------------------- #
  915 |     def accrue_fines(self, today, reader_id=None):
  916 |         """Daily accrual: ``min(fine_per_day × billable_days, fine_cap)`` (§3.2).
  917 | 
  918 |         Scans open overdue loans (optionally a single reader), computes the
  919 |         accrued fine after ``overdue_grace_days``, and upserts the fine row. A
  920 |         zero rate ⇒ fine-free (no row). Idempotent per ``(loan, day)`` because the
  921 |         amount is recomputed from ``today`` and stored on the single per-loan row
```

<!-- ─── страница 81 ─── -->

```py
  922 |         (re-running with the same ``today`` yields the same amount, no doubling).
  923 | 
  924 |         Returns a list of ``{'loan','reader','amount','days'}`` dicts for the
  925 |         loans that accrued a positive fine.
  926 |         """
  927 |         grace = self.policy['fine']['overdue_grace_days']
  928 |         cap = self.policy['fine']['fine_cap']
  929 |         results = []
  930 |         for ln in self.store.open_overdue_loans(today, grace_days=0):
  931 |             if reader_id is not None and ln['reader'] != reader_id:
  932 |                 continue
  933 |             reader = self.store.get_reader(ln['reader'])
  934 |             category = reader['category'] if reader else '_DEFAULT'
  935 |             rate = fine_per_day(self.policy, category, ln['item_kind'])
  936 |             if rate <= 0:
  937 |                 continue  # fine-free
  938 |             days_overdue = int((today - ln['due']) // SECONDS_PER_DAY)
  939 |             billable_days = max(0, days_overdue - grace)
  940 |             if billable_days <= 0:
  941 |                 continue
  942 |             amount = min(rate * billable_days, cap)
  943 |             if amount <= 0:
  944 |                 continue
  945 |             self.store.upsert_fine(ln['reader'], ln['id'], amount,
  946 |                                    kind='fine_overdue', status='accrued',
  947 |                                    accrued_through=today)
  948 |             results.append({'loan': ln['id'], 'reader': ln['reader'],
  949 |                             'amount': amount, 'days': billable_days})
  950 |         return results
  951 | 
  952 |     def waive_fine(self, loan_id, override_grant=False):
  953 |         """Waive (zero) an accrued/charged overdue fine — right ``circ.fine.waive``.
  954 | 
  955 |         Returns ALLOW only when authorised (``override_grant=True``).
  956 |         """
  957 |         if not override_grant:
  958 |             return Decision(DENY, ['waive_unauthorised'])
  959 |         fine = self.store.get_fine(loan_id, 'fine_overdue')
  960 |         if fine is None:
  961 |             return Decision(DENY, ['no_fine'])
  962 |         self.store.set_fine_status(fine['id'], 'waived')
  963 |         return Decision(ALLOW, [], {'waived': loan_id})
  964 | 
  965 |     def pay_fine(self, loan_id, kind='fine_overdue'):
  966 |         """Apply an incoming ``payment_received`` (§3.5): mark the fine paid.
  967 | 
  968 |         Clearing ``40^F`` (the on-hand marker) is *separate* — it happens only on
  969 |         physical return, not on payment (§3.5). Returns the recomputed debt-clear.
  970 |         """
  971 |         fine = self.store.get_fine(loan_id, kind)
  972 |         if fine is None:
  973 |             return Decision(DENY, ['no_fine'])
  974 |         self.store.set_fine_status(fine['id'], 'paid')
  975 |         ev = self._emit('fine_paid', fine['reader'],
  976 |                         {'ref': loan_id, 'amount': fine['amount']})
```

```py
  979 |     # ---- mark_lost (§4 candidate → confirm) ------------------------------- #
  980 |     def scan_lost_candidates(self, today):
  981 |         """Mark over-overdue loans as ``lost_candidate`` + a ``staff_alert`` (§4.1).
  982 | 
  983 |         Over ``lost_after_days`` past due ⇒ candidate (NOT an auto-debt). Returns
  984 |         the candidate loan ids + their staff-alert event intents.
  985 |         """
  986 |         lost_after = self.policy['lost']['lost_after_days']
  987 |         cutoff = today - lost_after * SECONDS_PER_DAY
  988 |         flagged = []
  989 |         events = []
  990 |         for ln in self.store.open_overdue_loans(today, grace_days=0):
  991 |             if ln['lost_status'] != 'none':
  992 |                 continue
  993 |             if ln['due'] < cutoff:
  994 |                 self.store.set_lost_status(ln['id'], 'lost_candidate')
  995 |                 flagged.append(ln['id'])
  996 |                 events.append(self._emit('staff_alert', ln['reader'],
  997 |                               {'ref': ln['id'], 'item': ln['item'],
  998 |                                'kind': 'lost_candidate'}))
  999 |         return Decision(ALLOW, [], {'candidates': flagged}, events)
 1000 | 
 1001 |     def mark_lost(self, loan_id, today, confirm=False, override_grant=False,
 1002 |                   ksu_disp_no=None):
 1003 |         """Lost workflow (§4): without ``confirm`` ⇒ ``lost_candidate`` (fail-safe,
 1004 |         no auto-debt); with ``confirm`` ⇒ ``lost`` + replacement charge.
 1005 | 
 1006 |         Confirm requires authorisation (``override_grant``, right
 1007 |         ``circ.lost.confirm``). Replacement = ``910^E × multiplier + handling_fee``
 1008 |         with a fallback when the item price is empty/0. ``lost_supersedes_fine``
 1009 |         waives the accrued overdue fine so the reader isn't billed twice (§4.2).
 1010 |         Emits ``lost_confirmed`` (→ ws2 write-off signal) + ``fine_charged`` (A6).
 1011 | 
 1012 |         Книговыдача→Комплектование (INTEGRATION_MAP ребро 5.2): when an
 1013 |         ``acquisition`` handle is wired, a confirmed loss is also reflected into the
 1014 |         acquisition КСУ выбытия — the lost copy is written off as выбытие
 1015 |         (``record_disposal``). ``ksu_disp_no`` is the акт списания number to book it
 1016 |         under (defaults to a per-day ``LOST-<YYYY-MM-DD>`` акт). The write-off is
 1017 |         best-effort: an acquisition error never blocks the loss confirmation.
 1018 |         """
 1019 |         loan = self.store.get_loan(loan_id)
 1020 |         if loan is None:
 1021 |             return Decision(DENY, ['unknown_loan'])
 1022 |         if loan['returned']:
 1023 |             return Decision(DENY, ['loan_closed'])
 1024 | 
 1025 |         if not confirm:
 1026 |             self.store.set_lost_status(loan_id, 'lost_candidate')
 1027 |             ev = self._emit('staff_alert', loan['reader'],
 1028 |                             {'ref': loan_id, 'item': loan['item'],
 1029 |                              'kind': 'lost_candidate'})
 1030 |             return Decision(ALLOW, ['lost_candidate'],
 1031 |                             {'lost_status': 'lost_candidate'}, [ev])
```

```py
 1033 |         # confirm → must be authorised (circ.lost.confirm)
 1034 |         if not override_grant:
 1035 |             return Decision(DENY, ['lost_confirm_unauthorised'])
 1036 | 
 1037 |         # §4.2 replacement value.
 1038 |         price = loan['item_price']
 1039 |         if not price or price <= 0:
 1040 |             price = self.policy['lost']['default_replacement_value']
 1041 |         replacement = (price * self.policy['lost']['multiplier']
 1042 |                        + self.policy['lost']['handling_fee'])
 1043 | 
 1044 |         self.store.set_lost_status(loan_id, 'lost')
 1045 |

 1047 |         if self.policy['lost']['lost_supersedes_fine']:
 1048 |             of = self.store.get_fine(loan_id, 'fine_overdue')
 1049 |             if of and of['status'] in ('accrued', 'charged'):

 1051 |
```
