```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """Authority-files service — first shippable slice (gap A4, epic #188).
    3 | 
    4 | Self-contained sqlite store + lookup + substitution pipeline for the six
    5 | ИРБИС authority bases (ATHRA/C/S/G/U/B), unified onto one polymorphic
    6 | ``authority_record`` table with JSON subfields — the model decided in
    7 | ``docs/design/specs/engines/SPEC_service_authority.md`` §1 and grounded on
    8 | ``docs/recon/deep/reference/databases/DB_AUTHORITY.md``.
    9 | 
   10 | What this module ships (SPEC §9 critical path, steps 1-3 — the search +
   11 | substitution core, minus autoin/merge/trees which are later slices):
   12 | 
   13 |   * ``AuthorityStore`` — own sqlite tables ``authority_record`` (id, kind, db,
   14 |     data_json) + ``authority_term`` (authority_id, term) created on init.
   15 |     The term table is the projection that ports the FST dictionary (SPEC §1.3):
   16 |     every searchable string of a record is denormalised into one ``term`` row.
   17 |   * ``search(db, q)`` — prefix/substring lookup over ``authority_term`` (SPEC §2.1,
   18 |     AC-L1); empty store → empty list, never an error.
   19 |   * ``get(db, id)`` — full record (SPEC §2.2, AC-L3).
   20 |   * ``substitute(catalog_field_tag, authority_record)`` — the fill-map step of the
   21 |     substitution pipeline (SPEC §3.1 step [4]/[5], §3.2 table): returns the subfield
   22 |     patch ``{'a':…,'b':…,'g':…,'3':authority_id}`` to write into the catalog field,
   23 |     always carrying the ``^3`` link = the authority record id (DB_AUTHORITY §5.2).
   24 | 
   25 | Design notes (paritet with ground truth):
   26 |   * ``db`` ∈ {athra, athrc, athrs, athrg, athru, athrb} ↔ ``kind`` ∈
```

```py
  140 | 
  141 | class AuthorityStore:
  142 |     """Self-contained sqlite store for authority records + the term projection.
  143 | 
  144 |     Pass ``:memory:`` or a temp path; schema is created on init (paritet
  145 |     AccessStore).  One connection per thread (sqlite objects aren't shareable
  146 |     across threads).
  147 |     """
  148 | 
  149 |     def __init__(self, db_path=':memory:'):
  150 |         self.db_path = db_path
  151 |         self._local = threading.local()
  152 |         self.ensure_schema()
  153 | 
  154 |     def _conn(self):
  155 |         c = getattr(self._local, 'conn', None)
  156 |         if c is None:
  157 |             c = sqlite3.connect(self.db_path)
  158 |             c.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
  159 |             c.execute('PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON')
  160 |             self._local.conn = c
  161 |         return c
  162 | 
  163 |     def ensure_schema(self):
  164 |         c = self._conn()
  165 |         c.executescript(SCHEMA_SQLITE)
  166 |         c.commit()
  167 | 
  168 |     # ---- write ----------------------------------------------------------- #
  169 |     def add_record(self, db, heading_210, terms=None, **extra):
  170 |         """Insert one authority record and project its search terms.
  171 | 
  172 |         ``db`` is one of DB_TO_KIND keys (kind is derived).  ``heading_210`` is the
  173 |         dict of accepted-heading subfields ({'a':…,'b':…,…}).  ``terms`` is an
  174 |         optional explicit list of searchable strings (ports the FST projection,
  175 |         SPEC §1.3); when omitted, the heading values are projected as terms so a
  176 |         record is always findable by its own subfield text.  ``extra`` is merged
  177 |         into the stored record (e.g. worklist, see_410, classmarks).
  178 | 
  179 |         Returns the new record id, which is the ``^3`` link target.
  180 |         """
  181 |         db = db.lower()
  182 |         if db not in DB_TO_KIND:
  183 |             raise ValueError('unknown authority db %r (expected one of %s)'
  184 |                              % (db, ', '.join(sorted(DB_TO_KIND))))
  185 |         kind = DB_TO_KIND[db]
  186 |         record = dict(extra)
  187 |         record['kind'] = kind
  188 |         record['db'] = db
  189 |         record['heading_210'] = dict(heading_210)
  190 |         c = self._conn()
  191 |         cur = c.execute(
```

```py
  215 |     # ---- read ------------------------------------------------------------ #
  216 |     def get(self, db, rec_id):
  217 |         """Full authority record (dict) for ``rec_id`` within ``db``, or None.
  218 | 
  219 |         Scoped by ``db`` so a personal-author id can't be fetched as a subject
  220 |         (paritet base isolation).  SPEC §2.2 / AC-L3.

  222 |         r = self._conn().execute(
  223 |             'SELECT data_json FROM authority_record WHERE id=? AND db=?',
  224 |             (rec_id, db.lower())).fetchone()
  225 |         return json.loads(r['data_json']) if r else None
  226 | 
  227 |     def search(self, db, q, mode='prefix', limit=20):
  228 |         """Lookup over the term projection within ``db``.  SPEC §2.1, AC-L1.
  229 | 
  230 |         ``mode='prefix'`` matches term_norm by right-truncation (paritet
  231 |         словарного поиска ``A=ТОЛС…``); ``mode='substring'`` matches anywhere.
  232 |         Empty store / no hit → ``[]`` (never an error — AC-L1).  Each hit is the
  233 |         full record dict with an added ``'matched'`` term for UI display, and a
  234 |         ready ``'fill_hint'`` echo of the heading (SPEC §2.1 ``fill`` block,
  235 |         AC-L4 — client doesn't reconstruct subfields).
  236 |         """
  237 |         qn = _norm(q)
  238 |         if not qn:
  239 |             return []
  240 |         if mode == 'substring':
  241 |             pattern = '%' + qn.replace('%', r'\%').replace('_', r'\_') + '%'
  242 |         else:  # prefix (default)
  243 |             pattern = qn.replace('%', r'\%').replace('_', r'\_') + '%'
  244 |         rows = self._conn().execute(
  245 |             "SELECT DISTINCT t.authority_id AS aid, r.data_json AS data_json "
  246 |             "FROM authority_term t JOIN authority_record r ON r.id = t.authority_id "
```

```py
  263 | 
  264 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  265 | # Substitution pipeline — fill-map step (SPEC §3.1 [4]/[5], §3.2).
  266 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  267 | def substitute(catalog_field_tag, authority_record):
  268 |     """Build the subfield patch to write into a catalog field from a chosen
  269 |     authority record (SPEC §3.1 steps map+link, §3.2 fill-map; AC-L4/AC-S1..S3).
  270 | 
  271 |     Returns a dict ``{catalog_subfield: value, …, '3': authority_id}`` —
  272 |     e.g. for a person in 700:
  273 |         {'a': 'Толстой', 'b': 'Л. Н.', 'g': 'Лев Николаевич', '3': '42'}
  274 | 
  275 |     The ``^3`` link is *always* added (= the authority record id) for fields that
  276 |     carry it, and is NEVER taken from the authority's own subfields
  277 |     (DB_AUTHORITY §5.2).  Index fields 675/621 have ``link=False`` → no ^3.
  278 | 
  279 |     Only fill-map subfields present (and truthy) in the authority's heading are
  280 |     written; absent subfields are simply omitted so the operator/ФЛК can fill
  281 |     role ^9 / ^4 etc. without the pipeline overwriting them (SPEC §3.2 note).
  282 | 
  283 |     Raises:
  284 |       AuthorityNotFound   — authority_record is None (missing authority, AC graceful).
  285 |       UnknownCatalogField — catalog_field_tag has no fill-map entry.
  286 |     """
  287 |     tag = str(catalog_field_tag)
  288 |     spec = FILL_MAP.get(tag)
  289 |     if spec is None:
  290 |         raise UnknownCatalogField(
  291 |             'no fill-map for catalog field %r (known: %s)'
  292 |             % (tag, ', '.join(sorted(FILL_MAP))))
  293 |     if authority_record is None:
  294 |         raise AuthorityNotFound(
```

### Файл: `irbis-web/backend/access/pft.py`  · строк: 1594

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """PFT formatting engine — the A1 keystone (epic #188, SPEC_engine_pft.md).
    3 | 
    4 | This is the self-contained *expression core* of the ИРБИС64 PFT formatting
    5 | language that the ФЛК (A2, ``access/flk.py``) and глобальная корректировка (A3,
    6 | ``.gbl``) engines delegate to. It is the in-process module contract formalized in
    7 | SPEC_api_contracts §3.1 / mismatch M4:
    8 | 
    9 |     pft.eval(fmt, record, ctx)       -> str          # single-line result
   10 |     pft.eval_lines(fmt, record, ctx) -> list[str]    # multi-line (repeat groups)
   11 | 
   12 | Scope (first shippable slice — SPEC §1.3 Phase 1 + a Phase-2 / Phase-3 subset)
   13 | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   14 | A *hybrid interpreter*: the format string is tokenized → parsed to a small AST →
   15 | interpreted against a record. Output is a plain string (the IR-document /
   16 | HTML/PDF rendering of SPEC §1.1 is a later phase; here the IR collapses to text).
   17 | 
   18 | Record shape (the I1 field/subfield draft — same as ``access/flk.py``)::
   19 | 
   20 |     {
   21 |       "200": [{"a": "Заглавие", "f": "Автор"}],   # repeating field = list of instances
   22 |       "910": [{"a": "0", "b": "INV1"}, {"b": "INV2"}],
   23 |       "101": "rus",                                # bare-string field
   24 |       "10":  {"a": "5-7654-0001-X"}                # single instance (dict)
   25 |     }
   26 | 
   27 | A field may be a bare string, a ``{subfield: value}`` dict, or a list of either.
   28 | 
   29 | Implemented PFT constructs
   30 | --------------------------
   31 |   * Field refs: ``v200``, ``v200^a``, ``v200^*`` (first subfield), fragment
   32 |     ``v200^a*off.len`` / ``v1*3.3`` (SPEC §2).
   33 |   * ``mfn`` / ``mfn(d)`` — record number from ``ctx['mfn']`` (SPEC §2).
   34 |   * Literals: conditional ``"…"`` (once if the *adjacent* field is present),
   35 |     repeatable ``|…|`` (per instance, incl. ``|x|+`` prefix / ``+|x|`` suffix),
   36 |     unconditional ``'…'`` (always) (SPEC §5).
   37 |   * Dummy output ``d<tag>`` / ``n<tag>`` (literal by field presence/absence, §6).
   38 |   * ``IF … THEN … ELSE … FI`` with nesting; conditions ``= <> < <= > >= :``
   39 |     (contains) and ``NOT / AND / OR``; predicates ``p(...)`` / ``a(...)`` (§7,§10).
```

<!-- ─── страница 126 ─── -->

```py
   40 |   * Repeat groups ``(… v910 …)`` — one pass per instance of the repeating field
   41 |     inside (SPEC §10); ``eval_lines`` surfaces them as separate lines.
   42 |   * Concatenation by ``,`` and ``+`` (SPEC §7).
   43 |   * ``f(expr[,width[,dec]])`` number→string; ``val(...)`` text→number;
   44 |     ``s(...)`` concatenation/implicit-OR (SPEC §8).
   45 |   * ``&unifor('…')`` / ``&uf('…')`` — the UNIFOR registry (see ``UNIFOR``).
   46 | 
   47 | UNIFOR codes implemented (vs degraded)
   48 | --------------------------------------
   49 | Implemented (first char of the argument):
   50 |   ``C`` ISBN/ISSN checksum control (0 ok / 1 error) — **the code ФЛК A2 reuses**;
   51 |   ``3`` date/time (``3`` ГГГГММДД, ``30`` year, ``31`` month, ``32`` day, ``33``
   52 |   YY, ``34``/``35`` no-lead-zero, ``39`` time, today's date from ``ctx['now']``);
   53 |   ``A``/``P`` Nth field occurrence (``Av910^a#2``); ``9`` strip double-quotes;
   54 |   ``Q`` lowercase; ``X`` strip ``<…>``; ``E`` first N words; ``!`` / ``+F``
   55 |   post-edit (collapse double separators / drop RTF); ``6`` nested template format
   56 |   (``6<name>`` resolved from ``ctx['modules']``, ``%N`` params).
   57 | Degraded to empty string (carried, never crash — SPEC §2.1 risk, §15 "Format
   58 | error 99" parity): every other code (``D``,``7``,``J``,``l``-index lookups,
   59 | ``+1``/``+7`` globals beyond ctx, ``T``,``+I``,``$`` …). Unknown ⇒ ``''``.
   60 | 
   61 | Graceful degradation
   62 | ---------------------
   63 | The engine NEVER raises on malformed/unsupported input: an unparsable construct
   64 | is skipped (emits ``''``), so a partially-understood real ``.pft`` still yields
   65 | the parts we understand rather than a 500. ``strict=True`` opts into raising
   66 | ``PftError`` (used by the format editor / preview path).
   67 | """
   68 | from __future__ import annotations
   69 | 
   70 | import datetime as _dt
   71 | import re
   72 | 
   73 | __all__ = [
   74 |     'eval', 'eval_lines', 'PftError', 'UNIFOR', 'register_unifor',
   75 |     'isbn_issn_ok',
   76 | ]
   77 | 
   78 | 
   79 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   80 | # Errors. In non-strict mode (the default, what A2/A3 use) we never raise.
   81 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   82 | class PftError(Exception):
   83 |     """A PFT format/parse error. Carries an ИРБИС-style numeric ``code``
   84 |     (SPEC §15 / PFT_LANGUAGE §15): 8 IF-without-THEN, 19/20 unbalanced parens,
   85 |     53 IF-without-FI, 99 unknown command."""
   86 | 
   87 |     def __init__(self, message, code=99):
   88 |         super().__init__(message)
   89 |         self.code = code
   90 | 
   91 | 
   92 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   93 | # Record-draft accessors. A field can be a bare string, a {subfield:val} dict,
   94 | # or a list of either (repeating). Mirrors access/flk.py so A2 and A1 agree.
```

```py
   95 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   96 | def _instances(record, tag):
   97 |     raw = record.get(tag) if record else None
   98 |     if raw is None:
   99 |         return []
  100 |     if isinstance(raw, list):
  101 |         return raw
  102 |     return [raw]
  103 | 
  104 | 
  105 | def _inst_value(inst, subfield):
  106 |     """Read ``subfield`` from one instance. subfield=None => whole field value."""
  107 |     if isinstance(inst, dict):
  108 |         if subfield is None or subfield == '':
  109 |             # whole-field value: the bare '' key, else join subfields in order
  110 |             if inst.get(''):
  111 |                 return inst['']
  112 |             if inst.get('value'):
  113 |                 return inst['value']
  114 |             return ''.join(str(v) for v in inst.values())
  115 |         return (inst.get(subfield) or inst.get(subfield.lower())
  116 |                 or inst.get(subfield.upper()) or '')
  117 |     # bare string instance: only the whole-field value exists
  118 |     return inst if (subfield is None or subfield == '') else ''
  119 | 
  120 | 
  121 | def _first_subfield(inst):
  122 |     """``v200^*`` — value of the first subfield of the first instance."""
  123 |     if isinstance(inst, dict):
  124 |         for v in inst.values():
  125 |             if v:
  126 |                 return v
  127 |         return ''
  128 |     return inst or ''
  129 | 
  130 | 
  131 | def field_value(record, tag, subfield=None):
  132 |     """First non-empty instance's subfield value (or '')."""
  133 |     for inst in _instances(record, tag):
  134 |         v = _inst_value(inst, subfield)
  135 |         if v:
  136 |             return v
  137 |     insts = _instances(record, tag)
  138 |     return _inst_value(insts[0], subfield) if insts else ''
  139 | 
  140 | 
  141 | def field_values(record, tag, subfield=None):
  142 |     """All instances' subfield values (skipping empties)."""
  143 |     out = []
  144 |     for inst in _instances(record, tag):
  145 |         v = _inst_value(inst, subfield)
  146 |         if v:
  147 |             out.append(v)
  148 |     return out
  149 |
```

```py
  156 | def _fragment(value, off, length):
  157 |     """``v…*off.len`` — substring (0-based offset, given length)."""
  158 |     if value is None:
  159 |         return ''
  160 |     s = str(value)
  161 |     if off is None:
  162 |         return s
  163 |     if off >= len(s):
  164 |         return ''
  165 |     return s[off:off + length] if length is not None else s[off:]

  174 |     return ''.join(c for c in s if c.isdigit() or c == 'X')
```

```py
  215 | 
  216 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  217 | # UNIFOR registry. Each handler: fn(arg:str, value:str, ctx, record) -> str.
  218 | # ``arg`` is the code-stripped remainder; ``value`` is the already-evaluated
  219 | # inline value (the part after the code literal, if the call embeds a vNNN).
  220 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  221 | UNIFOR = {}
  222 | 
  223 | 
  224 | def register_unifor(code, fn):
  225 |     """Register/override a UNIFOR handler keyed by its leading code char(s)."""
  226 |     UNIFOR[code] = fn
  227 | 
  228 | 
  229 | _MONTHS_NOM = ['', 'январь', 'февраль', 'март', 'апрель', 'май', 'июнь',
  230 |                'июль', 'август', 'сентябрь', 'октябрь', 'ноябрь', 'декабрь']
  231 | # Genitive (родительный падеж) — '36MM' vs '37MM' (PFT_LANGUAGE §9.1 code 3).
  232 | _MONTHS_GEN = ['', 'января', 'февраля', 'марта', 'апреля', 'мая', 'июня',
  233 |                'июля', 'августа', 'сентября', 'октября', 'ноября', 'декабря']
  234 | _MONTHS_ENG = ['', 'January', 'February', 'March', 'April', 'May', 'June',
  235 |                'July', 'August', 'September', 'October', 'November', 'December']
  236 | 
  237 | 
  238 | def _now(ctx):
  239 |     """The 'today' used by date codes — overridable via ctx['now'] for tests."""
  240 |     if ctx and ctx.get('now') is not None:
  241 |         return ctx['now']
  242 |     return _dt.datetime.now()
  243 | 
  244 | 
  245 | def _parse_yyyymmdd(s):
  246 |     """Parse a ``ГГГГММДД`` (YYYYMMDD) string into a ``date``; ``None`` on
  247 |     failure. Tolerates surrounding whitespace and separators (extracts the
  248 |     first 8 digits)."""
  249 |     digits = ''.join(c for c in str(s or '') if c.isdigit())
  250 |     if len(digits) < 8:
  251 |         return None
  252 |     try:
  253 |         return _dt.date(int(digits[0:4]), int(digits[4:6]), int(digits[6:8]))
  254 |     except ValueError:
  255 |         return None
  256 | 
  257 | 
  258 | def _uf_date(arg, value, ctx, record):
  259 |     """UNIFOR ``3`` — date/time (PFT_LANGUAGE §9.1 / decompiled case ``0x33``).
```

```py
  260 | 
  261 |     Today's date is ``ctx['now']`` (else ``datetime.now()``). Sub-codes
  262 |     (the ``3`` is already stripped by the dispatcher):
  263 | 
  264 |       * (empty) ГГГГММДД · ``0`` year · ``1`` month(zero-pad) · ``2`` day(pad)
  265 |         · ``3`` ГГ (YY) · ``4`` month no-lead-zero · ``5`` day no-lead-zero
  266 |         · ``9`` HHMMSS — current-clock fields.
  267 |       * ``6MM`` month name nominative · ``7MM`` genitive · ``8MM`` English
  268 |         (``MM`` = the 2-digit month, e.g. ``&unifor('36',&unifor('31'))``).
  269 |       * ``A`` day-of-year of the supplied/inline ``ГГГГММДД`` (decompiled 3A).
  270 |       * ``B<date>,<±days>`` shift a date by N days → ГГГГММДД (decompiled 3B).
  271 |       * ``C<date1>,<date2>`` whole-day difference ``date1 - date2`` (3C; ``0``
  272 |         if either side unparsable, matching the C default).
  273 |     """
  274 |     now = _now(ctx)
  275 |     sub = arg  # arg already had the leading '3' stripped by the dispatcher
  276 |     if sub == '' or sub is None:
  277 |         return now.strftime('%Y%m%d')
  278 |     if sub == '0':
  279 |         return now.strftime('%Y')
  280 |     if sub == '1':
  281 |         return now.strftime('%m')
  282 |     if sub == '2':
  283 |         return now.strftime('%d')
  284 |     if sub == '3':
  285 |         return now.strftime('%y')
  286 |     if sub == '4':
  287 |         return str(now.month)
  288 |     if sub == '5':
  289 |         return str(now.day)
  290 |     if sub == '9':
  291 |         return now.strftime('%H%M%S')
  292 |     # month-name codes 6MM/7MM/8MM — the MM is taken from the trailing two
  293 |     # digits (or, if absent, from the inline value, else today's month).
  294 |     if sub[0] in ('6', '7', '8'):
  295 |         table = {'6': _MONTHS_NOM, '7': _MONTHS_GEN, '8': _MONTHS_ENG}[sub[0]]
  296 |         mm = sub[1:3] if len(sub) >= 3 and sub[1:3].isdigit() else ''
  297 |         if not mm:
  298 |             mm = (value or '')[:2]
  299 |         try:
  300 |             month = int(mm) if mm else now.month
  301 |             return table[month]
  302 |         except (ValueError, IndexError):
  303 |             return ''
  304 |     # The date payload for A/B/C may be inline in the spec (e.g.
  305 |     # &unifor('3C20260621,20260601')) or in the evaluated value
  306 |     # (&unifor('3C'v210^d)); prefer the in-spec tail, fall back to the value.
  307 |     payload = (sub[1:] + value) if len(sub) > 1 else (value or '')
  308 |     payload = payload.strip()
  309 |     today = now.date() if hasattr(now, 'date') else now
  310 |     # 3A — day of the year of the supplied YYYYMMDD (else today).
  311 |     if sub[0] == 'A':
  312 |         d = _parse_yyyymmdd(payload) or today
  313 |         return str(d.timetuple().tm_yday)
  314 |     # 3B<date>,<±days> — shift a date by N days → ГГГГММДД.
```

```py
  317 |         base = _parse_yyyymmdd(base_s) or today
  318 |         try:
  319 |             days = int(off_s) if off_s.strip() else 0

  332 | 
  333 | def _uf_checksum(arg, value, ctx, record):
  334 |     """UNIFOR ``C`` — ISSN/ISBN control: '0' valid, '1' error (SPEC §9.1).
  335 |     The value to check is the inline ``value`` (e.g. ``&unifor('C'v10^a)``)."""
  336 |     target = value if value else arg
  337 |     return '0' if isbn_issn_ok(target) else '1'
  338 | 
  339 | 
  340 | def _uf_occurrence(arg, value, ctx, record):
  341 |     """UNIFOR ``A``/``P`` — specified field occurrence: ``Av<tag>^<sub>#<occ>``
  342 |     (SPEC §9.1, e.g. ``&unifor('Av920#1')``). 1-based occurrence."""
  343 |     m = re.match(r'v(\d+)(?:\^(.))?(?:\*(\d+)(?:\.(\d+))?)?(?:#(\d+))?$', arg, re.I)
  344 |     if not m:
  345 |         return ''
  346 |     tag, sub, off, length, occ = m.groups()
  347 |     insts = _instances(record, tag)
  348 |     idx = (int(occ) - 1) if occ else 0
  349 |     if idx < 0 or idx >= len(insts):
  350 |         return ''
  351 |     v = _first_subfield(insts[idx]) if sub == '*' else _inst_value(insts[idx], sub)
  352 |     if off is not None:
  353 |         v = _fragment(v, int(off), int(length) if length is not None else None)
  354 |     return v or ''
  355 | 
  356 | 
  357 | def _uf_strip_quotes(arg, value, ctx, record):
  358 |     """UNIFOR ``9`` — remove double-quote characters (SPEC §9.1)."""
  359 |     return (value or arg or '').replace('"', '')
  360 | 
  361 | 
  362 | def _uf_lower(arg, value, ctx, record):
  363 |     """UNIFOR ``Q`` — lowercase the string (SPEC §9.1)."""
  364 |     return (value or arg or '').lower()
  365 | 
  366 | 
  367 | def _uf_strip_angle(arg, value, ctx, record):
  368 |     """UNIFOR ``X`` — remove ``<…>`` fragments (SPEC §9.1)."""
  369 |     return re.sub(r'<[^>]*>', '', value or arg or '')
```

<!-- ─── страница 132 ─── -->

```py
  370 | 
  371 | 
  372 | def _uf_first_words(arg, value, ctx, record):
  373 |     """UNIFOR ``E`` — first N words: ``EN<string>`` (PFT_LANGUAGE §9.1).
  374 | 
  375 |     Whitespace runs collapse to single spaces (CDS/ISIS word semantics)."""
  376 |     m = re.match(r'(\d+)(.*)$', arg, re.S)
  377 |     if not m:
  378 |         return value or ''
  379 |     n = int(m.group(1))
  380 |     src = value if value else m.group(2)
  381 |     return ' '.join((src or '').split()[:n])
  382 | 
  383 | 
  384 | def _uf_cut_after_words(arg, value, ctx, record):
  385 |     """UNIFOR ``F`` — keep the line up to and including the Nth word, dropping
  386 |     the remainder: ``FN<string>`` (PFT_LANGUAGE §9.1).
  387 | 
  388 |     Distinct from ``E``: ``F`` preserves the *original* inter-word spacing of
  389 |     the kept prefix (it truncates the string after the Nth word boundary),
  390 |     whereas ``E`` re-joins on single spaces."""
  391 |     m = re.match(r'(\d+)(.*)$', arg, re.S)
  392 |     if not m:
  393 |         return value or ''
  394 |     n = int(m.group(1))
  395 |     src = value if value else m.group(2)
  396 |     src = src or ''
  397 |     if n <= 0:
  398 |         return ''
  399 |     # walk the string, counting word starts; cut at the end of the Nth word.
  400 |     words_seen = 0
  401 |     in_word = False
  402 |     cut = len(src)
  403 |     for i, ch in enumerate(src):
  404 |         if ch.isspace():
  405 |             if in_word and words_seen == n:
  406 |                 cut = i
  407 |                 break
  408 |             in_word = False
  409 |         else:
  410 |             if not in_word:
  411 |                 words_seen += 1
  412 |             in_word = True
  413 |     return src[:cut]
  414 | 
  415 | 
  416 | def _uf_substr_marker(arg, value, ctx, record):
  417 |     """UNIFOR ``G`` — substring relative to a marker char: ``GNA<string>``
  418 |     (PFT_LANGUAGE §9.1 / decompiled case ``0x33``-sibling).
  419 | 
  420 |     ``N`` = 0 → take the part *up to* (excluding) the marker; ``N`` = 1 → the
  421 |     part *from* (including) the marker. ``A`` is the marker character, with two
  422 |     wildcards: ``#`` = the first digit, ``$`` = the first (latin/cyrillic)
  423 |     letter. Marker not found → the whole string (mode 0) / empty (mode 1)."""
  424 |     m = re.match(r'([01])(.)(.*)$', arg, re.S)
```

```py
  425 |     if not m:
  426 |         return value or arg or ''
  427 |     mode, marker, tail = m.group(1), m.group(2), m.group(3)
  428 |     src = value if value else tail
  429 |     src = src or ''
  430 |     if marker == '#':
  431 |         pos = next((i for i, c in enumerate(src) if c.isdigit()), -1)
  432 |     elif marker == '$':
  433 |         pos = next((i for i, c in enumerate(src) if c.isalpha()), -1)
  434 |     else:
  435 |         pos = src.find(marker)
  436 |     if pos < 0:
  437 |         return src if mode == '0' else ''
  438 |     return src[:pos] if mode == '0' else src[pos:]
  439 | 
  440 | 
  441 | # Cyrillic→Latin transliteration tables (UNIFOR ``T``). Table 0 is a practical
  442 | # GOST-7.79-ish System B mapping; table 1 a simplified ASCII variant. Both are
  443 | # applied longest-key-first so digraphs (``щ``→``shh``) win over single letters.
  444 | _TRANSLIT_0 = {
  445 |     'а': 'a', 'б': 'b', 'в': 'v', 'г': 'g', 'д': 'd', 'е': 'e', 'ё': 'yo',
  446 |     'ж': 'zh', 'з': 'z', 'и': 'i', 'й': 'j', 'к': 'k', 'л': 'l', 'м': 'm',
  447 |     'н': 'n', 'о': 'o', 'п': 'p', 'р': 'r', 'с': 's', 'т': 't', 'у': 'u',
  448 |     'ф': 'f', 'х': 'x', 'ц': 'cz', 'ч': 'ch', 'ш': 'sh', 'щ': 'shh',
  449 |     'ъ': '"', 'ы': 'y', 'ь': "'", 'э': 'e', 'ю': 'yu', 'я': 'ya',
  450 | }
  451 | _TRANSLIT_1 = dict(_TRANSLIT_0, **{
  452 |     'й': 'i', 'х': 'kh', 'ц': 'ts', 'ъ': '', 'ь': '', 'э': 'eh',
  453 | })
  454 | 
  455 | 
  456 | def _translit(src, table):
  457 |     out = []
  458 |     for ch in src:
  459 |         low = ch.lower()
  460 |         rep = table.get(low)
  461 |         if rep is None:
  462 |             out.append(ch)
  463 |         elif ch == low:
  464 |             out.append(rep)
  465 |         else:                                # preserve title/upper-casing
  466 |             out.append(rep.upper() if len(rep) == 1 else rep.capitalize())
  467 |     return ''.join(out)
  468 | 
  469 | 
  470 | def _uf_translit(arg, value, ctx, record):
  471 |     """UNIFOR ``T`` — transliterate Cyrillic to Latin: ``TN<string>`` where
  472 |     ``N`` selects the table (``0`` default, ``1`` simplified) (PFT_LANGUAGE
  473 |     §9.1). Non-Cyrillic characters pass through unchanged."""
  474 |     m = re.match(r'(\d?)(.*)$', arg, re.S)
  475 |     tableno = m.group(1)
  476 |     src = value if value else (m.group(2) or '')
  477 |     table = _TRANSLIT_1 if tableno == '1' else _TRANSLIT_0
  478 |     return _translit(src, table)
  479 | 
```

```py
  480 | 
  481 | # Insertion-point markers ``%…%`` that the term-ending code ``L`` removes.
  482 | _ENDMARK_RE = re.compile(r'%([^%]*)%')
  483 | 
  484 | 
  485 | def _uf_term_ending(arg, value, ctx, record):
  486 |     """UNIFOR ``L`` — term ending: ``L<term>`` (PFT_LANGUAGE §9.1 / decompiled
  487 |     case ``0x4c``). The dictionary/morphology lookup needs the inverted file,
  488 |     which this slice does not carry, so we degrade to the documented *marker
  489 |     pass*: the real code strips the ``%…%`` insertion-point markers from the
  490 |     computed ending and concatenates the segments. With no index we apply that
  491 |     same marker-stripping to the supplied term so a template using ``L`` still
  492 |     renders clean text rather than echoing the ``%`` markers."""
  493 |     src = value if value else (arg or '')
  494 |     return _ENDMARK_RE.sub(r'\1', src or '')
  495 | 
  496 | 
  497 | def _uf_full_record(arg, value, ctx, record):
  498 |     """UNIFOR ``0`` — the whole document (format ALL): every field flattened to
  499 |     ``<tag>#<value>`` lines (PFT_LANGUAGE §9.1 / decompiled case ``0x30``).
  500 | 
  501 |     A plain readable dump of the record; repeating instances are each emitted
  502 |     on their own line. Useful for debug/diagnostic templates."""
  503 |     if not record:
  504 |         return ''
  505 |     lines = []
  506 |     for tag in record:
  507 |         for inst in _instances(record, tag):
  508 |             lines.append('%s#%s' % (tag, _inst_value(inst, None)))
  509 |     return '\n'.join(lines)
  510 | 
  511 | 
  512 | def _uf_record_status(arg, value, ctx, record):
  513 |     """UNIFOR ``+6`` — record status: ``1`` if the record is NOT logically
  514 |     deleted, ``0`` if it is (PFT_LANGUAGE §9.1 / decompiled case ``0x2b``→
  515 |     ``0x36`` via ``IrbisIsDeleted``). The flag is read from ``ctx['deleted']``
  516 |     (the A1↔store contract); absent ⇒ treated as live (``1``)."""
  517 |     deleted = bool((ctx or {}).get('deleted'))
  518 |     return '0' if deleted else '1'
  519 | 
  520 | 
  521 | def _uf_urlcode(arg, value, ctx, record):
  522 |     """UNIFOR ``+3E`` / ``+3D`` — URL-encode / -decode a string (PFT_LANGUAGE
  523 |     §9.1). The leading ``E``/``D`` selects the direction; payload is the inline
  524 |     value (or the spec tail)."""
  525 |     direction = arg[:1].upper()
  526 |     src = value if value else arg[1:]
  527 |     src = src or ''
  528 |     import urllib.parse as _url
  529 |     if direction == 'D':
  530 |         return _url.unquote(src)
  531 |     # default to encode (E); quote keeps unreserved chars, encodes the rest.
  532 |     return _url.quote(src, safe='')
  533 | 
  534 | 
```

```py
  535 | def _uf_postedit_seps(arg, value, ctx, record):
  536 |     """UNIFOR ``!`` — post-edit: collapse double subfield separators / spaces
  537 |     (SPEC §9.1 — a whole-output pass; here a no-op marker returning '')."""

  541 | def _uf_postedit_rtf(arg, value, ctx, record):
  542 |     """UNIFOR ``+F`` — post-edit: strip RTF constructs (SPEC §9.1). No-op here."""
  543 |     return ''

  545 | 
  546 | def _uf_module(arg, value, ctx, record):
  547 |     """UNIFOR ``6`` — nested/template format ``6<name>`` or ``6<tmpl>#<p1>,...``
  548 |     (SPEC §9.1/§9.2). Resolves the module body from ``ctx['modules']`` (a
  549 |     ``{name: pft_body}`` map), substitutes ``%N`` params, and recursively
  550 |     evaluates. Unknown module → '' (carried, not a crash)."""
  551 |     name = arg
  552 |     params = []
  553 |     if '#' in arg:
  554 |         name, raw = arg.split('#', 1)
  555 |         params = raw.split(',')
  556 |     modules = (ctx or {}).get('modules') or {}
  557 |     body = modules.get(name)
  558 |     if body is None:
  559 |         return ''
  560 |     # %1..%9 parameter substitution (SPEC §9.2 template formats).
  561 |     for i, p in enumerate(params, start=1):
  562 |         body = body.replace('%%%d' % i, p)
  563 |     return eval(body, record, ctx)       # noqa: recursion is intentional
  564 | 
  565 | 
  566 | # Default registry (longest code key first so '+F' beats 'F', etc.).
  567 | register_unifor('3', _uf_date)
  568 | register_unifor('C', _uf_checksum)
  569 | register_unifor('A', _uf_occurrence)
  570 | register_unifor('P', _uf_occurrence)
  571 | register_unifor('9', _uf_strip_quotes)
  572 | register_unifor('Q', _uf_lower)
  573 | register_unifor('X', _uf_strip_angle)
  574 | register_unifor('E', _uf_first_words)
  575 | register_unifor('F', _uf_cut_after_words)
  576 | register_unifor('G', _uf_substr_marker)
  577 | register_unifor('T', _uf_translit)
  578 | register_unifor('L', _uf_term_ending)
  579 | register_unifor('0', _uf_full_record)
  580 | register_unifor('!', _uf_postedit_seps)
  581 | register_unifor('+F', _uf_postedit_rtf)
  582 | register_unifor('+6', _uf_record_status)
  583 | register_unifor('+3', _uf_urlcode)
  584 | register_unifor('6', _uf_module)
  585 |

  587 | def _dispatch_unifor(spec, value, ctx, record):
  588 |     """Route a parsed UNIFOR call to its handler. ``spec`` is the leading-literal
  589 |     argument (code + remainder); ``value`` is the evaluated inline value, if any.
```

```py
  592 |         spec = ''
  593 |     # multi-char codes first
  594 |     for code in ('+F', '+I', '+1', '+7', '+3', '+9', '+5', '+4', '+6', '+0'):

  598 |     if not spec:

  606 | 
  607 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  608 | # Tokenizer. PFT is whitespace-insensitive between commands; literals carry their
  609 | # own delimiters. We emit a flat token stream, then a recursive-descent parser
  610 | # builds the AST. Comments ``/* … EOL`` are stripped first.
  611 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  612 | _COMMENT = re.compile(r'/\*[^\n]*')
  613 | 
  614 | # token kinds
  615 | T_FIELD, T_DUMMY, T_LIT, T_NUM, T_IDENT, T_PUNCT, T_UNIFOR = (
  616 |     'FIELD', 'DUMMY', 'LIT', 'NUM', 'IDENT', 'PUNCT', 'UNIFOR')
  617 | 
  618 | _KEYWORDS = {'if', 'then', 'else', 'fi', 'and', 'or', 'not', 'val', 'f', 's',
  619 |              'p', 'a', 'ref', 'mfn', 'rsum', 'rmin', 'rmax', 'ravr', 'l'}
  620 | 
  621 | 
  622 | class _Tok:
  623 |     __slots__ = ('kind', 'value', 'extra')
  624 | 
  625 |     def __init__(self, kind, value, extra=None):
  626 |         self.kind = kind
  627 |         self.value = value
  628 |         self.extra = extra
  629 | 
  630 |     def __repr__(self):
  631 |         return '<%s %r>' % (self.kind, self.value)
  632 | 
  633 | 
  634 | def _tokenize(src):
  635 |     src = _COMMENT.sub('', src)
  636 |     toks = []
  637 |     i, n = 0, len(src)
  638 |     while i < n:
  639 |         c = src[i]
  640 |         if c in ' \t\r\n':
  641 |             i += 1
  642 |             continue
  643 |         # literals
  644 |         if c == '"':
```

```py
  645 |             j = src.find('"', i + 1)
  646 |             if j < 0:
  647 |                 j = n
  648 |             toks.append(_Tok(T_LIT, src[i + 1:j], 'cond'))
  649 |             i = j + 1
  650 |             continue
  651 |         if c == "'":
  652 |             j = src.find("'", i + 1)
  653 |             if j < 0:
  654 |                 j = n
  655 |             toks.append(_Tok(T_LIT, src[i + 1:j], 'uncond'))
  656 |             i = j + 1
  657 |             continue
  658 |         if c == '|':
  659 |             j = src.find('|', i + 1)
  660 |             if j < 0:
  661 |                 j = n
  662 |             toks.append(_Tok(T_LIT, src[i + 1:j], 'rep'))
  663 |             i = j + 1
  664 |             continue
  665 |         # UNIFOR call: &name(arg) or &uf('...') — keep the raw call, parse later
  666 |         if c == '&':
  667 |             j, depth = i + 1, 0
  668 |             # read the name up to '('
  669 |             while j < n and src[j] != '(':
  670 |                 j += 1
  671 |             # now scan balanced parens
  672 |             k = j
  673 |             while k < n:
  674 |                 if src[k] == '(':
  675 |                     depth += 1
  676 |                 elif src[k] == ')':
  677 |                     depth -= 1
  678 |                     if depth == 0:
  679 |                         k += 1
  680 |                         break
  681 |                 k += 1
  682 |             toks.append(_Tok(T_UNIFOR, src[i:k]))
  683 |             i = k
  684 |             continue
  685 |         # field reference  v<tag>[^sub|*][*off.len]
  686 |         if c in 'vV':
  687 |             m = re.match(r'[vV](\d+)(\^[\*a-zA-Z0-9])?(\*\d+(?:\.\d+)?)?',
  688 |                          src[i:])
  689 |             if m and m.group(1):
  690 |                 toks.append(_Tok(T_FIELD, m.group(0)))
  691 |                 i += m.end()
  692 |                 continue
  693 |         # dummy output d<tag> / n<tag>
  694 |         if c in 'dDnN':
  695 |             m = re.match(r'[dDnN](\d+)(\^[a-zA-Z0-9])?', src[i:])
  696 |             if m:
  697 |                 toks.append(_Tok(T_DUMMY, m.group(0)))
  698 |                 i += m.end()
  699 |                 continue
```

```py
  700 |         # number
  701 |         if c.isdigit():

  703 |             toks.append(_Tok(T_NUM, m.group(0)))
  704 |             i += m.end()
  705 |             continue
  706 |         # identifier / keyword
  707 |         if c.isalpha():

  714 |         two = src[i:i + 2]

  717 |             i += 2
  718 |             continue
  719 |         if c in '(),+=<>:/#%':

  721 |             i += 1
  722 |             continue
  723 |         # mode commands Mmc, placement Xn/Cn, '*' etc — skipped (no-op in text IR)
  724 |         i += 1
  725 |     return toks

  727 | 
  728 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  729 | # AST nodes. Each ``render(record, ctx, state)`` returns a string. ``state`` is
  730 | # the per-render mutable workspace (current repeat-instance index, output flags).
  731 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  732 | class _Node:
  733 |     def render(self, record, ctx, state):
  734 |         return ''
  735 | 
  736 | 
  737 | class _Seq(_Node):
  738 |     """A concatenation of nodes (',' / '+' / juxtaposition)."""
  739 | 
  740 |     def __init__(self, parts):
  741 |         self.parts = parts
  742 | 
  743 |     def render(self, record, ctx, state):
  744 |         return ''.join(p.render(record, ctx, state) for p in self.parts)
  745 | 
  746 | 
  747 | class _Literal(_Node):
  748 |     def __init__(self, text, kind):
  749 |         self.text = text
  750 |         self.kind = kind            # 'cond' | 'rep' | 'uncond'
  751 | 
  752 |     def render(self, record, ctx, state):
  753 |         if self.kind == 'uncond':
  754 |             return self.text
```

```py
  764 | class _Field(_Node):
  765 |     def __init__(self, tag, subfield, first_sub, off, length,
  766 |                  prefix=None, suffix=None):
  767 |         self.tag = tag
  768 |         self.subfield = subfield
  769 |         self.first_sub = first_sub
  770 |         self.off = off
  771 |         self.length = length
  772 |         self.prefix = prefix          # list[_Literal] before the field
  773 |         self.suffix = suffix          # list[_Literal] after the field
  774 | 
  775 |     def _one(self, inst, record, ctx, state):
  776 |         if self.first_sub:
  777 |             v = _first_subfield(inst)
  778 |         else:
  779 |             v = _inst_value(inst, self.subfield)
  780 |         if self.off is not None:
  781 |             v = _fragment(v, self.off, self.length)
  782 |         return v or ''
  783 | 
  784 |     def render(self, record, ctx, state):
  785 |         insts = _instances(record, self.tag)
  786 |         # inside a repeat group, only the current instance is visible
  787 |         gi = state.get('group_index')
  788 |         if gi is not None and state.get('group_tag') == self.tag:
  789 |             insts = [insts[gi]] if 0 <= gi < len(insts) else []
  790 |         # only non-empty occurrences are emitted; literals key off emitted order
  791 |         emitted = [self._one(x, record, ctx, state) for x in insts]
  792 |         emitted = [v for v in emitted if v]
  793 |         present_any = bool(emitted)
  794 |         last = len(emitted) - 1
  795 |         out = []
  796 |         cond_done = False                    # conditional literals emit once
  797 |         for k, v in enumerate(emitted):
  798 |             piece = ''
  799 |             for lit in (self.prefix or []):
  800 |                 if lit.kind == 'rep_plus':       # |x|+ : not before the first
  801 |                     if k > 0:
  802 |                         piece += lit.text
  803 |                 elif lit.kind == 'cond':         # "x" prefix: once, with the field
  804 |                     if not cond_done:
  805 |                         piece += lit.text
  806 |                 else:                            # 'x' uncond / |x| rep
  807 |                     piece += lit.text
  808 |             piece += v
  809 |             for lit in (self.suffix or []):
```

```py
  826 | class _Dummy(_Node):
  827 |     """``d<tag>`` / ``n<tag>`` — emit attached conditional literals by presence."""
  828 | 
  829 |     def __init__(self, negate, tag, subfield, literals):

  831 |         self.tag = tag
  832 |         self.subfield = subfield
  833 |         self.literals = literals     # the preceding conditional literals

  837 |         fire = (not has) if self.negate else has
  838 |         if not fire:
  839 |             return ''
  840 |         return ''.join(l.text for l in self.literals)

  843 | class _Mfn(_Node):

  855 | class _Unifor(_Node):
  856 |     def __init__(self, spec_node, value_node):
  857 |         self.spec_node = spec_node       # _Node producing the code+arg literal
  858 |         self.value_node = value_node     # _Node producing the inline value (opt)
  859 | 
  860 |     def render(self, record, ctx, state):
  861 |         spec = self.spec_node.render(record, ctx, state) if self.spec_node else ''
  862 |         value = self.value_node.render(record, ctx, state) if self.value_node else ''
  863 |         try:
  864 |             return _dispatch_unifor(spec, value, ctx, record)
```

```py
  869 | class _Func(_Node):
  870 |     """val / f / s / rsum / rmin / rmax / ravr — numeric / string functions."""
  871 | 
  872 |     def __init__(self, name, args):
  873 |         self.name = name
  874 |         self.args = args                 # list[_Node]
  875 | 
  876 |     def render(self, record, ctx, state):
  877 |         name = self.name
  878 |         if name == 'val':
  879 |             # val() takes the FIRST numeric value of its formatted argument. For a
  880 |             # bare repeating field we read the first instance (so val(v999)==first
  881 |             # occurrence), else the whole formatted string (SPEC §8.1).
  882 |             arg = _unwrap_field(self.args[0]) if self.args else None
  883 |             if isinstance(arg, _Field):
  884 |                 txt = field_value(record, arg.tag, arg.subfield)
  885 |             else:
  886 |                 txt = self.args[0].render(record, ctx, state) if self.args else ''
  887 |             return _fmt_num(_to_num(txt))
  888 |         if name == 's':
  889 |             return ''.join(a.render(record, ctx, state) for a in self.args)
  890 |         if name == 'f':
  891 |             val = _to_num(self.args[0].render(record, ctx, state)) if self.args else 0
  892 |             width = int(_to_num(self.args[1].render(record, ctx, state))) \
  893 |                 if len(self.args) > 1 else 0
  894 |             dec = int(_to_num(self.args[2].render(record, ctx, state))) \
  895 |                 if len(self.args) > 2 else 0
  896 |             s = ('%.*f' % (dec, val)) if dec else str(int(val))
  897 |             return s.rjust(width) if width else s
  898 |         if name in ('rsum', 'rmin', 'rmax', 'ravr'):
  899 |             nums = []
  900 |             for a in self.args:
  901 |                 # a repeat-aware numeric scan over all instances it references
  902 |                 for piece in _iter_numeric(a, record, ctx, state):
  903 |                     nums.append(piece)
  904 |             if not nums:
  905 |                 return '0'
  906 |             if name == 'rsum':
  907 |                 return _fmt_num(sum(nums))
  908 |             if name == 'rmin':
  909 |                 return _fmt_num(min(nums))
  910 |             if name == 'rmax':
  911 |                 return _fmt_num(max(nums))
  912 |             return _fmt_num(sum(nums) / len(nums))
  913 |         return ''
  914 |
```

```py
  924 | 
  925 | 
  926 | def _iter_numeric(node, record, ctx, state):
  927 |     """Yield numeric values of a node across all repeat instances of any single
  928 |     field it references (rsum/rmin/... support). Falls back to one scalar."""
  929 |     inner = _unwrap_field(node)
  930 |     if isinstance(inner, _Field):
  931 |         for inst in _instances(record, inner.tag):
  932 |             v = inner._one(inst, record, ctx, state)
  933 |             if v:
  934 |                 yield _to_num(v)
  935 |         return
  936 |     txt = node.render(record, ctx, state)
  937 |     if txt:
  938 |         yield _to_num(txt)
  939 | 
  940 | 
  941 | class _Pred(_Node):
  942 |     """p(...) / a(...) used only inside IF conditions — returns '1' or ''."""
  943 | 
  944 |     def __init__(self, negate, field_node):
  945 |         self.negate = negate
  946 |         self.field_node = field_node
  947 | 
  948 |     def truth(self, record, ctx, state):
  949 |         if isinstance(self.field_node, _Field):
  950 |             has = present(record, self.field_node.tag, self.field_node.subfield)
  951 |         else:
  952 |             has = bool(self.field_node.render(record, ctx, state))
  953 |         return (not has) if self.negate else has
  954 | 
  955 |     def render(self, record, ctx, state):
  956 |         return '1' if self.truth(record, ctx, state) else ''
  957 | 
  958 | 
  959 | class _Compare(_Node):
  960 |     def __init__(self, left, op, right):
  961 |         self.left = left
  962 |         self.op = op
  963 |         self.right = right
  964 | 
  965 |     def truth(self, record, ctx, state):
  966 |         lv = self.left.render(record, ctx, state)
  967 |         rv = self.right.render(record, ctx, state)
  968 |         op = self.op
  969 |         if op == '=':
  970 |             return lv == rv
  971 |         if op == '<>':
  972 |             return lv != rv
  973 |         if op == ':':
  974 |             return rv.lower() in lv.lower()
```

```py
  975 |         # numeric/lexicographic for < <= > >=

  978 |             return ln < rn
  979 |         if op == '<=':
  980 |             return ln <= rn
  981 |         if op == '>':

  991 | class _BoolOp(_Node):
  992 |     def __init__(self, op, parts):
  993 |         self.op = op                    # 'and' | 'or'
  994 |         self.parts = parts
  995 | 
  996 |     def truth(self, record, ctx, state):
  997 |         vals = [_truth(p, record, ctx, state) for p in self.parts]
  998 |         return all(vals) if self.op == 'and' else any(vals)
  999 | 
 1000 |     def render(self, record, ctx, state):
 1001 |         return '1' if self.truth(record, ctx, state) else ''
 1002 | 
 1003 | 
 1004 | class _NotOp(_Node):
 1005 |     def __init__(self, part):
 1006 |         self.part = part
 1007 | 
 1008 |     def truth(self, record, ctx, state):
 1009 |         return not _truth(self.part, record, ctx, state)
 1010 | 
 1011 |     def render(self, record, ctx, state):
 1012 |         return '1' if self.truth(record, ctx, state) else ''
 1013 | 
 1014 | 
 1015 | def _truth(node, record, ctx, state):
 1016 |     """Evaluate any node as a boolean (IF condition context)."""
 1017 |     if hasattr(node, 'truth'):
 1018 |         return node.truth(record, ctx, state)
 1019 |     return bool(node.render(record, ctx, state))

 1022 | class _If(_Node):
 1023 |     def __init__(self, cond, then_node, else_node):
 1024 |         self.cond = cond
 1025 |         self.then_node = then_node
 1026 |         self.else_node = else_node
 1027 | 
 1028 |     def render(self, record, ctx, state):
 1029 |         if _truth(self.cond, record, ctx, state):
```

```py
 1033 | 
 1034 | class _Group(_Node):
 1035 |     """Repeat group ``(…)`` — evaluate the body once per instance of the (single)
 1036 |     repeating field referenced inside (SPEC §10). Emits joined; eval_lines splits
 1037 |     on the group boundary so multi-line group output is preserved."""
 1038 | 
 1039 |     def __init__(self, body, tags):
 1040 |         self.body = body
 1041 |         self.tags = tags                # field tags referenced in the group
 1042 | 
 1043 |     def _max_instances(self, record):
 1044 |         return max((len(_instances(record, t)) for t in self.tags), default=1)
 1045 | 
 1046 |     def render(self, record, ctx, state):
 1047 |         n = self._max_instances(record)
 1048 |         if n <= 1:
 1049 |             saved = state.get('group_index'), state.get('group_tag')
 1050 |             state['group_index'] = None
 1051 |             out = self.body.render(record, ctx, state)
 1052 |             state['group_index'], state['group_tag'] = saved
 1053 |             return out
 1054 |         lead = self.tags[0] if self.tags else None
 1055 |         out = []
 1056 |         saved_i, saved_t = state.get('group_index'), state.get('group_tag')
 1057 |         for k in range(n):
 1058 |             state['group_index'] = k
 1059 |             state['group_tag'] = lead
 1060 |             out.append(self.body.render(record, ctx, state))
 1061 |         state['group_index'], state['group_tag'] = saved_i, saved_t
 1062 |         return ''.join(out)
 1063 | 
 1064 |     def render_lines(self, record, ctx, state):
 1065 |         n = self._max_instances(record)
 1066 |         lead = self.tags[0] if self.tags else None
 1067 |         lines = []
 1068 |         saved_i, saved_t = state.get('group_index'), state.get('group_tag')
 1069 |         for k in range(max(n, 1)):
 1070 |             state['group_index'] = k if n > 1 else None
 1071 |             state['group_tag'] = lead
 1072 |             lines.append(self.body.render(record, ctx, state))
 1073 |         state['group_index'], state['group_tag'] = saved_i, saved_t
 1074 |         return lines
 1075 | 
 1076 | 
 1077 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
 1078 | # numeric helpers
 1079 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
 1080 | _NUM_RE = re.compile(r'-?\d+(?:\.\d+)?')
 1081 | 
 1082 | 
 1083 | def _to_num(text):
 1084 |     """``val()`` semantics — first number in the text, else 0."""
```

```py
 1089 |         return 0
 1090 |     s = m.group(0)
 1091 |     return float(s) if '.' in s else int(s)

 1094 | def _fmt_num(n):
 1095 |     if isinstance(n, float) and n.is_integer():

 1097 |     return str(n)

 1099 | 
 1100 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
 1101 | # Parser — recursive descent over the token stream.
 1102 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
 1103 | class _Parser:
 1104 |     def __init__(self, toks, strict=False):
 1105 |         self.toks = toks
 1106 |         self.pos = 0
 1107 |         self.strict = strict
 1108 | 
 1109 |     def _peek(self, k=0):
 1110 |         j = self.pos + k
 1111 |         return self.toks[j] if j < len(self.toks) else None
 1112 | 
 1113 |     def _next(self):
 1114 |         t = self._peek()
 1115 |         self.pos += 1
 1116 |         return t
 1117 | 
 1118 |     def _at_keyword(self, word):
 1119 |         t = self._peek()
 1120 |         return t is not None and t.kind == T_IDENT and t.value.lower() == word
 1121 | 
 1122 |     # ---- top: a sequence of terms separated by ',' / '+' / juxtaposition ---- #
 1123 |     def parse_sequence(self, stoppers=()):
 1124 |         parts = []
 1125 |         tags = []
 1126 |         while True:
 1127 |             t = self._peek()
 1128 |             if t is None:
 1129 |                 break
 1130 |             if t.kind == T_IDENT and t.value.lower() in stoppers:
 1131 |                 break
 1132 |             if t.kind == T_PUNCT and t.value in (',', '+'):
 1133 |                 self._next()
 1134 |                 continue
 1135 |             if t.kind == T_PUNCT and t.value == ')':
 1136 |                 break
 1137 |             node = self.parse_term(tags)
 1138 |             if node is not None:
 1139 |                 parts.append(node)
```

<!-- ─── страница 146 ─── -->

```py
 1140 |             else:
 1141 |                 # unrecognized token: skip (graceful degradation)
 1142 |                 if self.strict:
 1143 |                     raise PftError('unexpected token %r' % (t.value,), 99)
 1144 |                 self._next()
 1145 |         seq = _Seq(parts)
 1146 |         seq.tags = tags
 1147 |         return seq
 1148 | 
 1149 |     def parse_term(self, tags):
 1150 |         t = self._peek()
 1151 |         if t is None:
 1152 |             return None
 1153 |         # IF … FI
 1154 |         if t.kind == T_IDENT and t.value.lower() == 'if':
 1155 |             return self.parse_if(tags)
 1156 |         # repeat group
 1157 |         if t.kind == T_PUNCT and t.value == '(':
 1158 |             return self.parse_group(tags)
 1159 |         # field, with optional prefix/suffix literals
 1160 |         if t.kind == T_FIELD:
 1161 |             return self.parse_field(tags, prefix=None)
 1162 |         # leading conditional/repeat literal — may be a field prefix or dummy lit
 1163 |         if t.kind == T_LIT:
 1164 |             return self.parse_literal_led(tags)
 1165 |         if t.kind == T_DUMMY:
 1166 |             return self.parse_dummy(tags, leading=[])
 1167 |         if t.kind == T_UNIFOR:
 1168 |             self._next()
 1169 |             return self.parse_unifor(t.value)
 1170 |         if t.kind == T_IDENT:
 1171 |             return self.parse_ident(tags)
 1172 |         if t.kind == T_NUM:
 1173 |             self._next()
 1174 |             return _Literal(t.value, 'uncond')
 1175 |         if t.kind == T_PUNCT and t.value in ('/', '#', '%', '=', '<', '>', ':'):
 1176 |             self._next()                  # placement / stray operator -> no-op
 1177 |             return _Seq([])               # empty node (already advanced)
 1178 |         return None
 1179 | 
 1180 |     def parse_literal_led(self, tags):
 1181 |         """A literal that may prefix a field/dummy, or stand alone."""
 1182 |         lits = []
 1183 |         while self._peek() and self._peek().kind == T_LIT:
 1184 |             lit = self._next()
 1185 |             # |x|+ prefix marker
 1186 |             kind = lit.extra
 1187 |             nxt = self._peek()
 1188 |             if (kind == 'rep' and nxt and nxt.kind == T_PUNCT
 1189 |                     and nxt.value == '+'):
 1190 |                 self._next()
 1191 |                 lits.append(_Literal(lit.value, 'rep_plus'))
 1192 |             else:
 1193 |                 lits.append(_Literal(lit.value, kind))
 1194 |         nxt = self._peek()
```

<!-- ─── страница 147 ─── -->

```py
 1195 |         if nxt and nxt.kind == T_FIELD:
 1196 |             return self.parse_field(tags, prefix=lits)
 1197 |         if nxt and nxt.kind == T_DUMMY:
 1198 |             return self.parse_dummy(tags, leading=lits)
 1199 |         # standalone literals (no adjacent field): emit each once. A conditional
 1200 |         # "…" with nothing to be conditional on degenerates to always-emit
 1201 |         # (SPEC §5 — it is the field that gates it; absent, it just prints).
 1202 |         return _Seq([_Literal(l.text, 'uncond') for l in lits])
 1203 | 
 1204 |     def parse_field(self, tags, prefix):
 1205 |         t = self._next()
 1206 |         m = re.match(r'[vV](\d+)(?:\^(\*|[a-zA-Z0-9]))?(?:\*(\d+)(?:\.(\d+))?)?$',
 1207 |                      t.value)
 1208 |         if m is None:                     # defensive: never crash on a weird ref
 1209 |             return _Seq([])
 1210 |         tag = m.group(1)
 1211 |         sub = m.group(2)
 1212 |         first_sub = (sub == '*')
 1213 |         subfield = None if (sub is None or sub == '*') else sub
 1214 |         off = int(m.group(3)) if m.group(3) is not None else None
 1215 |         length = int(m.group(4)) if m.group(4) is not None else None
 1216 |         if tag not in tags:
 1217 |             tags.append(tag)
 1218 |         # gather trailing prefix/suffix literals. PFT separator idioms (SPEC §5):
 1219 |         #   |x|+  repeatable PREFIX before each instance except the FIRST
 1220 |         #   +|x|  repeatable SUFFIX after each instance except the LAST
 1221 |         #   |x|   plain repeatable, "x" conditional — emitted per instance.
 1222 |         # The first three are inter-instance separators; we fold them into the
 1223 |         # field node so a bare repeating field renders cleanly. Stop at ','.
 1224 |         sep_prefix = list(prefix or [])
 1225 |         suffix = []
 1226 |         while True:
 1227 |             nxt = self._peek()
 1228 |             if nxt and nxt.kind == T_PUNCT and nxt.value == '+':
 1229 |                 # +|x| suffix (not after last instance)
 1230 |                 after = self._peek(1)
 1231 |                 if after and after.kind == T_LIT and after.extra == 'rep':
 1232 |                     self._next()
 1233 |                     self._next()
 1234 |                     suffix.append(_Literal(after.value, 'plus_rep'))
 1235 |                     continue
 1236 |                 break
 1237 |             if nxt and nxt.kind == T_LIT:
 1238 |                 after = self._peek(1)
 1239 |                 if (nxt.extra == 'rep' and after and after.kind == T_PUNCT
 1240 |                         and after.value == '+'):
 1241 |                     # |x|+ prefix-except-first separator -> applies to this field
 1242 |                     self._next()
 1243 |                     self._next()
 1244 |                     sep_prefix.append(_Literal(nxt.value, 'rep_plus'))
 1245 |                     continue
 1246 |                 if nxt.extra in ('cond', 'rep'):
 1247 |                     self._next()
 1248 |                     suffix.append(_Literal(nxt.value, nxt.extra))
 1249 |                     continue
```

```py
 1251 |         return _Field(tag, subfield, first_sub, off, length,

 1254 |     def parse_dummy(self, tags, leading):
 1255 |         t = self._next()
 1256 |         m = re.match(r'([dDnN])(\d+)(?:\^([a-zA-Z0-9]))?$', t.value)
 1257 |         negate = m.group(1).lower() == 'n'
 1258 |         tag = m.group(2)
 1259 |         subfield = m.group(3)
 1260 |         # dummy literals are the leading conditional literals; if none captured,
 1261 |         # also pull any immediately-following ones (PFT allows either side here).
 1262 |         lits = list(leading)
 1263 |         return _Dummy(negate, tag, subfield, lits)
 1264 | 
 1265 |     def parse_unifor(self, raw):
 1266 |         """Parse the raw ``&name(...)`` text into a _Unifor node. The argument is
 1267 |         a mini-format whose FIRST literal carries the code + its static argument
 1268 |         (e.g. ``'C'``, ``'Av920#1'``, ``'30'``); anything after is the inline
 1269 |         VALUE format (e.g. ``v10^a`` in ``&unifor('C'v10^a)``).
 1270 | 
 1271 |         We peel the leading literal off the *raw* inner string directly so the
 1272 |         field-prefix folding (``parse_literal_led``) can't merge ``'C'`` into the
 1273 |         following ``v10^a`` (that merge is correct for normal output, wrong here).
 1274 |         """
 1275 |         inner = raw[raw.find('(') + 1:raw.rfind(')')]
 1276 |         inner = inner.strip()
 1277 |         spec_text = ''
 1278 |         rest = inner
 1279 |         if inner[:1] in ("'", '"', '|'):
 1280 |             close = inner.find(inner[0], 1)
 1281 |             if close >= 0:
 1282 |                 spec_text = inner[1:close]
 1283 |                 rest = inner[close + 1:]
 1284 |         spec_node = _Literal(spec_text, 'uncond') if spec_text or rest != inner \
 1285 |             else None
 1286 |         # the remaining text is the inline value mini-format (may be empty)
 1287 |         rest = rest.strip().lstrip(',').strip()
 1288 |         value_node = None
 1289 |         if rest:
 1290 |             value_node = _Parser(_tokenize(rest), self.strict).parse_sequence()
 1291 |         # if no quoted spec was found, fall back to evaluating the whole inner as
 1292 |         # the spec (covers &unifor(v910) style dynamic specs).
 1293 |         if spec_node is None:
 1294 |             value_seq = _Parser(_tokenize(inner), self.strict).parse_sequence()
 1295 |             return _Unifor(value_seq, None)
 1296 |         return _Unifor(spec_node, value_node)
 1297 | 
 1298 |     def parse_ident(self, tags):
 1299 |         t = self._next()
 1300 |         word = t.value.lower()
 1301 |         if word == 'mfn':
 1302 |             width = None
 1303 |             if self._peek() and self._peek().kind == T_PUNCT \
 1304 |                     and self._peek().value == '(':
```

```py
 1335 |             return _Seq([])
 1336 |         # unknown identifier -> emit as literal text (best-effort)

 1339 |     def _parse_call_args(self, tags):
 1340 |         args = []
 1341 |         if not (self._peek() and self._peek().kind == T_PUNCT
 1342 |                 and self._peek().value == '('):

 1344 |         self._next()                      # consume '('
 1345 |         depth = 1
 1346 |         cur = []

 1349 |             if t.kind == T_PUNCT and t.value == '(':
 1350 |                 depth += 1
 1351 |                 cur.append(t)

 1353 |                 depth -= 1

 1355 |                     break
 1356 |                 cur.append(t)
 1357 |             elif t.kind == T_PUNCT and t.value == ',' and depth == 1:
 1358 |                 args.append(_Parser(cur, self.strict).parse_sequence())
 1359 |                 cur = []
```

```py
 1370 |     def _collect_until_close(self):
 1371 |         depth = 1
 1372 |         out = []
 1373 |         while self.pos < len(self.toks) and depth > 0:
 1374 |             t = self._next()
 1375 |             if t.kind == T_PUNCT and t.value == '(':
 1376 |                 depth += 1
 1377 |             elif t.kind == T_PUNCT and t.value == ')':
 1378 |                 depth -= 1
 1379 |                 if depth == 0:
 1380 |                     break
 1381 |             out.append(t)
 1382 |         return out
 1383 | 
 1384 |     def parse_group(self, tags):
 1385 |         self._next()                      # consume '('
 1386 |         inner = self._collect_until_close()
 1387 |         sub = _Parser(inner, self.strict)
 1388 |         body = sub.parse_sequence()
 1389 |         gtags = list(getattr(body, 'tags', []))
 1390 |         for tg in gtags:
 1391 |             if tg not in tags:
 1392 |                 tags.append(tg)
 1393 |         return _Group(body, gtags)

 1395 |     # ---- IF condition / branches ---- #
 1396 |     def parse_if(self, tags):
 1397 |         self._next()                      # consume 'if'
 1398 |         cond = self.parse_condition(tags)
 1399 |         if not self._at_keyword('then'):
 1400 |             if self.strict:
 1401 |                 raise PftError('IF without THEN', 8)
 1402 |             return _Seq([])               # degrade
 1403 |         self._next()                      # consume 'then'
 1404 |         then_node = self.parse_sequence(stoppers=('else', 'fi'))
 1405 |         for tg in getattr(then_node, 'tags', []):
 1406 |             if tg not in tags:
 1407 |                 tags.append(tg)
 1408 |         else_node = None
 1409 |         if self._at_keyword('else'):
 1410 |             self._next()
 1411 |             else_node = self.parse_sequence(stoppers=('fi',))
 1412 |             for tg in getattr(else_node, 'tags', []):
 1413 |                 if tg not in tags:
 1414 |                     tags.append(tg)
```

```py
 1415 |         if self._at_keyword('fi'):

 1420 | 
 1421 |     def parse_condition(self, tags):
 1422 |         return self._parse_or(tags)
 1423 | 
 1424 |     def _parse_or(self, tags):
 1425 |         parts = [self._parse_and(tags)]
 1426 |         while self._at_keyword('or'):
 1427 |             self._next()
 1428 |             parts.append(self._parse_and(tags))
 1429 |         return parts[0] if len(parts) == 1 else _BoolOp('or', parts)
 1430 | 
 1431 |     def _parse_and(self, tags):
 1432 |         parts = [self._parse_not(tags)]
 1433 |         while self._at_keyword('and'):
 1434 |             self._next()
 1435 |             parts.append(self._parse_not(tags))
 1436 |         return parts[0] if len(parts) == 1 else _BoolOp('and', parts)
 1437 | 
 1438 |     def _parse_not(self, tags):
 1439 |         if self._at_keyword('not'):
 1440 |             self._next()
 1441 |             # NOT (expr) or NOT predicate
 1442 |             if self._peek() and self._peek().kind == T_PUNCT \
 1443 |                     and self._peek().value == '(':
 1444 |                 self._next()
 1445 |                 inner = self._collect_until_close()
 1446 |                 sub = _Parser(inner, self.strict).parse_condition(tags)
 1447 |                 return _NotOp(sub)
 1448 |             return _NotOp(self._parse_compare(tags))
 1449 |         return self._parse_compare(tags)
 1450 | 
 1451 |     def _parse_compare(self, tags):
 1452 |         # parenthesized condition
 1453 |         if self._peek() and self._peek().kind == T_PUNCT \
 1454 |                 and self._peek().value == '(':
 1455 |             self._next()
 1456 |             inner = self._collect_until_close()
 1457 |             return _Parser(inner, self.strict).parse_condition(tags)
 1458 |         left = self.parse_operand(tags)
 1459 |         t = self._peek()
 1460 |         if t and t.kind == T_PUNCT and t.value in ('=', '<>', '<', '<=',
 1461 |                                                    '>', '>=', ':'):
 1462 |             op = self._next().value
 1463 |             right = self.parse_operand(tags)
 1464 |             return _Compare(left, op, right)
 1465 |         # bare operand as a truth value (e.g. p(...) or a field)
 1466 |         return left
 1467 |

 1469 |         """A single value operand inside a condition (field, literal, unifor,
```

```py
 1509 | def eval(fmt, record, ctx=None, strict=False):
 1510 |     """Format ``record`` with the PFT ``fmt`` string → a single string.
 1511 | 
 1512 |     This is the keystone contract (SPEC_api_contracts §3.1 / M4):
 1513 |     ``pft.eval(fmt, record, ctx) -> str``.
 1514 | 
 1515 |     Parameters
 1516 |     ----------
 1517 |     fmt : str
 1518 |         Inline PFT source (a format body or a reference resolved by the caller).
 1519 |     record : dict
 1520 |         Field/subfield draft (see module docstring). ``None`` → empty record.
 1521 |     ctx : dict | None
 1522 |         Per-render workspace carrying, per the A1↔A3 contract:
 1523 |           * ``mfn``      — current record number (for ``mfn``/``mfn(d)``);
 1524 |           * ``now``      — datetime override for UNIFOR ``3`` (testability);
```

```py
 1527 |           * ``counters`` — persistent ``&uf('++C…')`` counters (A3);

 1560 | def eval_lines(fmt, record, ctx=None, strict=False):
 1561 |     """Format ``record`` → a list of strings, one per repeat-group instance.

 1566 |

 1568 |     instance becomes its own line. Otherwise the whole render is one line. Empty
 1569 |     lines are preserved (the caller decides whether to drop them).

 1571 |     ctx = ctx or {}
 1572 |     try:
 1573 |         ast = _compile(fmt, strict)
 1574 |     except PftError:
 1575 |         if strict:
 1576 |             raise
 1577 |         return []
 1578 |     state = {}
 1579 |     rec = record or {}
```

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """Global-correction ``.gbl`` executor (gap A3, epic #188).
    3 | 
    4 | Implements SPEC_engine_gbl.md, FIRST shippable slice: a self-contained,
    5 | pure-stdlib parser + applier + preview for the core ИРБИС batch-correction
    6 | operators, with the PFT (ФОРМАТ) evaluation deliberately factored out behind a
    7 | ``format_eval`` hook so the module works *before* the A1 PFT engine
    8 | (``access.pft``) lands and transparently delegates to it once it does.
    9 | 
   10 | What this module is
   11 | -------------------
   12 | ИРБИС ``IC_gbl`` ports a ``.gbl`` job — a parameter header plus a sequence of
   13 | operators — and applies it to each record of a selection. Each operator sees the
   14 | record in the state the previous operators left it in (GLOBAL_CORRECTION §1.0,
   15 | ``txt:5920``); ``apply`` mutates a *copy* so the caller's record is never touched.
   16 | 
   17 | Record model (the slice's JSON shape, SPEC §0 "Модель записи")
   18 | --------------------------------------------------------------
   19 | A record is a dict ``tag -> list of field instances``; each instance is a dict of
   20 | ``subfield letter -> value`` with the whole-field value under the empty key ``''``::
   21 | 
   22 |     {
   23 |       "200": [{"a": "Заглавие", "f": "Иванов"}],
   24 |       "910": [{"b": "1024365"}, {"b": "1024366"}],
   25 |       "920": [{"": "PAZK"}],
   26 |     }
   27 | 
   28 | Convenience: a bare string field value (``{"920": "PAZK"}``) or a single dict
   29 | (``{"200": {"a": "..."}}``) are accepted on input and normalised on the way in.
   30 | 
   31 | Operators implemented in THIS slice (GLOBAL_CORRECTION §1.3)
   32 | -----------------------------------------------------------
   33 |   * ``ADD``   — add a new field repetition (whole field) or a subfield.
   34 |   * ``REP``   — replace a field / subfield wholesale (empty ФОРМАТ 1 => delete).
   35 |   * ``CHA`` / ``CHAC`` — replace a substring (CHAC case-sensitive, CHA not).
   36 |   * ``DEL``   — delete a field repetition or a subfield (incl. F-mode '1'/'0').
   37 |   * ``DELR`` / ``UNDEL`` — logical record delete / undelete (status flag).
   38 |   * ``EMPTY`` — clear the record.
   39 |   * ``IF … FI`` — conditional block (ФОРМАТ result ``'1'`` gates the body).
   40 |   * ``REPEAT … UNTIL`` — loop the body while UNTIL's ФОРМАТ yields ``'1'``
```

<!-- ─── страница 155 ─── -->

```py
   41 |     (hard iteration cap, SPEC §3.4 R loop-guard).
   42 |   * ``PUTLOG`` / ``PUTFLD`` — append a protocol line (PUTFLD ≡ PUTLOG, deprecated).
   43 |   * ``//`` — comment (kept in the AST for protocol fidelity).
   44 | 
   45 | Occurrence selectors (3rd line, GLOBAL_CORRECTION §1.1): ``*`` ALL · ``N`` n-th ·
   46 | ``L`` last · ``L-N`` n-th from end · ``F`` by-format (i-th ФОРМАТ 1 line drives the
   47 | i-th repetition).
   48 | 
   49 | Cross-DB / other-record operators (GLOBAL_CORRECTION §1.3, INTEGRATION_MAP P0 #11.2)
   50 | -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   51 | The single P0 unblocker for cross-module edges (ToCat, Move691, MoveZakKp, CreateSZ,
   52 | InsteadLost, autoin) — these operators switch the «current record» to a NEW or a
   53 | FOREIGN record while their nested ``ADD/REP/…`` formats are still evaluated against
   54 | the *source* record of the source DB (``txt:5988-5991``):
   55 | 
   56 |   * ``NEWMFN`` — create a NEW record in (optionally another) DB; ФОРМАТ 1 → target
   57 |     db name (``'*'`` = current). The nested op-block builds the record; collected in
   58 |     ``ctx['_state']['new_records']`` keyed by target db, and ``emit(db, record)`` is
   59 |     called when a host store is wired.
   60 |   * ``NEWREC`` — undocumented conditional create (recon TODO #GBL-01). 2nd line is a
   61 |     *condition* format: result ``'1'`` ⇒ create a new record (in the current db),
   62 |     filled by the nested block; any other result ⇒ skip. (Best-effort: the
   63 |     ``autoin.gbl:2255`` `if … then '1'` flag reading; see comment at the parser.)
   64 |   * ``CORREC`` — apply a correction block to ANOTHER record/DB (cross-record edit):
   65 |     ФОРМАТ→db · ФОРМАТ→model-field-1001 key · ФОРМАТ→dictionary terms (``$``=trunc) ·
   66 |     [opt record count]. The target record is resolved via ``ctx['resolver']`` and
   67 |     the nested op-block is applied to it; the result is emitted back.
   68 |   * ``UNDOR`` — roll the current record back to the N-th / original copy from the
   69 |     reversible journal (``ctx['_state']['history']``), NOT by re-running the job.
   70 |   * ``END`` closes a NEWMFN/NEWREC/CORREC group; ``ALL`` copies every field of the
   71 |     source record into the record under construction (used right after NEWMFN/CORREC).
   72 | 
   73 | Cross-DB execution contract (the executor stays pure; the host wires real stores)
   74 | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   75 | ``apply(ast, record, ctx)`` accepts two optional hooks in ``ctx`` so a job running
   76 | over IBIS can read/write CMPL/RDR records without this module touching a database:
   77 | 
   78 |   * ``resolver(db, mfn) -> record | None`` — fetch a foreign record (CORREC target,
   79 |     or a NEWMFN that wants to seed from an existing record). Defaults to an
   80 |     in-memory store (``ctx['_stores']``) so the tests run with no host.
   81 |   * ``emit(db, record) -> mfn`` — persist a created/edited foreign record and return
   82 |     its mfn. Defaults to appending to the in-memory store and returning its index.
   83 | 
   84 | After the run, ``ctx['_state']['new_records']`` (dict ``db -> [record,…]``) and
   85 | ``ctx['_state']['correc']`` (dict ``db -> [{mfn, record},…]``) carry everything the
   86 | host needs to journal/persist; ``preview`` reports created/edited records too.
   87 | 
   88 | The format_eval boundary (SPEC §2)
   89 | ----------------------------------
   90 | ФОРМАТ 1/2 and every ``&uf(...)`` are ordinary PFT formats evaluated on the current
   91 | record — this module does NOT interpret PFT. It calls a single hook,
   92 | ``format_eval(fmt, record, ctx)`` (one string) / ``format_eval_lines`` (a list, for
   93 | F-mode and whole-field ADD). Resolution order (``resolve_format_eval``):
   94 | 
   95 |   1. an explicit ``ctx['format_eval']`` callable (tests inject literal/field stubs);
```

```py
  104 | import copy

  106 | 
  107 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  108 | # Record model — normalisation + field/subfield accessors.
  109 | # A field is stored as a list of instances; an instance is {subfield: value}
  110 | # with the whole-field text under the '' key. Input is liberally normalised.
  111 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  112 | def _norm_instance(inst):
  113 |     """Coerce one field instance to a ``{subfield: value}`` dict."""
  114 |     if isinstance(inst, dict):
  115 |         return dict(inst)
  116 |     # a bare string instance is the whole-field value
  117 |     return {'': '' if inst is None else str(inst)}
  118 | 
  119 | 
  120 | def normalize_record(record):
  121 |     """Return a canonical ``tag -> [instance dict]`` copy of ``record``.
  122 | 
  123 |     Accepts the liberal input shapes (bare string field, single dict, or list)
  124 |     and yields the strict internal form. Never mutates the input."""
  125 |     out = {}
  126 |     for tag, raw in (record or {}).items():
  127 |         tag = str(tag)
  128 |         if raw is None:
  129 |             out[tag] = []
  130 |         elif isinstance(raw, list):
  131 |             out[tag] = [_norm_instance(i) for i in raw]
  132 |         else:
  133 |             out[tag] = [_norm_instance(raw)]
  134 |     return out
  135 | 
  136 | 
  137 | def _field(record, tag):
  138 |     return record.setdefault(str(tag), [])
  139 | 
  140 | 
  141 | def field_values(record, tag, subfield=None):
  142 |     """All values of ``tag`` (a given subfield, or whole-field when subfield is
  143 |     None), one per repetition that actually carries it."""
  144 |     out = []
  145 |     for inst in record.get(str(tag), []):
  146 |         v = _inst_get(inst, subfield)
  147 |         if v != '':
  148 |             out.append(v)
  149 |     return out
  150 |
```

```py
  151 | 
  152 | def _inst_get(inst, subfield):
  153 |     """Read a subfield from one instance (None => whole-field ''). Subfield
  154 |     letters match case-insensitively (ИРБИС ^A == ^a)."""
  155 |     if subfield is None:
  156 |         return inst.get('', '')
  157 |     return inst.get(subfield) or inst.get(subfield.lower()) \
  158 |         or inst.get(subfield.upper()) or ''
  159 | 
  160 | 
  161 | def _inst_set(inst, subfield, value):
  162 |     inst['' if subfield is None else subfield] = value
  163 | 
  164 | 
  165 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  166 | # AST node types. Lightweight dict-like objects (no external deps); ``kind`` is
  167 | # the dispatch key for ``apply``. Closing markers (FI/UNTIL/END) are NOT nodes —
  168 | # they are consumed by the parser into block structure (SPEC §1.3).
  169 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  170 | class Op(dict):
  171 |     """An AST operator node. A thin dict subclass so it pretty-prints and is
  172 |     trivially serialisable, with attribute access for readability."""
  173 |     def __getattr__(self, k):
  174 |         try:
  175 |             return self[k]
  176 |         except KeyError:
  177 |             raise AttributeError(k)
  178 | 
  179 |     def __repr__(self):
  180 |         return 'Op(%r)' % (dict(self),)
  181 | 
  182 | 
  183 | class Program(dict):
  184 |     """Top-level AST: ``{'params': [...], 'body': [Op,...], 'warnings': [...]}``."""
  185 |     def __getattr__(self, k):
  186 |         try:
  187 |             return self[k]
  188 |         except KeyError:
  189 |             raise AttributeError(k)
  190 | 
  191 | 
  192 | class ParseError(ValueError):
  193 |     """Raised on a malformed job (unknown operator, unclosed block, bad %n)."""
  194 | 
  195 | 
  196 | # Occurrence selector kinds (3rd line of a field operator).
  197 | OCC_ALL, OCC_NTH, OCC_LAST, OCC_LAST_MINUS, OCC_BY_FORMAT = \
  198 |     'ALL', 'NTH', 'LAST', 'LAST_MINUS', 'BY_FORMAT'
  199 | 
  200 | _FIELD_OPS = ('ADD', 'REP', 'CHA', 'CHAC', 'DEL')
  201 | # Other-record / model-field operators: parsed for fidelity, not executed here.
  202 | _OTHER_RECORD = ('NEWMFN', 'NEWREC', 'CORREC')
  203 | _PLACEHOLDER = 'XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX'
  204 | 
  205 |
```

```py
  206 | def _is_placeholder(line):
```
