```py
  217 | def _parse_occ(line):
  218 |     """Parse the 3rd-line occurrence selector into an ``Occ`` tuple."""
  219 |     s = (line or '').strip()
  220 |     if s == '' or s == '*':
  221 |         return (OCC_ALL, None)
  222 |     up = s.upper()
  223 |     if up == 'F':
  224 |         return (OCC_BY_FORMAT, None)
  225 |     if up == 'L':
  226 |         return (OCC_LAST, None)
  227 |     if up.startswith('L-'):
  228 |         try:
  229 |             return (OCC_LAST_MINUS, int(up[2:]))
  230 |         except ValueError:
  231 |             return (OCC_ALL, None)
  232 |     try:
  233 |         return (OCC_NTH, int(s))
  234 |     except ValueError:
  235 |         # unknown selector — be permissive, treat as ALL (parity: server is lax)
  236 |         return (OCC_ALL, None)

  239 | def _parse_tag(line):
  240 |     """Split ``<tag>[^<sub>]`` (2nd line). Returns ``(tag, subfield|None)``."""
  241 |     s = (line or '').strip()
  242 |     if '^' in s:
  243 |         tag, sub = s.split('^', 1)
  244 |         return (tag.strip(), (sub.strip() or None))
  245 |     return (s, None)

  247 | 
  248 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  249 | # Parser — table-driven by operator "arity" in lines (SPEC §1.2).
  250 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  251 | def parse(gbl_text):
  252 |     """Parse a ``.gbl`` job (str or CP1251 bytes) into a :class:`Program` AST.
  253 | 
  254 |     Line-significant grammar (GLOBAL_CORRECTION §2):
  255 |       * line 1 = parameter count (0..9);
  256 |       * then 2 lines per parameter (value-or-.MNU/.WSS, then caption);
  257 |       * then operator groups, each a name line plus its fixed extra lines.
  258 | 
  259 |     Field operators (ADD/REP/CHA/CHAC/DEL) take 5 lines; flow operators their own
  260 |     count; ``IF…FI`` / ``REPEAT…UNTIL`` / ``NEWMFN…END`` parse into nested blocks.
```

```py
  261 |     Raises :class:`ParseError` on an unknown operator, an unclosed block, or a
  262 |     ``%n`` reference beyond the declared parameter count."""
  263 |     if isinstance(gbl_text, (bytes, bytearray)):
  264 |         gbl_text = bytes(gbl_text).decode('cp1251')
  265 |     # Keep blank lines (significant), drop the trailing newline split artefact.
  266 |     lines = gbl_text.replace('\r\n', '\n').replace('\r', '\n').split('\n')
  267 | 
  268 |     # ---- parameter header (line 1 = count, then 2 lines per param) ----------
  269 |     pos = 0
  270 |     if not lines or lines[0].strip() == '':
  271 |         raise ParseError('empty job: missing parameter-count line')
  272 |     try:
  273 |         nparams = int(lines[0].strip())
  274 |     except ValueError:
  275 |         raise ParseError('line 1: parameter count must be an integer, got %r'
  276 |                          % lines[0])
  277 |     pos = 1
  278 |     params = []
  279 |     for i in range(nparams):
  280 |         if pos + 1 >= len(lines):
  281 |             raise ParseError('parameter %d: truncated header' % (i + 1))
  282 |         value, caption = lines[pos], lines[pos + 1]
  283 |         source = 'prompt'
  284 |         if value.strip() == '':
  285 |             source = 'prompt'
  286 |         elif value.strip().lower().endswith('.mnu'):
  287 |             source = 'mnu'
  288 |         elif value.strip().lower().endswith('.wss'):
  289 |             source = 'wss'
  290 |         else:
  291 |             source = 'literal'
  292 |         params.append(Op(idx=i + 1, source=source,
  293 |                          value=value.strip(), caption=caption.strip()))
  294 |         pos += 2
  295 | 
  296 |     warnings = []
  297 |     body, pos = _parse_ops(lines, pos, nparams, warnings, stop=None)
  298 |     prog = Program(params=params, body=body, warnings=warnings)
  299 |     return prog
  300 | 
  301 | 
  302 | # Single-line flow / record operators -> their AST kind.
  303 | _RECORD_OPS = {'DELR': 'DELR', 'UNDEL': 'UNDEL', 'EMPTY': 'EMPTY', 'ALL': 'ALL'}
  304 | 
  305 | 
  306 | def _parse_ops(lines, pos, nparams, warnings, stop):
  307 |     """Parse operators until EOF or one of the ``stop`` closing markers.
  308 | 
  309 |     ``stop`` is a set of upper-case marker names (e.g. ``{'FI'}``) or None for
  310 |     top level. Returns ``(body, pos_after_marker_or_eof)``."""
  311 |     body = []
  312 |     n = len(lines)
  313 |     while pos < n:
  314 |         name_line = lines[pos]
  315 |         name = name_line.strip()
```

```py
  340 |             cond = _need(lines, pos + 1, 'IF: missing condition format')
  341 |             _check_params(cond, nparams)
  342 |             inner, pos = _parse_ops(lines, pos + 2, nparams, warnings, stop={'FI'})
  343 |             body.append(Op(kind='IF', cond=_norm_fmt(cond), body=inner))
  344 |             continue
  345 | 
  346 |         # ---- REPEAT … UNTIL ------------------------------------------------ #
  347 |         if up == 'REPEAT':
  348 |             inner, pos, until = _parse_repeat(lines, pos + 1, nparams, warnings)
  349 |             _check_params(until, nparams)
  350 |             body.append(Op(kind='REPEAT', body=inner, until=_norm_fmt(until)))
  351 |             continue
  352 | 
  353 |         # ---- UNDOR: roll current record back N copies (2 lines) ------------ #
  354 |         if up == 'UNDOR':
  355 |             steps = _need(lines, pos + 1, 'UNDOR: missing step-count format')
  356 |             _check_params(steps, nparams)
  357 |             body.append(Op(kind='UNDOR', steps=_norm_fmt(steps)))
  358 |             pos += 2
  359 |             continue
  360 | 
  361 |         # ---- PUTLOG / PUTFLD (deprecated alias) ---------------------------- #
  362 |         if up in ('PUTLOG', 'PUTFLD'):
  363 |             fmt = _need(lines, pos + 1, '%s: missing format line' % up)
  364 |             _check_params(fmt, nparams)
  365 |             if up == 'PUTFLD':
  366 |                 warnings.append('PUTFLD is deprecated; use PUTLOG (line %d)'
  367 |                                 % (pos + 1))
  368 |             body.append(Op(kind='PUTLOG', fmt=_norm_fmt(fmt), deprecated=(up == 'PUTFLD')))
  369 |             pos += 2
  370 |             continue
```

```py
  430 | def _parse_repeat(lines, pos, nparams, warnings):
  431 |     """Body of a REPEAT, returning ``(body, pos_after_until, until_fmt)``."""
  432 |     body, pos = _parse_ops(lines, pos, nparams, warnings, stop={'UNTIL'})
  433 |     # the UNTIL marker is consumed by _parse_ops; its format is the next line

  440 | def _parse_other_record(up, lines, pos, nparams, warnings):
  441 |     """Parse NEWMFN/NEWREC (2 header lines) or CORREC (4-5) plus their … END body.
  442 | 
  443 |     These now EXECUTE (GLOBAL_CORRECTION §1.3, INTEGRATION_MAP P0 #11.2). The
  444 |     nested body is parsed into a real op-block; ФОРМАТ 1 inside it is evaluated on
  445 |     the *source* record at apply time (``txt:5988-5991``). Returns
  446 |     ``(op, pos_after_END)``.
  447 | 
  448 |     * NEWMFN — line 2 = ФОРМАТ → target db name (``'*'`` = current db).
  449 |     * NEWREC — line 2 = ФОРМАТ → *condition* (recon TODO #GBL-01). The only field
  450 |       example (``IBIS/autoin.gbl:2255`` `if v920='J' … then '1'…`) reads it as a
  451 |       create *flag*: result ``'1'`` ⇒ create in the current db. We implement that
  452 |       documented behaviour; if a site instead uses the 2nd line as a db name, the
  453 |       flag test simply never fires (result ≠ '1') so nothing is silently created.
  454 |     * CORREC — db / key→model-field 1001 / dictionary terms (``$``=trunc) / opt
  455 |       record-count. The 5th line is taken as the count only when it is a bare
  456 |       integer (an operator name never is), else the body starts there."""
  457 |     if up == 'NEWMFN':
  458 |         fmt = _need(lines, pos + 1, 'NEWMFN: missing db-name format line')
  459 |         _check_params(fmt, nparams)
  460 |         header = {'kind': 'NEWMFN', 'dbFmt': _norm_fmt(fmt)}
  461 |         body_start = pos + 2
  462 |     elif up == 'NEWREC':
  463 |         fmt = _need(lines, pos + 1, 'NEWREC: missing condition format line')
  464 |         _check_params(fmt, nparams)
  465 |         # recon #GBL-01: 2nd line read as a create-condition (the documented case).
  466 |         header = {'kind': 'NEWREC', 'condFmt': _norm_fmt(fmt), 'experimental': True}
  467 |         warnings.append('NEWREC is experimental (recon #GBL-01: 2nd-line condition '
  468 |                         'semantics inferred from autoin.gbl) (line %d)' % (pos + 1))
  469 |         body_start = pos + 2
  470 |     else:  # CORREC: db / key→1001 / terms (4 lines; optional 5th = count)
  471 |         for off in range(1, 4):
  472 |             _need(lines, pos + off, 'CORREC: truncated header')
  473 |         _check_params(lines[pos + 1], nparams)
  474 |         _check_params(lines[pos + 2], nparams)
  475 |         _check_params(lines[pos + 3], nparams)
  476 |         header = {
  477 |             'kind': 'CORREC',
  478 |             'dbFmt': _norm_fmt(lines[pos + 1]),
  479 |             'keyFmt': _norm_fmt(lines[pos + 2]),
  480 |             'termsFmt': _norm_fmt(lines[pos + 3]),
```

```py
  494 | def _need(lines, idx, msg):
  495 |     if idx >= len(lines):
  496 |         raise ParseError(msg)

  500 | def _check_params(fmt, nparams):
  501 |     """Reject a ``%n`` reference beyond the declared parameter count (SPEC §1.4).
  502 | 
  503 |     Only bare ``%1..%9`` digit references count; ``%`` followed by a non-digit
  504 |     (e.g. format directives) is ignored."""
  505 |     i = 0
  506 |     s = fmt or ''
  507 |     while i < len(s) - 1:
  508 |         if s[i] == '%' and s[i + 1].isdigit():
  509 |             n = int(s[i + 1])
  510 |             if n == 0 or n > nparams:
  511 |                 raise ParseError('format references %%%d but only %d parameter(s) '
  512 |                                  'declared' % (n, nparams))
  513 |             i += 2
  514 |             continue
  515 |         i += 1
  516 | 
  517 | 
  518 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  519 | # Format evaluation hook — delegate to A1 (access.pft.eval) when importable,
  520 | # else a deliberately small literal/field stub so this slice runs standalone.
  521 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  522 | def stub_format_eval(fmt, record, ctx):
  523 |     """Minimal ФОРМАТ evaluator (used only when no PFT engine is wired).
  524 | 
  525 |     Understands just enough to drive the slice's tests and simple literal jobs:
  526 |       * the empty-string producer ``#`` and ∅ -> ``''``;
  527 |       * a single quoted literal ``'…'`` -> its contents (``%n`` substituted);
  528 |       * a bare field reference ``vNNN`` / ``vNNN^x`` -> the first repetition's
  529 |         value;
  530 |       * a parameter reference ``%n`` on its own -> the parameter value.
  531 |     Anything richer (``if…then…``, ``&uf(...)``) is beyond the stub: it returns the
  532 |     raw string so a misconfigured job degrades visibly rather than crashing."""
  533 |     s = (fmt or '').strip()
  534 |     if s == '' or s == '#':
  535 |         return ''
```

```py
  540 |     # bare field reference vNNN[^x]
  541 |     if s.lower().startswith('v') and len(s) > 1 and s[1].isdigit():

  554 | def _subst_params(s, ctx):
  555 |     """Replace ``%1..%9`` with the job parameter values from ``ctx['params']``."""
  556 |     params = (ctx or {}).get('params') or {}
  557 |     out = []
  558 |     i = 0
  559 |     while i < len(s):
  560 |         if s[i] == '%' and i + 1 < len(s) and s[i + 1].isdigit():
  561 |             out.append(str(params.get(s[i + 1], params.get(int(s[i + 1]), ''))))
  562 |             i += 2
  563 |             continue
  564 |         out.append(s[i])
  565 |         i += 1
  566 |     return ''.join(out)
  567 |

  569 | def _import_pft_eval():
  570 |     """Return ``access.pft.eval`` if the A1 module is importable, else None."""
  571 |     try:
  572 |         from access import pft            # noqa: F401  (A1, may not exist yet)
  573 |     except Exception:
  574 |         return None
  575 |     return getattr(pft, 'eval', None)

  578 | def resolve_format_eval(ctx):
  579 |     """Pick the active format evaluator (SPEC §2 delegation boundary).
  580 | 
  581 |     Precedence: an explicit ``ctx['format_eval']`` (tests inject this) > the A1
  582 |     ``access.pft.eval`` if importable > the built-in :func:`stub_format_eval`."""
  583 |     if ctx and ctx.get('format_eval'):
  584 |         return ctx['format_eval']
  585 |     pft_eval = _import_pft_eval()
  586 |     if pft_eval is not None:
  587 |         return pft_eval
  588 |     return stub_format_eval
```

```py
  591 | def _eval(fmt, record, ctx):
  592 |     return resolve_format_eval(ctx)(fmt, record, ctx)
  593 | 
  594 | 
  595 | def _fmt_rec(rec, ctx):
  596 |     """The record a field operator's ФОРМАТ is evaluated on.
  597 | 
  598 |     Normally the very record being mutated. Inside a NEWMFN/NEWREC/CORREC block the
  599 |     nested ``ADD/REP/…`` mutate the new/foreign record, yet ФОРМАТ 1 is computed on
  600 |     the *source* record of the source DB (``txt:5988-5991``) — the cross-DB executor
  601 |     sets ``ctx['_fmt_record']`` to that source so this returns it."""
  602 |     if ctx and ctx.get('_fmt_record') is not None:
  603 |         return ctx['_fmt_record']
  604 |     return rec
  605 | 
  606 | 
  607 | def _eval_lines(fmt, record, ctx):
  608 |     """Multi-line evaluation (F-mode / whole-field ADD). Uses
  609 |     ``ctx['format_eval_lines']`` / ``access.pft.evalLines`` when present, else
  610 |     splits the single-string result on newlines."""
  611 |     if ctx and ctx.get('format_eval_lines'):
  612 |         return list(ctx['format_eval_lines'](fmt, record, ctx))
  613 |     try:
  614 |         from access import pft
  615 |         if hasattr(pft, 'evalLines'):
  616 |             return list(pft.evalLines(fmt, record, ctx))
  617 |     except Exception:
  618 |         pass
  619 |     res = _eval(fmt, record, ctx)
  620 |     return res.split('\n') if res != '' else ['']
  621 | 
  622 | 
  623 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  624 | # Occurrence resolution — turn an ``Occ`` selector into a list of indices into a
  625 | # field's repetition list (GLOBAL_CORRECTION §1.1).
  626 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  627 | def _resolve_occ(occ, count):
  628 |     kind, arg = occ
  629 |     if count == 0:
  630 |         return []
  631 |     if kind == OCC_ALL:
  632 |         return list(range(count))
  633 |     if kind == OCC_NTH:
  634 |         idx = arg - 1                       # 1-based in the job
  635 |         return [idx] if 0 <= idx < count else []
  636 |     if kind == OCC_LAST:
  637 |         return [count - 1]
  638 |     if kind == OCC_LAST_MINUS:
  639 |         idx = count - 1 - arg
  640 |         return [idx] if 0 <= idx < count else []
  641 |     # BY_FORMAT handled at the call site (needs the format lines)
  642 |     return []
  643 | 
  644 | 
  645 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
```

```py
  646 | # Operator execution. ``apply`` runs the program body over a *copy* of the
  647 | # record; ``_apply_op`` dispatches by kind. ``state`` carries the protocol log,
  648 | # the deleted/empty flags and a record of skipped (unsupported) operators.
  649 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  650 | def apply(ast, record, ctx=None, resolver=None, emit=None):
  651 |     """Execute ``ast`` over a copy of ``record`` and return the new record.
  652 | 
  653 |     Operators run sequentially; each sees the record as the previous ones left
  654 |     it (SPEC §0 parity). ФОРМАТ lines are evaluated through the format_eval hook
  655 |     (delegated to A1 when available). The input record is never mutated.
  656 | 
  657 |     ``ctx`` may carry ``format_eval`` / ``params`` / ``max_iterations`` plus the
  658 |     cross-DB hooks (also accepted as explicit args, which win):
  659 | 
  660 |     * ``resolver(db, mfn) -> record | None`` — read a foreign record (CORREC
  661 |       target). Defaults to the in-memory store under ``ctx['_stores']``.
  662 |     * ``emit(db, record) -> mfn`` — persist a created/edited foreign record and
  663 |       return its mfn. Defaults to appending to the in-memory store.
  664 | 
  665 |     After the run, ``ctx['_state']`` (if ctx is a dict) holds ``{putlog, deleted,
  666 |     emptied, skipped, new_records, correc, history, db}``; the return value is the
  667 |     corrected source record. The host wires real stores via ``resolver``/``emit``;
  668 |     nothing here touches a database."""
  669 |     orig_ctx = ctx
  670 |     work_ctx = dict(ctx) if ctx else {}
  671 |     if resolver is not None:
  672 |         work_ctx['resolver'] = resolver
  673 |     if emit is not None:
  674 |         work_ctx['emit'] = emit
  675 |     # In-memory default cross-DB store so the executor runs standalone in tests.
  676 |     work_ctx.setdefault('_stores', dict(work_ctx.get('_stores') or {}))
  677 |     rec = normalize_record(record)
  678 |     state = {
  679 |         'putlog': [], 'deleted': False, 'emptied': False, 'skipped': [],
  680 |         'new_records': {},      # db -> [record, …] created via NEWMFN/NEWREC
  681 |         'correc': {},           # db -> [{mfn, record}, …] edited via CORREC
  682 |         'history': list(work_ctx.get('history') or []),  # copy journal for UNDOR
  683 |         'db': work_ctx.get('db', '*'),                   # the source db name
  684 |     }
  685 |     body = ast['body'] if isinstance(ast, dict) else ast
  686 |     _run_body(body, rec, work_ctx, state)
  687 |     # Surface the run state on BOTH the working copy and the caller's ctx dict
  688 |     # (so a caller that passed a dict can read ctx['_state'] after the call).
  689 |     work_ctx['_state'] = state
  690 |     if isinstance(orig_ctx, dict):
  691 |         orig_ctx['_state'] = state
  692 |     return rec
  693 | 
  694 | 
  695 | def _run_body(body, rec, ctx, state):
  696 |     for op in body:
  697 |         _apply_op(op, rec, ctx, state)
  698 | 
  699 | 
  700 | def _apply_op(op, rec, ctx, state):
```

```py
  711 | def _op_add(op, rec, ctx, state):
  712 |     """ADD: add a new repetition (whole field) or a subfield (GC §1.3 ADD).
  713 | 
  714 |     ``fr`` is the format record — the source record inside a cross-DB block."""

  721 |             if line == '' and op['fmt1'] == '':
  722 |                 continue
  723 |             field.append({'': line})
  724 |         return
  725 |     # subfield ADD: 1st ФОРМАТ 1 line -> subfield in the targeted repetition(s).
  726 |     val = _eval(op['fmt1'], fr, ctx)
  727 |     occ = op['occ']
  728 |     if occ[0] == OCC_BY_FORMAT:
  729 |         lines = _eval_lines(op['fmt1'], fr, ctx)
  730 |         for i, line in enumerate(lines):
  731 |             if i < len(field) and line != '':
  732 |                 _inst_set(field[i], sub, line)
  733 |         return
  734 |     idxs = _resolve_occ(occ, len(field))
  735 |     if not idxs:
  736 |         field.append({})
  737 |         idxs = [len(field) - 1]
  738 |     for i in idxs:
```

```py
  760 | def _rep_one(field, i, sub, val):
  761 |     if sub is None:
  762 |         if val == '':
  763 |             field[i] = None                 # marked for compaction (delete)
  764 |         else:
  765 |             field[i] = {'': val}
  766 |     else:
  767 |         if val == '':
  768 |             field[i].pop(sub, None)
  769 |             field[i].pop(sub.lower(), None)
  770 |             field[i].pop(sub.upper(), None)
  771 |         else:
  772 |             _inst_set(field[i], sub, val)
  773 | 
  774 | 
  775 | def _compact(field):
  776 |     field[:] = [f for f in field if f is not None]

  779 | def _op_cha(op, rec, ctx, state):
  780 |     """CHA/CHAC: replace substring A (ФОРМАТ 1) with B (ФОРМАТ 2) in the field /
  781 |     subfield. CHAC is case-sensitive; CHA matches case-insensitively."""
  782 |     tag, sub = op['tag'], op['subfield']
  783 |     field = _field(rec, tag)
  784 |     fr = _fmt_rec(rec, ctx)
  785 |     case_sensitive = (op['kind'] == 'CHAC')
  786 |     occ = op['occ']
  787 |     a_default = _eval(op['fmt1'], fr, ctx)
  788 |     b_default = _eval(op['fmt2'], fr, ctx)
  789 |     if occ[0] == OCC_BY_FORMAT:
  790 |         a_lines = _eval_lines(op['fmt1'], fr, ctx)
  791 |         b_lines = _eval_lines(op['fmt2'], fr, ctx)
  792 |         for i in range(len(field)):
  793 |             a = a_lines[i] if i < len(a_lines) else ''
  794 |             b = b_lines[i] if i < len(b_lines) else ''
  795 |             _cha_one(field[i], sub, a, b, case_sensitive)
  796 |         return
  797 |     for i in _resolve_occ(occ, len(field)):
  798 |         _cha_one(field[i], sub, a_default, b_default, case_sensitive)
  799 |
```

```py
  813 |     for k in keys:
  814 |         cur = inst.get(k, '')
  815 |         if a == '':
  816 |             inst[k] = cur + b               # A empty -> append B
  817 |         elif case_sensitive:
  818 |             inst[k] = cur.replace(a, b)
  819 |         else:
  820 |             inst[k] = _ci_replace(cur, a, b)
  821 | 
  822 | 
  823 | def _ci_replace(text, a, b):
  824 |     """Case-insensitive replace-all of ``a`` with ``b`` (CHA semantics)."""
  825 |     if not a:
  826 |         return text
  827 |     out = []
  828 |     low_a = a.lower()
  829 |     i = 0
  830 |     lt = text.lower()
  831 |     while i < len(text):
  832 |         if lt.startswith(low_a, i):
  833 |             out.append(b)
  834 |             i += len(a)
  835 |         else:
  836 |             out.append(text[i])
  837 |             i += 1
  838 |     return ''.join(out)
  839 | 
  840 | 
  841 | def _op_del(op, rec, ctx, state):
  842 |     """DEL: delete a field repetition or a subfield (GC §1.3 DEL).
  843 | 
  844 |     F-mode: ФОРМАТ 1 yields one line per repetition; ``'1'`` deletes it."""
  845 |     tag, sub = op['tag'], op['subfield']
  846 |     field = _field(rec, tag)
  847 |     occ = op['occ']
  848 |     if occ[0] == OCC_BY_FORMAT:
  849 |         lines = _eval_lines(op['fmt1'], _fmt_rec(rec, ctx), ctx)
  850 |         keep = []
  851 |         for i, inst in enumerate(field):
  852 |             flag = lines[i].strip() if i < len(lines) else '0'
  853 |             if flag == '1':
  854 |                 continue                    # delete this repetition
  855 |             keep.append(inst)
  856 |         field[:] = keep
  857 |         return
  858 |     idxs = set(_resolve_occ(occ, len(field)))
  859 |     if sub is None:
  860 |         field[:] = [inst for i, inst in enumerate(field) if i not in idxs]
  861 |     else:
  862 |         for i in idxs:
  863 |             field[i].pop(sub, None)
  864 |             field[i].pop(sub.lower(), None)
  865 |             field[i].pop(sub.upper(), None)
```

```py
  868 | # ---- record / flow operators ---------------------------------------------- #
  869 | def _op_delr(op, rec, ctx, state):
  870 |     state['deleted'] = True
  871 |     rec['*status'] = [{'': 'deleted'}]
  872 | 
  873 | 
  874 | def _op_undel(op, rec, ctx, state):
  875 |     state['deleted'] = False
  876 |     rec['*status'] = [{'': 'active'}]
  877 | 
  878 | 
  879 | def _op_empty(op, rec, ctx, state):
  880 |     state['emptied'] = True
  881 |     rec.clear()
  882 | 
  883 | 
  884 | def _op_if(op, rec, ctx, state):
  885 |     if _eval(op['cond'], _fmt_rec(rec, ctx), ctx).strip() == '1':
  886 |         _run_body(op['body'], rec, ctx, state)
  887 | 
  888 | 
  889 | def _op_repeat(op, rec, ctx, state):
  890 |     limit = (ctx or {}).get('max_iterations', 100000)
  891 |     iters = 0
  892 |     while True:
  893 |         _run_body(op['body'], rec, ctx, state)
  894 |         iters += 1
  895 |         if _eval(op['until'], _fmt_rec(rec, ctx), ctx).strip() != '1':
  896 |             break
  897 |         if iters >= limit:
  898 |             raise RuntimeError('REPEAT exceeded %d iterations (loop guard)' % limit)
  899 | 
  900 | 
  901 | def _op_putlog(op, rec, ctx, state):
  902 |     state['putlog'].append(_eval(op['fmt'], _fmt_rec(rec, ctx), ctx))
  903 | 
  904 | 
  905 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  906 | # Cross-DB / other-record operators (GLOBAL_CORRECTION §1.3; INTEGRATION_MAP P0
  907 | # #11.2). The nested ADD/REP/… mutate a NEW or FOREIGN record while their ФОРМАТ
  908 | # is still computed on the *source* record (txt:5988-5991); we model that by
  909 | # running the nested block on the target record with ctx['_fmt_record'] pinned to
  910 | # the source. Defaults to an in-memory store so the executor runs with no host.
  911 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  912 | def _store(ctx):
  913 |     """The in-memory default cross-DB store (db -> list of records)."""
  914 |     return ctx.setdefault('_stores', {})
  915 | 
  916 | 
  917 | def _default_resolver(ctx, db, mfn):
  918 |     recs = _store(ctx).get(db, [])
  919 |     if isinstance(mfn, int) and 0 <= mfn < len(recs):
  920 |         return recs[mfn]
```

```py
  924 | def _default_emit(ctx, db, record):

  929 | 
  930 | def _resolve_db(fmt, source_rec, ctx, state):
  931 |     """ФОРМАТ → target db name; ``'*'`` / ∅ resolve to the source db."""
  932 |     name = _eval(fmt, source_rec, ctx).strip()
  933 |     if name in ('', '*'):
  934 |         return state.get('db', '*')
  935 |     return name
  936 | 
  937 | 
  938 | def _run_crossdb_block(body, target_rec, source_rec, ctx, state):
  939 |     """Run a nested NEWMFN/NEWREC/CORREC body: mutate ``target_rec`` while ФОРМАТ
  940 |     evaluates on ``source_rec``. Restores the previous ``_fmt_record`` after (so
  941 |     nesting and the surrounding source-record context are preserved)."""
  942 |     prev = ctx.get('_fmt_record')
  943 |     ctx['_fmt_record'] = source_rec
  944 |     try:
  945 |         _run_body(body, target_rec, ctx, state)
  946 |     finally:
  947 |         if prev is None:
  948 |             ctx.pop('_fmt_record', None)
  949 |         else:
  950 |             ctx['_fmt_record'] = prev
  951 | 
  952 | 
  953 | def _emit_record(ctx, db, record):
  954 |     # In preview (dry-run) the host's persistence is suppressed (SPEC §4.1 / AC5):
  955 |     # we still register the record in the isolated in-memory store so the preview
  956 |     # report can show what WOULD be created/edited, but never call the host emit.
  957 |     if ctx.get('_preview'):
  958 |         return _default_emit(ctx, db, record)
  959 |     emit = ctx.get('emit')
  960 |     if emit is not None:
  961 |         return emit(db, record)
  962 |     return _default_emit(ctx, db, record)
  963 | 
  964 | 
  965 | def _op_all(op, rec, ctx, state):
  966 |     """ALL: copy every field of the SOURCE record into the record under
  967 |     construction (GC §1.3 ALL; used right after NEWMFN/CORREC). A no-op at top
  968 |     level (source == target), where it would just copy a record onto itself."""
  969 |     src = _fmt_rec(rec, ctx)
  970 |     if src is rec:                           # not inside a cross-DB block
  971 |         return
  972 |     for tag, insts in src.items():
  973 |         if tag.startswith('*'):
  974 |             continue
  975 |         rec.setdefault(tag, [])
```

```py
  979 | def _op_newmfn(op, rec, ctx, state):
  980 |     """NEWMFN: create a NEW record in the (optionally other) target db from the
  981 |     nested ADD-block; ФОРМАТ 1 inside it is computed on the source record."""
  982 |     db = _resolve_db(op['dbFmt'], rec, ctx, state)
  983 |     new_rec = {}

  989 | 
  990 | def _op_newrec(op, rec, ctx, state):
  991 |     """NEWREC (experimental, recon #GBL-01): conditional create. The 2nd-line
  992 |     format is read as a *condition* — result ``'1'`` creates a new record in the
  993 |     current db, filled by the nested block; any other result skips silently."""
  994 |     if _eval(op['condFmt'], rec, ctx).strip() != '1':
  995 |         return
  996 |     db = state.get('db', '*')
  997 |     new_rec = {}
  998 |     _run_crossdb_block(op['body'], new_rec, rec, ctx, state)
  999 |     state['new_records'].setdefault(db, []).append(new_rec)
 1000 |     mfn = _emit_record(ctx, db, new_rec)
 1001 |     state.setdefault('emitted', []).append({'op': 'NEWREC', 'db': db, 'mfn': mfn})
 1002 | 
 1003 | 
 1004 | def _op_correc(op, rec, ctx, state):
 1005 |     """CORREC: apply the nested op-block to ANOTHER record/db (cross-record edit).
 1006 | 
 1007 |     Target resolution (GC §1.3): ФОРМАТ→db · ФОРМАТ→model-field-1001 key (planted
 1008 |     on each target as field 1001 so the body can read it) · ФОРМАТ→dictionary
 1009 |     terms (``$``=truncation marker). Targets are fetched through ``ctx['resolver']``
 1010 |     by (db, term); each is edited (with 1001 stripped at the end, per the recon
 1011 |     note «не забывать удалять поле 1001»), emitted back, and recorded."""
 1012 |     db = _resolve_db(op['dbFmt'], rec, ctx, state)
 1013 |     key = _eval(op['keyFmt'], rec, ctx)
 1014 |     terms_raw = _eval_lines(op['termsFmt'], rec, ctx)
 1015 |     terms = [t.strip() for t in terms_raw if t.strip()]
 1016 |     resolver = ctx.get('resolver') or (lambda d, m: _default_resolver(ctx, d, m))
 1017 |     limit = op.get('limit')
 1018 |     edited = 0
 1019 |     for term in terms:
 1020 |         if limit is not None and edited >= limit:
 1021 |             break
 1022 |         target = resolver(db, term)          # resolver maps a term → its record
 1023 |         if target is None:
 1024 |             state.setdefault('correc_misses', []).append({'db': db, 'term': term})
 1025 |             continue
 1026 |         target = normalize_record(target)
 1027 |         target['1001'] = [{'': key}]         # model field 1001 = passed key
 1028 |         _run_crossdb_block(op['body'], target, rec, ctx, state)
 1029 |         target.pop('1001', None)             # strip the model field after the body
 1030 |         mfn = _emit_record(ctx, db, target)
```

```py
 1036 | def _op_undor(op, rec, ctx, state):
 1037 |     """UNDOR: roll the current record back to a previous copy from the journal
 1038 |     (GC §1.3 UNDOR, ``txt:6006-6031``) — NOT by re-running the job. The step format
 1039 |     yields ``N`` (N copies back, N=1 = previous), ``'*'`` (the original/first copy),
 1040 |     or ∅ (do nothing). History is ``state['history']`` (oldest → newest)."""
 1041 |     steps = _eval(op['steps'], _fmt_rec(rec, ctx), ctx).strip()
 1042 |     history = state.get('history') or []
 1043 |     if not steps or not history:
 1044 |         return
 1045 |     if steps == '*':
 1046 |         snap = history[0]                    # original copy
 1047 |     else:
 1048 |         try:
 1049 |             n = int(steps)
 1050 |         except ValueError:
 1051 |             return
 1052 |         if n <= 0:
 1053 |             return
 1054 |         idx = len(history) - n               # N copies back from the newest
 1055 |         if idx < 0:
 1056 |             return
 1057 |         snap = history[idx]
 1058 |     rec.clear()
 1059 |     rec.update(copy.deepcopy(normalize_record(snap)))
 1060 |

 1067 |     'ALL': _op_all, 'UNDOR': _op_undor,
 1068 |     'COMMENT': lambda *a: None,
 1069 | }

 1072 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
 1073 | # Preview — dry-run producing a per-record field-level before/after diff with NO
 1074 | # mutation of the inputs (SPEC §4.1). Counters/persistence side effects are out
 1075 | # of scope for this slice; the diff is computed purely from the record copies.
 1076 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
 1077 | def preview(ast, records, ctx=None):
 1078 |     """Dry-run ``ast`` over each record in ``records`` and return a diff report.
 1079 | 
 1080 |     Returns a list of per-record dicts::
 1081 | 
 1082 |         { 'index': i, 'status': 'changed'|'unchanged'|'deleted'|'emptied'|'error',
 1083 |           'changes': [ {tag, subfield, op, before, after}, ... ],
 1084 |           'putlog': [...], 'before': {...}, 'after': {...},
 1085 |           'created': [ {db, record}, ... ],            # NEWMFN/NEWREC would create
```

```py
 1126 |             'created': copy.deepcopy(created), 'correc': copy.deepcopy(correc),
 1127 |         })

 1131 | def diff_records(before, after):

 1137 |     tags = list(dict.fromkeys(list(before.keys()) + list(after.keys())))
 1138 |     for tag in tags:

 1140 |             continue
```

```py
 1153 | def _all_subfields(insts):

 1161 | def _vals(insts, sub):
 1162 |     key = '' if sub is None else sub
 1163 |     out = []
 1164 |     for inst in insts:
 1165 |         if sub is None:
 1166 |             if '' in inst:
 1167 |                 out.append(inst.get('', ''))
 1168 |         elif key in inst:
 1169 |             out.append(inst.get(key, ''))
 1170 |     return out
 1171 | 
 1172 | 
 1173 | def _emit_value_changes(changes, tag, sub, b_vals, a_vals):
 1174 |     """Pair up old/new repetition values and emit add/remove/modify changes."""
 1175 |     n = max(len(b_vals), len(a_vals))
 1176 |     for i in range(n):
 1177 |         b = b_vals[i] if i < len(b_vals) else None
 1178 |         a = a_vals[i] if i < len(a_vals) else None
 1179 |         if b == a:
 1180 |             continue
 1181 |         if b is None:
 1182 |             op = 'add'
 1183 |         elif a is None:
 1184 |             op = 'remove'
 1185 |         else:
 1186 |             op = 'modify'
 1187 |         changes.append({'tag': tag, 'subfield': sub, 'op': op,
 1188 |                         'before': b, 'after': a})
 1189 |
```

### Файл: `irbis-web/backend/access/holds.py`  · строк: 158

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """Reader holds + queue (#222) — reader-portal BACKEND.
    3 | 
    4 | A reader places a *hold* on a catalogue item (db, mfn). The hold and the FIFO
    5 | queue live in OUR access store (``reader_hold`` table), reader-scoped by the RDR
    6 | ticket — they are **NOT** written to the live ИРБИС server. This is the logical
    7 | reservation layer the portal owns; the eventual боевая интеграция with RQST/910
    8 | on the server is a separate concern (cf. ``core.order``'s TODO).
    9 | 
   10 | Queue model
   11 | -----------
   12 | Position is the reader's 1-based rank in the FIFO hold queue for that exact
   13 | (db, mfn), ordered by ``queued_at`` then id (oldest first). The first reader in
   14 | line is handed the copy when one is free:
   15 | 
   16 |   * If a free copy exists — the catalogue says ``is_available`` (via the wired
   17 |     catalog handle / exemplar API) AND no one is already ahead — the new hold is
   18 |     created ``ready`` at position 1.
   19 |   * Otherwise the hold is ``queued`` at its FIFO position (2, 3, …).
   20 | 
   21 | Without a catalog handle there is no availability signal, so a first-in-line hold
   22 | is still surfaced as ``ready`` (position 1) — the portal can show it as ready to
   23 | pick up — while later holders queue behind it. Re-holding the same item is
   24 | idempotent: the reader's existing live hold (and its current position) is
   25 | returned unchanged.
   26 | 
   27 | Title is resolved once at place time via the injected ``brief_read`` callable
   28 | (the existing OPAC brief read) so the holds list and the notification card can
   29 | show a human title without a second server round-trip.
   30 | 
   31 | This module is pure domain logic over the store + two small injected seams
   32 | (``catalog`` for availability, ``brief_read`` for the title); it performs no I/O
   33 | of its own and never raises out of the optional seams.
   34 | """
   35 | 
   36 | READY = 'ready'
   37 | QUEUED = 'queued'
   38 | CANCELLED = 'cancelled'
   39 | 
   40 | # Default pickup-shelf TTL (days) for a hold that becomes ready. Kept local so the
   41 | # module stays standalone; the circulation policy owns the authoritative value.
   42 | HOLD_SHELF_DAYS = 3
   43 | _SECONDS_PER_DAY = 86400
   44 | 
   45 | 
   46 | class HoldService:
   47 |     """Place / cancel / list reader holds over an access store.
   48 | 
   49 |     Parameters
   50 |     ----------
   51 |     store
   52 |         The access store (``AccessStore`` sqlite or ``PgAccessStore``) — provides
   53 |         the reader_hold CRUD. Reader-scoped by ticket.
   54 |     catalog : object | None
   55 |         Optional catalogue handle exposing ``is_available(db, item)`` /
```

<!-- ─── страница 177 ─── -->

```py
   56 |         ``find_exemplar(db, item)`` (``access.catalog.CatalogStore``). When wired,
   57 |         availability decides ready-vs-queued for the first holder. ``None`` ⇒ no
   58 |         availability opinion (first holder surfaced ready).
   59 |     brief_read : callable | None
   60 |         ``brief_read(db, mfn) -> {'title': str, ...}`` to resolve the display
   61 |         title at place time. ``None`` ⇒ title left blank (a "MFN n" stub).
   62 |     now : callable
   63 |         Clock injection (``time.time`` by default) so tests are deterministic.
   64 |     """
   65 | 
   66 |     def __init__(self, store, catalog=None, brief_read=None, now=None,
   67 |                  shelf_days=HOLD_SHELF_DAYS):
   68 |         self.store = store
   69 |         self.catalog = catalog
   70 |         self.brief_read = brief_read
   71 |         self.shelf_days = shelf_days
   72 |         if now is None:
   73 |             import time
   74 |             now = time.time
   75 |         self._now = now
   76 | 
   77 |     # ---- availability seam (catalog 910^A) -------------------------------- #
   78 |     def _catalog_free(self, db, mfn):
   79 |         """Catalogue availability for (db,mfn): True/False, or None (no opinion).
   80 | 
   81 |         ``mfn`` is the catalogue address; the catalog keys exemplars by inventory
   82 |         (``910^b``) so we ask by mfn-as-item. Any catalog error / absent copy ⇒
   83 |         None (caller falls back to its own queue state). Never raises."""
   84 |         if self.catalog is None:
   85 |             return None
   86 |         try:
   87 |             item = str(mfn)
   88 |             if hasattr(self.catalog, 'find_exemplar'):
   89 |                 if self.catalog.find_exemplar(db, item) is None:
   90 |                     return None
   91 |             return bool(self.catalog.is_available(db, item))
   92 |         except Exception:
   93 |             return None
   94 | 
   95 |     def _title(self, db, mfn):
   96 |         if self.brief_read is None:
   97 |             return ''
   98 |         try:
   99 |             it = self.brief_read(db, mfn) or {}
  100 |             t = (it.get('title') or '').strip()
  101 |             return '' if t == ('MFN %d' % mfn) else t
  102 |         except Exception:
  103 |             return ''
  104 | 
  105 |     def _position(self, ticket, db, mfn):
  106 |         """1-based FIFO rank of the reader's live hold in (db,mfn)'s queue (0=absent)."""
  107 |         for idx, h in enumerate(self.store.hold_queue(db, mfn)):
  108 |             if h['ticket'] == ticket:
  109 |                 return idx + 1
  110 |         return 0
```

```py
  111 | 
  112 |     # ---- operations ------------------------------------------------------- #
  113 |     def place(self, ticket, db, mfn):
  114 |         """Place (or return the existing) hold for ``ticket`` on (db, mfn).
  115 | 
  116 |         Idempotent: an existing live hold is returned with its current position.
  117 |         Returns ``{'holdId', 'status', 'position'}``.
  118 |         """
  119 |         existing = self.store.hold_find_live(ticket, db, mfn)
  120 |         if existing:
  121 |             return {'holdId': existing['id'], 'status': existing['status'],
  122 |                     'position': self._position(ticket, db, mfn)}
  123 | 
  124 |         now = self._now()
  125 |         # Is anyone already queued/ready ahead of us? (FIFO — they hold priority.)
  126 |         queue_before = self.store.hold_queue(db, mfn)
  127 |         free = self._catalog_free(db, mfn)
  128 |         # Ready iff no one is ahead AND the catalogue does not say "issued".
  129 |         # free is None ⇒ no catalog opinion ⇒ first-in-line is surfaced ready.
  130 |         ready = (len(queue_before) == 0) and (free is not False)
  131 | 
  132 |         status = READY if ready else QUEUED
  133 |         until = (now + self.shelf_days * _SECONDS_PER_DAY) if ready else None
  134 |         title = self._title(db, mfn)
  135 |         hold = self.store.hold_add(ticket, db, mfn, title, status, now, until)
  136 |         return {'holdId': hold['id'], 'status': hold['status'],
  137 |                 'position': self._position(ticket, db, mfn)}
  138 | 
  139 |     def list_for(self, ticket):
  140 |         """All of a reader's live holds as portal cards (with live positions)."""
  141 |         out = []
  142 |         for h in self.store.holds_for(ticket):
  143 |             out.append({
  144 |                 'holdId': h['id'], 'db': h['db'], 'mfn': h['mfn'],
  145 |                 'title': h.get('title') or ('MFN %d' % h['mfn']),
  146 |                 'status': h['status'],
  147 |                 'position': self._position(ticket, h['db'], h['mfn']),
  148 |                 'until': h.get('until'),
  149 |             })
  150 |         return out
  151 | 
  152 |     def cancel(self, ticket, hold_id):
  153 |         """Cancel the reader's own hold. Returns ``{'holdId','status'}`` or None
  154 |         when the hold isn't the reader's / isn't live."""
  155 |         row = self.store.hold_cancel(ticket, hold_id)
  156 |         if row is None:
  157 |             return None
  158 |         return {'holdId': hold_id, 'status': CANCELLED}
```

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """Reader notification engine — event-driven, multi-channel, idempotent.
    3 | 
    4 | Gap A6, epic #188. Implements SPEC_engine_notifications.md, FIRST shippable slice.
    5 | 
    6 | What this module is
    7 | -------------------
```

<!-- ─── страница 179 ─── -->

```py
    8 | A *self-contained* notification engine that turns circulation/account events
    9 | (hold ready, due soon, overdue, fine charged, …) into rendered, deduplicated,
   10 | channel-routed messages for a reader. It is deliberately standalone: pure stdlib
   11 | + ``sqlite3`` (dev parity with the rest of the backend, ADR-004), no network I/O.
   12 | Channels are abstract — a real e-mail/SMS gateway is plugged in behind the
   13 | ``Channel`` interface; tests drive it with an in-memory recorder.
   14 | 
   15 | The four moving parts
   16 | ---------------------
   17 |   1. ``EventCatalog`` — maps an *event type* (e.g. ``hold_ready``) to its
   18 |      ``template`` (subject/body with ``{placeholder}`` slots) and the
   19 |      ``default_channels`` fallback order (first-success wins).
   20 |   2. ``NotificationQueue`` — a durable sqlite-backed work queue. ``enqueue`` is
   21 |      **idempotent** by ``dedup_key`` (UNIQUE) so the same logical event handed in
   22 |      twice never double-sends. ``process_once`` claims pending rows, renders the
   23 |      template, and attempts the channel chain — marking ``sent`` / ``failed`` and
   24 |      bumping the ``attempts`` retry/backoff counter.
   25 |   3. Reader **preferences** — a per-reader ``Preferences`` (which channels to use
   26 |      for an event, and a global / per-event **opt-out**). Honored at enqueue
   27 |      time: opted-out → suppressed (never queued); otherwise the preferred channel
   28 |      order overrides the catalog default.
   29 |   4. ``Channel`` interface + implementations — ``MemoryChannel`` (records sends,
   30 |      for tests), ``EmailChannel`` / ``SmsChannel`` stubs (NO real send; raise or
   31 |      record per their ``available`` flag so fallback can be exercised).
   32 | 
   33 | Template rendering
   34 | ------------------
   35 | ``render`` uses ``access.pft`` when that engine is importable (it formats ИРБИС
   36 | PFT field-format expressions); otherwise it falls back to a safe ``str``-based
   37 | ``{placeholder}`` substitution. The fallback is what runs today since A1/PFT is
   38 | not yet built — a missing placeholder is left verbatim rather than raising, so a
   39 | template typo degrades gracefully instead of dropping a notification.
   40 | 
   41 | Queue / dedup / retry model
   42 | ---------------------------
   43 | ``notification(id, tenant, event, recipient, channel, status, dedup_key,
   44 | attempts, payload_json, ...)`` with ``UNIQUE(dedup_key)``. ``status`` walks
   45 | ``pending -> sent`` on success or ``pending -> failed`` once attempts exhaust the
   46 | chain; a transient channel error increments ``attempts`` and leaves the row
   47 | claimable on the next ``process_once`` (at-least-once, dedup makes the *send*
   48 | effectively once per channel). Backoff is advisory: ``next_attempt_at`` is set to
   49 | ``now + backoff(attempts)`` and respected by the claim query.
   50 | """
   51 | import json
   52 | import sqlite3
   53 | import threading
   54 | import time
   55 | 
   56 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   57 | # Template rendering — prefer the real PFT engine (A1) when present, else a
   58 | # safe stdlib {placeholder} substitution. Resolved once at import.
   59 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   60 | try:  # pragma: no cover - depends on sibling A1 module existing
   61 |     from access import pft as _pft  # type: ignore
   62 |     _HAVE_PFT = hasattr(_pft, 'format')
```

<!-- ─── страница 180 ─── -->

```py
   63 | except Exception:  # ImportError today; defensive against a half-built module
   64 |     _pft = None
   65 |     _HAVE_PFT = False
   66 | 
   67 | 
   68 | def render(template, payload):
   69 |     """Render a template string against ``payload`` (a flat dict).
   70 | 
   71 |     Uses ``access.pft.format`` when the PFT engine is importable, else a literal
   72 |     ``{key}`` substitution. Unknown placeholders are left verbatim (a template
   73 |     typo must not crash the engine or silently drop a notification).
   74 |     """
   75 |     if template is None:
   76 |         return ''
   77 |     if _HAVE_PFT:
   78 |         try:
   79 |             return _pft.format(template, payload)
   80 |         except Exception:
   81 |             pass  # fall through to the stdlib renderer on any PFT error
   82 |     out = template
   83 |     for key, val in (payload or {}).items():
   84 |         out = out.replace('{%s}' % key, '' if val is None else str(val))
   85 |     return out
   86 | 
   87 | 
   88 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   89 | # Channels — abstract interface + a test recorder + non-sending prod stubs.
   90 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   91 | class SendError(Exception):
   92 |     """Transient/permanent failure from a channel; engine treats it as a miss."""
   93 | 
   94 | 
   95 | class Channel:
   96 |     """A delivery surface (email / sms / push / …).
   97 | 
   98 |     Subclasses set ``name`` and implement ``send(recipient, subject, body,
   99 |     payload)``. ``send`` returns a provider message-id string on success and
  100 |     raises :class:`SendError` on failure (so the engine can fall through to the
  101 |     next channel in the chain).
  102 |     """
  103 |     name = 'abstract'
  104 | 
  105 |     def send(self, recipient, subject, body, payload):  # pragma: no cover
  106 |         raise NotImplementedError
  107 | 
  108 | 
  109 | class MemoryChannel(Channel):
  110 |     """In-memory recorder for tests. Captures every send; can be forced to fail.
  111 | 
  112 |     ``fail`` -> always raises (exercise the fallback chain).
  113 |     ``fail_times`` -> raises for the first N attempts then succeeds (exercise the
  114 |     retry/attempts counter).
  115 |     """
  116 | 
  117 |     def __init__(self, name='memory', fail=False, fail_times=0):
```

```py
  118 |         self.name = name
  119 |         self.fail = fail
  120 |         self.fail_times = fail_times
  121 |         self.sent = []          # list of dicts actually delivered
  122 |         self.attempts = 0       # total send() invocations (incl. failures)
  123 | 
  124 |     def send(self, recipient, subject, body, payload):
  125 |         self.attempts += 1
  126 |         if self.fail:
  127 |             raise SendError('%s: forced failure' % self.name)
  128 |         if self.fail_times > 0:
  129 |             self.fail_times -= 1
  130 |             raise SendError('%s: transient failure' % self.name)
  131 |         rec = {'recipient': recipient, 'subject': subject,
  132 |                'body': body, 'payload': payload}
  133 |         self.sent.append(rec)
  134 |         return '%s-%d' % (self.name, len(self.sent))
  135 |

  137 | class EmailChannel(Channel):
  138 |     """SMTP stub — NO real network send in this slice.
  139 | 
  140 |     ``available=False`` makes it raise (simulating an unconfigured gateway), which
  141 |     is how the fallback chain is exercised in prod-shaped wiring. When available it
  142 |     records to ``outbox`` exactly like a real adapter would hand off to a relay.
  143 |     """
  144 |     name = 'email'
  145 | 
  146 |     def __init__(self, available=True):
  147 |         self.available = available
  148 |         self.outbox = []
  149 | 
  150 |     def send(self, recipient, subject, body, payload):
  151 |         if not self.available:
  152 |             raise SendError('email: gateway not configured')
  153 |         self.outbox.append({'to': recipient, 'subject': subject, 'body': body})
  154 |         return 'email-stub-%d' % len(self.outbox)
  155 | 
  156 | 
  157 | class SmsChannel(Channel):

  160 |

  163 |         self.outbox = []
  164 | 
  165 |     def send(self, recipient, subject, body, payload):
  166 |         if not self.available:
  167 |             raise SendError('sms: gateway not configured')
  168 |         self.outbox.append({'to': recipient, 'text': body})
  169 |         return 'sms-stub-%d' % len(self.outbox)
  170 |
```

```py
  173 | # Event catalog — event type -> {template, default_channels}.

  175 | class EventCatalog:
  176 |     """Maps reader-account events to their template + default channel chain.
  177 | 
  178 |     Seeded with the SPEC event set: the reader-facing hold/loan/fine notices
  179 |     (hold_ready / due_soon / overdue / fine_charged / hold_cancelled /
  180 |     account_blocked / renewal_confirmed / fine_paid / lost_confirmed) plus the
  181 |     staff-facing ``staff_alert`` (write-off candidate). The set must cover every
  182 |     event ``CirculationEngine._emit`` raises, or that intent falls through
  183 |     silently (no template → never rendered/sent). ``register`` lets a tenant
  184 |     add/override an entry. Each entry is ``{'template': {'subject','body'},
  185 |     'default_channels': [<name>, ...]}`` — channel *names*, resolved to live
  186 |     :class:`Channel` objects at send time.
```

```py
  298 | 
  299 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  300 | # Reader preferences — channel order per event + opt-out.
  301 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  302 | class Preferences:
  303 |     """Per-reader channel preferences and opt-outs.
  304 | 
  305 |     * ``channels`` — optional ``{event: [channel_name, ...]}`` overriding the
  306 |       catalog default for that event (and the global default below).
  307 |     * ``default_channels`` — optional reader-wide channel order applied to events
  308 |       with no per-event entry.
  309 |     * ``opted_out`` — a set of events the reader refuses; ``'*'`` opts out of
  310 |       everything. ``is_opted_out(event)`` honours both.
  311 | 
  312 |     ``resolve(event, catalog)`` returns the effective channel-name chain:
  313 |     per-event pref → reader default → catalog default.
  314 |     """
  315 | 
  316 |     def __init__(self, channels=None, default_channels=None, opted_out=None):
  317 |         self.channels = dict(channels or {})
  318 |         self.default_channels = list(default_channels) if default_channels else None
  319 |         self.opted_out = set(opted_out or ())
  320 | 
  321 |     def is_opted_out(self, event):
  322 |         return '*' in self.opted_out or event in self.opted_out
  323 | 
  324 |     def resolve(self, event, catalog):
  325 |         if event in self.channels:
  326 |             return list(self.channels[event])
  327 |         if self.default_channels:
  328 |             return list(self.default_channels)
  329 |         return catalog.default_channels(event)
  330 | 
  331 | 
  332 | # Convenience: a reader who takes everything on the catalog defaults.
  333 | DEFAULT_PREFERENCES = Preferences()
  334 | 
  335 | 
  336 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  337 | # Queue — durable sqlite store with idempotent enqueue + claim/process.
```

<!-- ─── страница 185 ─── -->

```py
  338 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  339 | SCHEMA_SQLITE = """
  340 | CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS notification (
  341 |   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
  342 |   tenant TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'public',
  343 |   event TEXT NOT NULL,
  344 |   recipient TEXT NOT NULL,
  345 |   channel TEXT,                 -- channel that finally delivered (NULL until sent)
  346 |   status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending'
  347 |          CHECK (status IN ('pending','sent','failed','suppressed')),
  348 |   dedup_key TEXT NOT NULL,
  349 |   attempts INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  350 |   payload_json TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
  351 |   channels_json TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]',  -- resolved channel-name chain
  352 |   last_error TEXT,
  353 |   next_attempt_at REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  354 |   read_at REAL,                 -- reader inbox: NULL=unread, epoch=when marked read (#222)
  355 |   created_at REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT (strftime('%s','now')),
  356 |   updated_at REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT (strftime('%s','now')),
  357 |   UNIQUE (dedup_key)
  358 | );
  359 | CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS notification_claim_idx
  360 |   ON notification(status, next_attempt_at);
  361 | CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS notification_inbox_idx
  362 |   ON notification(recipient, read_at);
  363 | """
  364 | 
  365 | # attempts -> seconds of advisory backoff before the row is reclaimable.
  366 | _BACKOFF = [0, 60, 300, 900, 3600]
  367 | MAX_ATTEMPTS = 5
  368 | 
  369 | 
  370 | def backoff_seconds(attempts):
  371 |     """Advisory backoff for the Nth attempt (capped at the last bucket)."""
  372 |     if attempts < 0:
  373 |         attempts = 0
  374 |     return _BACKOFF[min(attempts, len(_BACKOFF) - 1)]
  375 | 
  376 | 
  377 | def make_dedup_key(tenant, event, recipient, payload):
  378 |     """Deterministic idempotency key.
  379 | 
  380 |     A caller may pass an explicit ``dedup_key`` in the payload (e.g. a circulation
  381 |     transaction id) — that wins. Otherwise we derive a stable key from the logical
  382 |     identity of the event so the same hold/loan can't notify twice. ``ref`` (a
  383 |     domain id such as the hold/loan mfn) is the canonical disambiguator.
  384 |     """
  385 |     if payload and payload.get('dedup_key'):
  386 |         return str(payload['dedup_key'])
  387 |     ref = '' if not payload else str(payload.get('ref', ''))
  388 |     return '|'.join([tenant, event, str(recipient), ref])
  389 | 
  390 | 
  391 | class NotificationQueue:
  392 |     """sqlite-backed notification work queue (idempotent enqueue, claim/send).
```

<!-- ─── страница 186 ─── -->

```py
  393 | 
  394 |     ``enqueue`` applies preferences/opt-out and inserts a single ``pending`` row
  395 |     keyed by ``dedup_key`` (a duplicate enqueue is a no-op, returning the existing
  396 |     row's id). ``process_once`` claims due ``pending`` rows and runs each through
  397 |     its resolved channel chain.
  398 |     """
  399 | 
  400 |     def __init__(self, db_path=':memory:', catalog=None, tenant='public'):
  401 |         self.db_path = db_path
  402 |         self.catalog = catalog or EventCatalog()
  403 |         self.tenant = tenant
  404 |         self._local = threading.local()
  405 |         self.ensure_schema()
  406 | 
  407 |     def _conn(self):
  408 |         c = getattr(self._local, 'conn', None)
  409 |         if c is None:
  410 |             c = sqlite3.connect(self.db_path)
  411 |             c.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
  412 |             self._local.conn = c
  413 |         return c
  414 | 
  415 |     def ensure_schema(self):
  416 |         c = self._conn()
  417 |         c.executescript(SCHEMA_SQLITE)
  418 |         # Lazy migration: a persistent queue created before the inbox feature (#222)
  419 |         # lacks read_at — add it so the inbox can mark notices read. Idempotent.
  420 |         cols = {r[1] for r in c.execute('PRAGMA table_info(notification)').fetchall()}
  421 |         if 'read_at' not in cols:
  422 |             c.execute('ALTER TABLE notification ADD COLUMN read_at REAL')
  423 |         c.execute('CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS notification_inbox_idx '
  424 |                   'ON notification(recipient, read_at)')
  425 |         c.commit()
  426 | 
  427 |     # ---- enqueue (idempotent) -------------------------------------------- #
  428 |     def enqueue(self, event, recipient, payload=None, prefs=None, tenant=None):
  429 |         """Queue one notification. Idempotent by ``dedup_key``; opt-out aware.
  430 | 
  431 |         Returns ``{'id', 'status', 'dedup_key', 'deduped'}``:
  432 |           * opted-out  -> a ``suppressed`` row (recorded, never sent),
  433 |           * duplicate  -> the existing row, ``deduped=True``, no new insert,
  434 |           * otherwise  -> a fresh ``pending`` row.
  435 |         """
  436 |         if not self.catalog.has(event):
  437 |             raise KeyError('unknown event type: %r' % event)
  438 |         payload = payload or {}
```

```py
  467 |     # ---- claim + send ----------------------------------------------------- #
  468 |     def _claim_due(self, now, limit):
  469 |         c = self._conn()
  470 |         return c.execute(

  475 |     def process_once(self, channels, now=None, limit=100):
  476 |         """Process up to ``limit`` due pending rows once. Returns a summary dict.
  477 | 
  478 |         ``channels`` is ``{name: Channel}``. For each claimed row we render the
  479 |         template and walk its resolved channel chain, stopping at the first
  480 |         success (records the delivering channel + marks ``sent``). If the whole
  481 |         chain misses we bump ``attempts``: under :data:`MAX_ATTEMPTS` the row goes
  482 |         back to ``pending`` with an advisory backoff; at the cap it is marked
  483 |         ``failed``.
  484 |         """
  485 |         now = time.time() if now is None else now
  486 |         c = self._conn()
  487 |         summary = {'processed': 0, 'sent': 0, 'failed': 0, 'retried': 0}
  488 | 
  489 |         for row in self._claim_due(now, limit):
  490 |             summary['processed'] += 1
  491 |             payload = json.loads(row['payload_json'])
  492 |             chain = json.loads(row['channels_json'])
  493 |             tmpl = self.catalog.template(row['event'])
  494 |             subject = render(tmpl.get('subject'), payload)
  495 |             body = render(tmpl.get('body'), payload)
  496 | 
  497 |             delivered_via = None
  498 |             last_error = None
  499 |             for cname in chain:
  500 |                 ch = channels.get(cname)
  501 |                 if ch is None:
  502 |                     last_error = 'no channel %r wired' % cname
```

```py
  508 |                 except SendError as e:
  509 |                     last_error = str(e)
  510 |                 except Exception as e:  # defensive: any channel bug is a miss
```

```py
  558 |     # rendered to a {subject, body} card via the event catalog. Suppressed notices
  559 |     # (opted-out, never meant for delivery) are excluded from the inbox view.
  560 |     def _inbox_card(self, row, tenant):
  561 |         """Render one stored notification row into an inbox card (id/ts/event/...)."""
  562 |         payload = json.loads(row['payload_json'])
  563 |         subject, body = '', ''
  564 |         if self.catalog.has(row['event']):
  565 |             tmpl = self.catalog.template(row['event'])
  566 |             subject = render(tmpl.get('subject'), payload)
  567 |             body = render(tmpl.get('body'), payload)
  568 |         return {'id': row['id'], 'ts': row['created_at'], 'event': row['event'],
  569 |                 'subject': subject, 'body': body, 'read': row['read_at'] is not None}
  570 | 
  571 |     def inbox(self, recipient, unread_only=False, tenant=None):
  572 |         """Dispatched notices addressed to ``recipient`` (newest first), as cards."""
  573 |         tenant = tenant or self.tenant
  574 |         sql = ("SELECT * FROM notification WHERE recipient=? AND tenant=? "
  575 |                "AND status!='suppressed'")
  576 |         params = [recipient, tenant]
  577 |         if unread_only:
  578 |             sql += ' AND read_at IS NULL'
  579 |         sql += ' ORDER BY id DESC'
  580 |         rows = self._conn().execute(sql, params).fetchall()
  581 |         return [self._inbox_card(r, tenant) for r in rows]
  582 | 
  583 |     def unread_count(self, recipient, tenant=None):
  584 |         tenant = tenant or self.tenant
  585 |         return self._conn().execute(
  586 |             "SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM notification WHERE recipient=? AND tenant=? "
  587 |             "AND status!='suppressed' AND read_at IS NULL",
  588 |             (recipient, tenant)).fetchone()['n']
  589 | 
  590 |     def mark_read(self, recipient, notif_id=None, mark_all=False, tenant=None):
  591 |         """Mark one notice (``notif_id``, must belong to ``recipient``) or all of a
  592 |         reader's notices read. Returns the reader's remaining unread count."""
  593 |         tenant = tenant or self.tenant
  594 |         c = self._conn()
  595 |         now = time.time()
  596 |         if mark_all:
  597 |             c.execute(
  598 |                 'UPDATE notification SET read_at=? WHERE recipient=? AND tenant=? '
  599 |                 'AND read_at IS NULL', (now, recipient, tenant))
  600 |         elif notif_id is not None:
  601 |             c.execute(
  602 |                 'UPDATE notification SET read_at=? WHERE id=? AND recipient=? '
  603 |                 'AND tenant=? AND read_at IS NULL',
  604 |                 (now, notif_id, recipient, tenant))
  605 |         c.commit()
  606 |         return self.unread_count(recipient, tenant)
```

```py
   44 | def _admin_conn(dsn):
```

```py
  132 | def is_module_enabled(tenant, module, dsn=None):
  133 |     """True iff ``module`` is licensed for ``tenant``.
  134 | 
  135 |     Fail-open for dev/single-tenant: the ``public`` (non-tenant) path, an
  136 |     unreachable control plane, or a tenant with no tenant_module rows all return
  137 |     True so sqlite dev and the guest flow keep working. Once a tenant exists on PG
  138 |     with explicit rows, the stored ``enabled`` flag is authoritative — a disabled
  139 |     module returns False even when the caller holds a valid grant.
  140 |     """
  141 |     if _is_public(tenant):
  142 |         return True
  143 |     dsn = dsn or default_pg_dsn()
  144 |     try:
  145 |         conn = _admin_conn(dsn)
  146 |     except Exception:
  147 |         return True                      # control plane down -> don't lock dev out
  148 |     try:
  149 |         tid = _tenant_id(conn, tenant)
  150 |         if tid is None:
  151 |             return True                  # tenant not provisioned in control -> dev/public
  152 |         r = conn.execute(
  153 |             'SELECT enabled FROM control.tenant_module WHERE tenant_id=%s AND module=%s',
  154 |             (tid, module)).fetchone()
  155 |         if r is None:
  156 |             return True                  # no explicit row -> not gated (default-enabled)
  157 |         return bool(r['enabled'])
  158 |     except Exception:
  159 |         return True
  160 |     finally:
  161 |         conn.close()
  162 | 
  163 | 
  164 | def _is_public(tenant):
  165 |     """The non-tenant/single-tenant dev path: no control plane, never gated."""
  166 |     return not tenant or tenant == 'public'
```

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """Billing skeleton (D5 / issue #209, epic #223 — MVP Phase 2).
    3 | 
    4 | A *skeleton*: tariff plans → the set of functional modules they license + a few
    5 | usage limits. NO payment gateway, NO invoicing/VAT, NO metering pipeline — those
```

<!-- ─── страница 193 ─── -->

```py
    6 | are later D5 work. What this provides is the part the MVP needs:
    7 | 
    8 |   * a small, declarative ``PLANS`` table (``free`` / ``standard`` / ``pro``);
    9 |   * ``plan_modules(plan)`` / ``plan_limits(plan)`` — what a plan unlocks;
   10 |   * ``set_tenant_plan(store, tenant, plan)`` — record the tenant's plan AND apply
   11 |     its module entitlements (turn the plan's modules ON, the rest OFF) via the
   12 |     existing per-tenant entitlement gate (``access.entitlements``, issue #101);
   13 |   * ``get_tenant_plan`` / ``tenant_usage`` / ``check_limit`` — read the plan, count
   14 |     current usage, and a soft-enforcement helper for a record-create style check.
   15 | 
   16 | **Where the plan is stored.** The plan name is a control-plane fact, so it lives
   17 | next to the tenant in ``control.tenant.plan`` on PostgreSQL (added idempotently by
   18 | ``_ensure_plan_column`` — a plain ``ALTER TABLE … ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS`` so an
   19 | already-provisioned DB upgrades with no migration step). Entitlements themselves
   20 | stay in ``control.tenant_module`` exactly as before — billing only *drives* them.
   21 | 
   22 | **Dev / single-tenant degradation.** The sqlite dev box and the non-tenant
   23 | ``public`` path have no control plane. There ``set_tenant_plan`` is a no-op that
   24 | still validates the plan name and reports it; ``get_tenant_plan`` returns the
   25 | ``DEFAULT_PLAN``; entitlements stay fail-open (everything enabled) exactly as
   26 | ``access.entitlements`` already behaves with no rows. So billing never locks dev
   27 | out and is *off by default until a plan is explicitly set on a provisioned PG
   28 | tenant* — matching the "best-effort, off by default if no plan set" contract.
   29 | """
   30 | import os
   31 | 
   32 | from . import entitlements
   33 | 
   34 | # All modules a tenant could be licensed for — the catalogue billing draws from.
   35 | # Kept in lock-step with ``entitlements.DEFAULT_MODULES`` (the product's functional
   36 | # module set); a plan selects a subset of these to enable.
   37 | ALL_MODULES = tuple(entitlements.DEFAULT_MODULES)
   38 | 
   39 | # Sentinel "no ceiling" for a limit (the ``pro`` plan and any unset limit).
   40 | UNLIMITED = None
   41 | 
   42 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   43 | # Plan catalogue. Each plan declares the modules it licenses and a few usage
   44 | # limits. ``limits`` keys are resources ``check_limit`` understands:
   45 | #   max_records   — bibliographic records the tenant may hold (catalog size)
   46 | #   max_readers   — registered readers
   47 | #   max_storage_mb— attached-file / cover storage budget, MB
   48 | # A limit of ``UNLIMITED`` (None) means no ceiling. A resource absent from a
   49 | # plan's limits is treated as unlimited too.
   50 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   51 | PLANS = {
   52 |     'free': {
   53 |         'title': 'Free',
   54 |         # A try-it tier: read/search + reader portal only; no staff write desks.
   55 |         'modules': ('opac', 'reader'),
   56 |         'limits': {'max_records': 1000, 'max_readers': 200, 'max_storage_mb': 100},
   57 |     },
   58 |     'standard': {
   59 |         'title': 'Standard',
   60 |         # A working small/medium library: catalog + circulation + reader portal +
```

<!-- ─── страница 194 ─── -->

```py
   61 |         # admin + analytics. No acquisition / book-provision (those are pro).
   62 |         'modules': ('opac', 'cataloging', 'circulation', 'reader', 'admin', 'analytics'),
   63 |         'limits': {'max_records': 100000, 'max_readers': 20000, 'max_storage_mb': 10240},
   64 |     },
   65 |     'pro': {
   66 |         'title': 'Pro',
   67 |         # Everything: all functional modules, no ceilings.
   68 |         'modules': ALL_MODULES,
   69 |         'limits': {'max_records': UNLIMITED, 'max_readers': UNLIMITED,
   70 |                    'max_storage_mb': UNLIMITED},
   71 |     },
   72 | }
   73 | 
   74 | # The plan a freshly provisioned tenant gets, and the dev/``public`` answer.
   75 | DEFAULT_PLAN = os.environ.get('DEFAULT_PLAN', 'standard')
   76 | 
   77 | # Resources ``check_limit`` knows how to enforce.
   78 | LIMIT_RESOURCES = ('max_records', 'max_readers', 'max_storage_mb')
   79 | 
   80 | 
   81 | class BillingError(Exception):
   82 |     """Raised on an unknown plan or an over-limit when enforcing hard."""
   83 | 
   84 | 
   85 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   86 | # Pure plan lookups (no DB) — usable from anywhere, including sqlite dev.
   87 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   88 | def is_plan(plan):
   89 |     return plan in PLANS
   90 | 
   91 | 
   92 | def _require_plan(plan):
   93 |     if plan not in PLANS:
   94 |         raise BillingError('unknown plan %r (known: %s)'
   95 |                            % (plan, ', '.join(sorted(PLANS))))
   96 |     return plan
   97 | 
   98 | 
   99 | def plan_modules(plan):
  100 |     """The functional modules a plan licenses (sorted), or raise BillingError."""
  101 |     return sorted(PLANS[_require_plan(plan)]['modules'])
  102 | 
  103 | 
  104 | def plan_limits(plan):
  105 |     """The usage limits for a plan as ``{resource: int|None}`` for every known
  106 |     resource (missing → UNLIMITED), or raise BillingError."""
  107 |     _require_plan(plan)
  108 |     declared = PLANS[plan]['limits']
  109 |     return {r: declared.get(r, UNLIMITED) for r in LIMIT_RESOURCES}
  110 | 
  111 | 
  112 | def plan_limit(plan, resource):
  113 |     """One limit value (int) or ``UNLIMITED`` (None). Raises on an unknown plan."""
  114 |     return plan_limits(plan).get(resource, UNLIMITED)
  115 |
```

```py
  116 | 
  117 | def plans_catalog():
  118 |     """The full catalogue for an admin UI: [{plan,title,modules,limits}]."""
  119 |     return [{'plan': name, 'title': PLANS[name]['title'],
  120 |              'modules': sorted(PLANS[name]['modules']), 'limits': plan_limits(name)}
  121 |             for name in sorted(PLANS)]

  123 | 
  124 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  125 | # Plan storage on the control plane (PostgreSQL). The sqlite dev / public path
  126 | # has no control plane → these degrade to DEFAULT_PLAN / no-op.
  127 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  128 | def _on_control_plane(store):
  129 |     """True iff billing should target a real PostgreSQL control plane.
  130 | 
  131 |     Real PG iff a ``PgAccessStore`` is in play OR the env selects the postgres
  132 |     backend. The sqlite dev ``AccessStore`` (single-tenant) → dev no-op path, even
  133 |     for a non-'public' slug (sqlite has no per-tenant schemas / control plane).
  134 |     """
  135 |     try:
  136 |         from .pgstore import PgAccessStore
  137 |         if isinstance(store, PgAccessStore):
  138 |             return True
  139 |     except Exception:
  140 |         pass
  141 |     return os.environ.get('ACCESS_BACKEND', 'sqlite').lower() in ('postgres', 'pg')
  142 | 
  143 | 
  144 | def _ensure_plan_column(dsn):
  145 |     """Add ``control.tenant.plan`` if missing (idempotent, no migration step).
  146 | 
  147 |     The control schema/table are created by ``ensure_control_schema``; we only add
  148 |     the billing column here so billing owns its own storage extension without
  149 |     editing the shared control DDL. Safe to call repeatedly. The DEFAULT is inlined
  150 |     (a validated PLANS key, not user input) because PG can't infer a parameter's
  151 |     type inside DDL.
  152 |     """
  153 |     from .pgstore import _admin_conn, ensure_control_schema
  154 |     ensure_control_schema(dsn)
  155 |     default = DEFAULT_PLAN if DEFAULT_PLAN in PLANS else 'standard'
  156 |     conn = _admin_conn(dsn)
  157 |     try:
  158 |         conn.execute(
  159 |             "ALTER TABLE control.tenant ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS plan TEXT "
  160 |             "NOT NULL DEFAULT '%s'" % default)
  161 |     finally:
  162 |         conn.close()
  163 | 
  164 | 
  165 | def get_tenant_plan(tenant, dsn=None):
  166 |     """The tenant's stored plan, or ``DEFAULT_PLAN`` for dev/public/unreachable.
  167 | 
  168 |     Fail-soft like ``entitlements``: the ``public`` path, a control plane that is
  169 |     down, or a tenant with no row all answer ``DEFAULT_PLAN`` so dev keeps working.
  170 |     """
```

```py
  193 | 
  194 | def set_tenant_plan(store, tenant, plan, dsn=None):
  195 |     """Assign ``plan`` to ``tenant`` and apply its module entitlements.
  196 | 
  197 |     Steps:
  198 |       1. validate the plan name (raise BillingError on unknown);
  199 |       2. record the plan on ``control.tenant.plan`` (PG only);
  200 |       3. drive ``control.tenant_module`` so exactly the plan's modules are enabled
  201 |          and every other known module is disabled — the plan becomes the single
  202 |          source of truth for what the tenant can reach.
  203 | 
  204 |     Dev / ``public`` (no control plane): a no-op that still validates + returns the
  205 |     plan (entitlements stay fail-open). ``store`` is accepted for symmetry with the
  206 |     other provisioning helpers and future per-tenant bookkeeping; the entitlement
  207 |     writes go through the control plane by slug.
  208 |     """
  209 |     _require_plan(plan)
  210 |     if entitlements._is_public(tenant) or not _on_control_plane(store):
  211 |         # dev / single-tenant: validate + report, but nothing to persist or gate.
  212 |         return {'tenant': tenant, 'plan': plan, 'modules': plan_modules(plan),
  213 |                 'applied': False}
  214 |     dsn = dsn or entitlements.default_pg_dsn()
  215 |     _ensure_plan_column(dsn)
  216 |     from .pgstore import _admin_conn
  217 |     conn = _admin_conn(dsn)
  218 |     try:
  219 |         conn.execute('UPDATE control.tenant SET plan=%s WHERE slug=%s', (plan, tenant))
  220 |     finally:
  221 |         conn.close()
  222 |     # Apply entitlements: enable the plan's modules, disable the rest. set_module
  223 |     # upserts, so this converges regardless of prior state (re-plan safe).
  224 |     wanted = set(plan_modules(plan))
  225 |     for module in ALL_MODULES:
```
