```py
  227 |     return {'tenant': tenant, 'plan': plan, 'modules': sorted(wanted), 'applied': True}
  228 | 
  229 | 
  230 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  231 | # Usage + soft enforcement.
  232 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  233 | def tenant_usage(store, tenant=None):
  234 |     """Best-effort current usage counters for a tenant ``store``.
  235 | 
  236 |     Counts what the Access store can see locally — registered staff accounts and
  237 |     the dictionary footprint — plus, when wired, catalog record / reader counts.
  238 |     A resource we can't count is simply omitted (callers treat absence as 0). Never
  239 |     raises: a store predating a table just yields a smaller dict.
  240 |     """
  241 |     usage = {}
  242 |     # Records: prefer a catalog handle on the store if present (max_records source).
  243 |     counter = getattr(store, 'record_count', None)
  244 |     if callable(counter):
  245 |         try:
  246 |             usage['max_records'] = int(counter())
  247 |         except Exception:
  248 |             pass
  249 |     # Readers: staff store doesn't hold readers, but a wired counter may.
  250 |     rcounter = getattr(store, 'reader_count', None)
  251 |     if callable(rcounter):
  252 |         try:
  253 |             usage['max_readers'] = int(rcounter())
  254 |         except Exception:
  255 |             pass
  256 |     return usage
  257 | 
  258 | 
  259 | def check_limit(store, tenant, resource, current, plan=None, hard=False, dsn=None):
  260 |     """Soft-enforce a usage limit. Returns True iff ``current`` is within budget.
  261 | 
  262 |     ``current`` is the count *if this operation proceeds* (so a record-create passes
  263 |     ``record_count + 1``). Resolves the tenant's plan (unless ``plan`` is given),
  264 |     looks up that resource's ceiling, and compares.
  265 | 
  266 |     Best-effort + off by default:
  267 |       * an UNLIMITED ceiling (pro plan, or a resource a plan doesn't cap) → True;
  268 |       * the dev/``public`` path uses ``DEFAULT_PLAN`` like everything else;
  269 |       * an unknown ``resource`` → True (we don't enforce what we don't model).
  270 | 
  271 |     ``hard=True`` raises ``BillingError`` instead of returning False when over —
  272 |     for a call site that wants to abort rather than branch.
  273 |     """
  274 |     if resource not in LIMIT_RESOURCES:
  275 |         return True
  276 |     plan = plan or get_tenant_plan(tenant, dsn)
  277 |     ceiling = plan_limit(plan, resource)
  278 |     if ceiling is UNLIMITED:
  279 |         return True
  280 |     ok = int(current) <= int(ceiling)
```

<!-- ─── страница 198 ─── -->

```py
  281 |     if not ok and hard:
  282 |         raise BillingError(
  283 |             'plan %r limit exceeded for %s: %s > %s'
  284 |             % (plan, resource, current, ceiling))
  285 |     return ok
```

### Файл: `irbis-web/backend/access/provision.py`  · строк: 254

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """Tenant provisioning (issue #187/#207, epic #223 — MVP Phase 2).
    3 | 
    4 | "A new library registers → gets a tenant → catalogues → lends." This is the
    5 | *one command* that stands a tenant up end-to-end, on top of the schema-per-tenant
    6 | machinery already in ``access.pgstore`` (issue #100):
    7 | 
    8 |   1. **schema** — ``CREATE SCHEMA t_<slug>`` + the Access DDL into it (done by
    9 |      ``PgAccessStore(tenant_schema=…)`` construction; recorded in
   10 |      ``control.tenant``);
   11 |   2. **vocabularies** — ``seed_vocabularies()`` populates the system dictionaries
   12 |      and creates the institution ones empty (gap A5, #188);
   13 |   3. **admin account** — the tenant's first staff account, carrying the seeded
   14 |      ``administrator`` role (so the library can immediately log in and run every
   15 |      desk);
   16 |   4. **plan + entitlements** — ``billing.set_tenant_plan`` records the plan and
   17 |      turns exactly that plan's modules ON (issue #101 / #209).
   18 | 
   19 | Everything is **idempotent**: re-provisioning the same slug re-converges (no dup
   20 | schema, no dup account, plan re-applied) and never destroys tenant data.
   21 | 
   22 | **sqlite / single-tenant dev degradation.** There is no control plane on the
   23 | sqlite dev box, and only the one ``DEFAULT_TENANT`` ('public') store. Provisioning
   24 | there still does the useful, real work it can — seed the roles + vocabularies into
   25 | that single store and create the admin account against it — and reports the plan
   26 | without a control plane to persist it on. So the same call works in dev (you get a
   27 | seeded store + a working admin login) and in prod (you additionally get a real
   28 | isolated PG schema + persisted plan/entitlements).
   29 | 
   30 | CLI::
   31 | 
   32 |     python -m access.provision <slug> [--name NAME] [--admin LOGIN]
   33 |                                        [--password PW] [--plan PLAN] [--kind KIND]
   34 |     python -m access.provision --list
   35 |     python -m access.provision --deprovision <slug>
   36 | """
   37 | import os
   38 | import sys
   39 | 
   40 | from . import billing
   41 | 
   42 | # Default library kind for ``control.tenant.kind`` (NOT NULL). 'публичная' = a
   43 | # public library; an operator can pass --kind for school/vuz/departmental.
   44 | DEFAULT_KIND = 'публичная'
   45 | # Dev-default admin password, env-overridable (no secret in code; access.db is
   46 | # gitignored, PG tenant pw should be set explicitly in prod).
   47 | DEFAULT_ADMIN_PASSWORD = os.environ.get('ADMIN_DEFAULT_PASSWORD', 'changeme')
   48 | # The role the tenant's first account gets — the seeded role that holds every
   49 | # staff grant (see access/seed.py ROLE_GRANTS['administrator']).
   50 | ADMIN_ROLE = 'administrator'
```

```py
   53 | def _is_pg_dsn(handle):
   54 |     """True if ``handle`` looks like a PostgreSQL DSN string (vs a store object)."""
   55 |     return isinstance(handle, str) and handle.startswith(('postgres://', 'postgresql://'))
   56 | 
   57 | 
   58 | def _on_postgres(store):
   59 |     """Decide whether this provisioning targets a real PG control plane.
   60 | 
   61 |     Real PG iff a ``PgAccessStore`` (or a DSN string) is in play, or the env
   62 |     selects the postgres backend. The sqlite dev ``AccessStore`` → single-tenant.
   63 |     """
   64 |     if _is_pg_dsn(store):
   65 |         return True
   66 |     try:
   67 |         from .pgstore import PgAccessStore
   68 |         if isinstance(store, PgAccessStore):
   69 |             return True
   70 |     except Exception:
   71 |         pass
   72 |     return os.environ.get('ACCESS_BACKEND', 'sqlite').lower() in ('postgres', 'pg')
   73 | 
   74 | 
   75 | def _seed_admin_account(store, login, password, full_name=''):
   76 |     """Create (idempotent) ``login`` and give it the ``administrator`` role.
   77 | 
   78 |     Reuses the same primitives the dev seed uses (``create_account`` hashes the
   79 |     password pbkdf2-sha256; ``add_role``/``assign_role`` wire the role). Safe to
   80 |     re-run: ``create_account`` is INSERT-OR-IGNORE, role assignment is idempotent.
   81 |     Returns the account row.
   82 |     """
   83 |     acc = store.create_account(login, password, full_name or login)
   84 |     rid = store.add_role(ADMIN_ROLE)
   85 |     store.assign_role(acc['id'], rid)
   86 |     return acc
   87 | 
   88 | 
   89 | def provision_tenant(store, slug, name, admin_login,
   90 |                      admin_password=None, plan='standard', kind=DEFAULT_KIND,
   91 |                      dsn=None):
   92 |     """Stand up tenant ``<slug>`` end-to-end. Idempotent. Returns a report dict.
   93 | 
   94 |     ``store`` is the control-plane handle: on sqlite dev pass the single
   95 |     ``AccessStore`` (the 'public' store); on PG pass the public ``PgAccessStore``,
   96 |     a DSN string, or ``None`` (the env DSN is used). The tenant-scoped store the
   97 |     rest of the steps operate on is built here.
   98 | 
   99 |     Steps: schema → vocabularies → admin account (administrator role) → plan +
  100 |     entitlements. On the sqlite dev path the single store IS the tenant store.
  101 |     """
  102 |     admin_password = admin_password or DEFAULT_ADMIN_PASSWORD
  103 |     billing._require_plan(plan)               # fail fast on a bad plan name
  104 |     # Validate the slug on BOTH paths (PG validates downstream too, but the sqlite
  105 |     # dev path must reject a bad slug just as firmly so the contract is uniform).
```

```py
  112 |         if dsn is None:

  147 | def deprovision_tenant(slug, dsn=None):
  148 |     """Drop tenant ``<slug>`` (PG: ``DROP SCHEMA t_<slug> CASCADE`` + control row).
  149 | 
  150 |     Delegates to ``pgstore.deprovision_tenant`` (idempotent). A no-op-safe call on
  151 |     a clean DB. On sqlite dev there is nothing per-tenant to drop, so this is a
  152 |     documented no-op there (the single store is shared).
  153 |     """
  154 |     from . import pgstore
  155 |     try:
  156 |         pgstore.deprovision_tenant(slug, dsn=dsn)
  157 |         return {'slug': slug, 'deprovisioned': True}
  158 |     except Exception as e:
  159 |         # psycopg missing / PG down → nothing to do on the dev box.
  160 |         return {'slug': slug, 'deprovisioned': False, 'reason': str(e).splitlines()[0]}
```

```py
  161 | 
  162 | 
  163 | def list_tenants(dsn=None):
  164 |     """All provisioned tenants from the control catalog, with their plan.
  165 | 
  166 |     Reuses ``pgstore.list_tenants`` and joins the billing plan
  167 |     (``billing.get_tenant_plan``). Returns [] cleanly when there is no control
  168 |     plane (sqlite dev / PG down).
  169 |     """
  170 |     from . import pgstore
  171 |     try:
  172 |         rows = pgstore.list_tenants(dsn=dsn)
  173 |     except Exception:
  174 |         return []
  175 |     out = []
  176 |     for r in rows:
  177 |         item = {'slug': r['slug'], 'name': r['name'], 'kind': r.get('kind')}
  178 |         try:
  179 |             item['plan'] = billing.get_tenant_plan(r['slug'], dsn=dsn)
  180 |         except Exception:
  181 |             item['plan'] = billing.DEFAULT_PLAN
  182 |         out.append(item)
  183 |     return out

  186 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #

  189 | def _build_store(dsn=None):
  190 |     """The control-plane store for the CLI: PG public store, or sqlite AccessStore."""
  191 |     backend = os.environ.get('ACCESS_BACKEND', 'sqlite').lower()
  192 |     if backend in ('postgres', 'pg') or _is_pg_dsn(dsn):
  193 |         from .pgstore import PgAccessStore, default_pg_dsn
  194 |         return PgAccessStore(dsn or default_pg_dsn())
  195 |     from .store import AccessStore
  196 |     here = os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)))
  197 |     path = os.environ.get('ACCESS_DB', os.path.join(here, 'access.db'))
  198 |     return AccessStore(path)

  201 | def _print(report):

  203 |     print('  postgres : %s' % report['postgres'])
  204 |     print('  kind     : %s' % report['kind'])
  205 |     print('  admin    : %s (roles: %s)'
  206 |           % (report['admin']['login'], ', '.join(report['admin']['roles'])))
  207 |     print('  plan     : %s' % report['plan'])
  208 |     print('  modules  : %s' % ', '.join(report['modules']))
```

```py
   86 |   path TEXT,
   87 |   sort INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
   88 |   origin TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'seed',
   89 |   UNIQUE (name, code)
   90 | );
   91 | CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS classification_node_tree_idx ON classification_node(name, parent);
   92 | -- Reader-portal state (#222) — holds + queue and reading-list shelves, persisted
   93 | -- in OUR store (NOT on the live ИРБИС server), reader-scoped by RDR ticket. Mirrors
   94 | -- schema_postgres.sql. A "reader" here is the ticket string (RI=) — readers are NOT
   95 | -- in staff_account, so these tables key on the ticket, not an account id.
   96 | CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS reader_hold (
   97 |   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
   98 |   ticket TEXT NOT NULL,             -- RDR ticket (RI=) that owns the hold
   99 |   db TEXT NOT NULL,
  100 |   mfn INTEGER NOT NULL,
  101 |   title TEXT,                       -- resolved at place time (brief read), for the inbox card
  102 |   status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'queued'   -- queued | ready | cancelled
  103 |        CHECK (status IN ('queued','ready','cancelled')),
  104 |   queued_at REAL NOT NULL,
  105 |   until REAL,                       -- pickup-shelf TTL once ready
  106 |   UNIQUE(ticket, db, mfn, status)   -- at most one live hold per reader per item (status-scoped)
  107 | );
  108 | CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS reader_hold_queue_idx ON reader_hold(db, mfn, status, queued_at);
  109 | CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS reader_hold_ticket_idx ON reader_hold(ticket, status);
  110 | CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS reader_shelf (
  111 |   id TEXT NOT NULL,                 -- 'want' | 'fav' (system) | custom 's<n>'
  112 |   ticket TEXT NOT NULL,
  113 |   name TEXT NOT NULL,
  114 |   system INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  115 |   created_at REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT (strftime('%s','now')),
  116 |   PRIMARY KEY (ticket, id)
  117 | );
  118 | CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS reader_shelf_item (
  119 |   ticket TEXT NOT NULL,
  120 |   list_id TEXT NOT NULL,
  121 |   db TEXT NOT NULL,
  122 |   mfn INTEGER NOT NULL,
  123 |   title TEXT,
  124 |   added_at REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT (strftime('%s','now')),
  125 |   PRIMARY KEY (ticket, list_id, db, mfn)
  126 | );
```

```py
  127 | CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS reader_shelf_item_list_idx ON reader_shelf_item(ticket, list_id);
  128 | -- Reader-portal v2 social layer (#134 engagement / #133 discovery), reader-scoped
  129 | -- by RDR ticket, persisted in OUR store (NOT on the live ИРБИС server). Mirrors
  130 | -- schema_postgres.sql.
  131 | -- reader_review: one editable review per (ticket, db, mfn) — upsert on that key.
  132 | CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS reader_review (
  133 |   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
  134 |   ticket TEXT NOT NULL,
  135 |   db TEXT NOT NULL,
  136 |   mfn INTEGER NOT NULL,
  137 |   rating INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK (rating BETWEEN 1 AND 5),
  138 |   text TEXT,
  139 |   reader_name TEXT,
  140 |   ts REAL NOT NULL,
  141 |   UNIQUE(ticket, db, mfn)
  142 | );
  143 | CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS reader_review_item_idx ON reader_review(db, mfn);
  144 | -- reader_history: every record-open, auto-logged. Dedup by (db,mfn) is done at
  145 | -- read time (latest ts wins); we append rows so the latest open updates ts.
  146 | CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS reader_history (
  147 |   ticket TEXT NOT NULL,
  148 |   db TEXT NOT NULL,
  149 |   mfn INTEGER NOT NULL,
  150 |   title TEXT,
  151 |   ts REAL NOT NULL,
  152 |   PRIMARY KEY (ticket, db, mfn)
  153 | );
  154 | CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS reader_history_ticket_idx ON reader_history(ticket, ts);
  155 | -- saved_search: a reader's stored query (name/db/prefix/query).
  156 | CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS saved_search (
  157 |   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
  158 |   ticket TEXT NOT NULL,
  159 |   name TEXT NOT NULL,
  160 |   db TEXT,
  161 |   prefix TEXT,
  162 |   query TEXT NOT NULL,
  163 |   created_at REAL NOT NULL
  164 | );
  165 | CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS saved_search_ticket_idx ON saved_search(ticket, id);
  166 | -- Consent (152-ФЗ ст.6/9, audit finding V9 / R9) — append-only: a new state is a
  167 | -- NEW row, never an UPDATE, so withdraw + re-consent is HISTORY not overwrite
  168 | -- (SPEC_compliance_152fz §2.5). The effective consent for a reader is the latest
  169 | -- row by ts. Reader-scoped by RDR ticket (readers are not in staff_account).
  170 | CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS reader_consent (
  171 |   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
  172 |   ticket TEXT NOT NULL,
  173 |   given INTEGER NOT NULL,            -- 1 granted | 0 withdrawn
  174 |   version INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1, -- privacy-policy version the consent is for
  175 |   ts REAL NOT NULL
  176 | );
  177 | CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS reader_consent_ticket_idx ON reader_consent(ticket, ts DESC);
  178 | """
  179 | 
  180 | 
  181 | class AccessStore:
```

```py
  182 |     def __init__(self, db_path):
  183 |         self.db_path = db_path
  184 |         self._local = threading.local()
  185 |         self.ensure_schema()
  186 | 
  187 |     def _conn(self):
  188 |         c = getattr(self._local, 'conn', None)
  189 |         if c is None:
  190 |             c = sqlite3.connect(self.db_path)
  191 |             c.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
  192 |             c.execute('PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON')
  193 |             self._local.conn = c
  194 |         return c
  195 | 
  196 |     def ensure_schema(self):
  197 |         c = self._conn()
  198 |         c.executescript(SCHEMA_SQLITE)
  199 |         c.commit()
  200 | 
  201 |     # ---- password hashing (pbkdf2-sha256, stdlib) ----
  202 |     @staticmethod
  203 |     def hash_password(pw, iterations=200000):
  204 |         salt = os.urandom(16)
  205 |         dk = hashlib.pbkdf2_hmac('sha256', pw.encode('utf-8'), salt, iterations)
  206 |         return 'pbkdf2$%d$%s$%s' % (iterations, salt.hex(), dk.hex())
  207 | 
  208 |     @staticmethod
  209 |     def verify_password(pw, stored):
  210 |         try:
  211 |             _algo, it, salt_hex, hash_hex = stored.split('$')
  212 |             dk = hashlib.pbkdf2_hmac('sha256', pw.encode('utf-8'),
  213 |                                      bytes.fromhex(salt_hex), int(it))
  214 |             return hmac.compare_digest(dk.hex(), hash_hex)
  215 |         except Exception:
  216 |             return False
  217 | 
  218 |     # ---- accounts ----
  219 |     def create_account(self, login, password, full_name=''):
  220 |         c = self._conn()
  221 |         c.execute('INSERT OR IGNORE INTO staff_account(login,pass_hash,full_name) VALUES(?,?,?)',
  222 |                   (login, self.hash_password(password), full_name))
  223 |         c.commit()
  224 |         return self.get_account(login)
  225 | 
  226 |     def get_account(self, login):
  227 |         r = self._conn().execute('SELECT * FROM staff_account WHERE login=?', (login,)).fetchone()
  228 |         return dict(r) if r else None
  229 | 
  230 |     def authenticate(self, login, password):
  231 |         a = self.get_account(login)
  232 |         if a and a['is_active'] and self.verify_password(password, a['pass_hash']):
  233 |             return a
  234 |         return None
  235 | 
  236 |     # ---- grants / roles ----
```

```py
  237 |     def add_grant(self, account_id, function, db, level):
  238 |         c = self._conn()
  239 |         c.execute('INSERT OR REPLACE INTO grant_entry(account_id,function,db,level) VALUES(?,?,?,?)',
  240 |                   (account_id, function, db, level))

  242 | 
  243 |     def add_role(self, name):
  244 |         c = self._conn()
  245 |         c.execute('INSERT OR IGNORE INTO role(name) VALUES(?)', (name,))
  246 |         c.commit()
  247 |         return c.execute('SELECT id FROM role WHERE name=?', (name,)).fetchone()['id']
  248 | 
  249 |     def add_role_grant(self, role_id, function, db, level):
  250 |         c = self._conn()
  251 |         c.execute('INSERT OR REPLACE INTO role_grant(role_id,function,db,level) VALUES(?,?,?,?)',
  252 |                   (role_id, function, db, level))

  255 |     def assign_role(self, account_id, role_id):
  256 |         c = self._conn()
  257 |         c.execute('INSERT OR IGNORE INTO account_role(account_id,role_id) VALUES(?,?)',
  258 |                   (account_id, role_id))
  259 |         c.commit()
  260 | 
  261 |     def effective_grants(self, account_id):
  262 |         """Union of direct grants and grants from all assigned roles."""
  263 |         c = self._conn()
  264 |         grants = [dict(r) for r in c.execute(
  265 |             'SELECT function,db,level FROM grant_entry WHERE account_id=?', (account_id,)).fetchall()]
  266 |         grants += [dict(r) for r in c.execute(
  267 |             '''SELECT rg.function, rg.db, rg.level FROM role_grant rg
  268 |                JOIN account_role ar ON ar.role_id=rg.role_id
  269 |                WHERE ar.account_id=?''', (account_id,)).fetchall()]
  270 |         return grants
  271 | 
  272 |     # ---- admin: account/role administration (АРМ Администратор, #187) ----
  273 |     def get_account_by_id(self, account_id):
  274 |         r = self._conn().execute(
  275 |             'SELECT * FROM staff_account WHERE id=?', (account_id,)).fetchone()
  276 |         return dict(r) if r else None
  277 | 
  278 |     def list_accounts(self):
  279 |         """All staff accounts (id/login/full_name/is_active), login-ordered."""
  280 |         return [dict(r) for r in self._conn().execute(
  281 |             'SELECT id,login,full_name,is_active FROM staff_account '
  282 |             'ORDER BY login').fetchall()]
  283 | 
  284 |     def account_roles(self, account_id):
  285 |         """Role names assigned to an account (sorted)."""
  286 |         return [r['name'] for r in self._conn().execute(
  287 |             'SELECT ro.name FROM role ro JOIN account_role ar ON ar.role_id=ro.id '
  288 |             'WHERE ar.account_id=? ORDER BY ro.name', (account_id,)).fetchall()]
  289 | 
  290 |     def set_account_roles(self, account_id, role_names):
  291 |         """Replace an account's role set with ``role_names`` (creates missing roles).
```

```py
  292 | 
  293 |         Idempotent and atomic: clears the account's account_role rows then assigns
  294 |         the requested roles, minting a role row for any unknown name (mirrors how
  295 |         ``add_role`` is used by the seed). Returns the resolved role-name list."""
  296 |         c = self._conn()
  297 |         c.execute('DELETE FROM account_role WHERE account_id=?', (account_id,))
  298 |         for name in role_names:

  300 |             c.execute('INSERT OR IGNORE INTO account_role(account_id,role_id) VALUES(?,?)',

  305 |     def set_account_active(self, account_id, active):
  306 |         """Enable/disable an account; returns the new is_active (0/1)."""
  307 |         c = self._conn()
  308 |         c.execute('UPDATE staff_account SET is_active=? WHERE id=?',
  309 |                   (1 if active else 0, account_id))
  310 |         c.commit()
  311 |         a = self.get_account_by_id(account_id)
  312 |         return a['is_active'] if a else None
  313 | 
  314 |     def list_roles(self):
  315 |         """All roles with their grants: [{name, grants:[{function,db,level}]}]."""
  316 |         c = self._conn()
  317 |         out = []
  318 |         for ro in c.execute('SELECT id,name FROM role ORDER BY name').fetchall():
  319 |             grants = [dict(r) for r in c.execute(
  320 |                 'SELECT function,db,level FROM role_grant WHERE role_id=? '
  321 |                 'ORDER BY function, db', (ro['id'],)).fetchall()]
  322 |             out.append({'name': ro['name'], 'grants': grants})
  323 |         return out

  325 |     # ---- audit ----
  326 |     def audit(self, actor, function, db, mfn, result, detail=None):
  327 |         c = self._conn()
  328 |         c.execute('INSERT INTO audit_log(ts,actor,function,db,mfn,result,detail) VALUES(?,?,?,?,?,?,?)',
  329 |                   (time.time(), actor, function, db, mfn, result,
  330 |                    json.dumps(detail or {}, ensure_ascii=False)))
  331 |         c.commit()
  332 | 
  333 |     def recent_audit(self, limit=50):
  334 |         return [dict(r) for r in self._conn().execute(
  335 |             'SELECT * FROM audit_log ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT ?', (limit,)).fetchall()]
  336 | 
  337 |     # ---- vocabularies (seeding engine, gap A5 #188) ----
  338 |     def upsert_vocabulary(self, name, title, kind, field_hint, seed_version):
  339 |         """Insert/update a dictionary's metadata row (idempotent on name)."""
  340 |         c = self._conn()
  341 |         c.execute(
  342 |             'INSERT INTO vocabulary(name,title,kind,field_hint,seed_version) '
  343 |             'VALUES(?,?,?,?,?) ON CONFLICT(name) DO UPDATE SET '
  344 |             'title=excluded.title, kind=excluded.kind, field_hint=excluded.field_hint',
  345 |             (name, title, kind, field_hint, seed_version))
  346 |         c.commit()
```

```py
  348 |     def upsert_vocabulary_value(self, vocab, code, label, sort, origin='seed'):
  349 |         """Insert/update one (code,label) of a dictionary (idempotent on vocab,code).
  350 | 
  351 |         Never downgrades origin: a value the library marked 'custom'/'imported' keeps
  352 |         that origin on a re-seed so reseed merge logic (SPEC §3.3) can protect it.
  353 |         """
  354 |         c = self._conn()
  355 |         c.execute(
  356 |             'INSERT INTO vocabulary_value(vocab,code,label,sort,origin) VALUES(?,?,?,?,?) '
  357 |             'ON CONFLICT(vocab,code) DO UPDATE SET label=excluded.label, sort=excluded.sort',
  358 |             (vocab, code, label, sort, origin))
  359 |         c.commit()

  361 |     def get_vocabulary(self, name):
```

```py
  425 |     def hold_cancel(self, ticket, hold_id):
  426 |         """Cancel a reader's own hold; returns the row if it was theirs and live."""
  427 |         c = self._conn()
  428 |         row = c.execute(
  429 |             "SELECT * FROM reader_hold WHERE id=? AND ticket=? "
  430 |             "AND status IN ('queued','ready')", (hold_id, ticket)).fetchone()
  431 |         if not row:
  432 |             return None
  433 |         c.execute("UPDATE reader_hold SET status='cancelled' WHERE id=?", (hold_id,))
  434 |         c.commit()
```

```py
  488 |     @staticmethod
  489 |     def _decrypt_review(row):
  490 |         """Return the review dict with ``reader_name`` decrypted (V1).
  491 | 
  492 |         The column is stored as ciphertext at rest (crypto.encrypt); decryption is
  493 |         transparent — a legacy plaintext row passes through unchanged."""
  494 |         if row is None:
  495 |             return None
  496 |         d = dict(row)
  497 |         if 'reader_name' in d:
  498 |             d['reader_name'] = crypto.decrypt(d['reader_name'])
  499 |         return d

  501 |     def review_upsert(self, ticket, db, mfn, rating, text, reader_name, ts):
  502 |         """Insert/replace the reader's review of (db,mfn) (one per reader+item).
  503 | 
  504 |         ``reader_name`` is the reader's resolved display name (ПДн); it is
  505 |         ENCRYPTED at rest (V1) so a raw DB dump never reveals it. The returned row
  506 |         is decrypted for the caller (transparent)."""
  507 |         c = self._conn()
  508 |         enc_name = crypto.encrypt(reader_name)
  509 |         c.execute(
  510 |             'INSERT INTO reader_review(ticket,db,mfn,rating,text,reader_name,ts) '
  511 |             'VALUES(?,?,?,?,?,?,?) ON CONFLICT(ticket,db,mfn) DO UPDATE SET '
```

```py
  515 |         c.commit()
  516 |         r = c.execute('SELECT * FROM reader_review WHERE ticket=? AND db=? AND mfn=?',
  517 |                       (ticket, db, mfn)).fetchone()

  533 |         return self._decrypt_review(r) if r else None
  534 | 
  535 |     def review_name_ciphertext(self, ticket, db, mfn):
  536 |         """The RAW stored ``reader_name`` value (no decrypt) — for tests/forensics
```

```py
  598 |     # ---- consent (V9 / R9) — append-only; latest row by ts is effective ----
  599 |     def consent_record(self, ticket, given, version, ts):
  600 |         """Append a consent state (granted/withdrawn) for the reader. Never an
  601 |         UPDATE — the prior state is kept as history (SPEC_compliance_152fz §2.5).
  602 |         Returns the effective consent after the write."""
  603 |         c = self._conn()
  604 |         c.execute('INSERT INTO reader_consent(ticket,given,version,ts) VALUES(?,?,?,?)',
  605 |                   (ticket, 1 if given else 0, int(version), ts))

  607 |         return self.consent_current(ticket)

  609 |     def consent_current(self, ticket):

  613 |         r = self._conn().execute(
  614 |             'SELECT given,version,ts FROM reader_consent WHERE ticket=? '
  615 |             'ORDER BY ts DESC, id DESC LIMIT 1', (ticket,)).fetchone()
  616 |         if not r:
  617 |             return None
  618 |         return {'given': bool(r['given']), 'ts': r['ts'], 'version': r['version']}
```

```py
  622 |     # ticket. Does NOT touch the live ИРБИС RDR (that is the system of record);
  623 |     # only the portal-side state we persisted. Reader-scoped: a ticket can only
  624 |     # erase its own rows (the route guards the ticket == session).
  625 |     def erase_reader(self, ticket):
  626 |         """Delete all of ``ticket``'s portal data; return a per-table count dict."""
  627 |         c = self._conn()
  628 |         counts = {}
  629 |         for key, sql in (
  630 |                 ('reviews', 'DELETE FROM reader_review WHERE ticket=?'),
  631 |                 ('holds', 'DELETE FROM reader_hold WHERE ticket=?'),
  632 |                 ('shelves', 'DELETE FROM reader_shelf WHERE ticket=?'),
  633 |                 ('shelfItems', 'DELETE FROM reader_shelf_item WHERE ticket=?'),
  634 |                 ('history', 'DELETE FROM reader_history WHERE ticket=?'),
  635 |                 ('savedSearches', 'DELETE FROM saved_search WHERE ticket=?'),
  636 |                 ('consent', 'DELETE FROM reader_consent WHERE ticket=?')):
  637 |             cur = c.execute(sql, (ticket,))
  638 |             counts[key] = cur.rowcount if cur.rowcount is not None and cur.rowcount >= 0 else 0
  639 |         c.commit()
  640 |         return counts

  642 |     # ---- PDn-access journal read (V5 / R5) — entries are written via audit() ----
  643 |     def pdn_access_log(self, limit=50):
  644 |         """The most-recent ``pdn.read`` audit entries, newest first, capped.
  645 | 
  646 |         The journal reuses the append-only ``audit_log`` with function='pdn.read'
  647 |         (MVP: a dedicated pdn_access_log table is specified for a later slice,
  648 |         SPEC_compliance_152fz §3.5). Each entry carries actor, subject ticket (in
  649 |         detail), ts, action."""
  650 |         return [dict(r) for r in self._conn().execute(
  651 |             "SELECT * FROM audit_log WHERE function='pdn.read' "
  652 |             'ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT ?', (limit,)).fetchall()]
```

### Файл: `irbis-web/backend/access/pgstore.py`  · строк: 700

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """PostgreSQL-backed Access store (prod target, ADR-004). Mirrors the sqlite
    3 | ``AccessStore`` public surface exactly — same methods, same return shapes — so
    4 | ``core.py`` and the Access test suite run unchanged against either backend
    5 | (switch via env ``ACCESS_BACKEND=sqlite|postgres``).
    6 | 
    7 | Holds staff accounts, grants, roles and the audit log (readers live in RDR, not
    8 | here). DDL is the shared ``schema_postgres.sql``. Password hashing (pbkdf2-sha256)
    9 | is reused verbatim from ``AccessStore`` so hashes are interchangeable across
   10 | backends.
   11 | 
   12 | Driver: psycopg 3 (``pip install "psycopg[binary]"``). psycopg is imported
   13 | lazily so this module still imports on a Python without psycopg installed
   14 | (e.g. 3.14) when the sqlite backend is selected — only constructing
   15 | ``PgAccessStore`` requires the driver.
   16 | """
   17 | import os
   18 | import re
   19 | import threading
   20 | 
   21 | from .store import AccessStore   # reuse pbkdf2 hash/verify (interchangeable hashes)
   22 | from . import crypto             # ПДн field encryption (V1): pgcrypto key + token fallback
   23 | 
   24 | # DDL lives next to this module as the shared production schema.
```

<!-- ─── страница 215 ─── -->

```py
   25 | _HERE = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))
   26 | _SCHEMA_SQL_PATH = os.path.join(_HERE, 'schema_postgres.sql')      # Access DDL (per tenant)
   27 | _CONTROL_SQL_PATH = os.path.join(_HERE, 'schema_control.sql')      # control.tenant catalog
   28 | 
   29 | # A slug is the tenant key and becomes part of a SQL identifier (t_<slug>). It is
   30 | # NEVER interpolated into SQL without passing this gate, so schema names can't be
   31 | # used for injection. Lowercase letters/digits/underscore, must start with a letter.
   32 | _SLUG_RE = re.compile(r'^[a-z][a-z0-9_]{0,40}$')
   33 | 
   34 | 
   35 | def _validate_slug(slug):
   36 |     if not isinstance(slug, str) or not _SLUG_RE.match(slug):
   37 |         raise ValueError(
   38 |             'invalid tenant slug %r (need ^[a-z][a-z0-9_]{0,40}$)' % (slug,))
   39 |     return slug
   40 | 
   41 | 
   42 | def schema_for(slug):
   43 |     """Schema name that holds tenant <slug>'s Access data: ``t_<slug>``."""
   44 |     return 't_' + _validate_slug(slug)
   45 | 
   46 | 
   47 | class PgAccessStore:
   48 |     """Same public API as ``access.store.AccessStore`` on PostgreSQL/psycopg3.
   49 | 
   50 |     A dedicated database/schema is expected (default DSN points at
   51 |     ``irbis_access``) so the Access tables never collide with own-server
   52 |     catalog data.
   53 | 
   54 |     **Multi-tenant (issue #100):** pass ``tenant_schema='t_<slug>'`` to scope the
   55 |     SAME store class to one tenant. Every connection checkout then runs
   56 |     ``SET search_path = <schema>, public`` so all unqualified table access reads/
   57 |     writes that tenant's schema only — data is fully separated by schema, with no
   58 |     leakage when a pooled connection is reused (the path is set on every checkout).
   59 |     ``tenant_schema=None`` (the default) keeps the back-compat single-``public``
   60 |     behaviour unchanged. Prefer ``make_tenant_store(dsn, slug)`` to construct one.
   61 |     """
   62 | 
   63 |     def __init__(self, dsn, tenant_schema=None):
   64 |         self.dsn = dsn
   65 |         self.db_path = dsn          # parity with AccessStore.db_path (a few callers introspect it)
   66 |         self.tenant_schema = tenant_schema   # 't_<slug>' for a tenant, or None for public
   67 |         self._local = threading.local()
   68 |         self.ensure_schema()
   69 | 
   70 |     # ---- connection (thread-local, autocommit like the sqlite store's per-op commit) ----
   71 |     def _conn(self):
   72 |         import psycopg
   73 |         from psycopg.rows import dict_row
   74 |         conn = getattr(self._local, 'conn', None)
   75 |         if conn is None or conn.closed:
   76 |             conn = psycopg.connect(self.dsn, row_factory=dict_row, autocommit=True)
   77 |             self._apply_search_path(conn)
   78 |             self._local.conn = conn
   79 |         return conn
```

<!-- ─── страница 216 ─── -->

```py
   80 | 
   81 |     def _apply_search_path(self, conn):
   82 |         """Pin this connection to the tenant schema (then ``public``).
   83 | 
   84 |         Called on every checkout of a fresh connection, so a connection reused
   85 |         across tenants never leaks: the search_path is (re)set deterministically.
   86 |         The schema identifier is built only from a validated slug (see
   87 |         ``schema_for``), so it is safe to embed without quoting injection risk.
   88 |         Run only when this store is tenant-scoped; the public store keeps PG's
   89 |         default search_path untouched (back-compat).
   90 |         """
   91 |         if self.tenant_schema:
   92 |             from psycopg import sql
   93 |             conn.execute(
   94 |                 sql.SQL('SET search_path = {}, public').format(
   95 |                     sql.Identifier(self.tenant_schema)))
   96 | 
   97 |     def ensure_schema(self):
   98 |         """Create the Access tables. For a tenant store, create the schema first
   99 |         and apply the (unqualified) Access DDL *into* it via ``search_path``."""
  100 |         conn = self._conn()   # also applies search_path = t_<slug>, public
  101 |         # pgcrypto: enable best-effort BEFORE the DDL (V1 ПДн encryption). It lives
  102 |         # in the public/extension schema, so it is shared across tenant schemas. A
  103 |         # role without CREATE EXTENSION privilege must not abort schema creation —
  104 |         # we record availability and degrade to the Python-side cipher token.
  105 |         self._pgcrypto = self._ensure_pgcrypto(conn)
  106 |         if self.tenant_schema:
  107 |             from psycopg import sql
  108 |             conn.execute(sql.SQL('CREATE SCHEMA IF NOT EXISTS {}').format(
  109 |                 sql.Identifier(self.tenant_schema)))
  110 |             # search_path is already pinned (PG resolves it lazily at query time),
  111 |             # so the unqualified DDL below creates tables inside t_<slug>.
  112 |         with open(_SCHEMA_SQL_PATH, encoding='utf-8') as f:
  113 |             ddl = f.read()
  114 |         conn.execute(ddl)
  115 | 
  116 |     @staticmethod
  117 |     def _ensure_pgcrypto(conn):
  118 |         """Best-effort ``CREATE EXTENSION pgcrypto`` + verify pgp_sym_* are callable.
  119 | 
  120 |         Returns True iff in-database encryption is available. On a role without the
  121 |         privilege (or pgcrypto absent) returns False so review writes fall back to
  122 |         the Python-side cipher token — still ciphertext at rest, just not pgcrypto."""
  123 |         try:
  124 |             conn.execute('CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pgcrypto')
  125 |         except Exception:
  126 |             pass
  127 |         try:
  128 |             conn.execute("SELECT pgp_sym_decrypt(pgp_sym_encrypt('x', 'k'), 'k')").fetchone()
  129 |             return True
  130 |         except Exception:
  131 |             return False
  132 | 
  133 |     # ---- password hashing — reuse the sqlite store's pbkdf2 (hashes interchangeable) ----
  134 |     @staticmethod
```

```py
  381 |     # ---- reader-portal v2 social (#134/#133) — reviews / history / saved searches ----
  382 |     # reader_name is ПДн: stored ENCRYPTED at rest (V1) in the BYTEA column. The
  383 |     # real prod path is pgcrypto pgp_sym_encrypt(name, PDN_KEY) IN-DATABASE,
  384 |     # decrypted on read with pgp_sym_decrypt (key bound as a parameter, never
  385 |     # interpolated). When pgcrypto is unavailable (role lacks the extension) the
  386 |     # store falls back to the Python-side cipher TOKEN stored as the column's bytes
  387 |     # — still ciphertext at rest, decrypted with crypto.decrypt. Either way the
  388 |     # caller sees plaintext transparently.
  389 |     def _review_select_cols(self):
  390 |         """SELECT column list that yields a decrypted ``reader_name`` for the
  391 |         active backend. pgcrypto decrypts in-DB; the fallback returns raw bytes
  392 |         which _row_decrypt() turns back into the plaintext via the token cipher."""
  393 |         if getattr(self, '_pgcrypto', False):
  394 |             return ('id, ticket, db, mfn, rating, text, ts, '
  395 |                     'pgp_sym_decrypt(reader_name, %s) AS reader_name'), (crypto.pg_key(),)
  396 |         return 'id, ticket, db, mfn, rating, text, ts, reader_name', ()
  397 | 
  398 |     @staticmethod
  399 |     def _row_decrypt(row):
  400 |         """Normalize a review row: decode a token-fallback bytea reader_name back to
  401 |         plaintext. pgcrypto rows already carry plaintext text; pass them through."""
  402 |         if row is None:
  403 |             return None
  404 |         d = dict(row)
  405 |         v = d.get('reader_name')
  406 |         if isinstance(v, (bytes, bytearray, memoryview)):
  407 |             d['reader_name'] = crypto.decrypt(bytes(v).decode('utf-8', 'replace'))
  408 |         return d
  409 |
```

```py
  539 | def make_store(cfg=None):
  540 |     """Factory: return the Access store for the configured backend.
  541 | 
  542 |     ``ACCESS_BACKEND=sqlite`` (default) -> sqlite ``AccessStore`` at ``cfg.access_db``
  543 |     (or ``ACCESS_DB`` env). ``ACCESS_BACKEND=postgres`` -> ``PgAccessStore`` at
  544 |     ``ACCESS_PG_DSN`` (default the local ``irbis-pg`` container's ``irbis_access`` db).
  545 | 
  546 |     Mirrors ``server-own/store.make_store()``. Sqlite stays the default so nothing
  547 |     breaks where psycopg is unavailable.
  548 |     """
  549 |     backend = os.environ.get('ACCESS_BACKEND', 'sqlite').lower()
  550 |     if backend in ('postgres', 'pg'):
  551 |         dsn = os.environ.get(
  552 |             'ACCESS_PG_DSN', 'postgresql://postgres:pg@127.0.0.1:5433/irbis_access')
  553 |         return PgAccessStore(dsn)
  554 |     # sqlite (default)
  555 |     if cfg is not None and getattr(cfg, 'access_db', None):
  556 |         path = cfg.access_db
  557 |     else:
  558 |         here = os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)))
  559 |         path = os.environ.get('ACCESS_DB', os.path.join(here, 'access.db'))
  560 |     return AccessStore(path)
  561 | 
  562 | 
  563 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  564 | # Multi-tenancy: control schema + provisioning (issue #100, I1).

  568 |     return os.environ.get(
  569 |         'ACCESS_PG_DSN', 'postgresql://postgres:pg@127.0.0.1:5433/irbis_access')
  570 |

  573 |     """A short-lived autocommit connection for cross-tenant (control-plane) DDL.
  574 |
```

```py
  575 |     Kept separate from any store's thread-local connection so its search_path
  576 |     stays at PG's default — provisioning addresses schemas explicitly.
  577 |     """
  578 |     import psycopg
  579 |     from psycopg.rows import dict_row
  580 |     return psycopg.connect(dsn, row_factory=dict_row, autocommit=True)
  581 | 
  582 | 
  583 | def ensure_control_schema(dsn=None):
  584 |     """Create the ``control`` schema + ``control.tenant`` table (idempotent)."""
  585 |     dsn = dsn or default_pg_dsn()
  586 |     with open(_CONTROL_SQL_PATH, encoding='utf-8') as f:
  587 |         ddl = f.read()
  588 |     conn = _admin_conn(dsn)
  589 |     try:
  590 |         conn.execute(ddl)
  591 |     finally:
  592 |         conn.close()
  593 | 
  594 | 
  595 | def make_tenant_store(slug, dsn=None):
  596 |     """A ``PgAccessStore`` scoped to tenant ``<slug>`` (schema ``t_<slug>``)."""
  597 |     return PgAccessStore(dsn or default_pg_dsn(), tenant_schema=schema_for(slug))
  598 | 
  599 | 
  600 | def schema_exists(slug, dsn=None):
  601 |     """True iff tenant ``<slug>``'s schema (``t_<slug>``) has been provisioned.
  602 | 
  603 |     A cheap ``information_schema.schemata`` probe over a short-lived admin
  604 |     connection (PG's default search_path, so the lookup is unambiguous). Used by
  605 |     the auth path to decide whether the default tenant's accounts live in
  606 |     ``t_<slug>`` (provisioned) or still in the public store (un-provisioned dev).
  607 |     Returns False (never raises) if PG/psycopg is unavailable, so the sqlite dev
  608 |     box and a control-plane-less PG both degrade cleanly.
  609 |     """
  610 |     try:
  611 |         schema = schema_for(slug)
  612 |     except ValueError:
  613 |         return False
  614 |     try:
  615 |         conn = _admin_conn(dsn or default_pg_dsn())
  616 |     except Exception:
  617 |         return False
  618 |     try:
  619 |         return conn.execute(
  620 |             'SELECT 1 FROM information_schema.schemata WHERE schema_name=%s',
  621 |             (schema,)).fetchone() is not None
  622 |     except Exception:
  623 |         return False
  624 |     finally:
  625 |         conn.close()
  626 | 
  627 | 
  628 | def list_tenants(dsn=None):
  629 |     """All provisioned tenants from the control catalog (ordered by slug)."""
```

```py
  640 | 
  641 | def provision_tenant(slug, name, kind, dsn=None, do_seed=True):
  642 |     """Create (or re-create idempotently) tenant ``<slug>`` and return its store.
  643 | 
  644 |     Steps (all idempotent — safe to re-run):
  645 |       1. ensure ``control`` schema + record the tenant row (upsert on slug);
  646 |       2. ``CREATE SCHEMA IF NOT EXISTS t_<slug>`` and apply the Access DDL into it
  647 |          (done by ``PgAccessStore(tenant_schema=...)`` on construction);
  648 |       3. run the idempotent seed (admin/admin, librarian/librarian) *inside* that
  649 |          tenant schema.
  650 | 
  651 |     Returns the tenant-scoped ``PgAccessStore``.
  652 |     """
  653 |     _validate_slug(slug)
  654 |     dsn = dsn or default_pg_dsn()
  655 |     ensure_control_schema(dsn)
  656 |     conn = _admin_conn(dsn)
  657 |     try:
  658 |         conn.execute(
  659 |             'INSERT INTO control.tenant(slug, name, kind) VALUES(%s,%s,%s) '
  660 |             'ON CONFLICT(slug) DO UPDATE SET name=EXCLUDED.name, kind=EXCLUDED.kind',
  661 |             (slug, name, kind))
  662 |     finally:
  663 |         conn.close()
  664 |     # Constructing the store creates t_<slug> and the Access tables within it
  665 |     # (including the vocabulary/classification tables from schema_postgres.sql).
  666 |     store = make_tenant_store(slug, dsn)
  667 |     if do_seed:
  668 |         from .seed import seed
  669 |         seed(store)
  670 |         # Licensing (#101): turn the default module set ON for the new tenant.
  671 |         # Idempotent; leaves any operator-disabled module untouched on re-provision.
  672 |         from . import entitlements
  673 |         entitlements.seed_modules(slug, dsn)
  674 |         # Seeding engine (A5, #188): system vocabs populated, institution vocabs
  675 |         # created empty — runs AFTER schema migrate, idempotent (re-provision safe).
  676 |         from . import seed_vocab
  677 |         seed_vocab.ensure_seed_catalog(dsn)     # master catalog (control plane)
  678 |         seed_vocab.seed_vocabularies(store, dsn)  # copy into t_<slug>
  679 |     return store
  680 | 
  681 | 
  682 | def deprovision_tenant(slug, dsn=None):
  683 |     """Drop tenant ``<slug>``: ``DROP SCHEMA t_<slug> CASCADE`` + remove its
  684 |     control row. Idempotent (no error if the tenant was never provisioned)."""
```

### Файл: `irbis-web/backend/access/jwt.py`  · строк: 109

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """Dependency-free JWT (HMAC-SHA256 / HS256) for signed bearer sessions (issue #101).
    3 | 
    4 | The frontend sends ``Authorization: Bearer <token>`` and treats the token as an
    5 | opaque string, so issuing a signed JWT here needs NO frontend change (api.ts is
    6 | untouched). We deliberately use only the stdlib (``hmac``/``hashlib``/``base64``/
    7 | ``json``) — no pip dependency (PyJWT etc.) — because disk is tight on the build box.
    8 | 
    9 | Claims we issue (see ``Api._new_session`` in core.py):
   10 |   sub     subject  — staff login / reader "RI=<ticket>" / "guest"
   11 |   tenant  tenant slug the session is scoped to ('public' when single-tenant/dev)
   12 |   kind    'guest' | 'reader' | 'staff'
   13 |   grants  the session's effective grants (list of {function, db, level})
   14 |   iat     issued-at  (unix seconds)
   15 |   exp     expiry     (unix seconds) — verified on every request
   16 | 
   17 | ``encode`` signs; ``decode`` verifies the signature (constant-time) AND the exp
   18 | claim. A tampered payload/signature or an expired token raises ``JwtError`` →
   19 | the API edge maps that to 401. Only HS256 is accepted (``alg`` is pinned on
   20 | decode, so the "alg: none" downgrade attack is rejected).
   21 | """
   22 | import base64
   23 | import hashlib
   24 | import hmac
   25 | import json
   26 | import time
   27 | 
   28 | _ALG = 'HS256'
   29 | _HEADER = {'alg': _ALG, 'typ': 'JWT'}
   30 | 
   31 | 
   32 | class JwtError(Exception):
   33 |     """Raised when a token fails to verify (bad signature, malformed, expired)."""
   34 | 
   35 | 
   36 | def _b64u_encode(raw):
   37 |     """URL-safe base64 without padding (JWT convention)."""
   38 |     return base64.urlsafe_b64encode(raw).rstrip(b'=').decode('ascii')
   39 | 
```

<!-- ─── страница 228 ─── -->

```py
   40 | 
   41 | def _b64u_decode(seg):
   42 |     """Decode a URL-safe base64 segment, restoring the stripped padding."""
   43 |     pad = '=' * (-len(seg) % 4)
   44 |     return base64.urlsafe_b64decode(seg + pad)
   45 | 
   46 | 
   47 | def _sign(signing_input, secret):
   48 |     return hmac.new(_secret_bytes(secret), signing_input, hashlib.sha256).digest()
   49 | 
   50 | 
   51 | def _secret_bytes(secret):
   52 |     return secret.encode('utf-8') if isinstance(secret, str) else secret
   53 | 
   54 | 
   55 | def encode(claims, secret, ttl_seconds=43200, now=None):
   56 |     """Sign ``claims`` into a compact JWS string. Sets iat/exp if not already present.
   57 | 
   58 |     ``ttl_seconds`` defaults to 12h. ``now`` is injectable for tests.
   59 |     """
   60 |     now = int(now if now is not None else time.time())
   61 |     payload = dict(claims)
   62 |     payload.setdefault('iat', now)
   63 |     payload.setdefault('exp', now + int(ttl_seconds))
   64 |     h = _b64u_encode(json.dumps(_HEADER, separators=(',', ':'), sort_keys=True).encode('utf-8'))
   65 |     p = _b64u_encode(json.dumps(payload, separators=(',', ':'),
   66 |                                 ensure_ascii=False, sort_keys=True).encode('utf-8'))

   91 |         raise JwtError('unexpected alg %r' % header.get('alg'))
```

### Файл: `irbis-web/backend/access/crypto.py`  · строк: 263

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """Field-level encryption of stored ПДн at rest — audit finding **V1** / R1
    3 | (152-ФЗ ст.19; ПП-1119; ФСТЭК-21). Closes the recon gap "ПДн читателей не
    4 | зашифрованы при хранении": any reader PII this product persists in OUR store
    5 | (currently the resolved reader display name in ``reader_review.reader_name``,
    6 | category ``pii`` per SPEC_pki_keys §1.2) is written as CIPHERTEXT and decrypted
    7 | only in the handler that hands it back.
    8 | 
    9 | Scope (MVP Phase 3, deliberately small — this is the application-side seam, not
   10 | the full KMS).  The full key hierarchy (L0 master → per-tenant KEK → per-category
   11 | DEK envelope, rotation/revocation, certified СКЗИ provider) is specified in
   12 | ``docs/design/specs/platform/SPEC_pki_keys.md`` (ADR-006, gap D2) and lands with
   13 | the ``kms`` domain.  Here we provide the **abstract crypto seam** those specs call
   14 | (``encrypt`` / ``decrypt``) with a single env-keyed category key, structured so the
   15 | backend can later be swapped for a KMS/СКЗИ provider WITHOUT touching callers.
   16 | 
   17 | Backends (auto-selected, strongest available wins)
   18 | --------------------------------------------------
   19 | 1. **pgcrypto** (the real prod path for the PostgreSQL store) — sensitive columns
   20 |    are encrypted with ``pgp_sym_encrypt`` / decrypted with ``pgp_sym_decrypt`` IN
   21 |    THE DATABASE, keyed by the same ``PDN_KEY`` secret. See ``access/pgstore.py``;
   22 |    this module supplies the Python-side counterpart + key for the sqlite dev store
   23 |    and as a fallback.
   24 | 2. **AES-256-GCM** (AEAD) via the ``cryptography`` lib when importable — the
   25 |    preferred Python-side cipher.  Key = HKDF-SHA256(PDN_KEY, info="pdn:<keyid>").
   26 | 3. **dev fallback** (stdlib only, when ``cryptography`` is absent) — a clearly
   27 |    marked, keyed HMAC-SHA256 keystream-XOR transform.  It makes the stored value
   28 |    ciphertext at rest (a raw DB dump no longer reveals the name) but is **NOT an
   29 |    AEAD and NOT a certified СКЗИ** — it exists so dev/CI without ``cryptography``
   30 |    still stores ciphertext and the suite stays green.  Production uses pgcrypto
   31 |    (PG) or AES-GCM (with ``cryptography`` installed); for УЗ-2 a certified ФСТЭК
   32 |    СКЗИ provider replaces this layer (SPEC_pki_keys §4.1).
   33 | 
   34 | Key + rotation (env-driven; no secret in code)
   35 | ----------------------------------------------
   36 | The key secret comes from the environment — ``PDN_KEY`` by default, never a
   37 | literal in the repo (BEST_PRACTICES §6; prod sources it from Vault/SOPS, not env).
   38 | Every ciphertext token embeds a **key id** so keys can be ROTATED without a bulk
   39 | re-encrypt (SPEC_pki_keys §2 "version, not replace"):
   40 | 
```

<!-- ─── страница 230 ─── -->

```py
   41 |   * the active key id is ``PDN_KEY_ID`` (default ``'1'``); its secret is the env
   42 |     var ``PDN_KEY`` (or ``PDN_KEY_<id>`` for a specific version);
   43 |   * a token written under key id ``k`` is ``pdn:v1:<backend>:<k>:<payload>``;
   44 |   * ``decrypt`` reads the token's own key id and looks up ``PDN_KEY_<that id>``
   45 |     (falling back to ``PDN_KEY``), so old-version ciphertext stays readable in the
   46 |     grace window while NEW writes use the active id — lazy re-encrypt on the next
   47 |     upsert.  To rotate: set ``PDN_KEY_ID=2`` + ``PDN_KEY_2=<new secret>`` (keep
   48 |     ``PDN_KEY_1`` for reads), redeploy; rows re-wrap as they are next written.
   49 | 
   50 | Transparency
   51 | ------------
   52 | ``decrypt`` is tolerant: a value that is NOT a recognized ``pdn:`` token (e.g. a
   53 | legacy plaintext row written before this landed, or an empty string) is returned
   54 | unchanged.  ``encrypt('')`` / ``encrypt(None)`` return the input unchanged (no
   55 | point ciphering an empty PII slot).  So the cipher is transparent to callers and
   56 | safe to roll out over a store that already has plaintext rows.
   57 | """
   58 | import base64
   59 | import hashlib
   60 | import hmac
   61 | import os
   62 | 
   63 | # Token wire format:  pdn:<fmt_ver>:<backend>:<key_id>:<b64payload>
   64 | _PREFIX = 'pdn'
   65 | _FMT_VER = 'v1'
   66 | _B_GCM = 'gcm'        # AES-256-GCM (cryptography lib)
   67 | _B_DEV = 'dev'        # stdlib HMAC keystream XOR (dev/CI fallback)
   68 | 
   69 | # Env knobs (overridable). PDN_KEY holds the active secret; PDN_KEY_ID names the
   70 | # active key version; PDN_KEY_<id> can hold a specific version's secret for reads.
   71 | _ENV_SECRET = 'PDN_KEY'
   72 | _ENV_KEY_ID = 'PDN_KEY_ID'
   73 | _DEFAULT_KEY_ID = '1'
   74 | 
   75 | # A documented dev default so the suite + a fresh dev box work with zero config.
   76 | # This is NOT a production secret: prod MUST set PDN_KEY from Vault/SOPS. The value
   77 | # being public is fine for the dev fallback (it only protects a dev sqlite file).
   78 | _DEV_DEFAULT_SECRET = 'irbis-dev-pdn-key-not-for-production'
   79 | 
   80 | 
   81 | def active_key_id():
   82 |     """The key id NEW ciphertext is written under (env ``PDN_KEY_ID``, default '1')."""
   83 |     return (os.environ.get(_ENV_KEY_ID) or _DEFAULT_KEY_ID).strip() or _DEFAULT_KEY_ID
   84 | 
   85 | 
   86 | def _secret_for(key_id):
   87 |     """Resolve the raw secret bytes for ``key_id``.
   88 | 
   89 |     Looks up ``PDN_KEY_<id>`` first (per-version, for rotation), then the generic
   90 |     ``PDN_KEY``, then the documented dev default.  Returns bytes."""
   91 |     val = os.environ.get('%s_%s' % (_ENV_SECRET, key_id)) or os.environ.get(_ENV_SECRET)
   92 |     if not val:
   93 |         val = _DEV_DEFAULT_SECRET
   94 |     return val.encode('utf-8')
   95 |
```

```py
   96 | 
   97 | def _derive(secret, key_id, length=32):
   98 |     """Derive a category key from the env secret bound to the key id.
   99 | 
  100 |     HKDF-SHA256 (RFC 5869) with ``info='pdn:<key_id>'`` so the derived key is
  101 |     domain-separated per category/version (SPEC_pki_keys §1.2 — HKDF on L2 with
  102 |     info-binding is the sanctioned use). Stdlib-only via ``hashlib``."""
  103 |     info = ('%s:%s' % (_PREFIX, key_id)).encode('utf-8')
  104 |     # HKDF-Extract then HKDF-Expand (single block: length<=32 for SHA-256).
  105 |     salt = b'\x00' * hashlib.sha256().digest_size
  106 |     prk = hmac.new(salt, secret, hashlib.sha256).digest()
  107 |     okm = hmac.new(prk, info + b'\x01', hashlib.sha256).digest()
  108 |     return okm[:length]
  109 | 
  110 | 
  111 | def _has_cryptography():
  112 |     try:
  113 |         import cryptography  # noqa: F401
  114 |         from cryptography.hazmat.primitives.ciphers.aead import AESGCM  # noqa: F401
  115 |         return True
  116 |     except Exception:
  117 |         return False
  118 | 
  119 | 
  120 | def backend():
  121 |     """The Python-side backend that ``encrypt`` will use: 'gcm' or 'dev'.
  122 | 
  123 |     'gcm' (AES-256-GCM) when the ``cryptography`` lib is importable, else the
  124 |     stdlib 'dev' fallback. (The PG store additionally uses pgcrypto in-database;
  125 |     this names the in-process Python cipher.)"""
  126 |     return _B_GCM if _has_cryptography() else _B_DEV
  127 | 
  128 | 
  129 | def aead_available():
  130 |     """True iff a real AEAD (AES-GCM) backend is available in this environment.
  131 | 
  132 |     Tests use this to SKIP-WITH-NOTE the strong-cipher leg (and exercise the dev
  133 |     fallback instead) rather than fail CI when ``cryptography`` is not installed."""
  134 |     return _has_cryptography()
  135 | 
  136 | 
  137 | def is_token(value):
  138 |     """True iff ``value`` looks like one of our ciphertext tokens (``pdn:v1:…``)."""
  139 |     return isinstance(value, str) and value.startswith(_PREFIX + ':' + _FMT_VER + ':')
  140 | 
  141 | 
  142 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  143 | # AES-256-GCM (AEAD) — preferred Python cipher when `cryptography` is present.
  144 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  145 | def _gcm_encrypt(plaintext, key_id):
  146 |     from cryptography.hazmat.primitives.ciphers.aead import AESGCM
  147 |     key = _derive(_secret_for(key_id), key_id, 32)
  148 |     nonce = os.urandom(12)
  149 |     ct = AESGCM(key).encrypt(nonce, plaintext.encode('utf-8'), None)
  150 |     payload = base64.b64encode(nonce + ct).decode('ascii')
```

```py
  154 | def _gcm_decrypt(payload, key_id):
  155 |     from cryptography.hazmat.primitives.ciphers.aead import AESGCM
  156 |     key = _derive(_secret_for(key_id), key_id, 32)
  157 |     raw = base64.b64decode(payload)

  160 | 
  161 | 
  162 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  163 | # Dev fallback (stdlib only) — keyed HMAC-SHA256 keystream XOR. NOT an AEAD and
  164 | # NOT a certified СКЗИ; makes the stored value ciphertext-at-rest so a raw dump
  165 | # does not reveal the name, and keeps dev/CI green without `cryptography`.
  166 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  167 | def _dev_keystream(key, nonce, n):
  168 |     """CTR-style keystream: HMAC-SHA256(key, nonce||counter) blocks, truncated."""
  169 |     out = bytearray()
  170 |     counter = 0
  171 |     while len(out) < n:
  172 |         block = hmac.new(key, nonce + counter.to_bytes(8, 'big'), hashlib.sha256).digest()
  173 |         out.extend(block)
  174 |         counter += 1
  175 |     return bytes(out[:n])
  176 | 
  177 | 
  178 | def _dev_encrypt(plaintext, key_id):
  179 |     key = _derive(_secret_for(key_id), key_id, 32)
  180 |     data = plaintext.encode('utf-8')
  181 |     nonce = os.urandom(16)
  182 |     ks = _dev_keystream(key, nonce, len(data))
  183 |     ct = bytes(a ^ b for a, b in zip(data, ks))
  184 |     # Bind a keyed tag over (nonce||ct) so tampering/empty payloads are detected
  185 |     # on decrypt (tamper-evident, though not a true AEAD).
  186 |     tag = hmac.new(key, nonce + ct, hashlib.sha256).digest()[:16]
  187 |     payload = base64.b64encode(nonce + tag + ct).decode('ascii')
  188 |     return _join(_B_DEV, key_id, payload)
  189 | 
  190 | 
  191 | def _dev_decrypt(payload, key_id):
  192 |     key = _derive(_secret_for(key_id), key_id, 32)
  193 |     raw = base64.b64decode(payload)
  194 |     nonce, tag, ct = raw[:16], raw[16:32], raw[32:]
  195 |     expect = hmac.new(key, nonce + ct, hashlib.sha256).digest()[:16]
  196 |     if not hmac.compare_digest(tag, expect):
  197 |         raise ValueError('pdn dev-cipher tag mismatch (wrong key or tampered)')
  198 |     ks = _dev_keystream(key, nonce, len(ct))
  199 |     return bytes(a ^ b for a, b in zip(ct, ks)).decode('utf-8')
  200 | 
  201 | 
  202 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  203 | # Public seam.
  204 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
```

```py
  209 | def _split(token):

  216 | 
  217 | def encrypt(plaintext):
  218 |     """Encrypt a PII string to a self-describing ciphertext token.
  219 | 
  220 |     Empty / None passes through unchanged (no point ciphering an empty slot, and
  221 |     it keeps NULL columns NULL). Uses AES-GCM when available, else the dev
  222 |     fallback; the active key id is embedded for rotation. Idempotent-safe: an
  223 |     already-encrypted token is returned as-is (never double-wrapped)."""

  225 |         return plaintext
  226 |     if is_token(plaintext):
  227 |         return plaintext
  228 |     key_id = active_key_id()

  230 |         return _gcm_encrypt(plaintext, key_id)
  231 |     return _dev_encrypt(plaintext, key_id)

  233 | 
  234 | def decrypt(value):

  236 | 
  237 |     A value that is not a recognized ``pdn:`` token (legacy plaintext, empty,
  238 |     None) is returned unchanged, so callers can decrypt unconditionally and roll
  239 |     out over a store that still holds plaintext rows. The token's own key id +
  240 |     backend select the secret/cipher, so rotated/old-version ciphertext still
  241 |     reads."""
  242 |     if not is_token(value):
  243 |         return value
  244 |     b, key_id, payload = _split(value)
  245 |     if b == _B_GCM:
  246 |         return _gcm_decrypt(payload, key_id)
  247 |     if b == _B_DEV:
  248 |         return _dev_decrypt(payload, key_id)
  249 |     raise ValueError('unknown pdn backend %r' % b)
  250 |

  255 | # bound parameter (never interpolated). ``pgcrypto`` ships with PostgreSQL
  256 | # (CREATE EXTENSION pgcrypto) — the store creates it best-effort.
  257 | def pg_key():
  258 |     """The symmetric key string handed to pgcrypto pgp_sym_encrypt/decrypt.
  259 | 
  260 |     Bound to the active key id via the same env secret so PG and Python agree on
```

```jsx
  111 |         toast({ variant: "success", title: "Вход выполнен", message: r.json.data.name || login });
  112 |       } else toast({ variant: "warning", title: "Неверный логин или пароль", message: "Проверьте учётные данные." });
  113 |     }
  114 | 
  115 |     function recView(d) {
  116 |       const sub = (f, c) => (f.subfields[c] || f.subfields[c.toLowerCase()] || f.subfields[c.toUpperCase()] || "");
  117 |       const F = (tag) => d.fields.filter((x) => x.tag === tag);
  118 |       const F1 = (tag) => F(tag)[0];
  119 | 
  120 |       // авторы 700/701/702 -> "Фамилия, Имя"
  121 |       const authors = ["700", "701", "702"].flatMap(F).map((f) => {
  122 |         const s = sub(f, "A"), g = sub(f, "G") || sub(f, "B");
  123 |         return (s + (g ? ", " + g : "")).trim();
  124 |       }).filter(Boolean);
  125 |       // организации-авторы 710/711
  126 |       const orgs = ["710", "711"].flatMap(F).map((f) => sub(f, "A")).filter(Boolean);
  127 | 
  128 |       const imprint = (() => { const f = F1("210"); if (!f) return ""; const a = sub(f, "A"), c = sub(f, "C"), y = sub(f, "D"); return [a, c].filter(Boolean).join(" : ") + (y ? (a || c ? ", " : "") + y : ""); })();
  129 |       const volume = (() => { const f = F1("215"); if (!f) return ""; return [sub(f, "A"), sub(f, "C"), sub(f, "E")].filter(Boolean).join(" ; "); })();
  130 |       const isbn = (() => { const f = F1("10"); return f ? sub(f, "A") : ""; })();
  131 |       const langCode = (() => { const f = F1("101"); return f ? (sub(f, "A") || f.value) : ""; })();
  132 |       const lang = LANG[langCode] || langCode;
  133 |       const udk = (F1("675") && sub(F1("675"), "A")) || (F1("675") || {}).value || "";
  134 |       const note = (F1("331") && sub(F1("331"), "A")) || (F1("330") && sub(F1("330"), "A")) || (F1("300") || {}).value || "";
  135 | 
  136 |       const meta = [
  137 |         { label: "Авторы", value: authors.concat(orgs).join("; ") },
  138 |         { label: "Выходные данные", value: imprint },
  139 |         { label: "Объём", value: volume },
  140 |         { label: "ISBN", value: isbn },
  141 |         { label: "Язык", value: lang },
  142 |         { label: "УДК", value: udk },
  143 |         { label: "Примечание", value: note },
  144 |       ].filter((r) => r.value);
  145 | 
  146 |       const subjects = F("606").map((f) => [sub(f, "A"), sub(f, "B"), sub(f, "C"), sub(f, "D")].filter(Boolean).join(" — ")).filter(Boolean);
  147 |       const holds = F("910").map((h) => ({ loc: sub(h, "D") || "Фонд", inv: sub(h, "B"), st: sub(h, "A") }));
  148 |       const files = ["951", "955"].flatMap(F).map((f) => sub(f, "A") || sub(f, "T")).filter(Boolean);
  149 |       const rawRows = d.fields.map((x) =>
  150 |         `<tr><td style="color:var(--text-subtle);font-family:var(--font-mono);padding-right:12px;vertical-align:top">${x.tag}</td><td style="font-family:var(--font-mono);font-size:12px">${esc(x.value)}</td></tr>`).join("");
  151 |       return { brief: d.brief || "", meta, subjects, holds, files, rawRows };
  152 |     }
  153 | 
  154 |     const rootTheme = a11y ? "a11y" : (theme === "working" ? undefined : theme);
  155 |     const pageCount = Math.max(1, Math.ceil(total / pageSize));
  156 |     const hbtn = (active) => ({ background: active ? "rgba(255,255,255,.25)" : "transparent", color: "#fff", border: "1px solid rgba(255,255,255,.45)", borderRadius: 8, padding: "5px 10px", cursor: "pointer", fontSize: "var(--text-xs)" });
  157 | 
  158 |     if (!ready) return <div style={{ padding: 40, color: "var(--text-subtle)" }}>Загрузка каталога…</div>;
  159 | 
  160 |     return (
  161 |       <div data-theme={rootTheme} style={{ minHeight: "100vh", background: "var(--bg-page)", color: "var(--text-body)", display: "flex", flexDirection: "column" }}>
  162 |         <header style={{ background: "var(--accent)", color: "#fff", padding: "12px 20px", display: "flex", alignItems: "center", gap: 12 }}>
```

```jsx
  188 |                   {PREFIXES.map((p) => <option key={p.code} value={p.code}>{p.label}</option>)}
  189 |                 </select>
  190 |                 <div style={{ flex: 1 }}>
  191 |                   <SearchBar value={q} onChange={onQuery} onSearch={(v) => runSearch(prefix, v, 1)}
  192 |                     suggestions={sug} onPickSuggestion={(s) => { const term = typeof s === "string" ? s : s.term; setQ(term); runSearch(prefix, term, 1); }}
  193 |                     placeholder="Поиск по каталогу" buttonLabel="Найти" />
  194 |                 </div>
  195 |               </div>
  196 | 
  197 |               {loading ? <div style={{ color: "var(--text-subtle)" }}>Поиск…</div> :
  198 |                 total === 0 ? <EmptyState icon="search" title="Ничего не найдено" description="Измените запрос или область поиска." /> :
  199 |                   <React.Fragment>
  200 |                     <div style={{ color: "var(--text-subtle)", fontSize: "var(--text-sm)", margin: "4px 0 10px" }}>Найдено: {total} · страница {page} из {pageCount}</div>
  201 |                     {items.map((it) => <ResultCard key={it.mfn} item={it} dbTag="Книги" typeIcon="book" onOpen={() => openRecord(it.mfn)} />)}
  202 |                     <div style={{ marginTop: 14 }}><Pagination page={page} pageCount={pageCount} total={total} onPage={(p) => runSearch(prefix, q, p)} /></div>
  203 |                   </React.Fragment>}
  204 |             </React.Fragment>
  205 |           )}
  206 | 
  207 |           {rec && (() => {
  208 |             const v = recView(rec);
  209 |             const lbl = { fontWeight: 600, fontSize: "var(--text-sm)", margin: "18px 0 8px" };
  210 |             return (
  211 |               <div>
  212 |                 <Button iconLeft="arrow-left" onClick={() => { setRec(null); setShowRaw(false); }}>К результатам</Button>
  213 |                 <div style={{ display: "flex", gap: 24, marginTop: 12, alignItems: "flex-start", flexWrap: "wrap" }}>
  214 |                   {rec.hasCover && <img alt="обложка" src={API.coverUrl(DB, rec.mfn)} onError={(e) => { e.target.style.display = "none"; }} style={{ width: 180, borderRadius: 10, border: "1px solid var(--border-subtle)", boxShadow: "var(--shadow-sm)" }} />}
  215 |                   <div style={{ flex: 1, minWidth: 300 }}>
  216 |                     {/* заголовок — серверное БО (PFT) */}
  217 |                     <h2 style={{ fontFamily: "var(--font-serif, inherit)", fontSize: "var(--text-2xl, 1.5rem)", lineHeight: 1.3, margin: "2px 0 4px" }}>{v.brief}</h2>
```

```jsx
  279 |   function LoginOverlay({ onClose, onSubmit }) {
  280 |     const [t, setT] = React.useState("");
  281 |     return (
  282 |       <div onClick={onClose} style={{ position: "fixed", inset: 0, background: "rgba(20,16,14,.45)", display: "flex", alignItems: "center", justifyContent: "center", zIndex: 50 }}>
  283 |         <div onClick={(e) => e.stopPropagation()} style={{ background: "var(--surface-card, #fff)", color: "var(--text-body)", borderRadius: 16, padding: 22, width: 320, boxShadow: "var(--shadow-lg, 0 20px 50px rgba(0,0,0,.25))" }}>
  284 |           <div style={{ fontWeight: 600, fontSize: "var(--text-lg)", marginBottom: 8 }}>Вход в личный кабинет</div>
  285 |           <p style={{ margin: "0 0 12px", color: "var(--text-subtle)", fontSize: "var(--text-sm)" }}>Войдите по номеру читательского билета.</p>
  286 |           <input value={t} onChange={(e) => setT(e.target.value)} placeholder="Номер билета"
  287 |             onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === "Enter") onSubmit(t); }}
  288 |             style={{ width: "100%", boxSizing: "border-box", padding: "9px 12px", borderRadius: 8, border: "1px solid var(--border-strong, #cdd3da)", marginBottom: 12 }} />
  289 |           <div style={{ display: "flex", gap: 8, justifyContent: "flex-end" }}>
  290 |             <NS.Button variant="ghost" onClick={onClose}>Отмена</NS.Button>
  291 |             <NS.Button onClick={() => onSubmit(t)}>Войти</NS.Button>
  292 |           </div>
  293 |         </div>
  294 |       </div>
  295 |     );
  296 |   }

  298 |   // ===================== Staff side (live) =====================
  299 |   const DOMAINS = [
  300 |     { id: "cataloging", label: "Каталогизация", icon: "book", grant: "record.write", desc: "Создание и правка библиографических записей", route: "cataloging" },
  301 |     { id: "circ", label: "Книговыдача", icon: "package", grant: "circ.issue", desc: "Выдача, возврат, очередь, бронеполка, ячейки", route: "stub" },
  302 |     { id: "acq", label: "Комплектование", icon: "archive", grant: "acq.receipt", desc: "Заказ, поступление, КСУ, списание", route: "stub" },
  303 |     { id: "inv", label: "Инвентаризация", icon: "scan-line", grant: "record.read", desc: "Сверка фонда с ТСД", route: "stub" },
  304 |     { id: "analytics", label: "Аналитика", icon: "bar-chart", grant: "record.read", desc: "BI-дашборды: выдачи, фонд, читатели", route: "stub" },
  305 |     { id: "admin", label: "Администрирование", icon: "sliders", grant: "admin.users", desc: "Учётки, гранты, роли, аудит", route: "stub" },
  306 |   ];
  307 |   const hasGrant = (grants, fn) => (grants || []).some((g) => g.function === fn);
  308 | 
  309 |   function emptyValues(wl) {
  310 |     const v = {};
  311 |     (wl || []).forEach((fd) => { v[fd.code] = fd.repeatable ? [] : (fd.subfields ? {} : ""); });
  312 |     return v;
  313 |   }
  314 |   function valuesToFields(wl, values) {
  315 |     const out = [];
  316 |     (wl || []).forEach((fd) => {
  317 |       const v = values[fd.code];
  318 |       const occs = fd.repeatable ? (Array.isArray(v) ? v : []) : [v];
  319 |       occs.forEach((occ) => {
  320 |         let str = "";
  321 |         if (fd.subfields) { if (occ && typeof occ === "object") str = fd.subfields.map((sf) => { const t = (occ[sf.code] || "").trim(); return t ? "^" + sf.code + t : ""; }).join(""); }
  322 |         else str = (occ || "").toString().trim();
  323 |         if (str) out.push({ tag: fd.code, value: str });
  324 |       });
  325 |     });
  326 |     return out;
  327 |   }
```

```jsx
  328 |   function recordToValues(fields, wl) {
  329 |     const values = {};
  330 |     const pick = (f, c) => f.subfields[c] || f.subfields[c.toUpperCase()] || f.subfields[c.toLowerCase()] || "";
  331 |     (wl || []).forEach((fd) => {
  332 |       const matches = fields.filter((f) => f.tag === fd.code);
  333 |       if (fd.subfields) {
  334 |         const toObj = (f) => { const o = {}; fd.subfields.forEach((sf) => { o[sf.code] = pick(f, sf.code); }); return o; };
  335 |         values[fd.code] = fd.repeatable ? matches.map(toObj) : (matches[0] ? toObj(matches[0]) : {});
  336 |       } else {
  337 |         const f = matches[0];
  338 |         values[fd.code] = f ? (f.text || f.value || "") : "";
  339 |       }
  340 |     });
  341 |     return values;
  342 |   }
  343 | 
  344 |   function StaffLoginOverlay({ onClose, onSubmit }) {
  345 |     const [l, setL] = React.useState("");
  346 |     const [p, setP] = React.useState("");
  347 |     const inp = { width: "100%", boxSizing: "border-box", padding: "9px 12px", borderRadius: 8, border: "1px solid var(--border-strong, #cdd3da)", marginBottom: 10 };
  348 |     return (
  349 |       <div onClick={onClose} style={{ position: "fixed", inset: 0, background: "rgba(20,16,14,.45)", display: "flex", alignItems: "center", justifyContent: "center", zIndex: 50 }}>
  350 |         <div onClick={(e) => e.stopPropagation()} style={{ background: "var(--surface-card, #fff)", color: "var(--text-body)", borderRadius: 16, padding: 22, width: 340, boxShadow: "var(--shadow-lg, 0 20px 50px rgba(0,0,0,.25))" }}>
  351 |           <div style={{ fontWeight: 600, fontSize: "var(--text-lg)", marginBottom: 8 }}>Вход сотрудника</div>
  352 |           <p style={{ margin: "0 0 12px", color: "var(--text-subtle)", fontSize: "var(--text-sm)" }}>Доступ определяется грантами учётной записи.</p>
  353 |           <input value={l} onChange={(e) => setL(e.target.value)} placeholder="Логин" style={inp} />
  354 |           <input value={p} onChange={(e) => setP(e.target.value)} type="password" placeholder="Пароль"
  355 |             onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === "Enter") onSubmit(l, p); }} style={inp} />
  356 |           <div style={{ fontSize: "var(--text-xs)", color: "var(--text-subtle)", marginBottom: 12 }}>демо: <b>admin / admin</b> · <b>librarian / librarian</b></div>
  357 |           <div style={{ display: "flex", gap: 8, justifyContent: "flex-end" }}>
  358 |             <NS.Button variant="ghost" onClick={onClose}>Отмена</NS.Button>
  359 |             <NS.Button onClick={() => onSubmit(l, p)}>Войти</NS.Button>
  360 |           </div>
  361 |         </div>
  362 |       </div>
  363 |     );
  364 |   }
  365 | 
  366 |   function StaffArea({ staff, route, setRoute, toast }) {
  367 |     if (route === "cataloging") return <CatalogingWorksheet staff={staff} onBack={() => setRoute("desktop")} toast={toast} />;
  368 |     if (route && route.name === "stub") return <StaffStub title={route.title} onBack={() => setRoute("desktop")} />;
  369 |     return <StaffDesktop staff={staff} onOpen={(d) => setRoute(d.route === "cataloging" ? "cataloging" : { name: "stub", title: d.label })} />;
  370 |   }
  371 | 
  372 |   function StaffDesktop({ staff, onOpen }) {
  373 |     const tiles = DOMAINS.filter((d) => hasGrant(staff.grants, d.grant));
  374 |     return (
  375 |       <div>
  376 |         <h2 style={{ fontSize: "var(--text-2xl,1.5rem)", margin: "4px 0 2px" }}>Рабочий стол сотрудника</h2>
  377 |         <p style={{ color: "var(--text-subtle)", fontSize: "var(--text-sm)", marginTop: 0 }}>{staff.name} · доступно задач: {tiles.length} — собрано <b>по грантам</b>, не «по АРМам».</p>
  378 |         <div style={{ display: "grid", gridTemplateColumns: "repeat(auto-fill, minmax(260px,1fr))", gap: 14, marginTop: 14 }}>
  379 |           {tiles.map((d) => (
  380 |             <div key={d.id} onClick={() => onOpen(d)} style={{ cursor: "pointer", background: "var(--surface-card,#fff)", border: "1px solid var(--border-subtle)", borderRadius: 12, padding: 16, display: "flex", gap: 12, alignItems: "flex-start" }}>
  381 |               <span style={{ background: "var(--accent-weak,#eef2f7)", color: "var(--accent)", borderRadius: 10, padding: 8, flex: "none" }}><Icon name={d.icon} size={22} /></span>
  382 |               <div>
```

```jsx
  403 |   function CatalogingWorksheet({ staff, onBack, toast }) {
  404 |     const SANDBOX = "WORK";
  405 |     const DF = NS.DynamicField;
  406 |     const [wl, setWl] = React.useState(null);
  407 |     const [values, setValues] = React.useState({});

  414 | 
  415 |     const set = (code, val) => setValues((v) => ({ ...v, [code]: val }));
  416 |     const newRecord = () => { setValues(emptyValues(wl)); setMfn(0); setSaved(null); setErrors({}); };
  417 |     async function loadFromIbis() {
  418 |       const id = parseInt(openMfn, 10); if (!id) return;
  419 |       const r = await API.record("IBIS", id);

  421 |       else toast({ variant: "warning", title: "Не найдено", message: "Нет записи MFN " + id + " в IBIS." });
  422 |     }
  423 |     async function save() {
  424 |       const errs = {};
  425 |       (wl || []).forEach((fd) => { if (fd.required) { const ok = fd.subfields ? (values[fd.code] && Object.values(values[fd.code]).some(Boolean)) : !!values[fd.code]; if (!ok) errs[fd.code] = "Обязательное поле (ФЛК)"; } });

  427 |       if (Object.keys(errs).length) { toast({ variant: "warning", title: "Заполните обязательные поля", message: "Тип записи и Заглавие." }); return; }
  428 |       const fields = valuesToFields(wl, values);
  429 |       const r = await API.saveRecord(SANDBOX, mfn, fields);
  430 |       if (r.status === 200 && r.json && r.json.ok) { setSaved(r.json.data); setMfn(r.json.data.mfn); toast({ variant: "success", title: r.json.data.created ? "Запись создана" : "Запись обновлена", message: SANDBOX + " · MFN " + r.json.data.mfn }); }
  431 |       else if (r.status === 403) toast({ variant: "info", title: "Недостаточно прав", message: "Нужен грант record.write." });
  432 |       else toast({ variant: "error", title: "Не сохранено", message: "Повторите попытку." });
  433 |     }
  434 | 
  435 |     const tbtn = { padding: "8px 12px", borderRadius: 8, border: "1px solid var(--border-strong,#cdd3da)", background: "var(--surface-card,#fff)", color: "var(--text-body)" };
  436 |     return (
  437 |       <div>
```
