```jsx
  470 |   // Fallback editor if the bundled DynamicField is unavailable.
  471 |   function BasicField({ fd, value, onChange, error }) {
  472 |     const lab = { fontSize: "var(--text-sm)", fontWeight: 600, marginBottom: 4 };
  473 |     const inp = { width: "100%", boxSizing: "border-box", padding: "8px 11px", borderRadius: 8, border: "1px solid " + (error ? "var(--status-issued,#c66)" : "var(--border-strong,#cdd3da)") };
  474 |     if (fd.subfields && !fd.repeatable) {
  475 |       const occ = value || {};
  476 |       return <div><div style={lab}>{fd.label}{fd.required ? " *" : ""}</div>
  477 |         <div style={{ display: "grid", gap: 6 }}>{fd.subfields.map((sf) => <input key={sf.code} placeholder={sf.label + " (^" + sf.code + ")"} value={occ[sf.code] || ""} onChange={(e) => onChange({ ...occ, [sf.code]: e.target.value })} style={inp} />)}</div>
  478 |         {error && <div style={{ color: "var(--status-issued,#c66)", fontSize: "var(--text-xs)" }}>{error}</div>}</div>;
  479 |     }
  480 |     if (fd.type === "menu") {
  481 |       return <div><div style={lab}>{fd.label}{fd.required ? " *" : ""}</div>
  482 |         <select value={value || ""} onChange={(e) => onChange(e.target.value)} style={inp}><option value="">—</option>{(fd.options || []).map((o) => <option key={o} value={o}>{o}</option>)}</select></div>;
  483 |     }
  484 |     return <div><div style={lab}>{fd.label}</div><input value={value || ""} onChange={(e) => onChange(e.target.value)} style={inp} /></div>;
  485 |   }
  486 | 
  487 |   window.IrbisLiveApp = App;
  488 | })();
```

### Файл: `irbis-web/backend/access/authz.py`  · строк: 36

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """Authorization: grant = (function x db x level). Pure functions, no I/O.
    3 | 
    4 | Decision (P0_BUILDKIT §4): a grant matches when function equals and db is exact or '*';
    5 | an exact-db grant outranks a '*' grant; the effective level must be >= the level needed.
    6 | Server's -3338 (CLIENT_NOT_ALLOWED) is mapped to HTTP 403 at the API edge.
    7 | """
    8 | LEVEL_RANK = {'read': 1, 'write': 2, 'admin': 3}
    9 | 
   10 | # Implicit grant sets for non-staff sessions (not stored in DB).
   11 | GUEST_GRANTS = [
   12 |     {'function': 'search', 'db': '*', 'level': 'read'},
   13 |     {'function': 'record.read', 'db': '*', 'level': 'read'},
   14 |     {'function': 'terms', 'db': '*', 'level': 'read'},
   15 |     {'function': 'file', 'db': '*', 'level': 'read'},
   16 | ]
   17 | READER_GRANTS = GUEST_GRANTS + [
   18 |     {'function': 'order', 'db': '*', 'level': 'write'},
   19 |     {'function': 'cabinet', 'db': '*', 'level': 'read'},
   20 | ]
   21 | 
   22 | 
   23 | def best_grant(grants, function, db):
   24 |     cands = [g for g in grants if g['function'] == function and g['db'] in (db, '*')]
   25 |     if not cands:
   26 |         return None
   27 |     exact = [g for g in cands if g['db'] == db]
   28 |     pool = exact or cands                      # exact db beats '*'
   29 |     return max(pool, key=lambda g: LEVEL_RANK.get(g['level'], 0))
   30 | 
   31 | 
   32 | def authorize(grants, function, db, level_needed):
   33 |     g = best_grant(grants, function, db)
   34 |     if not g:
   35 |         return False
   36 |     return LEVEL_RANK.get(g['level'], 0) >= LEVEL_RANK.get(level_needed, 99)
```

### Файл: `irbis-web/backend/access/mnu.py`  · строк: 121

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """ИРБИС ``.mnu`` / ``.tre`` parsers (gap A5, epic #188).
    3 | 
    4 | These are the import side of the seeding engine — used when migrating an
    5 | institution's own dictionaries (``kv.mnu``, ``mhr.mnu``, ``spec.tre``) off a
    6 | source ИРБИС server (SPEC §4.2). The parsers themselves are pure and fully
    7 | unit-tested; the *live-server extraction* (pulling the files over the ИРБИС
    8 | protocol / from ``C:\\IRBIS\\DATA``) is a documented stub below — its only
    9 | external dependency is the connection, which is supplied from env, never the
   10 | repo.
   11 | 
   12 | Formats (recon #VOC-03):
   13 |   * ``.mnu`` — alternating lines: ``code`` then ``label``, repeated, terminated
   14 |     by a ``*****`` marker line. CP1251 on disk. A trailing empty pair (the
   15 |     artefact left before ``*****``) is skipped.
   16 |   * ``.tre`` — one node per line; leading dots encode depth (``.`` == 1 level).
```

<!-- ─── страница 244 ─── -->

```py
   17 |     The node value follows the dots. ``parent`` is reconstructed by a level stack.
   18 | """
   19 | 
   20 | MNU_TERMINATOR = '*****'
   21 | 
   22 | 
   23 | def decode_irbis(data):
   24 |     """Decode ИРБИС file bytes (CP1251 on disk) to ``str``.
   25 | 
   26 |     Accepts ``str`` unchanged (already-decoded input). Bytes are tried as CP1251
   27 |     first (the ИРБИС on-disk encoding) and fall back to UTF-8 for files that were
   28 |     already re-encoded, so both round-trip.
   29 |     """
   30 |     if isinstance(data, str):
   31 |         return data
   32 |     try:
   33 |         return data.decode('cp1251')
   34 |     except UnicodeDecodeError:
   35 |         return data.decode('utf-8', errors='replace')
   36 | 
   37 | 
   38 | def parse_mnu(data):
   39 |     """Parse an ИРБИS ``.mnu`` menu into ``[(code, label), ...]``.
   40 | 
   41 |     ``data`` may be ``bytes`` (CP1251) or ``str``. Parsing stops at the first
   42 |     ``*****`` terminator line. Lines are paired (code, label); a final pair whose
   43 |     code is empty (the ``*****`` artefact, recon #VOC-03) is dropped. A label with
   44 |     no following partner is paired with ``''``.
   45 |     """
   46 |     text = decode_irbis(data)
   47 |     # Normalise newlines; keep interior whitespace of labels but strip line ends.
   48 |     raw = [ln.strip('\r\n') for ln in text.split('\n')]
   49 |     lines = []
   50 |     for ln in raw:
   51 |         if ln.strip() == MNU_TERMINATOR:
   52 |             break
   53 |         lines.append(ln)
   54 |     # Drop a trailing wholly-empty line (artefact before the terminator).
   55 |     while lines and lines[-1].strip() == '':
   56 |         lines.pop()
   57 |     pairs = []
   58 |     i = 0
   59 |     while i < len(lines):
   60 |         code = lines[i].strip()
   61 |         label = lines[i + 1].strip() if i + 1 < len(lines) else ''
   62 |         i += 2
   63 |         if code == '':
   64 |             continue                       # skip empty-code artefact pair (#VOC-03)
   65 |         pairs.append((code, label))
   66 |     return pairs
   67 | 
   68 | 
   69 | def parse_tre(data):
   70 |     """Parse an ИРБИS ``.tre`` tree into a flat node list with parents.
   71 | 
```

<!-- ─── страница 245 ─── -->

```py
   72 |     Returns ``[{'code', 'label', 'depth', 'parent'}, ...]`` in document order.
   73 |     Leading dots = depth (``.`` per level); the text after the dots is the node
   74 |     value (used as both code and label — recon does not split them for ``.tre``).
   75 |     ``parent`` is the index (into the returned list) of the nearest preceding node
   76 |     one level shallower, or ``None`` at the root.
   77 | 
   78 |     Blank lines and a ``*****`` terminator (if present) are ignored.
   79 |     """
   80 |     text = decode_irbis(data)
   81 |     nodes = []
   82 |     stack = []                              # stack[d] = index of last node at depth d
   83 |     for raw in text.split('\n'):
   84 |         ln = raw.strip('\r\n')
   85 |         if ln.strip() == MNU_TERMINATOR:
   86 |             break
   87 |         if ln.strip() == '':
   88 |             continue
   89 |         depth = 0
   90 |         while depth < len(ln) and ln[depth] == '.':
   91 |             depth += 1
   92 |         value = ln[depth:].strip()
   93 |         if value == '':
   94 |             continue
   95 |         idx = len(nodes)
   96 |         parent = stack[depth - 1] if depth > 0 and depth - 1 < len(stack) else None
   97 |         nodes.append({'code': value, 'label': value, 'depth': depth, 'parent': parent})
   98 |         # Truncate the stack to this depth and record us as the last node here.
   99 |         del stack[depth:]
  100 |         stack.append(idx)
  101 |     return nodes
  102 | 
  103 | 
  104 | def import_mnu_from_source(name, *, dsn=None):  # pragma: no cover - documented stub
  105 |     """STUB: pull dictionary ``name`` off a *source* ИРБИС server and parse it.
  106 | 
  107 |     The primary onboarding path (SPEC §2.3) auto-imports institution
  108 |     dictionaries from the ИРБИS the tenant is migrating off — by reading the
  109 |     ``.mnu`` file over the protocol (FILE command ``L`` / ``EXPORTMNU``) or from
  110 |     the server's data directory. Connection + credentials come from the onboarding
  111 |     env/config (``SOURCE_IRBIS_*``), NEVER from the repository.
  112 | 
  113 |     Not wired here: the live transport is out of scope for this slice. Once a
  114 |     connection is supplied, the bytes flow straight through ``parse_mnu`` above —
  115 |     which is the part that is implemented and tested. Raises to make the missing
  116 |     piece explicit.
  117 |     """
  118 |     raise NotImplementedError(
  119 |         'live source-ИРБИС extraction is a documented stub (SPEC §2.3); '
  120 |         'supply file bytes to parse_mnu()/parse_tre() instead. Source-server '
  121 |         'credentials come from SOURCE_IRBIS_* env, never the repo.')
```

```py
    6 | ROLE_GRANTS = {
    7 |     'reader-service': [
    8 |         ('search', '*', 'read'), ('record.read', '*', 'read'), ('terms', '*', 'read'),
    9 |         ('file', '*', 'read'), ('order', '*', 'write'), ('cabinet', '*', 'read'),
   10 |         ('circ.issue', '*', 'write'), ('circ.return', '*', 'write'),
   11 |     ],
   12 |     'cataloger': [
   13 |         ('search', '*', 'read'), ('record.read', '*', 'read'), ('terms', '*', 'read'),
   14 |         ('file', '*', 'read'), ('record.write', 'IBIS', 'write'), ('cat.gbl', 'IBIS', 'write'),
   15 |     ],
   16 |     'administrator': [
   17 |         ('search', '*', 'read'), ('record.read', '*', 'read'), ('record.write', '*', 'write'),
   18 |         ('record.delete', '*', 'admin'), ('terms', '*', 'read'), ('file', '*', 'read'),
   19 |         ('order', '*', 'write'), ('cabinet', '*', 'read'),
   20 |         ('circ.issue', '*', 'write'), ('circ.return', '*', 'write'),
   21 |         ('admin.db', '*', 'admin'), ('admin.users', '*', 'admin'),
   22 |         ('acq.receipt', '*', 'write'), ('acq.read', '*', 'read'),
   23 |         ('bp.write', '*', 'write'), ('bp.read', '*', 'read'),
   24 |         ('cat.gbl', '*', 'write'),

   27 | 
   28 | # login -> (password, full_name, [roles])
   29 | ACCOUNTS = {
   30 |     'admin':     ('admin', 'Администратор (демо)', ['administrator']),
   31 |     'librarian': ('librarian', 'Библиотекарь (демо)', ['reader-service', 'cataloger']),
   32 | }
   33 | 
   34 | 
   35 | def seed(store):
   36 |     role_ids = {}
   37 |     for name, grants in ROLE_GRANTS.items():
   38 |         rid = store.add_role(name)
   39 |         role_ids[name] = rid
   40 |         for fn, db, lvl in grants:
   41 |             store.add_role_grant(rid, fn, db, lvl)
   42 |     for login, (pw, full, roles) in ACCOUNTS.items():
   43 |         acc = store.create_account(login, pw, full)
   44 |         for rn in roles:
   45 |             store.assign_role(acc['id'], role_ids[rn])
   46 |     return {'roles': list(role_ids), 'accounts': list(ACCOUNTS)}
   47 | 
   48 | 
   49 | if __name__ == '__main__':
   50 |     import sys, os
   51 |     sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))))
   52 |     from config import Config
   53 |     from access.store import AccessStore
   54 |     cfg = Config()
   55 |     st = AccessStore(cfg.access_db)
   56 |     print('seeded:', seed(st))
```

```py
    5 | ``docs/recon/deep/reference/format/CONTROL_VOCABULARIES.{md,csv}`` — the
    6 | vendor-standard (DB=Deposit/RQST) ИРБИС control dictionaries that every tenant
    7 | must have for ФЛК-by-dictionary and code→label rendering to work.
    8 | 
    9 | This is a *first slice*, not the full 940-value catalog: a representative system
   10 | set that proves the seeding design end-to-end.

   12 |   * ``jz.mnu``           — languages (10, representative subset of 108)
   13 |   * ``str.mnu``          — countries (10, representative subset of 149)
   14 |   * ``vd.mnu``           — document types (10, representative subset of 54)
   15 |   * ``ste.mnu``          — exemplar statuses (14, FULL — drives circulation ws3)
   16 |   * ``reservstatus.mnu`` — reservation statuses (5, FULL — drives the hold shelf)
   17 | 
   18 | Institution-specific dictionaries are listed with ``kind='institution'`` and NO
   19 | values: they are created EMPTY at provision time and populated by the library
   20 | (import from source ИРБИС, or manual entry in the ws5 editor). See SPEC §2.3.
   21 | 
   22 | ``SEED_VERSION`` is the global seed epoch; it stamps ``vocabulary.seed_version``
   23 | on every seeded row so a future ``reseed`` (SPEC §3.3) can three-way-merge.
```

```py
  115 | }
  116 | 
  117 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  118 | # INSTITUTION dictionaries: kind='institution', seeded EMPTY (seed_version=NULL).
  119 | # Created so the tenant has the metadata row + a field hint; the library fills
  120 | # the values (import from source ИРБИС §2.3, or the ws5 editor). `kv.mnu` empty
  121 | # is the literal onboarding blocker this engine unblocks.
  122 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  123 | INSTITUTION_VOCABS = {
  124 |     'kv.mnu': {'title': 'Места обслуживания / выдачи (МВ)', 'field_hint': 'RQST'},
  125 |     'mhr.mnu': {'title': 'Места хранения (МХ)', 'field_hint': '910^D'},
  126 | }
  127 | 
  128 | # Institution-specific TREE dictionaries: created EMPTY (kind='institution').
  129 | INSTITUTION_TREES = {
  130 |     'spec.tre': {'title': 'Специальности (дерево)', 'field_hint': '—'},
  131 | }
  132 | 
  133 | 
  134 | def system_catalog_rows():
  135 |     """Rows for ``control.seed_catalog`` for the SYSTEM dictionaries.
  136 | 
  137 |     Yields (name, code, label, kind, seed_version, title, field_hint, sort).
  138 |     The catalog holds both the per-vocab metadata (carried on every value row's
  139 |     title/field_hint) and the value pairs — a flat master copy that
  140 |     ``seed_vocabularies`` reads to populate a tenant.
  141 |     """
  142 |     for name, spec in SYSTEM_VOCABS.items():
  143 |         for sort, (code, label) in enumerate(spec['values']):
  144 |             yield (name, code, label, 'system', SEED_VERSION,
  145 |                    spec['title'], spec['field_hint'], sort)
  146 | 
  147 | 
  148 | def all_vocab_meta():
  149 |     """All vocab metadata rows: (name, title, kind, field_hint, seed_version).
  150 | 
  151 |     System vocabs carry ``seed_version``; institution vocabs carry NULL.
  152 |     """
  153 |     for name, spec in SYSTEM_VOCABS.items():
  154 |         yield (name, spec['title'], 'system', spec['field_hint'], SEED_VERSION)
  155 |     for name, spec in INSTITUTION_VOCABS.items():
  156 |         yield (name, spec['title'], 'institution', spec['field_hint'], None)
```

```py
    2 | """Vocabulary seeding orchestration (gap A5, epic #188).
    3 | 
    4 | Two halves:
    5 | 
    6 |   1. ``ensure_seed_catalog(dsn)`` — populate ``control.seed_catalog`` (the master
    7 |      copy of the SYSTEM dictionaries) from ``seed_data.py``. PostgreSQL only; runs
    8 |      once per control plane, idempotent (upsert on (vocab, code)). Called inside
    9 |      provisioning before a tenant is seeded.
   10 | 
   11 |   2. ``seed_vocabularies(store, dsn=...)`` — populate ONE tenant's schema:
```

```py
   14 |        * INSTITUTION vocabs/trees: create EMPTY metadata rows (no values) so the

   23 | from . import seed_data
   24 | 
   25 | 
   26 | def ensure_seed_catalog(dsn=None):
   27 |     """Fill ``control.seed_catalog`` from ``seed_data.SYSTEM_VOCABS`` (idempotent).
   28 | 
   29 |     PostgreSQL only — the control plane holds the master copy. Imported lazily so
   30 |     the sqlite dev path (no psycopg) never touches it.
   31 |     """
   32 |     from .pgstore import default_pg_dsn, _admin_conn, ensure_control_schema
   33 |     dsn = dsn or default_pg_dsn()
   34 |     ensure_control_schema(dsn)              # creates control.seed_catalog if absent
   35 |     conn = _admin_conn(dsn)
   36 |     try:
   37 |         for (name, code, label, kind, version, title, hint, sort) in \
   38 |                 seed_data.system_catalog_rows():
   39 |             conn.execute(
   40 |                 'INSERT INTO control.seed_catalog'
   41 |                 '(vocab,code,label,kind,seed_version,title,field_hint,sort) '
   42 |                 'VALUES(%s,%s,%s,%s,%s,%s,%s,%s) '
   43 |                 'ON CONFLICT(vocab,code) DO UPDATE SET '
   44 |                 'label=EXCLUDED.label, title=EXCLUDED.title, '
   45 |                 'field_hint=EXCLUDED.field_hint, sort=EXCLUDED.sort, '
   46 |                 'seed_version=EXCLUDED.seed_version',
   47 |                 (name, code, label, kind, version, title, hint, sort))
   48 |     finally:
   49 |         conn.close()
   50 | 
   51 | 
   52 | def _seed_institution_empties(store):
   53 |     """Create EMPTY metadata rows for institution dictionaries + trees.
   54 | 
   55 |     The library fills the values later (import/manual). seed_version stays NULL —
   56 |     this is what makes ``kv.mnu`` exist-but-empty rather than missing, so the UI can
   57 |     prompt "fill me in" and reseed knows to leave it alone.
   58 |     """
   59 |     for name, spec in seed_data.INSTITUTION_VOCABS.items():
   60 |         store.upsert_vocabulary(name, spec['title'], 'institution',
   61 |                                 spec.get('field_hint'), None)
   62 | 
   63 |

   67 |     ``from_catalog``:
   68 |       * ``True``  — read SYSTEM values from ``control.seed_catalog`` (PG path).
```

```py
  124 |     ``path`` of codes, then upserts. Used by the import pipeline (SPEC §4.2).
```

```py
   53 |     def _ensure_system(self, ticket):
   54 |         """Seed the two system lists for a reader if not present (idempotent)."""
   55 |         have = {r['id'] for r in self.store.shelf_lists(ticket)}
   56 |         for list_id, name in SYSTEM_LISTS:

   60 |     # ---- operations ------------------------------------------------------- #
   61 |     def lists(self, ticket):
   62 |         """All of a reader's lists (system seeded lazily), each with its items."""
   63 |         self._ensure_system(ticket)
   64 |         out = []
   65 |         for lst in self.store.shelf_lists(ticket):
   66 |             items = [{'db': it['db'], 'mfn': it['mfn'],
   67 |                       'title': it.get('title') or ('MFN %d' % it['mfn'])}
   68 |                      for it in self.store.shelf_items(ticket, lst['id'])]
   69 |             out.append({'id': lst['id'], 'name': lst['name'],
   70 |                         'system': bool(lst['system']), 'items': items})

   73 |     def create(self, ticket, name):
   74 |         """Create a custom list with an auto-assigned id. Returns ``{'id','name'}``."""

   76 |         name = (name or '').strip() or 'Список'
   77 |         list_id = self.store.shelf_next_custom_id(ticket)
   78 |         self.store.shelf_create(ticket, list_id, name, system=0)
   79 |         return {'id': list_id, 'name': name}

   81 |     def add_item(self, ticket, list_id, db, mfn):
   82 |         """Add (db,mfn) to ``list_id`` (deduped). Returns ``{'listId'}`` or None
   83 |         when the list doesn't exist for this reader."""
   84 |         self._ensure_system(ticket)
   85 |         if self.store.shelf_get(ticket, list_id) is None:
   86 |             return None
   87 |         title = self._title(db, mfn)
   88 |         self.store.shelf_add_item(ticket, list_id, db, mfn, title)
   89 |         return {'listId': list_id}
   90 | 
   91 |     def remove_item(self, ticket, list_id, db, mfn):
   92 |         """Remove (db,mfn) from ``list_id``. Returns ``{'listId'}`` or None when the
   93 |         list doesn't exist for this reader."""
   94 |         self._ensure_system(ticket)
   95 |         if self.store.shelf_get(ticket, list_id) is None:
   96 |             return None
   97 |         self.store.shelf_remove_item(ticket, list_id, db, mfn)
   98 |         return {'listId': list_id}
```

### Файл: `irbis-web/backend/access/social.py`  · строк: 334

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """Reader-portal v2 social layer (#134 engagement / #133 discovery) — BACKEND.
    3 | 
    4 | Reader-scoped reviews + ratings, reading history, saved searches, and
    5 | content-based recommendations — all persisted in OUR access store
    6 | (``reader_review`` / ``reader_history`` / ``saved_search`` tables), reader-scoped
    7 | by the RDR ticket (RI=). NOTHING here is written to the live ИРБИС server: the
    8 | catalog is only ever READ through injected seams to compute "similar" / "for you"
    9 | candidates (the same posture as ``holds.py`` / ``shelves.py``, #222).
   10 | 
   11 | This module is pure domain logic over the store + three small injected seams,
   12 | mirroring the holds/shelves pattern:
   13 | 
   14 |   * ``read_terms(db, mfn) -> {'subjects': [...], 'authors': [...],
   15 |     'collectives': [...]}`` — the seed record's 606 / 700 / 610 values, used to
   16 |     find similar records and (for "for you") to weight the reader's history. A
   17 |     ``None`` seam ⇒ no terms ⇒ empty recommendations (degrades, never raises).
   18 |   * ``search(db, prefix, term) -> [mfn, ...]`` — run a catalog/OPAC index search
   19 |     for a single term under a prefix ('S' subject, 'A' author, 'K' collective),
   20 |     returning candidate mfns. ``None`` ⇒ no candidates.
   21 |   * ``brief_read(db, mfn) -> {'title': str, 'author': str, ...}`` — resolve a
   22 |     candidate / history item's display title + author (reuses the OPAC brief read).
   23 | 
   24 | Reviews
   25 | -------
   26 | One editable review per (reader, db, mfn) — a second POST upserts (rating + text
   27 | replace the prior). ``reviews(db, mfn)`` aggregates ``avg`` + ``count`` over all
   28 | readers and, for a signed-in reader, returns ``mine``. Deleting is own-review
   29 | only (the route enforces 403 for someone else's id).
   30 | 
   31 | Recommendations ("similar")
   32 | ---------------------------
   33 | Read the seed record's subjects/authors/collectives; for each term run the
   34 | matching index search; collect candidate mfns and count how many distinct seed
   35 | terms each candidate shares (overlap). Rank by that overlap descending (ties by
   36 | mfn for determinism), drop the seed itself and any already-held duplicate, cap at
   37 | ``RECO_CAP``. Each item carries a human ``reason`` — "Общая тема: …" for a shared
   38 | subject (the highest-overlap explanation), "Тот же автор" for a shared author,
   39 | "Та же организация" for a shared collective.
   40 | 
   41 | "For you"
   42 | ---------
   43 | Union the subjects of the reader's recent history records (most-recent first,
   44 | capped), then run the SAME shared-term ranking against that union — excluding the
   45 | records already in the reader's history. Empty history ⇒ empty list.
   46 | """
   47 | 
   48 | # Cap on how many recommendation items we return (both "similar" and "for you").
   49 | RECO_CAP = 10
   50 | # How many recent history records feed the "for you" subject union.
   51 | FORYOU_HISTORY_DEPTH = 20
   52 | # Cap on history items returned to the portal.
   53 | HISTORY_CAP = 50
   54 | # How many candidate mfns to pull per term search (keeps the fan-out bounded).
   55 | PER_TERM_CAP = 30
```

```py
   56 | 
   57 | _RATING_MIN = 1
   58 | _RATING_MAX = 5
   59 | 
   60 | 
   61 | def _clamp_rating(rating):
   62 |     """Coerce ``rating`` to an int in 1..5, or raise ValueError. The route turns
   63 |     a ValueError into a 400 so a bad rating never reaches the store."""
   64 |     try:
   65 |         r = int(rating)
   66 |     except (TypeError, ValueError):
   67 |         raise ValueError('rating must be an integer 1..5')
   68 |     if r < _RATING_MIN or r > _RATING_MAX:
   69 |         raise ValueError('rating out of range 1..5')
   70 |     return r
   71 | 
   72 | 
   73 | class SocialService:
   74 |     """Reviews/ratings, history, saved searches + recommendations over a store.
   75 | 
   76 |     Parameters
   77 |     ----------
   78 |     store
   79 |         Access store (``AccessStore`` sqlite or ``PgAccessStore``) providing the
   80 |         reader_review / reader_history / saved_search CRUD. Reader-scoped by ticket.
   81 |     read_terms : callable | None
   82 |         ``read_terms(db, mfn) -> {'subjects','authors','collectives'}`` for the
   83 |         seed record (606/700/610). ``None`` ⇒ no recommendation terms.
   84 |     search : callable | None
   85 |         ``search(db, prefix, term) -> [mfn,...]`` index search. ``None`` ⇒ no
   86 |         candidates (recommendations come back empty).
   87 |     brief_read : callable | None
   88 |         ``brief_read(db, mfn) -> {'title','author',...}`` display resolution.
   89 |         ``None`` ⇒ blank title/author.
   90 |     reader_name : callable | None
   91 |         ``reader_name(ticket) -> str`` to label a review with the reader's name.
   92 |         ``None`` ⇒ '' (the route can still show "anonymous").
   93 |     now : callable
   94 |         Clock injection (``time.time`` by default) for deterministic tests.
   95 |     """
   96 | 
   97 |     # Which index prefix each seed-term kind searches under (IRBIS inverted files:
   98 |     # S= subject heading <-606, A= author <-700, K= keyword/collective <-610).
   99 |     _PREFIX = {'subjects': 'S', 'authors': 'A', 'collectives': 'K'}
  100 | 
  101 |     def __init__(self, store, read_terms=None, search=None, brief_read=None,
  102 |                  reader_name=None, now=None):
  103 |         self.store = store
  104 |         self.read_terms = read_terms
  105 |         self.search = search
  106 |         self.brief_read = brief_read
  107 |         self.reader_name = reader_name
  108 |         if now is None:
  109 |             import time
  110 |             now = time.time
```

```py
  122 |         if title == ('MFN %d' % mfn):
  123 |             title = ''
  124 |         return {'title': title, 'author': (it.get('author') or '').strip()}
  125 | 
  126 |     def _name(self, ticket):
  127 |         if self.reader_name is None:
  128 |             return ''
  129 |         try:
  130 |             return (self.reader_name(ticket) or '').strip()
  131 |         except Exception:
  132 |             return ''
  133 | 
  134 |     # ===================================================================== #
  135 |     # REVIEWS / RATINGS (#134) — one editable review per (reader, db, mfn).
  136 |     # ===================================================================== #
  137 |     def post_review(self, ticket, db, mfn, rating, text=''):
  138 |         """Upsert the reader's review of (db, mfn). Returns ``{'id'}``.
  139 | 
  140 |         Rating is validated to 1..5 (ValueError → the route returns 400). A second
  141 |         post by the same reader on the same item replaces the prior rating/text."""
  142 |         rating = _clamp_rating(rating)
  143 |         text = (text or '').strip()
  144 |         name = self._name(ticket)
  145 |         row = self.store.review_upsert(ticket, db, mfn, rating, text, name,
  146 |                                        self._now())
  147 |         return {'id': row['id']}
  148 | 
  149 |     def reviews(self, db, mfn, ticket=None):
  150 |         """Aggregate reviews of (db, mfn): ``{avg, count, items, mine?}``.
  151 | 
  152 |         ``avg`` is the mean rating rounded to 1 dp (0 when none); ``count`` the
  153 |         number of reviews; ``items`` every review as a card (newest first). When a
  154 |         ``ticket`` is given and that reader has a review, ``mine`` carries it."""
  155 |         rows = self.store.reviews_for(db, mfn)
  156 |         count = len(rows)
  157 |         avg = round(sum(r['rating'] for r in rows) / count, 1) if count else 0
  158 |         items = [{
  159 |             'id': r['id'],
  160 |             'readerName': r.get('reader_name') or 'Читатель',
  161 |             'rating': r['rating'],
  162 |             'text': r.get('text') or '',
  163 |             'ts': r['ts'],
  164 |         } for r in rows]
  165 |         out = {'avg': avg, 'count': count, 'items': items}
```

```py
  194 |     def history(self, ticket):

  197 |         rows = self.store.history_for(ticket, limit=HISTORY_CAP)

  199 |                            'title': r.get('title') or ('MFN %d' % r['mfn']),
```

```py
  228 |     # ===================================================================== #
  229 |     # RECOMMENDATIONS (#133) — content-based, ranked by shared-term overlap.
  230 |     # ===================================================================== #
  231 |     def _candidates_for_terms(self, db, terms_by_kind):
  232 |         """Run the per-term index search and tally, per candidate mfn, which seed
  233 |         terms it shares.
  234 | 
  235 |         ``terms_by_kind`` is ``{'subjects':[...], 'authors':[...],
  236 |         'collectives':[...]}``. Returns ``{mfn: {'overlap': n,
  237 |         'subjects': set(), 'authors': set(), 'collectives': set()}}`` where overlap
  238 |         is the count of DISTINCT seed terms (across all kinds) the candidate shares.
  239 |         """
  240 |         if self.search is None:
  241 |             return {}
  242 |         hits = {}
  243 |         for kind, prefix in self._PREFIX.items():
  244 |             for term in terms_by_kind.get(kind) or []:
  245 |                 term = (term or '').strip()
  246 |                 if not term:
  247 |                     continue
  248 |                 try:
  249 |                     mfns = self.search(db, prefix, term) or []
  250 |                 except Exception:
  251 |                     mfns = []
  252 |                 for mfn in mfns[:PER_TERM_CAP]:
  253 |                     h = hits.setdefault(mfn, {'overlap': 0, 'subjects': set(),
  254 |                                               'authors': set(), 'collectives': set()})
  255 |                     if term not in h[kind]:
  256 |                         h[kind].add(term)
  257 |                         h['overlap'] += 1
  258 |         return hits
  259 | 
  260 |     @staticmethod
  261 |     def _reason(info):
  262 |         """Human explanation for a recommended item, by the strongest shared kind:
  263 |         a shared subject first (most meaningful), then author, then collective."""
  264 |         if info['subjects']:
  265 |             return 'Общая тема: %s' % sorted(info['subjects'])[0]
  266 |         if info['authors']:
  267 |             return 'Тот же автор'
  268 |         if info['collectives']:
  269 |             return 'Та же организация'
  270 |         return 'Похожая запись'
  271 |

  274 |         mfns, cap at ``RECO_CAP``, and attach title/author/reason to each."""
  275 |         ranked = sorted(
```

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """aiohttp transport over the SAME Api core (Python 3.12: `py -3.12 -m pip install aiohttp`).
    3 | Run:  py -3.12 irbis-web/backend/app_aiohttp.py
    4 | 
    5 | Blocking IRBIS sockets and sqlite calls run in a thread executor so the event loop
    6 | is never blocked. Logic/authz/audit live entirely in core.Api — this file is transport."""
    7 | import asyncio
    8 | import json
    9 | from aiohttp import web
   10 | 
   11 | from core import Api, Raw
   12 | from reader_page import READER_HTML
   13 | import static_files
   14 | 
   15 | API = Api()
   16 | CORS = {
   17 |     'Access-Control-Allow-Origin': '*',
   18 |     'Access-Control-Allow-Headers': 'Content-Type, Authorization',
   19 |     'Access-Control-Allow-Methods': 'GET, POST, OPTIONS',
   20 | }
   21 | 
   22 | 
   23 | def _query(request):
   24 |     mq = request.rel_url.query
   25 |     return {k: mq.getall(k) for k in mq.keys()}
   26 | 
   27 | 
   28 | async def handle(request):
   29 |     if request.method == 'GET' and (request.path.rstrip('/') or '/') == '/':
   30 |         if static_files.has_dist():
   31 |             return web.Response(body=static_files.dist_index(), content_type='text/html', charset='utf-8', headers=CORS)
   32 |         return web.Response(text=READER_HTML, content_type='text/html', headers=CORS)
   33 |     if request.method == 'GET' and request.path.startswith('/assets/'):
   34 |         st, data, ct = static_files.serve_dist(request.path)
   35 |         return web.Response(status=st, body=data, content_type=ct.split(';')[0], headers=CORS)
   36 |     if request.method == 'GET' and request.path.rstrip('/') == '/app':
   37 |         return web.Response(status=302, headers={'Location': '/ui/app/'})
   38 |     if request.method == 'GET' and request.path.startswith('/ui/'):
   39 |         st, data, ct = static_files.serve(request.path)
   40 |         ctype = ct.split(';')[0]
   41 |         return web.Response(status=st, body=data, content_type=ctype, headers=CORS)
   42 |     if request.method == 'OPTIONS':
   43 |         return web.Response(status=204, headers=CORS)
   44 |     body = None
   45 |     if request.method == 'POST' and request.can_read_body:
   46 |         try:
   47 |             body = await request.json()
   48 |         except Exception:
   49 |             body = {}
   50 |     headers = {k.lower(): v for k, v in request.headers.items()}
   51 |     loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
```

```py
   21 |         exp[e.address] = e.name.decode('latin-1')      # rva -> name
   22 |

   24 | def read_str(rva):

   26 |         d = pe.get_data(rva, 80)
   27 |     except Exception:
   28 |         return None
   29 |     # ascii
   30 |     a = bytes(d).split(b'\x00')[0]
   31 |     if len(a) >= 3 and all(32 <= c < 127 for c in a):
   32 |         return a.decode('latin-1')
   33 |     # utf-16le
   34 |     try:
   35 |         w = bytes(d)
```

```py
   44 | 
   45 | md = Cs(CS_ARCH_X86, CS_MODE_32)
   46 | data = pe.__data__
   47 | for t in TARGETS:
   48 |     rva = next((r for r, n in exp.items() if n == t), None)
   49 |     if rva is None:

   51 |         continue

   54 |     print('\n=== %s  (rva %#x) ===' % (t, rva))

   56 |     for ins in md.disasm(bytes(code), base + rva):
   57 |         ann = ''
   58 |         ops = ins.op_str
   59 |         if ins.mnemonic in ('call', 'jmp') and ops.startswith('0x'):
   60 |             tgt = int(ops, 16) - base
   61 |             if tgt in exp:
   62 |                 ann = '   ; -> %s()' % exp[tgt]
   63 |         for tok in ops.replace('[', ' ').replace(']', ' ').split():
   64 |             if tok.startswith('0x') and len(tok) >= 8:
   65 |                 s = read_str(int(tok, 16) - base)
   66 |                 if s:
   67 |                     ann = '   ; "%s"' % s[:48]
   68 |                     break
   69 |         print('  %08x  %-6s %s%s' % (ins.address, ins.mnemonic, ops, ann))
   70 |         n += 1
   71 |         if (ins.mnemonic == 'ret' and n > 4) or n >= MAXINS:
   72 |             break
```

### Файл: `irbis-web/backend/irbis/client.py`  · строк: 163

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """Synchronous IRBIS64 client — production-shaped, derived empirically in Prohod B.
    3 | 
    4 | Wire protocol (see docs/recon/deep/reference/protocol/WIRE_PROTOCOL.md):
    5 |   * ONE TCP connection per request; session persists via client_id.
    6 |   * Request frame: "<bodyLen>\\n" + lines("\\n"):
    7 |         command, workstation, command, client_id, query_id, password, username, "","",""  [+args]
    8 |   * Response frame: lines("\\r\\n"); return_code at index 10; data from index 11.
    9 |   * Encodings: bibliographic data = UTF-8; INI/resources = CP1251.
   10 | This module is transport-agnostic: an aiohttp/async port only swaps `_execute`.
   11 | """
```

<!-- ─── страница 263 ─── -->

```py
   12 | import socket
   13 | import random
   14 | from .parser import parse_record
   15 | 
   16 | DELIM = '\x1f\x1e'   # IRBIS record field delimiter on the wire
   17 | 
   18 | 
   19 | class IrbisError(Exception):
   20 |     def __init__(self, code, message=''):
   21 |         super().__init__('IRBIS error %s: %s' % (code, message))
   22 |         self.code = code
   23 |         self.message = message
   24 | 
   25 | 
   26 | class Response:
   27 |     """Parsed server response frame."""
   28 |     def __init__(self, raw: bytes):
   29 |         self.raw = raw
   30 |         self.lines = raw.decode('utf-8', 'replace').split('\r\n')
   31 |         self.return_code = None
   32 |         if len(self.lines) > 10 and self.lines[10].lstrip('-').isdigit():
   33 |             self.return_code = int(self.lines[10])
   34 |         self.data = self.lines[11:] if len(self.lines) > 11 else []
   35 | 
   36 |     def ok(self):
   37 |         return self.return_code is not None and self.return_code >= 0
   38 | 
   39 | 
   40 | class IrbisClient:
   41 |     def __init__(self, host='127.0.0.1', port=6666, workstation='A', timeout=8.0):
   42 |         self.host, self.port = host, port
   43 |         self.workstation = workstation
   44 |         self.timeout = timeout
   45 |         self.user = self.password = ''
   46 |         self.client_id = 100000 + random.randint(0, 899999)
   47 |         self.query_id = 0
   48 |         self.connected = False
   49 |         self.server_version = ''
   50 | 
   51 |     # ---- transport ----
   52 |     def _execute(self, command, args=None):
   53 |         self.query_id += 1
   54 |         lines = [command, self.workstation, command, str(self.client_id),
   55 |                  str(self.query_id), self.password, self.user, '', '', '']
   56 |         if args:
   57 |             lines += [a if isinstance(a, str) else str(a) for a in args]
   58 |         body = ('\n'.join(lines) + '\n').encode('utf-8')
   59 |         packet = (str(len(body)) + '\n').encode('ascii') + body
   60 |         s = socket.create_connection((self.host, self.port), timeout=self.timeout)
   61 |         s.settimeout(self.timeout)
   62 |         s.sendall(packet)
   63 |         data = b''
   64 |         try:
   65 |             while True:
   66 |                 ch = s.recv(65536)
```

```py
   67 |                 if not ch:
   68 |                     break          # server closed -> response complete
   69 |                 data += ch
   70 |         except socket.timeout:
   71 |             pass
   72 |         finally:
   73 |             try:
   74 |                 s.close()
   75 |             except Exception:
   76 |                 pass
   77 |         return Response(data)
   78 | 
   79 |     # ---- session ----
   80 |     def connect(self, user, password):
   81 |         self.user, self.password = user, password
   82 |         r = self._execute('A', [user, password])
   83 |         if r.return_code == 0:
   84 |             self.connected = True
   85 |             if len(r.lines) > 4:
   86 |                 self.server_version = r.lines[4]
   87 |         return r
   88 | 
   89 |     def disconnect(self):
   90 |         if self.connected:
   91 |             try:
   92 |                 self._execute('B', [self.user])
   93 |             finally:
   94 |                 self.connected = False
   95 | 
   96 |     def nop(self):
   97 |         return self._execute('N')
   98 | 
   99 |     # ---- read ----
  100 |     def max_mfn(self, db):
  101 |         r = self._execute('O', [db])
  102 |         return r.return_code
  103 | 
  104 |     def search(self, db, expr, fmt='', first=1, maxn=0):
  105 |         """Return (count, [mfn,...]). expr must be a valid IRBIS query, e.g. '"K=...".'"""
  106 |         r = self._execute('K', [db, expr, '0', str(first), str(maxn), fmt])
  107 |         if r.return_code is not None and r.return_code < 0:
  108 |             raise IrbisError(r.return_code, 'search failed')
  109 |         count = int(r.data[0]) if r.data and r.data[0].lstrip('-').isdigit() else 0
  110 |         mfns = []
  111 |         for line in r.data[1:]:
  112 |             head = line.split('#', 1)[0]
  113 |             if head.isdigit():
  114 |                 mfns.append(int(head))
  115 |         return count, mfns
  116 | 
  117 |     def read_record(self, db, mfn):
  118 |         """Return a parsed record dict (mfn/status/version/guid/fields[])."""
  119 |         r = self._execute('C', [db, str(mfn), '0'])
  120 |         if r.return_code is not None and r.return_code < 0:
  121 |             raise IrbisError(r.return_code, 'read failed')
```

```py
  122 |         return parse_record(r.data)
  123 | 
  124 |     def format_record(self, db, mfn, pft='@brief'):
  125 |         """Server-side PFT rendering of one record. Returns text."""
  126 |         r = self._execute('G', [db, pft, '1', str(mfn)])
  127 |         if r.return_code is not None and r.return_code < 0:
  128 |             raise IrbisError(r.return_code, 'format failed')
  129 |         return '\n'.join(x for x in r.data if x).strip()
  130 | 
  131 |     # codes that still carry a valid term list (exact term may not exist)
  132 |     _TERM_OK = (0, -202, -203, -204)
  133 | 
  134 |     def read_terms(self, db, start, count=20):
  135 |         """Dictionary terms from `start` (command 'H'). Returns [(posting_count, term), ...]."""
  136 |         r = self._execute('H', [db, start, str(count)])
  137 |         if r.return_code is not None and r.return_code not in self._TERM_OK:
  138 |             raise IrbisError(r.return_code, 'read_terms failed')
  139 |         terms = []
  140 |         for line in r.data:
  141 |             if '#' in line:
  142 |                 cnt, term = line.split('#', 1)
  143 |                 terms.append((int(cnt) if cnt.isdigit() else 0, term))
  144 |         return terms
  145 | 
  146 |     def read_file(self, spec):
  147 |         """Read a text resource (command 'L'). spec = '<pathcode>.<db>.<file>'
  148 |         (e.g. '2.IBIS.brief.pft', '3.IBIS.kv.mnu'). Returns decoded text (CP1251), '' if absent.
  149 |         Server separates internal lines with \\x1F\\x1E; content follows the 10-line preamble."""
  150 |         r = self._execute('L', [spec])
  151 |         # round-trip via latin1 to recover exact bytes, then decode CP1251
  152 |         segs = r.raw.decode('latin1').split('\r\n')
  153 |         content = '\r\n'.join(segs[10:]).encode('latin1')
  154 |         return content.decode('cp1251', 'replace').replace('\x1f\x1e', '\n').strip()
  155 | 
  156 |     # ---- write (guarded; use only with write grants) ----
  157 |     def update_record(self, db, record_lines, lock=0, actualize=1):
  158 |         """record_lines: list of 'tag#value' (mfn#status and 0#version prepended by caller)."""
  159 |         rec = DELIM.join(record_lines)
  160 |         r = self._execute('D', [db, str(lock), str(actualize), rec])
  161 |         if r.return_code is not None and r.return_code < 0:
  162 |             raise IrbisError(r.return_code, 'update failed')
  163 |         return r
```

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """Parse the IRBIS client record representation into structured fields/subfields.
    3 | 
    4 | Record (from READ 'C', data lines):
    5 |     <mfn>#<status>
    6 |     0#<version>
    7 |     2147483647#{GUID}          # optional system GUID field (tag = MaxInt32)
    8 |     <tag>#<value>              # value may contain ^X subfields
    9 |     ...
   10 | Subfield delimiter is '^' (confirmed in Prohod B).
   11 | """
   12 | 
   13 | SYSTEM_GUID_TAG = '2147483647'
```

<!-- ─── страница 266 ─── -->

```py
   14 | 
   15 | 
   16 | def parse_subfields(value: str):
   17 |     """Return (head_text, {code: text}). head_text = text before the first '^'."""
   18 |     if '^' not in value:
   19 |         return value, {}
   20 |     parts = value.split('^')
   21 |     head = parts[0]
   22 |     subs = {}
   23 |     for p in parts[1:]:
   24 |         if not p:
   25 |             continue
   26 |         code, text = p[0], p[1:]
   27 |         # repeatable subfield codes: keep first, expose list under code+'*' if needed
   28 |         if code in subs:
   29 |             subs.setdefault('_repeats', []).append({code: text})
   30 |         else:
   31 |             subs[code] = text
   32 |     return head, subs
   33 | 
   34 | 
   35 | def parse_record(data_lines):
   36 |     rec = {'mfn': None, 'status': None, 'version': None, 'guid': None, 'fields': []}
   37 |     seen_mfn = False
   38 |     seen_version = False
   39 |     for line in data_lines:
   40 |         if '#' not in line:
   41 |             continue
   42 |         tag, value = line.split('#', 1)
   43 |         if not seen_mfn:
   44 |             rec['mfn'] = int(tag) if tag.isdigit() else None
   45 |             rec['status'] = value
   46 |             seen_mfn = True
   47 |             continue
   48 |         if not seen_version and tag == '0':
   49 |             rec['version'] = value
   50 |             seen_version = True
   51 |             continue
   52 |         if tag == SYSTEM_GUID_TAG:
   53 |             rec['guid'] = value
   54 |             continue
   55 |         head, subs = parse_subfields(value)
   56 |         rec['fields'].append({'tag': tag, 'value': value, 'text': head, 'subfields': subs})
   57 |     return rec
   58 | 
   59 | 
   60 | def field(rec, tag):
   61 |     """First field value with given tag, or None."""
   62 |     for f in rec['fields']:
   63 |         if f['tag'] == tag:
   64 |             return f
   65 |     return None
   66 | 
   67 | 
   68 | def fields(rec, tag):
```

### Файл: `irbis-web/backend/irbis/session.py`  · строк: 72

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """Thread-safe IRBIS session manager.
    3 | 
    4 | One long-lived registered session (client_id) is shared across HTTP requests and
    5 | serialized with a lock (the protocol increments query_id and the demo server caps
    6 | concurrent clients, so a single reused session is the safe P0 choice). Auto-(re)connect
    7 | on first use or after a dropped connection.
    8 | """
    9 | import threading
   10 | import socket
   11 | from .client import IrbisClient, IrbisError
   12 | 
   13 | 
   14 | class SessionManager:
   15 |     def __init__(self, host, port, workstation, user, password, timeout=8.0):
   16 |         self._lock = threading.Lock()
   17 |         self._client = IrbisClient(host, port, workstation, timeout)
   18 |         self._creds = (user, password)
   19 |         self.connected = False
   20 | 
   21 |     def _ensure(self):
   22 |         if self.connected:
   23 |             return
   24 |         r = self._client.connect(*self._creds)
   25 |         if r.return_code != 0:
   26 |             raise IrbisError(r.return_code, 'register failed (workstation/creds?)')
   27 |         self.connected = True
   28 | 
   29 |     def _call(self, fn):
   30 |         with self._lock:
   31 |             try:
   32 |                 self._ensure()
   33 |                 return fn(self._client)
   34 |             except (ConnectionError, OSError, socket.error):
   35 |                 # dropped connection -> re-register once and retry
   36 |                 self.connected = False
   37 |                 self._ensure()
   38 |                 return fn(self._client)
   39 | 
   40 |     # public, thread-safe operations
   41 |     def server_version(self):
   42 |         with self._lock:
   43 |             self._ensure()
   44 |             return self._client.server_version
   45 | 
   46 |     def max_mfn(self, db):
   47 |         return self._call(lambda c: c.max_mfn(db))
   48 | 
   49 |     def search(self, db, expr, first=1, maxn=0):
   50 |         return self._call(lambda c: c.search(db, expr, first=first, maxn=maxn))
   51 | 
   52 |     def read_record(self, db, mfn):
   53 |         return self._call(lambda c: c.read_record(db, mfn))
   54 | 
```

```py
   55 |     def format_record(self, db, mfn, pft='@brief'):
   56 |         return self._call(lambda c: c.format_record(db, mfn, pft))
   57 | 
   58 |     def read_terms(self, db, start, count=20):
   59 |         return self._call(lambda c: c.read_terms(db, start, count))
   60 | 
   61 |     def read_file(self, spec):
   62 |         return self._call(lambda c: c.read_file(spec))
   63 | 
   64 |     def update_record(self, db, record_lines, lock=0, actualize=1):
   65 |         return self._call(lambda c: c.update_record(db, record_lines, lock, actualize))
   66 | 
   67 |     def close(self):
   68 |         with self._lock:
   69 |             try:
   70 |                 self._client.disconnect()
   71 |             finally:
   72 |                 self.connected = False
```

```py
    2 | """READ-ONLY RE probe of the IRBIS DLLs: exports (API), imports, sections, and
    3 | 'speaking' strings (format/prefix/error/SQL/protocol hints). No modification."""

    9 | import pefile
   10 | 
   11 | DLLS = [r'C:\IRBIS64\irbis64_client.dll', r'C:\IRBIS64\IRBIS64.dll']

   13 | KEEP = re.compile(

   16 |     r'RECORD|\bMFN\b|TERM|SEARCH|UNIFOR|RUSMARC|MARC|Z39|\bRDR\b|\bRQST\b',
   17 |     re.I)
   18 | 
   19 |

   23 |     for m in re.finditer((b'(?:[\x20-\x7e]\x00){%d,}' % minlen), data):
   24 |         yield m.group().decode('utf-16le', 'replace')
   25 |

   30 |     pe.parse_data_directories([

   34 |         pe.FILE_HEADER.Machine, pe.OPTIONAL_HEADER.Subsystem,
```

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """Smoke test: backend IRBIS layer against the live server (no HTTP). Read-only.
    3 | Proves end-to-end: SessionManager -> IrbisClient -> live :6666 -> real IBIS data.
    4 | """
    5 | import sys
    6 | import os
    7 | sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)))
    8 | from config import Config
    9 | from irbis import SessionManager
   10 | 
   11 | def main():
   12 |     out = open(os.path.join(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)), 'smoke_results.txt'),
   13 |                'w', encoding='utf-8')
   14 |     def p(*a): print(*a, file=out)
   15 | 
   16 |     cfg = Config()
   17 |     sm = SessionManager(cfg.irbis_host, cfg.irbis_port, cfg.workstation,
   18 |                         cfg.irbis_user, cfg.irbis_pass, cfg.timeout)
   19 |     db = cfg.db_default
   20 |     p('HEALTH: server %s:%d, version=%s, maxmfn(%s)=%s'
   21 |       % (cfg.irbis_host, cfg.irbis_port, sm.server_version(), db, sm.max_mfn(db)))
   22 | 
   23 |     count, mfns = sm.search(db, '"V=05"')
   24 |     p('SEARCH V=05: total=%s, first=%s' % (count, mfns[:8]))
   25 | 
   26 |     mfn = mfns[0] if mfns else 1
   27 |     rec = sm.read_record(db, mfn)
   28 |     p('\nREAD MFN %s: version=%s, guid=%s, fields=%d'
   29 |       % (rec['mfn'], rec['version'], rec['guid'], len(rec['fields'])))
   30 |     for f in rec['fields'][:12]:
   31 |         sub = (' subfields=' + str(f['subfields'])) if f['subfields'] else ''
   32 |         p('  %-5s | %s%s' % (f['tag'], f['value'][:70], sub))
   33 | 
   34 |     p('\nRENDER @brief: %s' % sm.format_record(db, mfn, '@brief'))
   35 | 
   36 |     p('\nTERMS (H) start "A=КОЛИСНИЧЕНКО" x6:')
   37 |     for cnt, term in sm.read_terms(db, 'A=КОЛИСНИЧЕНКО', 6):
   38 |         p('   %4d  %s' % (cnt, term))
   39 | 
   40 |     p('\nFILE (L) 3.IBIS.kv.mnu (menu vocabulary, first 6 lines):')
   41 |     txt = sm.read_file('3.%s.kv.mnu' % db)
   42 |     for line in txt.splitlines()[:6]:
   43 |         p('   ' + line)
   44 | 
   45 |     sm.close()
   46 |     p('\nOK')
   47 |     out.close()
   48 | 
   49 | if __name__ == '__main__':
   50 |     main()
```

```py
    3 | returns (status, bytes, content_type). Used by both server.py and app_aiohttp.py."""
    4 | import os
    5 | import posixpath
```

<!-- ─── страница 272 ─── -->

```py
    6 | 
    7 | FRONTEND = os.path.normpath(os.path.join(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)), '..', 'frontend'))
    8 | DIST = os.path.join(FRONTEND, 'dist')
    9 | 
   10 | _CT = {
   11 |     '.html': 'text/html; charset=utf-8',
   12 |     '.css': 'text/css; charset=utf-8',
   13 |     '.js': 'text/javascript; charset=utf-8',
   14 |     '.jsx': 'text/javascript; charset=utf-8',
   15 |     '.json': 'application/json; charset=utf-8',
   16 |     '.map': 'application/json',
   17 |     '.svg': 'image/svg+xml',
   18 |     '.md': 'text/markdown; charset=utf-8',
   19 |     '.png': 'image/png', '.jpg': 'image/jpeg', '.jpeg': 'image/jpeg',
   20 |     '.woff2': 'font/woff2', '.woff': 'font/woff',
   21 | }
   22 | 
   23 | 
   24 | def has_dist():
   25 |     return os.path.isfile(os.path.join(DIST, 'index.html'))
   26 | 
   27 | 
   28 | def dist_index():
   29 |     with open(os.path.join(DIST, 'index.html'), 'rb') as f:
   30 |         return f.read()
   31 | 
   32 | 
   33 | def serve_dist(url_path):
   34 |     """Serve a built asset, e.g. /assets/index-xxx.js. Returns (status, bytes, ctype)."""
   35 |     rel = posixpath.normpath('/' + url_path.lstrip('/')).lstrip('/')
   36 |     full = os.path.join(DIST, *rel.split('/'))
   37 |     if not os.path.isfile(full):
   38 |         return 404, b'not found', 'text/plain'
   39 |     ext = os.path.splitext(full)[1].lower()
   40 |     with open(full, 'rb') as f:
   41 |         return 200, f.read(), _CT.get(ext, 'application/octet-stream')
   42 | 
   43 | 
   44 | def serve(url_path):
   45 |     """url_path starts with '/ui/'. Returns (status, data_bytes, content_type)."""
   46 |     rel = url_path[len('/ui/'):] if url_path.startswith('/ui/') else url_path.lstrip('/')
   47 |     rel = posixpath.normpath('/' + rel).lstrip('/')          # block ../ traversal
   48 |     full = os.path.join(FRONTEND, *rel.split('/')) if rel else FRONTEND
   49 |     if os.path.isdir(full):
   50 |         full = os.path.join(full, 'index.html')
   51 |     if not os.path.isfile(full):
   52 |         return 404, b'not found', 'text/plain'
   53 |     ext = os.path.splitext(full)[1].lower()
   54 |     with open(full, 'rb') as f:
   55 |         return 200, f.read(), _CT.get(ext, 'application/octet-stream')
```

```py
   13 | def call(method, path, token=None, body=None):
   14 |     url = BASE + path
   15 |     data = json.dumps(body).encode('utf-8') if body is not None else None
   16 |     req = urllib.request.Request(url, data=data, method=method)
   17 |     if token:
   18 |         req.add_header('Authorization', 'Bearer ' + token)

   20 |         req.add_header('Content-Type', 'application/json')
   21 |     try:

   25 |         return e.code, e.read(), e.headers.get('Content-Type', '')
```

```py
  204 | def module_checks(modname):
  205 |     """Run a self-contained engine test module (issue #188 A1/A3/A4/A6): import it,
  206 |     run every *_checks() it defines, fold its PASS/FAIL tally in. Each lives in its
  207 |     own module but is invoked here so the CI test_access.py step also exercises it."""
  208 |     try:
  209 |         mod = __import__(modname)
  210 |     except Exception as e:
  211 |         print('-- %s NOT RUN (import failed: %s)' % (modname, e)); FAIL[0] += 1; return
  212 |     for fn in sorted(dir(mod)):
  213 |         if fn.endswith('_checks') and callable(getattr(mod, fn)):
  214 |             # Isolate each check group: a raising group is a FAIL but must not
  215 |             # abort the runner, else one broken module hides every module after it
  216 |             # (and silently drops their coverage). The non-zero exit gate stands.
  217 |             try:
  218 |                 getattr(mod, fn)()
  219 |             except SystemExit:
  220 |                 raise
  221 |             except Exception as e:
  222 |                 print('  FAIL %s.%s raised: %s' % (modname, fn, e)); FAIL[0] += 1
  223 |     PASS[0] += mod.PASS[0]
  224 |     FAIL[0] += mod.FAIL[0]
  225 |
```

### Файл: `irbis-web/backend/tests/test_acq_bp_routes.py`  · строк: 317

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """HTTP-route tests for the ACQUISITION + BOOK-PROVISION engines (epic #188, #101).
    3 | 
    4 | The engines (``access/acquisition.py`` / ``access/bookprovision.py``) are unit-
    5 | tested standalone (``test_acquisition.py`` / ``test_bookprovision.py``); THIS suite
    6 | proves they are reachable over the HTTP surface — every assertion goes through the
    7 | real ``core.Api.route()`` dispatcher (the same path server.py / app_aiohttp.py use),
    8 | with a constructed ``Api`` (the constructor does NOT connect to ИРБИС).
    9 | 
   10 | Covered (the task contract):
   11 |   * a STAFF session can place an order / receive copies (→ КСУ + ToCat) / read КСУ
   12 |     and order-status THROUGH route();
   13 |   * a STAFF session can build a связка (faculty→specialty→discipline) + bind
   14 |     literature and compute the per-discipline / per-specialty Кко THROUGH route();
   15 |   * GUEST and READER sessions get 403 on EVERY acq/bp route (these are АРМ
   16 |     Комплектатор / Книгообеспеченность functions — staff-only, not public);
   17 |   * the ENTITLEMENT gate refuses the module when disabled (a valid grant on a
   18 |     disabled 'acquisition' / 'bookprovision' module is still 403).
   19 | 
   20 | Wired into the test_access.py runner (module list) so the CI sqlite + postgres
   21 | legs both run it. Standalone-runnable in the house style:
   22 |   py -3.12 tests/test_acq_bp_routes.py  -> ok ... + "N passed, M failed" + exit code.
   23 | """
   24 | import os
   25 | import sys
   26 | 
   27 | sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))))
   28 | 
   29 | from access.authz import GUEST_GRANTS, READER_GRANTS
   30 | from access import acquisition as _acq
   31 | from access import bookprovision as _bp
   32 | 
   33 | PASS = [0]
   34 | FAIL = [0]
   35 | 
   36 |

   38 |     if cond:
   39 |         PASS[0] += 1
   40 |         print('  ok  ', name)
   41 |     else:
   42 |         FAIL[0] += 1
   43 |         print('  FAIL', name)
   44 | 
   45 | 
   46 | # Staff grants for the АРМ Комплектатор / Книгообеспеченность functions wired in
```

```py
   47 | # core.py (acq.receipt/acq.read writes+reads acquisition; bp.write/bp.read book-
   48 | # provision). A real account would carry these via a seeded role; here we mint them

   50 | STAFF_GRANTS = [
   51 |     {'function': 'acq.receipt', 'db': '*', 'level': 'write'},
   52 |     {'function': 'acq.read', 'db': '*', 'level': 'read'},
   53 |     {'function': 'bp.write', 'db': '*', 'level': 'write'},

   57 | 
   58 | def _api():
   59 |     """A constructed Api with NO live ИРБИС and fresh in-memory engine stores.
   60 | 
   61 |     The acquisition / book-provision engines are rebuilt over ':memory:' stores so
   62 |     each test group starts empty and isolated; the shared CatalogStore is the ToCat
   63 |     seam (acquisition writes it, book-provision could read it)."""

   66 |     import core as _core
   67 |     importlib.reload(_core)
   68 |     api = _core.Api()
   69 |     from access.catalog import CatalogStore
   70 |     api.catalog = CatalogStore(':memory:', access_store=api.access)
   71 |     api.acquisition = _acq.AcquisitionEngine(
   72 |         store=_acq.AcquisitionStore(':memory:'), catalog=api.catalog,
   73 |         catalog_db=api.cfg.db_default)
   74 |     api.bookprovision = _bp.BookProvisionEngine(':memory:', catalog=api.catalog)
   75 |     return api, _core
   76 | 
   77 | 
   78 | def _staff(api, login='compl'):
   79 |     tok, _sess = api._new_session('staff', login, STAFF_GRANTS, tenant='public')
   80 |     return {'authorization': 'Bearer ' + tok}
   81 | 
   82 | 
   83 | def _guest_headers(api):
   84 |     tok, _ = api._new_session('guest', 'guest', GUEST_GRANTS, tenant='public')
   85 |     return {'authorization': 'Bearer ' + tok}
   86 | 
   87 | 
   88 | def _reader_headers(api, ticket='111'):
   89 |     tok, _ = api._new_session('reader', 'RI=%s' % ticket, READER_GRANTS,
   90 |                               tenant='public', rdr_mfn=1)
   91 |     return {'authorization': 'Bearer ' + tok}
```

```py
  223 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  224 | # 3. Auth — guest AND reader get 403 on every acq/bp route (staff-only АРМ).
  225 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  226 | ACQ_BP_ROUTES = [
  227 |     ('POST', '/api/acq/order', {'title': 'x', 'copies': 1}),
  228 |     ('POST', '/api/acq/order/cancel', {'id': 1}),
  229 |     ('POST', '/api/acq/receive', {'orderId': 1, 'ksuNo': 'k', 'copies': 1}),
  230 |     ('GET', '/api/acq/order', None),       # ?id= via query (default 0)
  231 |     ('GET', '/api/acq/ksu', None),
  232 |     ('POST', '/api/bp/faculty', {'code': 'F'}),
  233 |     ('POST', '/api/bp/specialty', {'facultyId': 1}),
  234 |     ('POST', '/api/bp/discipline', {'specialtyId': 1, 'discId': 'D'}),
  235 |     ('POST', '/api/bp/contingent', {'disciplineId': 1, 'students': 1}),
  236 |     ('POST', '/api/bp/bind', {'disciplineId': 1, 'title': 't'}),
  237 |     ('GET', '/api/bp/discipline', None),
  238 |     ('GET', '/api/bp/specialty', None),
  239 | ]
  240 | 
  241 | 
  242 | def auth_checks():
  243 |     print('-- auth: guest + reader 403 on every acq/bp route')
  244 |     api, _core = _api()
  245 | 
  246 |     for label, headers in (('guest', _guest_headers(api)),
  247 |                            ('reader', _reader_headers(api))):
  248 |         for m, path, b in ACQ_BP_ROUTES:
  249 |             q = {'id': ['1'], 'no': ['k']}    # harmless for GET routes
  250 |             st, _payload = api.route(m, path, q, b, headers)
  251 |             check('%s denied on %s %s (403)' % (label, m, path), st == 403)
  252 | 
  253 |     # No session at all -> 401/403, never 200.
  254 |     for m, path, b in ACQ_BP_ROUTES:
  255 |         st, _ = api.route(m, path, {'id': ['1'], 'no': ['k']}, b, {})
  256 |         check('no session on %s %s -> 401/403' % (m, path), st in (401, 403))
  257 | 
  258 |     # Sanity: a staff session IS admitted (the guard isn't blanket-denying).
  259 |     H = _staff(api)
  260 |     st, _ = api.route('POST', '/api/acq/order', {},
  261 |                       {'title': 'OK', 'copies': 1}, H)
  262 |     check('staff admitted to /api/acq/order (200)', st == 200)
  263 | 
  264 | 
  265 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  266 | # 4. Entitlement gate — a disabled module refuses even a valid grant.
```

```py
  267 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  268 | def entitlement_gate_checks():
  269 |     print('-- entitlement: disabled module refuses a valid grant (403)')
  270 |     api, _core = _api()
  271 |     H = _staff(api)
  272 | 
  273 |     # Baseline: with entitlements open, the staff grant places an order (200).
  274 |     st, _ = api.route('POST', '/api/acq/order', {},
  275 |                       {'title': 'Baseline', 'copies': 1}, H)
  276 |     check('acq order allowed when acquisition module licensed', st == 200)
  277 | 
  278 |     orig = _core.entitlements.is_module_enabled
  279 |     # Disable BOTH acquisition and bookprovision for this tenant.
  280 |     _core.entitlements.is_module_enabled = (
  281 |         lambda tenant, module, dsn=None:
  282 |         module not in ('acquisition', 'bookprovision'))
  283 |     try:
  284 |         st, p = api.route('POST', '/api/acq/order', {},
  285 |                           {'title': 'Gated', 'copies': 1}, H)
  286 |         check('acq order refused when acquisition module disabled (403)', st == 403)
  287 |         check('refusal cites module not licensed',
  288 |               'module not licensed' in (p.get('error', {}).get('message', '')))
  289 | 
  290 |         st, p = api.route('GET', '/api/acq/ksu', {'no': ['x']}, None, H)
  291 |         check('acq КСУ read refused when module disabled (403)', st == 403)
  292 | 
  293 |         st, p = api.route('POST', '/api/bp/faculty', {}, {'code': 'F'}, H)
  294 |         check('bp faculty refused when bookprovision disabled (403)', st == 403)
  295 | 
  296 |         st, p = api.route('GET', '/api/bp/specialty', {'id': ['1']}, None, H)
  297 |         check('bp specialty read refused when module disabled (403)', st == 403)
  298 |     finally:
  299 |         _core.entitlements.is_module_enabled = orig
  300 | 
  301 |     # Restored: the order works again once the module is licensed.
  302 |     st, _ = api.route('POST', '/api/acq/order', {},
  303 |                       {'title': 'Restored', 'copies': 1}, H)
  304 |     check('acq order allowed again after module re-enabled', st == 200)
  305 | 
  306 |

  308 |     acquisition_route_checks()
  309 |     bookprovision_route_checks()
  310 |     auth_checks()
  311 |     entitlement_gate_checks()
  312 |     print('\n%d passed, %d failed' % (PASS[0], FAIL[0]))
  313 |     sys.exit(1 if FAIL[0] else 0)
  314 | 
  315 | 
  316 | if __name__ == '__main__':
  317 |     main()
```

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """Acquisition engine tests (gap E1, epic #188).
    3 | 
    4 | Scenario-based tests of the acquisition lifecycle (order → receipt → КСУ → ToCat)
```

<!-- ─── страница 285 ─── -->

```py
    5 | + the ККО formulas, per SPEC_business_acquisition.md and INTEGRATION_MAP cluster 1
    6 | (the ToCat edge). Everything runs against in-memory stores so the lifecycle is
    7 | deterministic and needs no DB server.
    8 | 
    9 | Standalone-runnable in the house style of ``test_circulation.py``::
   10 | 
   11 |     py -3.12 tests/test_acquisition.py   ->  ok ...  +  "N passed, M failed"  + exit code
   12 | 
   13 | Covered:
   14 |   * order lifecycle: create → partial receipt → full receipt → status walk;
   15 |     cancel rules (can't cancel after a receipt);
   16 |   * receipt → КСУ summary entry (88^E titles / 88^F copies / 88^G sum + act ref),
   17 |     inventory numbers (explicit + auto MIN+1), accumulation across receipts;
   18 |   * the ККО ratio incl. the §2.2 division-by-zero policy (all 4 cells),
   19 |     average_kko (None excluded), normalize-to-1, reorder_need (§2.6);
   20 |   * the ToCat edge: receipt creates a catalog record + a 910 exemplar with
   21 |     ^A=free; re-supply of an existing title adds a copy to the existing record
   22 |     (NOT a new record); standalone (no catalog) still works (ToCat no-op).
   23 | """
   24 | import os
   25 | import sys
   26 | 
   27 | sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))))
   28 | from access import acquisition as acq
   29 | from access.acquisition import (
   30 |     AcquisitionStore, AcquisitionEngine, AcquisitionError,
   31 |     kko, average_kko, reorder_need, title_key,
   32 |     ORDER_ORDERED, ORDER_PARTIAL, ORDER_RECEIVED, ORDER_CANCELLED,
   33 |     EXEMPLAR_FREE,
   34 | )
   35 | 
   36 | # The catalog handle is optional; import it for the ToCat tests. If it can't be
   37 | # imported (run in true isolation), the catalog tests degrade to a skip.
   38 | try:
   39 |     from access import catalog as _catalog
   40 | except Exception:  # pragma: no cover
   41 |     _catalog = None
   42 | 
   43 | PASS = [0]
   44 | FAIL = [0]
   45 | 
   46 | 
   47 | def check(name, cond):
   48 |     if cond:
   49 |         PASS[0] += 1
   50 |         print('  ok  ', name)
   51 |     else:
   52 |         FAIL[0] += 1
   53 |         print('  FAIL', name)
   54 | 
   55 | 
   56 | def fresh(catalog=None):
   57 |     """A fresh engine over an in-memory store (optionally with a catalog handle)."""
   58 |     return AcquisitionEngine(store=AcquisitionStore(':memory:'), catalog=catalog)
   59 |
```

```py
  195 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  196 | # 3. ККО ratio + division-by-zero policy (SPEC §2.2, all 4 cells).
  197 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  198 | def kko_checks():
  199 |     print('-- ККО ratio + div-by-zero policy')
  200 |     # cell 1: students>0, copies>0 -> copies/students
  201 |     check('ККО 40/100 = 0.4', kko(40, 100) == 0.4)
  202 |     check('ККО 10/100 = 0.1', kko(10, 100) == 0.1)
  203 |     # cell 2: students>0, copies=0 -> 0 (deficit, дозаказ candidate)
  204 |     check('ККО 0/100 = 0.0 (not provided)', kko(0, 100) == 0.0)
  205 |     # cell 3: students=0, copies>0 -> 1.0 (provided, no consumers)
  206 |     check('ККО copies>0,students=0 -> 1.0', kko(50, 0) == 1.0)
  207 |     # cell 4: students=0, copies=0 -> None (no data, NOT 0)
  208 |     check('ККО 0/0 -> None (no data)', kko(0, 0) is None)
  209 |     check('None is distinct from 0.0', kko(0, 0) is not kko(0, 100))
  210 | 
  211 |     # average: SPEC §6 П2 — (0.40 + 0.10) / 2 = 0.25
  212 |     avg = average_kko([kko(40, 100), kko(10, 100)])
  213 |     check('average ККО of П2 = 0.25', avg == 0.25)
  214 | 
  215 |     # None cells excluded from the average (archival orphan doesn't drag it down)
  216 |     avg2 = average_kko([kko(40, 100), kko(0, 0), kko(10, 100)])
  217 |     check('None excluded from average -> still 0.25', avg2 == 0.25)
  218 | 
  219 |     # all-None -> None (nothing to average)
  220 |     check('all-None average -> None', average_kko([None, None]) is None)
  221 | 
  222 |     # normalize to 1: a 2.0 over-provision capped to 1.0
  223 |     # values [2.0, 0.0] -> raw avg 1.0; normalized -> (1.0 + 0.0)/2 = 0.5
  224 |     check('normalize caps terms at 1',
```

```py
  338 | def _val(record, field, subfield):
  339 |     for inst in _instances(record, field):
  340 |         if isinstance(inst, dict) and inst.get(subfield):
  341 |             return inst[subfield]
  342 |     return ''
```

### Файл: `irbis-web/backend/tests/test_admin_routes.py`  · строк: 390

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """HTTP-route tests for the АРМ Администратор (#187, epic #223).
    3 | 
    4 | The administration workstation over the EXISTING access store (staff accounts,
    5 | roles/grants, audit log, DB list). THIS suite proves that surface is reachable
    6 | over HTTP — every assertion goes through the real ``core.Api.route()`` dispatcher
    7 | (the same path server.py / app_aiohttp.py use), with a constructed ``Api`` (the
    8 | constructor does NOT connect to ИРБИС).
    9 | 
   10 | Covered (the task contract, all through route()):
   11 |   * ADMIN lists accounts (GET /api/admin/users) — the seeded admin/librarian show
   12 |     up with their roles + effective grants;
   13 |   * ADMIN creates a user (POST /api/admin/users) with roles -> it then appears in
   14 |     the list with those roles, AND the created user can AUTHENTICATE (the password
   15 |     was hashed the same way seed.py hashes — pbkdf2, never plaintext);
   16 |   * ADMIN replaces a user's roles (POST /api/admin/users/roles) and toggles active
   17 |     (POST /api/admin/users/active) — disabling blocks authentication;
   18 |   * ADMIN reads roles (GET /api/admin/roles) with their grants;
   19 |   * ADMIN reads the audit log (GET /api/admin/audit) — an action issued first shows
   20 |     up, newest-first, capped by ?limit=;
   21 |   * ADMIN reads the full DB list (GET /api/admin/databases) — every base (staff
   22 |     view), not just the public OPAC ones;
   23 |   * a NON-ADMIN STAFF session gets 403 on every admin route (has no admin.* grant);
   24 |   * GUEST/READER get 403, and no session gets 401/403 — never 200.
   25 | 
   26 | Wired into the test_access.py runner (module list) so the CI sqlite + postgres
   27 | legs both run it. Standalone-runnable in the house style:
   28 |   py tests/test_admin_routes.py  -> ok ... + "N passed, M failed" + exit code.
   29 | """
   30 | import os
   31 | import sys
```

```py
   32 | 
   33 | sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))))

   35 | from access.authz import GUEST_GRANTS, READER_GRANTS

   37 | PASS = [0]
   38 | FAIL = [0]

   40 | 
   41 | def check(name, cond):
   42 |     if cond:
   43 |         PASS[0] += 1
   44 |         print('  ok  ', name)
   45 |     else:
   46 |         FAIL[0] += 1
   47 |         print('  FAIL', name)
   48 | 
   49 | 
   50 | # An administrator session carries admin.users + admin.db at the admin level (the
   51 | # seeded 'administrator' role; seed.py). We mint them onto the token directly so the
   52 | # suite needs no DB role wiring — same technique as test_circ_routes.STAFF_GRANTS.
   53 | ADMIN_GRANTS = [
   54 |     {'function': 'admin.users', 'db': '*', 'level': 'admin'},
   55 |     {'function': 'admin.db', 'db': '*', 'level': 'admin'},
   56 |     # plus the everyday read grants an admin role also carries (not needed by the
   57 |     # admin routes, but realistic — proves the guard keys on admin.* specifically).
   58 |     {'function': 'search', 'db': '*', 'level': 'read'},
   59 | ]
   60 | 
   61 | # A non-admin staff session (e.g. a cataloger): real staff, but NO admin.* grant.
   62 | NONADMIN_STAFF_GRANTS = [
   63 |     {'function': 'record.write', 'db': '*', 'level': 'write'},
   64 |     {'function': 'search', 'db': '*', 'level': 'read'},
   65 | ]
   66 | 
   67 | # A fake database menu (code\nname pairs, '*****' terminator) so /api/admin/databases
   68 | # works without a live ИРБИС: we stub api.irbis.read_file to return it. IBIS is on the
   69 | # public allow-list; RDR/PAY are service bases (non-public) — the admin view must show
   70 | # ALL of them (unlike a reader, who would only see IBIS).
   71 | _DB_MENU = 'IBIS\nЭлектронный каталог\nRDR\nЧитатели\nPAY\nОплата\n*****\n'
   72 | 
   73 | 
   74 | def _api():
   75 |     """A constructed Api with NO live ИРБИС and a fresh in-memory access store.
   76 | 
   77 |     The access store is rebuilt over ':memory:' so each run is isolated (and the
   78 |     seed runs into it via core.Api.__init__). ``irbis.read_file`` is stubbed to a
   79 |     fake DB menu (no live server) and ``max_mfn`` to 0 so /api/admin/databases is
   80 |     deterministic without a server."""
   81 |     os.environ['JWT_SECRET'] = 'admin-routes-test-secret'
   82 |     os.environ['ACCESS_BACKEND'] = 'sqlite'
   83 |     os.environ['ACCESS_DB'] = ':memory:'
   84 |     import importlib
   85 |     import core as _core
   86 |     importlib.reload(_core)
```

```py
  267 |     admin_role = next(r for r in roles if r['name'] == 'administrator')
  268 |     check('role carries a grants list', isinstance(admin_role['grants'], list))
  269 |     check('administrator role holds admin.users grant',
  270 |           any(g['function'] == 'admin.users' and g['level'] == 'admin'
  271 |               for g in admin_role['grants']))
  272 | 
  273 | 
  274 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  275 | # 4. Audit log — issue an action first, then see it logged (newest-first, capped).
  276 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  277 | def audit_route_checks():
  278 |     print('-- admin routes: GET /api/admin/audit (recent, newest-first, capped)')
  279 |     api, _core = _api()
  280 |     H = _admin(api)
  281 | 
  282 |     # Issue an auditable admin action FIRST (a create writes an audit row).
  283 |     api.route('POST', '/api/admin/users', {},
  284 |               {'login': 'audited', 'password': 'pw', 'roles': ['cataloger']}, H)
  285 | 
  286 |     st, p = api.route('GET', '/api/admin/audit', {}, None, H)
  287 |     check('GET /api/admin/audit -> 200', st == 200)
  288 |     items = p['data']['items']
  289 |     check('audit returns items', isinstance(items, list) and len(items) >= 1)
  290 |     row = items[0]
  291 |     check('audit row carries the contract fields',
  292 |           all(k in row for k in ('ts', 'actor', 'function', 'db', 'mfn', 'result', 'detail')))
  293 |     check('the create action is logged',
  294 |           any(i['function'] == 'admin.users'
  295 |               and (i.get('detail') or {}).get('op') == 'create' for i in items))
  296 | 
  297 |     # newest-first: issue two distinguishable actions, the lat+ comes first.
  298 |     api.route('POST', '/api/admin/users', {},
  299 |               {'login': 'aud-first', 'password': 'pw'}, H)
  300 |     api.route('POST', '/api/admin/users', {},
  301 |               {'login': 'aud-second', 'password': 'pw'}, H)
  302 |     st, p = api.route('GET', '/api/admin/audit', {}, None, H)
  303 |     creates = [i for i in p['data']['items']
  304 |                if (i.get('detail') or {}).get('op') == 'create']
  305 |     check('audit newest-first (last create on top)',
  306 |           creates and creates[0]['detail'].get('login') == 'aud-second')
```

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """Authority-files service tests (gap A4, epic #188).
    3 | 
    4 | Covers the first shippable slice of the authority service (access/authority.py):
    5 | 
    6 |   1. AuthorityStore (in-memory sqlite, no server): seed a few person/subject/
    7 |      corporate records -> search finds by term (prefix + substring), get returns
    8 |      the full record, base isolation (athra id not fetchable as athrs), empty
    9 |      store / no hit returns [] not error (SPEC AC-L1).
   10 |   2. Substitution fill-map (SPEC §3.2): person -> 700 fills ^a/^b/^g/^f/^9 + ^3;
   11 |      corporate -> 710 fills ^a/^b/^c/^s + ^3; subject -> 606 collects heading +
   12 |      subrubrics + ^3; ^3 always == authority id and never copied from the
   13 |      authority's own subfields; index field 675 carries NO ^3; absent subfields
   14 |      omitted (operator role ^9/^4 not clobbered).
   15 |   3. Error paths: missing authority (None) and unknown catalog field both raise;
   16 |      kind/field mismatch (person record into a corporate field) raises.
   17 | 
   18 | Standalone-runnable (``py -3.12 tests/test_authority.py``) in the house style of
   19 | tests/test_seeding.py: ``ok …`` lines + ``N passed, M failed`` + exit code.
```
