```py
  500 | 
  501 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  502 | # 6. ToCat-shaped end-to-end job: read an IBIS draft БО → NEWMFN into the IBIS
  503 | # catalog with reformatted fields + a 938 subscription link → in-memory stores.
  504 | # Mirrors INTEGRATION_MAP cluster 1 (ToCat 938/910/VD=DEL) without an acquisition
  505 | # host: the executor stays pure, the store is wired via emit/_stores.
  506 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  507 | def tocat_shaped_checks():
  508 |     print('-- ToCat-shaped job (CMPL draft -> IBIS catalog)')
  509 | 
  510 |     # ToCat: a CMPL acquisition draft is reformatted into an IBIS catalog record.
  511 |     #  * 920 reset to the catalog kind 'PAZK'
  512 |     #  * title carried over (200^a -> 200^a) — reformat-on-source
  513 |     #  * the holdings copy carried over (910 -> 910)
  514 |     #  * a 938 link back to the subscription period is planted
  515 |     #  * VD=DEL marks the source draft for deletion (no-dup guarantee, AC3)
  516 |     job = (
  517 |         "0\n"
  518 |         "NEWMFN\n'IBIS'\n"
  519 |         "ADD\n920\n\n'PAZK'\n\n"             # catalog record kind
  520 |         "ADD\n200^a\n1\nv200^a\n\n"          # title reformatted from source
  521 |         "ADD\n910\n\nv910\n\n"               # holdings carried over
  522 |         "ADD\n938\n\nv938\n\n"               # subscription-period link (cluster 1.3)
  523 |         "END\n"
  524 |         "ADD\n2102\n\n'VD=DEL'\n\n"          # mark source draft for deletion (AC3)
  525 |     )
  526 |     prog = gbl.parse(job)
  527 | 
  528 |     # in-memory IBIS catalog store; emit returns the new MFN (store index).
  529 |     stores = {'IBIS': []}
  530 |     def emit(db, record):
  531 |         stores.setdefault(db, []).append(record)
  532 |         return len(stores[db]) - 1
  533 | 
  534 |     draft = {
  535 |         '920': [{'': 'OJK'}],                # acquisition kind (subscription)
  536 |         '200': [{'a': 'Вестник науки'}],
  537 |         '910': [{'': 'inv-1001'}],
  538 |         '938': [{'': 'CMPL/2026/Q2'}],
  539 |     }
  540 |     ctx = {'format_eval': lit_eval}
  541 |     src_after = gbl.apply(prog, draft, ctx, emit=emit)
  542 |     st = ctx['_state']
  543 | 
```

### Файл: `irbis-web/backend/tests/test_identity.py`  · строк: 324

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """Identity / Tenancy / Licensing tests (issue #101, I1).
    3 | 
    4 | Covers the three new mechanisms layered on the merged Access store:
    5 | 
    6 |   1. JWT sessions — dependency-free HS256 issue->verify round-trip, and that a
    7 |      TAMPERED or EXPIRED token is rejected (-> no session -> 401 at the edge).
    8 |   2. Tenant context — the JWT carries a `tenant` claim; the session is scoped to
```

<!-- ─── страница 371 ─── -->

```py
    9 |      it; on PostgreSQL the per-tenant store sees only that tenant's data.
   10 |   3. Entitlements (licensing) — a request to a DISABLED module is refused even
   11 |      with a valid grant (grants = can-do, entitlements = is-licensed). On the
   12 |      dev/`public` path entitlements fail open so the guest flow keeps working.
   13 | 
   14 | Backends:
   15 |   * sqlite / pure  — JWT round-trip, tamper/expiry rejection, public fail-open
   16 |     entitlement, and the _guard entitlement-gating path with a monkeypatched
   17 |     entitlement check (no DB needed). Always run.
   18 |   * postgres only  — tenant-claim data scoping and real `control.tenant_module`
   19 |     gating. Runs when ACCESS_BACKEND=postgres (or ACCESS_TEST_PG=1) AND a PG is
   20 |     reachable; otherwise SKIPPED cleanly (never red-flags the sqlite dev box).
   21 | 
   22 | Invoked by the test_access.py runner (identity_checks) so the CI postgres step
   23 | exercises it with NO .github/ change.
   24 | """
   25 | import os
   26 | import sys
   27 | import time
   28 | 
   29 | sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))))
   30 | from access import jwt as _jwt
   31 | from access import entitlements
   32 | from access.authz import authorize, GUEST_GRANTS

   49 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #

   51 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   52 | def jwt_checks():
   53 |     print('-- jwt (HS256, dep-free)')
   54 |     claims = {'sub': 'admin', 'tenant': 'acme', 'kind': 'staff',
   55 |               'grants': [{'function': 'search', 'db': '*', 'level': 'read'}]}
   56 |     tok = _jwt.encode(claims, SECRET)
   57 |     check('token is a plain string (bearer)', isinstance(tok, str) and tok.count('.') == 2)
   58 | 
   59 |     out = _jwt.decode(tok, SECRET)
   60 |     check('round-trip preserves sub', out['sub'] == 'admin')
   61 |     check('round-trip preserves tenant', out['tenant'] == 'acme')
   62 |     check('round-trip preserves kind', out['kind'] == 'staff')
   63 |     check('round-trip preserves grants', out['grants'] == claims['grants'])
```

```py
  101 |

  105 |     rejected = False
```

```py
  135 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  136 | # 2. Api session + entitlement gating — uses a constructed Api (no live IRBIS:
  137 | #    the constructor does not connect; we only call _new_session/_session/_guard).
  138 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  139 | def api_session_checks():
  140 |     print('-- api session + guard')
  141 |     os.environ['JWT_SECRET'] = SECRET
  142 |     import importlib
  143 |     import core as _core
  144 |     importlib.reload(_core)            # pick up JWT_SECRET set above
  145 |     api = _core.Api()
  146 | 
  147 |     # issue a guest session -> verify it round-trips through _session
  148 |     tok, _ = api._new_session('guest', 'guest', GUEST_GRANTS, tenant='public')
  149 |     sess = api._session(tok)
  150 |     check('guest token verifies to session', sess is not None and sess['kind'] == 'guest')
  151 |     check('session carries tenant claim', sess['tenant'] == 'public')
  152 |     check('session actor == sub', sess['actor'] == 'guest')
  153 | 
  154 |     # tampered token -> no session (the edge maps None -> 401)
  155 |     h, p, s = tok.split('.')
  156 |     bad = '%s.%s.%s' % (h, p, s[:-2] + ('zz' if s[-2:] != 'zz' else 'aa'))
  157 |     check('tampered token -> no session (401)', api._session(bad) is None)
  158 | 
  159 |     # expired token -> no session
  160 |     expired = _jwt.encode({'sub': 'guest', 'tenant': 'public', 'kind': 'guest',
  161 |                            'grants': GUEST_GRANTS}, api.jwt_secret, ttl_seconds=-5)
  162 |     check('expired token -> no session (401)', api._session(expired) is None)
  163 | 
  164 |     # _guard: a guest can search on the public path (entitlements fail open)
  165 |     guard_ok = True
  166 |     try:
  167 |         api._guard(sess, 'search', 'IBIS', 'read')
  168 |     except _core.Denied:
  169 |         guard_ok = False
  170 |     check('guest search allowed on public (grant + open entitlement)', guard_ok)
  171 | 
  172 |     # _guard: grant gate still bites (guest cannot order/write)
  173 |     denied = False
```

```py
  240 | def pg_checks(dsn):
  241 |     print('-- identity: postgres', dsn.rsplit('@', 1)[-1])
  242 |     from access import pgstore
  243 | 
  244 |     # clean slate
  245 |     pgstore.deprovision_tenant(A_SLUG, dsn)
  246 |     pgstore.deprovision_tenant(B_SLUG, dsn)
  247 | 
  248 |     sa = pgstore.provision_tenant(A_SLUG, 'Identity A', 'публичная', dsn)
  249 |     sb = pgstore.provision_tenant(B_SLUG, 'Identity B', 'школьная', dsn)
  250 | 
  251 |     # --- tenant-claim data scoping: a distinct account per tenant, invisible across ---
  252 |     sa.create_account('alice', 'pw_a', 'Alice')
  253 |     sb.create_account('bob', 'pw_b', 'Bob')
  254 |     check('tenant A sees its own account', sa.authenticate('alice', 'pw_a') is not None)
  255 |     check('tenant A cannot see B account', sa.get_account('bob') is None)
  256 |     check('tenant B cannot see A account', sb.get_account('alice') is None)
  257 | 
  258 |     # --- entitlements seeded enabled by provisioning ---
  259 |     mods_a = entitlements.enabled_modules(A_SLUG, dsn)
  260 |     check('provisioning seeds default modules enabled',
  261 |           set(entitlements.DEFAULT_MODULES).issubset(set(mods_a)))
  262 |     check('is_module_enabled True for seeded module',
  263 |           entitlements.is_module_enabled(A_SLUG, 'cataloging', dsn))
  264 | 
  265 |     # --- disable one module -> is_module_enabled False, dropped from the list ---
  266 |     entitlements.set_module(A_SLUG, 'cataloging', False, dsn)
  267 |     check('disabled module -> is_module_enabled False',
  268 |           entitlements.is_module_enabled(A_SLUG, 'cataloging', dsn) is False)
  269 |     check('disabled module dropped from enabled list',
  270 |           'cataloging' not in entitlements.enabled_modules(A_SLUG, dsn))
  271 |     # other tenant unaffected (isolation of the licensing axis too)
  272 |     check('other tenant module unaffected',
  273 |           entitlements.is_module_enabled(B_SLUG, 'cataloging', dsn) is True)
  274 |
```

### Файл: `irbis-web/backend/tests/test_integration.py`  · строк: 625

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """Cross-module integration tests — the «связующая ткань» seams (INTEGRATION_MAP).
    3 | 
    4 | Wires the two highest-value межмодульные рёбра that the integration map flagged
    5 | as designed-but-not-wired, and proves them end-to-end over the existing in-memory
    6 | stores (no core.py, no network):
    7 | 
    8 |   * Edges 2.1/2.2 — Catalog↔Circulation 910^A flip. ``CirculationEngine`` is given
    9 |     a ``catalog`` handle; ``checkout`` flips the linked catalog exemplar's
   10 |     ``910^A`` 0→1 (issued), ``return_item`` flips it 1→0 (free), and ``place_hold``
   11 |     /availability reads honour the catalog ``910^A``. The item↔record link is
   12 |     resolved by inventory number (``910^b``), as the spec describes.
   13 | 
   14 |   * Edge 6.1 — Authority↔Catalog ^3 on save. ``CatalogStore`` is given an
```

```py
   15 |     ``authority`` handle; a field instance carrying ``_authority_ref`` (the
   16 |     authority record id) is resolved through ``authority.substitute`` on ``save``,
   17 |     filling ``^a/^b/^g`` + the ``^3`` link before ФЛК/index.
   18 | 
   19 | Both seams are exercised WITH the handle (wired) and the back-compat paths

   22 | Standalone-runnable in the house style of tests/test_catalog.py /
```

```py
   73 | def _book_with_copy(inv='1024365', status=EXEMPLAR_FREE):
   74 |     """A valid book carrying one 910 exemplar keyed by inventory ``inv`` whose
   75 |     910^A starts at ``status`` (default free)."""
   76 |     return {
   77 |         '920': 'PAZK',
   78 |         '200': [{'a': 'Основы каталогизации', 'f': 'Иванова И.И.'}],
   79 |         '700': [{'a': 'Петров П.П.'}],
   80 |         '101': 'rus',
   81 |         '910': [{'a': status, 'b': inv}],
   82 |         '907': [{'a': 'Сидорова С.С.'}],
   83 |     }
   84 | 
   85 | 
   86 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   87 | # Edge 2.1/2.2 — Catalog↔Circulation 910^A flip (the original break).
   88 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   89 | def checkout_return_flip_checks():
   90 |     print('-- edge 2.1/2.2: checkout/return flip catalog 910^A')
   91 |     cat = CatalogStore(':memory:', access_store=_seeded_access_store())
   92 |     inv = '1024365'
   93 |     cat.save('IBIS', _book_with_copy(inv, EXEMPLAR_FREE))
   94 | 
   95 |     # the catalog accessors locate + read the exemplar by inventory number (910^b)
   96 |     check('exemplar located by 910^b', cat.find_exemplar('IBIS', inv) is not None)
   97 |     check('exemplar starts FREE (910^A=0)',
   98 |           cat.exemplar_status('IBIS', inv) == EXEMPLAR_FREE)
   99 |     check('is_available True before loan', cat.is_available('IBIS', inv) is True)
  100 | 
  101 |     store = CirculationStore(':memory:')
  102 |     eng = CirculationEngine(store=store, policy=default_policy(),
  103 |                             catalog=cat, catalog_db='IBIS')
  104 |     store.add_reader('R1', category='В01')
  105 | 
  106 |     # CHECKOUT: 910^A flips 0 -> 1 (issued) on the linked catalog record.
  107 |     d = eng.checkout('R1', inv, T0)
  108 |     check('checkout allowed', d.decision == ALLOW)
  109 |     check('checkout flips catalog 910^A 0->1',
  110 |           cat.exemplar_status('IBIS', inv) == EXEMPLAR_ISSUED)
  111 |     check('catalog item not available after checkout',
  112 |           cat.is_available('IBIS', inv) is False)
  113 |     check('checkout reports the flipped catalog mfn',
  114 |           d.computed.get('catalog_mfn') == 1
  115 |           and d.computed.get('exemplar_status') == EXEMPLAR_ISSUED)
  116 |     # the loan itself still recorded in circulation's own store
  117 |     check('loan recorded on hand', store.count_on_hand('R1') == 1)
  118 | 
  119 |     # RETURN: 910^A flips 1 -> 0 (free).
  120 |     loan_id = d.computed['loan']['id']
  121 |     dr = eng.return_item(loan_id, T0 + 5 * DAY)
  122 |     check('return allowed', dr.decision == ALLOW)
  123 |     check('return flips catalog 910^A 1->0',
  124 |           cat.exemplar_status('IBIS', inv) == EXEMPLAR_FREE)
```

```py
  132 | def availability_read_checks():
  133 |     print('-- edge 2.2: hold/availability honours catalog 910^A')
  134 |     cat = CatalogStore(':memory:', access_store=_seeded_access_store())
  135 |     inv = '777001'

  145 |     check('hold on catalog-free item is ready',

  150 |     # Now mark the copy ISSUED in the catalog OUT OF BAND (e.g. a parallel desk),

  152 |     # because the catalog says the copy is not free (edge 2.2).
  153 |     cat.set_exemplar_status('IBIS', inv, EXEMPLAR_ISSUED)

  158 |     # R2 is alone in the queue per circulation, but the catalog status blocks ready.
  159 |     check('hold NOT ready when catalog marks copy issued',
  160 |           store.get_hold(d2.computed['hold']['id'])['status'] == 'queued')
```

```py
  180 |     # Catalog wired but the item is UNKNOWN to the catalog → graceful no-op flip.
  181 |     cat = CatalogStore(':memory:', access_store=_seeded_access_store())
  182 |     eng2 = CirculationEngine(store=CirculationStore(':memory:'),
  183 |                              policy=default_policy(), catalog=cat)
  184 |     eng2.store.add_reader('R2', category='В01')
  185 |     d2 = eng2.checkout('R2', 'NO-SUCH-INV', T0)
  186 |     check('checkout of catalog-unknown item still allowed', d2.decision == ALLOW)
  187 |     check('catalog-unknown item flip is a no-op (no catalog_mfn)',
  188 |           'catalog_mfn' not in d2.computed)
  189 | 
  190 | 
  191 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  192 | # Edge 6.1 — Authority↔Catalog ^3 on save.
  193 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  194 | def _authority_store_with_tolstoy():
  195 |     auth = AuthorityStore(':memory:')
  196 |     pid = auth.add_record(
  197 |         'athra',
  198 |         {'a': 'Толстой', 'b': 'Л. Н.', 'g': 'Лев Николаевич',
  199 |          'f': '1828-1910', '9': '1'},
  200 |         terms=['Толстой, Л. Н.'])
  201 |     sid = auth.add_record(
  202 |         'athrs',
  203 |         {'a': 'Литература', 'b': 'История и критика'},
  204 |         terms=['Литература'])
  205 |     return auth, pid, sid
  206 | 
  207 | 
  208 | def authority_on_save_checks():
  209 |     print('-- edge 6.1: catalog.save fills ^3 + subfields from authority')
  210 |     auth, pid, sid = _authority_store_with_tolstoy()
  211 |     cat = CatalogStore(':memory:', access_store=_seeded_access_store(),
  212 |                        authority=auth)
  213 | 
  214 |     # A record whose 700 carries only an _authority_ref (the authority id). On
  215 |     # save, the seam resolves it: fills ^a/^b/^g/^f/^9 AND the ^3 link.
  216 |     rec = {
  217 |         '920': 'PAZK',
  218 |         '200': [{'a': 'Война и мир'}],
  219 |         '700': [{'_authority_ref': pid}],
  220 |         '101': 'rus',
  221 |         '907': [{'a': 'X'}],
  222 |     }
  223 |     res = cat.save('IBIS', rec)
  224 |     check('save with authority ref succeeds', res['saved'] is True)
  225 |     saved = cat.get('IBIS', res['mfn'])
  226 |     inst = saved['700'][0]
  227 |     check('authority filled 700^a (фамилия)', inst.get('a') == 'Толстой')
  228 |     check('authority filled 700^b (инициалы)', inst.get('b') == 'Л. Н.')
  229 |     check('authority filled 700^g (расширение)', inst.get('g') == 'Лев Николаевич')
  230 |     check('authority filled 700^f (даты)', inst.get('f') == '1828-1910')
  231 |     check('^3 link == authority id', inst.get('3') == str(pid))
  232 |     check('_authority_ref marker dropped', '_authority_ref' not in inst)
  233 | 
  234 |     # subject heading 606 from an athrs record — also gets ^3 + heading subfields.
```

```py
  249 | def apply_authority_helper_checks():
  250 |     print('-- edge 6.1: apply_authority() helper (explicit call)')
  251 |     auth, pid, _sid = _authority_store_with_tolstoy()
  252 |     cat = CatalogStore(':memory:', access_store=_seeded_access_store())  # no default
  253 | 
  254 |     # Helper works with a per-call authority handle even when the store has none.
  255 |     rec = {'700': [{}]}
  256 |     cat.apply_authority(rec, '700', pid, instance=0, authority=auth)
  257 |     check('apply_authority fills ^a', rec['700'][0].get('a') == 'Толстой')
  258 |     check('apply_authority sets ^3', rec['700'][0].get('3') == str(pid))
  259 | 
  260 |     # Operator-supplied subfields not requested by the fill-map survive; the role
  261 |     # ^9 etc. is only set when the authority carries it.
  262 |     rec2 = {'701': [{'_authority_ref': pid, '4': '070'}]}  # ^4 role = operator's
  263 |     cat.resolve_authority_refs(rec2, authority=auth)

  265 |     check('resolve fills ^a + ^3 on 701',

  267 |           and rec2['701'][0].get('3') == str(pid))
  268 | 
  269 |     # A broken ^3 reference (unknown authority id) is surfaced loudly, not silent.
  270 |     raised = False

  272 |         cat.apply_authority({'700': [{}]}, '700', 999999, authority=auth)
  273 |     except A.AuthorityNotFound:

  275 |     check('unknown authority id raises AuthorityNotFound', raised)
```

```py
  302 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  303 | # Combined seam — both edges together over one shared catalog.
  304 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  305 | def combined_seam_checks():
  306 |     print('-- combined: authority-linked record + circulation flip on its copy')
  307 |     auth, pid, _sid = _authority_store_with_tolstoy()
  308 |     cat = CatalogStore(':memory:', access_store=_seeded_access_store(),
  309 |                        authority=auth)
  310 |     inv = '900900'
  311 |     rec = {
  312 |         '920': 'PAZK',
  313 |         '200': [{'a': 'Анна Каренина'}],
  314 |         '700': [{'_authority_ref': pid}],
  315 |         '101': 'rus',
  316 |         '910': [{'a': EXEMPLAR_FREE, 'b': inv}],
  317 |         '907': [{'a': 'X'}],
  318 |     }
  319 |     res = cat.save('IBIS', rec)
  320 |     saved = cat.get('IBIS', res['mfn'])
  321 |     check('record has ^3 from authority', saved['700'][0].get('3') == str(pid))
  322 |     check('record copy starts free', cat.exemplar_status('IBIS', inv) == EXEMPLAR_FREE)
  323 | 
  324 |     eng = CirculationEngine(store=CirculationStore(':memory:'),
  325 |                             policy=default_policy(), catalog=cat)
  326 |     eng.store.add_reader('R1', category='В01')
  327 |     d = eng.checkout('R1', inv, T0)
  328 |     check('checkout flips the authority-linked record copy 0->1',
  329 |           cat.exemplar_status('IBIS', inv) == EXEMPLAR_ISSUED)
  330 |     # the ^3 link is untouched by the circulation flip (it only edits 910^A)
  331 |     re_saved = cat.get('IBIS', res['mfn'])
  332 |     check('^3 survives the 910^A flip', re_saved['700'][0].get('3') == str(pid))
  333 |     eng.return_item(d.computed['loan']['id'], T0 + 3 * DAY)
  334 |     check('return flips it back 1->0', cat.exemplar_status('IBIS', inv) == EXEMPLAR_FREE)
  335 | 
  336 | 
  337 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  338 | # Edge A — Комплектование → Книговыдача (поступление → экземпляр выдаётся).
  339 | #
  340 | # AcquisitionEngine получает опциональный ``circulation`` handle. После receive()
  341 | # (ToCat создаёт 910-экземпляр со статусом «свободен») комплектование
  342 | # подтверждает через circulation, что поступивший инв.№ доступен к выдаче, и сам
  343 | # экземпляр реально выдаётся.
  344 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
```

```py
  345 | def acq_to_circulation_checks():
  346 |     print('-- edge A: поступление (acq) → экземпляр сразу выдаётся (circ)')

  348 |     circ = CirculationEngine(store=CirculationStore(':memory:'),
  349 |                              policy=default_policy(), catalog=cat,

  353 |     order = acq.create_order('Алгоритмы', author='Кормен Т.', copies=2,
  354 |                              price=1000.0)
  355 |     inv = ['ACQ-A-1', 'ACQ-A-2']

  371 |     check('received copy checks out', d.decision == ALLOW)
  372 |     check('checkout flipped the acquired copy 0->1 in catalog',
  373 |           cat.exemplar_status('IBIS', inv[0]) == EXEMPLAR_ISSUED)
  374 |     # the second, untouched copy is still free

  379 | def acq_to_circulation_backcompat_checks():

  383 |     acq = AcquisitionEngine(catalog=cat, catalog_db='IBIS')  # no circulation
  384 |     order = acq.create_order('Без выдачи', copies=1, price=10.0)

  388 |     check('standalone КСУ still accumulated',
  389 |           acq.ksu_summary('KSU-BC-1')['copies'] == 1)
  390 | 
  391 |     # Circulation wired but with NO catalog → register defaults to lendable=True
  392 |     # (circulation lends against the shifr, keeps no inventory of its own).
```

```py
  400 | 
  401 | 
  402 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  403 | # Edge B — Книгообеспеченность → Книговыдача (Кко учитывает выданные/на руках).
  404 | #
  405 | # BookProvisionEngine получает опциональный ``circulation`` handle. Выданный
  406 | # экземпляр (910^A=1) по-прежнему принадлежит фонду — Кко не должна ронять его
  407 | # в 0. С circulation-хендлом такой экземпляр считается обеспеченным, если он на
  408 | # руках (on-hand loan).
  409 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  410 | def _bp_one_disc_one_binding(cat, circ, inv):
  411 |     """A book-provision engine with one discipline (1 student) bound to one
  412 |     catalog holding keyed by ``inv``."""
  413 |     bp = BookProvisionEngine(catalog=cat, circulation=circ)
  414 |     fac = bp.add_faculty('F1', 'Факультет')
  415 |     spec = bp.add_specialty(fac, napr='09.03.01', name='Информатика')
  416 |     disc = bp.add_discipline(spec, '3001', name='Алгоритмы', students=1)
  417 |     bp.bind_literature(disc, 'Алгоритмы', kind=KIND_MAIN,
  418 |                        catalog_db='IBIS', inv_key=inv)
  419 |     return bp, disc
  420 | 
  421 | 
  422 | def bp_counts_issued_copies_checks():
  423 |     print('-- edge B: Кко учитывает выданный (на руках) экземпляр')
  424 |     cat = CatalogStore(':memory:', access_store=_seeded_access_store())
  425 |     inv = 'BP-1'
  426 |     cat.save('IBIS', _book_with_copy(inv, EXEMPLAR_FREE))
  427 |     circ = CirculationEngine(store=CirculationStore(':memory:'),
  428 |                              policy=default_policy(), catalog=cat,
  429 |                              catalog_db='IBIS')
  430 |     bp, disc = _bp_one_disc_one_binding(cat, circ, inv)
  431 | 
  432 |     # Free copy, 1 student → Кко = 1/1 = 1.0 (обеспечено).
  433 |     rep = bp.discipline_provision(disc)
  434 |     check('free copy: exemplars=1', rep['bindings'][0]['exemplars'] == 1)
  435 |     check('free copy: Кко = 1.0', rep['average_kko'] == 1.0)
  436 | 
  437 |     # Now issue the copy through circulation (910^A 0->1) to a student.
  438 |     circ.store.add_reader('S1', category='STD')
  439 |     d = circ.checkout('S1', inv, T0)
  440 |     check('copy checked out', d.decision == ALLOW)
  441 |     check('catalog now marks the copy issued',
  442 |           cat.is_available('IBIS', inv) is False)
  443 | 
  444 |     # Invariant (ребро 4.4/4.7): the copy is on a reader's hands but STILL фонд —
  445 |     # Кко must keep counting it as 1 provisioned copy, not drop to 0.
  446 |     rep2 = bp.discipline_provision(disc)
  447 |     check('issued-but-on-hand copy still counts as provisioned',
  448 |           rep2['bindings'][0]['exemplars'] == 1)
  449 |     check('Кко unchanged at 1.0 while copy on loan',
  450 |           rep2['average_kko'] == 1.0)
  451 |     check('discipline NOT under-provisioned while copy on loan',
  452 |           rep2['under_provisioned'] is False)
  453 | 
  454 |     # After return, the copy is free again — still 1 (sanity).
```

```py
  455 |     circ.return_item(d.computed['loan']['id'], T0 + DAY)
  456 |     rep3 = bp.discipline_provision(disc)

  459 | 
  460 | def bp_lost_copy_not_counted_checks():
  461 |     print('-- edge B: подтверждённая утрата покидает фонд (Кко падает)')
  462 |     cat = CatalogStore(':memory:', access_store=_seeded_access_store())
  463 |     inv = 'BP-2'
  464 |     cat.save('IBIS', _book_with_copy(inv, EXEMPLAR_FREE))
  465 |     circ = CirculationEngine(store=CirculationStore(':memory:'),
  466 |                              policy=default_policy(), catalog=cat,
  467 |                              catalog_db='IBIS')
  468 |     bp, disc = _bp_one_disc_one_binding(cat, circ, inv)
  469 | 
  470 |     circ.store.add_reader('S1', category='STD')
  471 |     d = circ.checkout('S1', inv, T0)
  472 |     # Confirm the loss: lost_status='lost' ⇒ the copy has LEFT the фонд, so
  473 |     # item_on_hand is now False and Кко drops to 0 (не обеспечено).
  474 |     circ.mark_lost(d.computed['loan']['id'], T0 + 100 * DAY, confirm=True,
  475 |                    override_grant=True)
  476 |     check('lost copy no longer on-hand in circulation',
  477 |           circ.item_on_hand(inv) is False)
  478 |     rep = bp.discipline_provision(disc)
  479 |     check('lost copy not counted as provisioned',
  480 |           rep['bindings'][0]['exemplars'] == 0)
  481 |     check('Кко drops to 0 after confirmed loss', rep['average_kko'] == 0.0)
  482 |     check('discipline under-provisioned after loss',
  483 |           rep['under_provisioned'] is True)
  484 | 
  485 | 
  486 | def bp_backcompat_checks():
  487 |     print('-- edge B back-compat: book-provision without circulation handle')
  488 |     cat = CatalogStore(':memory:', access_store=_seeded_access_store())
  489 |     inv = 'BP-BC'
  490 |     cat.save('IBIS', _book_with_copy(inv, EXEMPLAR_FREE))
  491 |     # No circulation handle: an issued copy reads as NOT provisioned (the original
  492 |     # free/not-free behaviour) — proving the new branch is opt-in.
  493 |     bp = BookProvisionEngine(catalog=cat)  # no circulation
  494 |     fac = bp.add_faculty('F1')
  495 |     spec = bp.add_specialty(fac, napr='09.03.01')
  496 |     disc = bp.add_discipline(spec, '3001', students=1)
  497 |     bp.bind_literature(disc, 'Алгоритмы', catalog_db='IBIS', inv_key=inv)
  498 |     check('free copy provisioned (no circ handle)',
  499 |           bp.discipline_provision(disc)['bindings'][0]['exemplars'] == 1)
  500 |     cat.set_exemplar_status('IBIS', inv, EXEMPLAR_ISSUED)
  501 |     check('issued copy NOT counted without circulation handle',
  502 |           bp.discipline_provision(disc)['bindings'][0]['exemplars'] == 0)
  503 | 
  504 | 
  505 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  506 | # Edge C — Книговыдача → Комплектование (списание/утрата → КСУ выбытия).
  507 | #
  508 | # CirculationEngine получает опциональный ``acquisition`` handle. Подтверждённая
  509 | # утрата (mark_lost confirm=True) пишет проводку в КСУ выбытия комплектования
```

```py
  510 | # (910^V № акта, 910^X кол-во), с кросс-ссылкой на КСУ поступления, если инв.№
  511 | # известен комплектованию.
  512 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  513 | def circ_lost_to_acq_disposal_checks():
  514 |     print('-- edge C: подтверждённая утрата (circ) → КСУ выбытия (acq)')
  515 |     cat = CatalogStore(':memory:', access_store=_seeded_access_store())
  516 |     acq = AcquisitionEngine(catalog=cat, catalog_db='IBIS')
  517 |     # Receive a copy first so acquisition KNOWS the inv.№ (cross-link to its КСУ
  518 |     # поступления).
  519 |     order = acq.create_order('Утрата', copies=1, price=500.0)
  520 |     inv = 'DISP-1'
  521 |     acq.receive(order['id'], 'KSU-RECV-9', 1, inv_numbers=[inv])
  522 | 
  523 |     circ = CirculationEngine(store=CirculationStore(':memory:'),
  524 |                              policy=default_policy(), catalog=cat,
  525 |                              catalog_db='IBIS', acquisition=acq)
  526 |     circ.store.add_reader('R1', category='В01')
  527 |     d = circ.checkout('R1', inv, T0)
  528 |     res = circ.mark_lost(d.computed['loan']['id'], T0 + 100 * DAY, confirm=True,
  529 |                          override_grant=True, ksu_disp_no='KSU-DISP-2026-1')
  530 | 
  531 |     # Invariant: a disposal row exists in acquisition's КСУ выбытия for the copy.
  532 |     check('mark_lost reports a disposal', res.computed.get('disposal') is not None)
  533 |     disp = res.computed['disposal']
  534 |     check('disposal keyed by the lost inv.№', disp['inv_no'] == inv)
  535 |     check('disposal booked under the акт списания',
  536 |           disp['ksu_disp_no'] == 'KSU-DISP-2026-1')
  537 |     check('disposal reason is lost', disp['reason'] == 'lost')
  538 |     # cross-link to the original КСУ поступления (inv.№ known to acquisition).
  539 |     check('disposal cross-links the КСУ поступления',
  540 |           disp['ksu_recv_no'] == 'KSU-RECV-9')
  541 |     check('disposal ref carries the loan id',
  542 |           disp['ref'] == str(d.computed['loan']['id']))
  543 | 
  544 |     # acquisition's выбытие rollup sees one copy written off.
  545 |     summ = acq.disposal_summary('KSU-DISP-2026-1')
  546 |     check('acq disposal summary counts 1 copy', summ['copies'] == 1)
  547 | 
  548 |     # Idempotent: re-confirming the same loss doesn't double the disposal row.
  549 |     circ.mark_lost(d.computed['loan']['id'], T0 + 100 * DAY, confirm=True,
  550 |                    override_grant=True, ksu_disp_no='KSU-DISP-2026-1')
  551 |     check('disposal stays idempotent on re-confirm',
  552 |           acq.disposal_summary('KSU-DISP-2026-1')['copies'] == 1)
  553 | 
  554 | 
  555 | def circ_lost_default_act_checks():
  556 |     print('-- edge C: default акт списания when none supplied')
  557 |     acq = AcquisitionEngine()  # standalone acquisition store
  558 |     circ = CirculationEngine(store=CirculationStore(':memory:'),
  559 |                              policy=default_policy(), acquisition=acq)
  560 |     circ.store.add_reader('R1', category='В01')
  561 |     inv = 'DISP-DEF'

  564 |                          override_grant=True)  # no ksu_disp_no
```

```py
  569 |     check('disposal of unknown inv has no recv cross-link',

  571 |     check('disposal still records the copy', disp['inv_no'] == inv)
  572 | 
  573 | 
  574 | def circ_lost_backcompat_checks():
  575 |     print('-- edge C back-compat: circulation lost without acquisition handle')
  576 |     # No acquisition handle → mark_lost works exactly as before; no disposal key.
  577 |     circ = CirculationEngine(store=CirculationStore(':memory:'),
  578 |                              policy=default_policy())  # no acquisition
  579 |     circ.store.add_reader('R1', category='В01')
  580 |     d = circ.checkout('R1', 'NOACQ-1', T0)
  581 |     res = circ.mark_lost(d.computed['loan']['id'], T0 + 100 * DAY, confirm=True,
  582 |                          override_grant=True)
  583 |     check('standalone mark_lost still ALLOW', res.decision == ALLOW)
  584 |     check('standalone mark_lost still charges replacement',
  585 |           res.computed.get('replacement_value') is not None)
  586 |     check('standalone mark_lost reports no disposal',
  587 |           'disposal' not in res.computed)
  588 |     # An unconfirmed candidate never writes a disposal even with a handle wired.
  589 |     acq = AcquisitionEngine()
  590 |     circ2 = CirculationEngine(store=CirculationStore(':memory:'),
  591 |                               policy=default_policy(), acquisition=acq)
  592 |     circ2.store.add_reader('R2', category='В01')
  593 |     d2 = circ2.checkout('R2', 'CAND-1', T0)
  594 |     res2 = circ2.mark_lost(d2.computed['loan']['id'], T0 + 100 * DAY,
  595 |                            confirm=False)  # candidate only
  596 |     check('lost_candidate writes no disposal', 'disposal' not in res2.computed)
  597 |     check('acq disposal ledger empty for unconfirmed candidate',
  598 |           acq.store.disposal_for_inv('CAND-1') == [])
  599 | 
  600 | 
  601 | def main():
  602 |     checkout_return_flip_checks()
  603 |     availability_read_checks()
  604 |     circulation_backcompat_checks()
  605 |     authority_on_save_checks()
  606 |     apply_authority_helper_checks()
  607 |     authority_backcompat_checks()
  608 |     combined_seam_checks()
  609 |     # Edge A — Комплектование → Книговыдача.
  610 |     acq_to_circulation_checks()
  611 |     acq_to_circulation_backcompat_checks()
  612 |     # Edge B — Книгообеспеченность → Книговыдача.
  613 |     bp_counts_issued_copies_checks()
  614 |     bp_lost_copy_not_counted_checks()
  615 |     bp_backcompat_checks()
  616 |     # Edge C — Книговыдача → Комплектование (КСУ выбытия).
  617 |     circ_lost_to_acq_disposal_checks()
  618 |     circ_lost_default_act_checks()
  619 |     circ_lost_backcompat_checks()
```

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """Тесты офлайн-адаптера мастер-файлов ИРБИС64 (#225, эпик #223 — tools/irbis_mst.py).
    3 | 
    4 | Проверяют прямое чтение `.mst`+`.xrf` с диска (без сервера) на **синтетических**
    5 | фикстурах: пара `.mst`/`.xrf` строится в памяти/во временном каталоге функцией
    6 | ``build_mst_xrf`` (точная инверсия парсера) — НИКАКИХ реальных данных/ПДн.
    7 | 
    8 | Покрытие:
    9 |   1. форма записи: ``read_record`` возвращает канон ``{поле: [{подполе: значение}]}``
   10 |      (как ``access.catalog`` / ``tools.migrate_irbis``); подполя -> {код: значение}
   11 |      с кодами в нижнем регистре; повторяющееся поле -> список экземпляров.
   12 |   2. голое поле без '^' -> голая строка (а не dict).
   13 |   3. CP1251: кириллица декодируется корректно.
   14 |   4. удаление: запись с битом STATUS 0x01 И запись с флагом удаления в `.xrf`
   15 |      обе пропускаются (``read_record`` -> None, ``read_records`` их не отдаёт).
   16 |   5. ``max_mfn`` = NXTMFN-1 из управляющей записи; ``read_records`` перебирает
   17 |      все живые записи; ``list_databases`` находит БД по ``<db>.mst``.
   18 |   6. защита: короткая/битая запись, MFN вне диапазона, директорий-вне-границ ->
   19 |      пропуск со счётчиком предупреждений, без исключения.
   20 | 
   21 | Запускается автономно (дом. стиль tests/test_migrate.py)::
   22 | 
   23 |     py -3 tests/test_irbis_mst.py   ->  'ok …' + 'N passed, M failed', exit!=0
   24 | 
   25 | Без зависимости от живого сервера и без реальных файлов БД (CI — Linux, без Datai).
   26 | """
   27 | import os
   28 | import struct
   29 | import sys
   30 | import tempfile
   31 | 
   32 | sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))))

   37 |     STATUS_DELETED, XRF_DELETED_FLAG, MST_LEADER_SIZE, SYSTEM_GUID_TAG,
   38 | )

   41 | FAIL = [0]
   42 | 
   43 | 
   44 | def check(name, cond):
   45 |     if cond:
   46 |         PASS[0] += 1
   47 |         print('  ok  ', name)
   48 |     else:
   49 |         FAIL[0] += 1
```

```py
   53 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   54 | # Помощник: записать синтетическую пару .mst/.xrf во временный каталог БД.
   55 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   56 | def _write_db(tmp, db, records, deleted_mfns=()):
   57 |     """Создать ``<tmp>/<db>/<db>.mst|.xrf`` из записей. Возвращает data_path=tmp."""
   58 |     db_dir = os.path.join(tmp, db)
   59 |     os.makedirs(db_dir, exist_ok=True)
   60 |     mst, xrf = build_mst_xrf(records, deleted_mfns=deleted_mfns)
   61 |     with open(os.path.join(db_dir, db + '.mst'), 'wb') as f:
   62 |         f.write(mst)
   63 |     with open(os.path.join(db_dir, db + '.xrf'), 'wb') as f:
   64 |         f.write(xrf)
   65 |     return tmp
   66 | 
   67 | 
   68 | # Набор записей с подполями, голым полем, кириллицей, повтором и удалённой.
   69 | def _sample_records():
   70 |     return [
   71 |         # MFN 1 — заглавие с подполями + голое поле языка + повтор автора +
   72 |         # обязательное 920 (вид документа), чтобы запись проходила ФЛК.
   73 |         {
   74 |             '200': [{'a': 'Основы каталогизации', 'f': 'Иванова И.И.'}],
   75 |             '101': ['rus'],
   76 |             '700': [{'a': 'Иванова И.И.'}, {'a': 'Петров П.П.'}],
   77 |             '910': [{'a': '0', 'b': '1024365'}],
   78 |             '920': ['PAZK'],
   79 |         },
   80 |         # MFN 2 — простая запись с кириллицей в значении.
   81 |         {
   82 |             '200': [{'a': 'Библиотечное дело'}],
   83 |             '610': ['каталогизация'],
   84 |         },
   85 |         # MFN 3 — будет помечена удалённой по STATUS.
   86 |         {
   87 |             '200': [{'a': 'Удалённая запись'}],
   88 |         },
   89 |         # MFN 4 — будет помечена удалённой по флагу .xrf.
   90 |         {
   91 |             '200': [{'a': 'Тоже удалена'}],
   92 |         },
   93 |     ]
   94 | 
   95 | 
   96 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   97 | # 1+2+3. Форма записи, подполя, голые поля, CP1251.

   99 | def shape_checks():
  100 |     with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
  101 |         _write_db(tmp, 'TEST', _sample_records())
  102 |         rec = read_record(tmp, 'TEST', 1)
  103 |         check('read_record возвращает dict', isinstance(rec, dict))
  104 |         check('поле -> список экземпляров', isinstance(rec.get('200'), list))
```

```py
  160 |         os.makedirs(db_dir)
  161 |         mst, xrf = build_mst_xrf([{'200': [{'a': 'Запись'}]}])

  164 |         # Перепишем XRF-слот 1: тот же offset, но с флагом удаления.
  165 |         low, _high = struct.unpack('>2i', xrf[:8])
  166 |         low_u = (low & 0xffffffff) | XRF_DELETED_FLAG
  167 |         signed = low_u - 0x100000000 if low_u & 0x80000000 else low_u
  168 |         patched = struct.pack('>2i', signed, 0) + xrf[8:12]
  169 |         with open(os.path.join(db_dir, 'X.xrf'), 'wb') as f:
  170 |             f.write(patched)
  171 |         check('флаг удаления в .xrf -> запись пропущена',
```

<!-- ─── страница 393 ─── -->

```py
  270 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  271 | # 8. Кодировка: CP1251-фикстуры читаются корректно (базовый формат), а UTF-8-
  272 | #    поля (встречаются на реальных установках ИРБИС64) — тоже декодируются.
  273 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  274 | def encoding_checks():
  275 |     with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
  276 |         # build_mst_xrf пишет в CP1251 — проверяем round-trip кириллицы.
  277 |         _write_db(tmp, 'C', [{'200': [{'a': 'Каталогизация и поиск'}]}])
  278 |         rec = read_record(tmp, 'C', 1)
  279 |         check('CP1251 round-trip кириллицы', rec['200'][0]['a'] == 'Каталогизация и поиск')
  280 | 
  281 |         # Соберём запись с UTF-8-полем вручную (как в реальной БД на этой машине).
  282 |         db_dir = os.path.join(tmp, 'U')
  283 |         os.makedirs(db_dir)
  284 |         title = 'Компьютерное моделирование'
  285 |         val = ('^a' + title).encode('utf-8')        # UTF-8, не CP1251
  286 |         nvf = 1
  287 |         base = MST_LEADER_SIZE + nvf * 12
  288 |         directory = struct.pack('>3i', 200, 0, len(val))
  289 |         mfrl = base + len(val)
  290 |         leader = struct.pack('>8i', 1, mfrl, 0, 0, base, nvf, 1, 0x20)
  291 |         ctl = struct.pack('>4i', 0, 2, 0, 0) + b'\x00' * (36 - 16)
  292 |         rec_bytes = leader + directory + val
  293 |         with open(os.path.join(db_dir, 'U.mst'), 'wb') as f:
  294 |             f.write(ctl + rec_bytes)
  295 |         with open(os.path.join(db_dir, 'U.xrf'), 'wb') as f:
  296 |             f.write(struct.pack('>2i', len(ctl), 0) + b'\x00' * 4)
  297 |         urec = read_record(tmp, 'U', 1)
  298 |         check('UTF-8 поле декодировано корректно', urec['200'][0]['a'] == title)
  299 | 
  300 | 
  301 | def main():
  302 |     shape_checks()
  303 |     deleted_checks()
  304 |     xrf_deleted_flag_checks()
  305 |     control_and_listing_checks()
  306 |     robustness_checks()
  307 |     guid_field_checks()
  308 |     encoding_checks()
  309 |     print('\n%d passed, %d failed' % (PASS[0], FAIL[0]))
  310 |     sys.exit(1 if FAIL[0] else 0)
  311 | 
  312 | 
  313 | if __name__ == '__main__':
  314 |     main()
```

### Файл: `irbis-web/backend/tests/test_migrate.py`  · строк: 365

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """ИРБИС → Biblio migrator tests (pilot enabler, epic #223 — tools/migrate_irbis.py).
    3 | 
    4 | Exercises the migrator end-to-end against a **FakeIrbis** source (synthetic
    5 | records + readers, NO live server) loading into in-memory Biblio stores:
    6 | 
    7 |   1. catalog: synthetic IBIS records map + load with correct fields/exemplars
    8 |      (200/700/210/910 preserved; subfield codes canonicalized to 200^a/910^b);
    9 |      the loaded records are findable by T=/A=/IN= and the 910 exemplar survives.
   10 |   2. deleted / empty / unreadable source records are SKIPPED (counted), nothing
```

```py
   11 |      loaded for them.
   12 |   3. idempotent re-run: migrating the same source twice does NOT duplicate — the
   13 |      source-MFN idempotency key resolves the existing target record and upserts.
   14 |   4. readers: RDR records map to circulation readers (ticket + category) and the
   15 |      reader display name is stored as CIPHERTEXT at rest (V1 crypto seam) — a raw
   16 |      read of the at-rest column never reveals the plaintext name. Idempotent by
   17 |      ticket; PII never appears in plaintext in the store.
   18 |   5. dry-run: reads + maps + reports the would-load counts but writes NOTHING
   19 |      (catalog empty, no reader rows, no PII persisted).
   20 |   6. report shape + redaction units.
   21 | 
   22 | Standalone-runnable, mirroring the house style of tests/test_catalog.py::
   23 | 
   24 |     py -3.12 tests/test_migrate.py   ->  'ok …' lines + 'N passed, M failed', exit!=0
   25 | 
   26 | NO live-server dependency. (A `--dry-run` smoke against a real 127.0.0.1:6666 is a
   27 | valid manual validation but is deliberately NOT in this committed suite.)

   48 | def check(name, cond):

   50 |         PASS[0] += 1
   51 |         print('  ok  ', name)

   53 |         FAIL[0] += 1
   54 |         print('  FAIL', name)

   56 | 
   57 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   58 | # FakeIrbis — a synthetic source satisfying the migrator's read interface
   59 | # (max_mfn / read_record). Records are in the irbis.parser shape:
   60 | #   {'mfn','status','fields':[{'tag','value','text','subfields'}…]}
   61 | # Subfield codes are UPPER-case, mirroring the live server (the mapper lower-cases
   62 | # them). A record may be: a dict (returned), 'DELETED' (status carries the delete
   63 | # bit), 'EMPTY' (no fields), or 'ERROR' (read raises, as a locked/-605 MFN does).
   64 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   65 | class FakeIrbisError(Exception):
```

<!-- ─── страница 395 ─── -->

```py
   66 |     pass
   67 | 
   68 | 
   69 | class FakeIrbis:
   70 |     def __init__(self, dbs):
   71 |         # dbs: {db_name: [record-or-sentinel, …]} 1-indexed by position+1
   72 |         self._dbs = dbs
   73 | 
   74 |     def max_mfn(self, db):
   75 |         return len(self._dbs.get(db, []))
   76 | 
   77 |     def read_record(self, db, mfn):
   78 |         recs = self._dbs.get(db, [])
   79 |         if mfn < 1 or mfn > len(recs):
   80 |             raise FakeIrbisError('out of range')
   81 |         item = recs[mfn - 1]
   82 |         if item == 'ERROR':
   83 |             raise FakeIrbisError('-605 locked/deleted')
   84 |         if item == 'EMPTY':
   85 |             return {'mfn': mfn, 'status': '', 'fields': []}
   86 |         if item == 'DELETED':
   87 |             return {'mfn': mfn, 'status': '1', 'fields': [_fld('200', subs={'A': 'Удалённая'})]}
   88 |         return item
   89 | 
   90 |     def close(self):
   91 |         pass
   92 | 
   93 | 
   94 | def _fld(tag, value=None, text='', subs=None):
   95 |     """Build a parser-shape field dict (UPPER-case subfield codes, like live)."""
   96 |     subs = subs or {}
   97 |     if value is None:
   98 |         value = ('^' + '^'.join('%s%s' % (k, v) for k, v in subs.items())) if subs else text
   99 |     return {'tag': tag, 'value': value, 'text': text, 'subfields': dict(subs)}
  100 | 
  101 | 
  102 | def _bib(mfn, title, author=None, inv=None, status=''):
  103 |     """A synthetic IBIS bibliographic record (parser shape)."""
  104 |     fields = [
  105 |         _fld('920', text='PAZK'),
  106 |         _fld('200', subs={'A': title, 'F': 'отв. ' + (author or 'N.N.')}),
  107 |         _fld('101', text='rus'),
  108 |         _fld('907', subs={'A': 'Каталогизатор'}),
  109 |     ]
  110 |     if author:
  111 |         fields.append(_fld('700', subs={'A': author}))
  112 |     if inv:
  113 |         fields.append(_fld('910', subs={'A': '0', 'B': inv, 'D': 'ХР'}))
  114 |     fields.append(_fld('610', text='тест'))
  115 |     fields.append(_fld('210', subs={'A': 'СПб', 'D': '2013', 'C': 'Изд-во'}))
  116 |     return {'mfn': mfn, 'status': status, 'fields': fields}
  117 | 
  118 | 
  119 | def _reader(mfn, ticket, surname, name='', patronymic='', email='', category='В01',
  120 |             status=''):
```

```py
  169 |     check('210 imprint preserved (210^d)', rec['210'][0]['d'] == '2013')
```

```py
  275 |     check('reader name still decrypts after re-run',
```

```py
  318 |     check('full run loaded 1 reader', rep['readers_loaded'] == 1)
```

### Файл: `irbis-web/backend/tests/test_migrate_introspect.py`  · строк: 693

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """Тесты интроспекции источника + HTTP-эндпойнтов миграции (epic #223, #225).
    3 | 
    4 | Дополняют tests/test_migrate.py (тот покрывает сам перенос). Здесь проверяется:
    5 | 
    6 |   1. ИНТРОСПЕКЦИЯ (tools.migrate_irbis.introspect) на FakeIrbis с синтетическими
    7 |      записями, ВКЛЮЧАЯ нештатное доппole: перечисляет БД, для каждой отдаёт число
    8 |      записей и инвентарь полей; флаг ``custom`` стоит на нештатном поле (и на
    9 |      нештатном подполе штатного поля) и НЕ стоит на штатных.
   10 |   2. АДАПТИВНЫЙ экспорт: миграция переносит запись с доппolem БЕЗ потери —
   11 |      кастомное поле/подполе сохраняется в целевом каталоге (field-agnostic, не
   12 |      whitelist).
   13 |   3. ЛОКАЛЬНЫЙ адаптер: LocalSource поверх инжектированного адаптера приводит
   14 |      каноническую запись к parser-shape и работает с интроспекцией; при
   15 |      отсутствии tools.irbis_mst поднимается LocalAdapterUnavailable (graceful).
   16 |   4. ЭНДПОЙНТЫ через core.Api.route(): POST /api/admin/migrate/inspect и
   17 |      /api/admin/migrate/run работают для super-admin (200), отказывают
   18 |      reader/guest/не-админ-staff (403), а креды источника НЕ эхо-отражаются в
   19 |      ответе и НЕ попадают в аудит (только redacted host:port/user, без пароля).
   20 | 
   21 | Подключён в раннер test_access.py (модуль в списке). Standalone в стиле дома:
   22 |   py tests/test_migrate_introspect.py  -> ok ... + "N passed, M failed" + exit code.
   23 | 
   24 | НЕТ зависимости от живого сервера — всё на FakeIrbis / инжектированных стора́х.
   25 | """
   26 | import os
   27 | import sys
   28 |
```

```py
   56 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   57 | # FakeIrbis с перечислением БД (list_databases) — синтетический источник для
   58 | # интроспекции. Запись — в parser-shape; коды подполей UPPER-case (как у живого).
   59 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   60 | def _fld(tag, value=None, text='', subs=None):
   61 |     subs = subs or {}
   62 |     if value is None:
   63 |         value = ('^' + '^'.join('%s%s' % (k, v) for k, v in subs.items())) if subs else text
   64 |     return {'tag': tag, 'value': value, 'text': text, 'subfields': dict(subs)}
   65 | 
   66 | 
   67 | def _bib_with_custom(mfn, title):
   68 |     """Библиозапись со штатными полями (200/700/210/910) И нештатными:
   69 |     поле 996 (доппole целиком) + подполе 200^z (нештатное подполе штатного поля)."""
   70 |     return {'mfn': mfn, 'status': '', 'fields': [
   71 |         _fld('920', text='PAZK'),
   72 |         _fld('200', subs={'A': title, 'F': 'отв.', 'Z': 'локальное подполе'}),  # ^z custom
   73 |         _fld('700', subs={'A': 'Автор А.'}),
   74 |         _fld('210', subs={'A': 'СПб', 'D': '2020'}),
   75 |         _fld('910', subs={'A': '0', 'B': '777', 'D': 'ХР'}),
   76 |         _fld('996', subs={'Q': 'институтские данные'}),                         # доппole
   77 |         _fld('610', text='ключевое'),
   78 |     ]}
   79 | 
   80 | 
   81 | def _reader(mfn, ticket, surname):
   82 |     return {'mfn': mfn, 'status': '', 'fields': [
   83 |         _fld('30', text=ticket), _fld('920', text='RDR'),
   84 |         _fld('50', text='В01'), _fld('10', text=surname),
   85 |         _fld('555', text='нештатное читательское поле'),                        # доппole RDR
```

<!-- ─── страница 402 ─── -->

```py
   86 |     ]}
   87 | 
   88 | 
   89 | class FakeIrbisError(Exception):
   90 |     pass
   91 | 
   92 | 
   93 | class FakeIrbis:
   94 |     """Источник с перечислением БД (list_databases) + чтением записей."""
   95 | 
   96 |     def __init__(self, dbs, names=None):
   97 |         # dbs: {code: [parser-record, …]}; names: {code: human-name}
   98 |         self._dbs = dbs
   99 |         self._names = names or {}
  100 | 
  101 |     def list_databases(self):
  102 |         return [{'code': code, 'name': self._names.get(code, code)} for code in self._dbs]
  103 | 
  104 |     def max_mfn(self, db):
  105 |         return len(self._dbs.get(db, []))
  106 | 
  107 |     def read_record(self, db, mfn):
  108 |         recs = self._dbs.get(db, [])
  109 |         if mfn < 1 or mfn > len(recs):
  110 |             raise FakeIrbisError('out of range')
  111 |         return recs[mfn - 1]
  112 | 
  113 |     def close(self):
  114 |         pass
  115 | 
  116 | 
  117 | class _CountingSource(FakeIrbis):
  118 |     """FakeIrbis, считающий обращения: ``reads`` = число read_record,
  119 |     ``maxmfn_calls`` = число max_mfn. Так фаза 1 проверяется на «ноль чтений
  120 |     записей», а фаза 2 — на верхний предел выборки."""
  121 | 
  122 |     def __init__(self, dbs, names=None):
  123 |         super().__init__(dbs, names)
  124 |         self.reads = 0
  125 |         self.maxmfn_calls = 0
  126 | 
  127 |     def max_mfn(self, db):
  128 |         self.maxmfn_calls += 1
  129 |         return super().max_mfn(db)
  130 | 
  131 |     def read_record(self, db, mfn):
  132 |         self.reads += 1
  133 |         return super().read_record(db, mfn)
  134 | 
  135 | 
  136 | def _fresh_targets():
  137 |     access = AccessStore(':memory:')
  138 |     catalog = CatalogStore(':memory:', access_store=access)
  139 |     circ = CirculationStore(':memory:')
  140 |     return Targets(catalog, circ, access, catalog_db='IBIS', tenant='public')
```

```py
  318 | 
  319 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  320 | # 3. Локальный адаптер: bridge каноническая -> parser-shape + graceful absence.
  321 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  322 | class FakeMstAdapter:
  323 |     """Инжектируемый адаптер вида tools.irbis_mst (канонические записи)."""
  324 | 
  325 |     def __init__(self, dbs):
  326 |         # dbs: {code: [ {tag: [значение|{подполе: значение}]}, … ]}
  327 |         self._dbs = dbs
  328 | 
  329 |     def list_databases(self, path):
  330 |         return sorted(self._dbs)              # список кодов-строк, как реальный адаптер
  331 | 
  332 |     def max_mfn(self, path, db):
  333 |         return len(self._dbs.get(db, []))
  334 | 
  335 |     def read_records(self, path, db):
  336 |         for i, rec in enumerate(self._dbs.get(db, []), start=1):
  337 |             yield i, rec                       # (mfn, каноническая запись)
  338 | 
  339 | 
  340 | def local_source_checks():
  341 |     print('-- локальный адаптер: bridge + интроспекция через LocalSource')
  342 |     adapter = FakeMstAdapter({
  343 |         'IBIS': [
  344 |             {'200': [{'a': 'Локальная книга', 'z': 'нештатное'}],
  345 |              '700': [{'a': 'Локальный автор'}],
  346 |              '996': [{'q': 'доппole'}],
  347 |              '920': ['PAZK']},
  348 |         ],
  349 |     })
  350 |     src = LocalSource('/любой/путь', adapter=adapter)
  351 |     check('LocalSource.list_databases -> [{code,name}]',
  352 |           src.list_databases() == [{'code': 'IBIS', 'name': 'IBIS'}])
  353 |     check('LocalSource.max_mfn', src.max_mfn('IBIS') == 1)
  354 |     parsed = src.read_record('IBIS', 1)
  355 |     check('read_record отдаёт parser-shape (есть fields)',
  356 |           isinstance(parsed.get('fields'), list))
  357 |     tags = {f['tag'] for f in parsed['fields']}
  358 |     check('bridge сохранил все теги (вкл. доппole 996)', {'200', '700', '996', '920'} <= tags)
  359 | 
  360 |     # интроспекция поверх LocalSource распознаёт доппole.
```

```py
  361 |     plan = introspect(src, dbs=['IBIS'])
  362 |     ibis = plan['databases'][0]
  363 |     by_tag = {f['tag']: f for f in ibis['fields']}
  364 |     check('LOCAL: 996 помечено custom', by_tag['996']['custom'] is True)

  367 | 
  368 | 
  369 | class _CountingMstAdapter:
  370 |     """Адаптер вида tools.irbis_mst, считающий, СКОЛЬКО записей реально вычитано из
  371 |     потока ``read_records`` — так проверяется, что LocalSource.read_records(limit=)
  372 |     прерывает чтение и не материализует всю крупную БД ради выборки."""
  373 | 
  374 |     def __init__(self, db, count):
  375 |         self._db = db
  376 |         self._count = count
  377 |         self.yielded = 0
  378 | 
  379 |     def list_databases(self, path):
  380 |         return [self._db]
  381 | 
  382 |     def max_mfn(self, path, db):
  383 |         return self._count if db == self._db else 0
  384 | 
  385 |     def read_records(self, path, db):
  386 |         # «Бесконечно крупная» БД: яркий маркер, что итерация ДОЛЖНА оборваться.
  387 |         for i in range(1, self._count + 1):
  388 |             self.yielded += 1
  389 |             yield i, {'200': [{'a': 'rec %d' % i}], '996': [{'q': 'допполе'}]}
  390 | 
  391 | 
  392 | def local_source_sample_cap_checks():
  393 |     print('-- локальный адаптер: read_records(limit=) обрывает чтение крупной БД')
  394 |     big = INSPECT_SAMPLE_SIZE + 500
  395 |     adapter = _CountingMstAdapter('BIG', big)
  396 |     src = LocalSource('/любой/путь', adapter=adapter)
  397 |     # потоковый перебор с лимитом отдаёт ровно limit записей и НЕ дочитывает остаток.
  398 |     got = list(src.read_records('BIG', limit=INSPECT_SAMPLE_SIZE))
  399 |     check('read_records(limit) отдаёт ровно limit записей',
  400 |           len(got) == INSPECT_SAMPLE_SIZE)
  401 |     check('read_records(limit) НЕ вычитал всю БД (поток оборван)',
  402 |           adapter.yielded <= INSPECT_SAMPLE_SIZE + 1)
  403 |     check('read_records отдаёт parser-shape', isinstance(got[0].get('fields'), list))
  404 | 
  405 |     # фаза 2 через introspect использует потоковый путь => не материализует всю БД.
  406 |     adapter2 = _CountingMstAdapter('BIG', big)
  407 |     src2 = LocalSource('/любой/путь', adapter=adapter2)
  408 |     plan = introspect(src2, dbs=['BIG'])
  409 |     d = plan['databases'][0]
  410 |     check('фаза 2 LOCAL: recordCount = вся БД (max_mfn)', d['recordCount'] == big)
  411 |     check('фаза 2 LOCAL: 996 помечено custom', any(
  412 |         f['tag'] == '996' and f['custom'] for f in d['fields']))
  413 |     check('фаза 2 LOCAL: прочитано НЕ больше выборки (крупная БД не материализована)',
  414 |           adapter2.yielded <= INSPECT_SAMPLE_SIZE + 1)
  415 | 
```

```py
  416 | 
  417 | def canonical_bridge_checks():
  418 |     print('-- bridge: canonical_to_parsed сохраняет поля и подполя')
  419 |     rec = {'101': ['rus'], '200': [{'a': 'Заглавие', 'f': 'отв.'}],
  420 |            '910': [{'a': '0', 'b': '123'}, {'a': '1', 'b': '456'}]}
```

```py
  663 |             check('без сессии -> 401/403 на %s' % path, st in (401, 403))
```

### Файл: `irbis-web/backend/tests/test_notifications.py`  · строк: 332

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """Notification engine tests (gap A6, epic #188).
    3 | 
    4 | Covers the self-contained notification engine (access/notifications.py):
    5 | 
    6 |   1. EventCatalog: SPEC event set present with template + default channel chain;
    7 |      register/override; unknown event rejected at enqueue.
    8 |   2. Rendering: stdlib {placeholder} fallback (PFT engine A1 absent today) fills
    9 |      known slots and leaves unknown ones verbatim (no crash, no dropped notice).
   10 |   3. Queue happy path: enqueue -> process delivers via the preferred channel and
   11 |      marks the row 'sent' with the delivering channel recorded.
   12 |   4. Dedup: a second enqueue of the same logical event is a no-op (single send);
   13 |      explicit dedup_key honoured.
   14 |   5. Opt-out: an opted-out reader yields a 'suppressed' row, never sent; '*'
   15 |      opts out of everything.
   16 |   6. Preferences: per-event channel order overrides the catalog default.
   17 |   7. Fallback chain: first channel fails -> next channel delivers (first-success).
   18 |   8. Retry: a transiently-failing chain bumps attempts and re-queues; once the
   19 |      transient clears the next process_once delivers; exhausted chain -> 'failed'.
   20 | 
   21 | Standalone-runnable in the house style of tests/test_seeding.py:
   22 |   py -3.12 tests/test_notifications.py  ->  'ok ...' lines + 'N passed, M failed'
   23 |   and exit code 1 on any failure (so CI fails loud).
   24 | """
   25 | import os
   26 | import sys
   27 | 
   28 | sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))))
   29 | from access import notifications as nt
   30 | 
   31 | PASS = [0]
   32 | FAIL = [0]
   33 | 
   34 | 
   35 | def check(name, cond):
   36 |     if cond:
   37 |         PASS[0] += 1
   38 |         print('  ok  ', name)
   39 |     else:
   40 |         FAIL[0] += 1
   41 |         print('  FAIL', name)
   42 | 
   43 | 
   44 | def _q(catalog=None):
   45 |     """A fresh in-memory queue (each test isolated)."""
   46 |     return nt.NotificationQueue(':memory:', catalog=catalog)
   47 | 
   48 | 
   49 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   50 | # 1. Event catalog.
   51 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   52 | # Every event CirculationEngine._emit raises (grep circulation.py for `_emit(`).
```

```py
   53 | # A name here with no catalog entry is an intent that falls through silently —
   54 | # rendered/sent never. Keep this list in lock-step with circulation.py.
   55 | CIRCULATION_EVENTS = (
   56 |     'hold_ready', 'due_soon', 'overdue', 'fine_charged',
   57 |     'renewal_confirmed', 'fine_paid', 'lost_confirmed', 'staff_alert',
   58 | )
   59 | 
   60 | 
   61 | def catalog_checks():
   62 |     print('-- event catalog')
   63 |     cat = nt.EventCatalog()
   64 |     for ev in ('hold_ready', 'due_soon', 'overdue', 'fine_charged'):
   65 |         check('catalog has %s' % ev, cat.has(ev))
   66 |     check('hold_ready template has subject+body',
   67 |           set(cat.template('hold_ready')) >= {'subject', 'body'})
   68 |     check('hold_ready default channels are a non-empty list',
   69 |           isinstance(cat.default_channels('hold_ready'), list)
   70 |           and cat.default_channels('hold_ready'))
   71 |     check('overdue prioritises sms first',
   72 |           cat.default_channels('overdue')[0] == 'sms')
   73 | 
   74 |     # No circulation event may fall through for lack of a template: each must
   75 |     # have a non-empty subject+body and at least one default channel.
   76 |     for ev in CIRCULATION_EVENTS:
   77 |         check('catalog covers circulation event %s' % ev, cat.has(ev))
   78 |         if not cat.has(ev):
   79 |             continue
   80 |         tmpl = cat.template(ev)
   81 |         check('%s has non-empty subject+body' % ev,
   82 |               bool(tmpl.get('subject')) and bool(tmpl.get('body')))
   83 |         check('%s has a non-empty channel chain' % ev,
   84 |               isinstance(cat.default_channels(ev), list)
   85 |               and bool(cat.default_channels(ev)))
   86 | 
   87 |     # staff_alert is staff-facing: an e-mail default, and addressed to the
   88 |     # librarian (not a reader-style greeting / SMS).
   89 |     check('staff_alert defaults to email', cat.default_channels('staff_alert') == ['email'])
   90 |     check('staff_alert subject reads as a staff notice',
   91 |           'Служебное' in cat.template('staff_alert')['subject'])
   92 | 
   93 |     # register/override does not leak into the class-level DEFAULTS
   94 |     cat.register('renewal_ok', {'subject': 'OK', 'body': 'Renewed {title}'},
   95 |                  ['email'])
   96 |     check('register adds a custom event', cat.has('renewal_ok'))
   97 |     check('register does not mutate DEFAULTS',
   98 |           'renewal_ok' not in nt.EventCatalog.DEFAULTS)
   99 | 
  100 |     # unknown event rejected at enqueue
  101 |     q = _q()
  102 |     raised = False
  103 |     try:
  104 |         q.enqueue('not_a_real_event', 'r1', {})
  105 |     except KeyError:
  106 |         raised = True
  107 |     check('enqueue rejects unknown event', raised)
```

```py
  108 | 
  109 | 
  110 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  111 | # 2. Rendering — stdlib fallback (PFT/A1 not built yet).
  112 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  113 | def render_checks():
  114 |     print('-- template rendering (stdlib fallback)')
  115 |     out = nt.render('«{title}» до {due_date}', {'title': 'Война и мир',
  116 |                                                 'due_date': '2026-07-01'})
  117 |     check('render fills known placeholders',
  118 |           out == '«Война и мир» до 2026-07-01')
  119 |     check('render leaves unknown placeholder verbatim (no crash)',
  120 |           nt.render('hi {missing}', {'title': 'x'}) == 'hi {missing}')
  121 |     check('render of None -> empty string', nt.render(None, {}) == '')
  122 |     check('render None value -> empty', nt.render('x={v}', {'v': None}) == 'x=')
  123 | 
  124 | 
  125 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  126 | # 3. Happy path: enqueue -> process delivers via preferred channel.
  127 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  128 | def deliver_checks():
  129 |     print('-- enqueue -> process delivers')
  130 |     q = _q()
  131 |     email = nt.MemoryChannel('email')
  132 |     sms = nt.MemoryChannel('sms')
  133 | 
  134 |     res = q.enqueue('hold_ready', 'reader-42',
  135 |                     {'reader_name': 'Иван', 'title': 'Идиот',
  136 |                      'hold_until': '2026-06-30', 'pickup': 'Абонемент',
  137 |                      'ref': 'hold-100'})
  138 |     check('enqueue returns pending row', res['status'] == 'pending'
  139 |           and res['deduped'] is False)
  140 |     check('one pending in queue', q.pending_count() == 1)
  141 | 
  142 |     summary = q.process_once({'email': email, 'sms': sms})
  143 |     check('process reports one sent', summary['sent'] == 1)
  144 |     check('delivered via email (first preferred)', len(email.sent) == 1)
  145 |     check('sms not used (email succeeded)', len(sms.sent) == 0)
  146 | 
  147 |     row = q.get(res['id'])
  148 |     check('row marked sent', row['status'] == 'sent')
  149 |     check('delivering channel recorded', row['channel'] == 'email')
  150 |     check('attempts == 1', row['attempts'] == 1)
  151 |     check('rendered body reached channel',
  152 |           'Идиот' in email.sent[0]['body'])
  153 |     check('rendered subject reached channel',
  154 |           'Идиот' in email.sent[0]['subject'])
  155 | 
  156 |     # idempotent processing: nothing pending -> second pass is a no-op
  157 |     again = q.process_once({'email': email, 'sms': sms})
  158 |     check('second process_once sends nothing', again['sent'] == 0)
  159 |     check('no double-send on reprocess', len(email.sent) == 1)
  160 | 
  161 | 
  162 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
```

```py
  163 | # 4. Dedup prevents double-send.
  164 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  165 | def dedup_checks():
  166 |     print('-- dedup (idempotent enqueue)')
  167 |     q = _q()
  168 |     email = nt.MemoryChannel('email')
  169 |     payload = {'title': 'Обломов', 'ref': 'hold-7'}
  170 | 
  171 |     a = q.enqueue('hold_ready', 'reader-1', payload)
  172 |     b = q.enqueue('hold_ready', 'reader-1', payload)   # same logical event
  173 |     check('second enqueue is deduped', b['deduped'] is True)
  174 |     check('same row id returned', a['id'] == b['id'])
  175 |     check('only one row queued', len(q.all()) == 1)
  176 | 
  177 |     q.process_once({'email': email})
  178 |     check('dedup -> single send', len(email.sent) == 1)
  179 | 
  180 |     # re-enqueue after delivery still deduped (no resurrection of a sent notice)
  181 |     c = q.enqueue('hold_ready', 'reader-1', payload)
  182 |     check('post-send enqueue still deduped', c['deduped'] is True)
  183 |     q.process_once({'email': email})
  184 |     check('no extra send after re-enqueue', len(email.sent) == 1)
  185 | 
  186 |     # explicit dedup_key wins over the derived one
  187 |     q2 = _q()
  188 |     q2.enqueue('fine_charged', 'reader-2', {'dedup_key': 'fine-xyz', 'amount': 50})
  189 |     d = q2.enqueue('fine_charged', 'reader-2',
  190 |                    {'dedup_key': 'fine-xyz', 'amount': 999})  # different payload
  191 |     check('explicit dedup_key dedupes regardless of payload', d['deduped'] is True)
  192 | 
  193 | 
  194 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  195 | # 5. Opt-out suppresses.
  196 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  197 | def optout_checks():
  198 |     print('-- opt-out suppression')
  199 |     q = _q()
  200 |     email = nt.MemoryChannel('email')
  201 | 
  202 |     prefs = nt.Preferences(opted_out={'due_soon'})
  203 |     res = q.enqueue('due_soon', 'reader-9', {'title': 'X', 'ref': 'loan-1'},
  204 |                     prefs=prefs)
  205 |     check('opted-out enqueue -> suppressed', res['status'] == 'suppressed')
  206 |     check('no pending row created', q.pending_count() == 0)
  207 | 
  208 |     q.process_once({'email': email})
  209 |     check('suppressed notice never sent', len(email.sent) == 0)
  210 |     check('row persisted as suppressed (audit trail)',
  211 |           len(q.by_status('suppressed')) == 1)
  212 | 
  213 |     # a NON-opted event for the same reader still goes through
  214 |     res2 = q.enqueue('overdue', 'reader-9', {'title': 'X', 'days_overdue': 3,
  215 |                                              'ref': 'loan-1'}, prefs=prefs)
  216 |     check('non-opted event still queued', res2['status'] == 'pending')
  217 | 
```

```py
  224 | 
  225 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  226 | # 6. Preferences override the catalog channel order.
  227 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  228 | def preference_checks():
  229 |     print('-- preferences (channel order override)')
  230 |     q = _q()
  231 |     email = nt.MemoryChannel('email')
  232 |     sms = nt.MemoryChannel('sms')
  233 | 
  234 |     # hold_ready defaults to [email, sms]; reader prefers sms-first.
  235 |     prefs = nt.Preferences(channels={'hold_ready': ['sms', 'email']})
  236 |     q.enqueue('hold_ready', 'reader-5', {'title': 'Y', 'ref': 'h9'}, prefs=prefs)
  237 |     q.process_once({'email': email, 'sms': sms})
  238 |     check('preferred sms used first', len(sms.sent) == 1)
  239 |     check('email skipped when sms preferred', len(email.sent) == 0)
  240 | 
  241 | 
  242 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  243 | # 7. Fallback chain — first channel fails, next delivers.
  244 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  245 | def fallback_checks():
  246 |     print('-- fallback chain (first-success)')
  247 |     q = _q()
  248 |     email = nt.MemoryChannel('email', fail=True)   # always fails
  249 |     sms = nt.MemoryChannel('sms')                  # succeeds
  250 | 
  251 |     res = q.enqueue('hold_ready', 'reader-3', {'title': 'Z', 'ref': 'h3'})
  252 |     summary = q.process_once({'email': email, 'sms': sms})
  253 |     check('fallback delivered exactly one', summary['sent'] == 1)
  254 |     check('email attempted', email.attempts == 1)
  255 |     check('email recorded no successful send', len(email.sent) == 0)
  256 |     check('sms delivered as fallback', len(sms.sent) == 1)
  257 | 
  258 |     row = q.get(res['id'])
  259 |     check('row sent via fallback channel', row['status'] == 'sent'
  260 |           and row['channel'] == 'sms')
  261 | 
  262 |     # EmailChannel stub with available=False also triggers fallback
  263 |     q2 = _q()
  264 |     em = nt.EmailChannel(available=False)
  265 |     sm = nt.SmsChannel(available=True)
  266 |     q2.enqueue('hold_ready', 'reader-4', {'title': 'Q', 'ref': 'h4'})
  267 |     q2.process_once({'email': em, 'sms': sm})
  268 |     check('unavailable email stub falls back to sms', len(sm.outbox) == 1)
  269 |     check('email stub outbox empty', len(em.outbox) == 0)
  270 | 
  271 | 
  272 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
```

<!-- ─── страница 418 ─── -->

```py
  273 | # 8. Retry increments attempts; exhausted chain -> failed.
  274 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  275 | def retry_checks():
  276 |     print('-- retry / attempts counter')
  277 |     # Channel fails once then succeeds; no fallback wired, so the row must
  278 |     # re-queue (attempts++) and deliver on the next process_once.
  279 |     q = _q()
  280 |     flaky = nt.MemoryChannel('email', fail_times=1)
  281 |     res = q.enqueue('hold_ready', 'reader-6', {'title': 'R', 'ref': 'h6'})
  282 | 
  283 |     s1 = q.process_once({'email': flaky})
  284 |     check('first pass retried (no send)', s1['sent'] == 0 and s1['retried'] == 1)
  285 |     row = q.get(res['id'])
  286 |     check('attempts incremented to 1', row['attempts'] == 1)
  287 |     check('row back to pending', row['status'] == 'pending')
  288 |     check('last_error recorded', bool(row['last_error']))
  289 | 
  290 |     # backoff sets next_attempt_at into the future; process at that time.
  291 |     s2 = q.process_once({'email': flaky}, now=row['next_attempt_at'])
  292 |     check('second pass delivers after transient clears', s2['sent'] == 1)
  293 |     row2 = q.get(res['id'])
  294 |     check('attempts now 2', row2['attempts'] == 2)
  295 |     check('row finally sent', row2['status'] == 'sent')
  296 | 
  297 |     # Always-failing chain with no fallback walks attempts to MAX -> failed.
  298 |     q2 = _q()
  299 |     dead = nt.MemoryChannel('email', fail=True)
  300 |     r2 = q2.enqueue('hold_ready', 'reader-7', {'title': 'D', 'ref': 'h7'})
  301 |     t = 0.0
  302 |     for _ in range(nt.MAX_ATTEMPTS):
  303 |         row = q2.get(r2['id'])
  304 |         if row['status'] != 'pending':
  305 |             break
  306 |         q2.process_once({'email': dead}, now=max(t, row['next_attempt_at']))
  307 |         t = row['next_attempt_at'] + 1
  308 |     final = q2.get(r2['id'])
  309 |     check('exhausted chain marked failed', final['status'] == 'failed')
  310 |     check('attempts reached the cap', final['attempts'] == nt.MAX_ATTEMPTS)
  311 | 
  312 |     # backoff helper is monotonic-ish and capped
  313 |     check('backoff(0) == 0', nt.backoff_seconds(0) == 0)
  314 |     check('backoff caps at last bucket',
  315 |           nt.backoff_seconds(999) == nt.backoff_seconds(nt.MAX_ATTEMPTS))
  316 | 
  317 | 
  318 | def main():
  319 |     catalog_checks()
  320 |     render_checks()
  321 |     deliver_checks()
  322 |     dedup_checks()
  323 |     optout_checks()
  324 |     preference_checks()
  325 |     fallback_checks()
  326 |     retry_checks()
  327 |     print('\n%d passed, %d failed' % (PASS[0], FAIL[0]))
```

### Файл: `irbis-web/backend/tests/test_pft.py`  · строк: 412

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """PFT formatting-engine tests (gap A1, epic #188).
    3 | 
    4 | Covers the first shippable slice of the PFT interpreter (``access/pft.py``) — the
    5 | keystone the ФЛК (A2) and .gbl (A3) engines delegate to (SPEC_engine_pft.md,
    6 | SPEC_api_contracts §3.1 / mismatch M4). Standalone-runnable, mirroring the house
    7 | style of tests/test_flk.py / tests/test_seeding.py::
    8 | 
    9 |     py -3.12 tests/test_pft.py   ->  'ok …' lines + 'N passed, M failed', exit!=0 on fail
   10 | 
   11 | What is exercised
   12 |   1. Field substitution: v200^a, whole-field v101, v200^* first subfield, fragment.
   13 |   2. Repeating fields: all instances of v910^b, repeatable |…| literals, prefix/suffix.
   14 |   3. IF / THEN / ELSE / FI: 920-branching with '=' and ':' (contains), nesting.
   15 |   4. Concatenation: ',' and '+', conditional/unconditional literals.
   16 |   5. UNIFOR implemented: 'C' (ISBN/ISSN checksum — the code ФЛК reuses), '3' (date
   17 |      today, via ctx['now']), 'Av…#n' (Nth occurrence), 'Q'/'9' string ops.
   18 |   5b. UNIFOR deepened batch: date '36'/'37'/'38' (month name nom/gen/eng),
   19 |      '3A' day-of-year, '3B' date±days, '3C' date difference; 'E'/'F' first/keep-N
   20 |      words, 'G' substring up-to/from a marker (#/$ wildcards), 'T' translit
   21 |      cyrillic→latin, 'L' term-ending marker strip, '0' full-record dump, '+6'
   22 |      record status, '+3E'/'+3D' URL encode/decode.
   23 |   6. Graceful skip of an unsupported construct (ref()/l() index lookups, unknown
   24 |      UNIFOR) — degrades to '' without crashing the surrounding render.
   25 |   7. Dummy output d/n, val()/f()/rsum(), p()/a() predicates, eval_lines groups.
   26 |   8. A golden-ish slice from PFT_LANGUAGE (920-branch idiom from brief.pft).
   27 | """
   28 | import os
   29 | import sys
   30 | 
   31 | sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))))
   32 | 
   33 | import datetime
   34 | from access import pft
   35 | from access.pft import eval as pft_eval   # the importable contract name
   36 | 
   37 | PASS = [0]
   38 | FAIL = [0]
   39 | 
   40 | 
   41 | def check(name, cond):
   42 |     if cond:
   43 |         PASS[0] += 1
   44 |         print('  ok  ', name)
   45 |     else:
   46 |         FAIL[0] += 1
   47 |         print('  FAIL', name)
   48 | 
   49 | 
   50 | # A representative book record (field -> list/dict/str of subfields).
```
