```py
   51 | def _book():
   52 |     return {
   53 |         '200': [{'a': 'Основы каталогизации', 'f': 'Иванова И.И.', 'e': 'учебник'}],
   54 |         '910': [{'a': '0', 'b': 'INV1'}, {'a': '0', 'b': 'INV2'},
   55 |                 {'a': '0', 'b': 'INV3'}],
   56 |         '101': 'rus',
   57 |         '920': 'PAZK',
   58 |         '10': {'a': '5-7654-0001-9'},          # valid ISBN-10
   59 |         '999': [{'': '3'}, {'': '4'}, {'': '5'}],
   60 |     }
   61 | 
   62 | 
   63 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   64 | # 1. Field substitution.
   65 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   66 | def field_checks():
   67 |     print('-- field substitution')
   68 |     rec = _book()
   69 |     check('v200^a -> title', pft_eval('v200^a', rec) == 'Основы каталогизации')
   70 |     check('v101 whole-field -> rus', pft_eval('v101', rec) == 'rus')
   71 |     check('v200^f -> author', pft_eval('v200^f', rec) == 'Иванова И.И.')
   72 |     check('v200^* -> first subfield', pft_eval('v200^*', rec) == 'Основы каталогизации')
   73 |     check('absent subfield -> empty', pft_eval('v200^z', rec) == '')
   74 |     check('absent field -> empty', pft_eval('v700^a', rec) == '')
   75 |     # fragment v…*off.len (0-based)
   76 |     check('fragment v101*0.2 -> "ru"', pft_eval('v101*0.2', rec) == 'ru')
   77 |     # mfn from ctx
   78 |     check('mfn(6) zero-padded', pft_eval('mfn(6)', rec, {'mfn': 42}) == '000042')
   79 |     check('bare mfn', pft_eval('mfn', rec, {'mfn': 7}) == '7')
   80 | 
   81 | 
   82 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   83 | # 2. Repeating fields.
   84 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   85 | def repeat_checks():
   86 |     print('-- repeating fields')
   87 |     rec = _book()
   88 |     # A bare field command outputs ALL occurrences concatenated (PFT semantics).
   89 |     check('v910^b emits all occurrences', pft_eval('v910^b', rec) == 'INV1INV2INV3')
   90 |     # |x|+ repeatable prefix = separator before each instance except the first.
   91 |     check('|sep|+ separator between repeats',
   92 |           pft_eval('| ; |+v910^b', rec) == 'INV1 ; INV2 ; INV3')
   93 |     # +|x| repeatable suffix = after each instance except the last.
   94 |     check('+|sep| separator after repeats',
   95 |           pft_eval('v910^b+| ; |', rec) == 'INV1 ; INV2 ; INV3')
   96 |     # repeat group emits each instance
   97 |     grp = pft_eval('(v910^b)', rec)
   98 |     check('repeat group over v910 -> all 3', grp == 'INV1INV2INV3')
   99 |     # eval_lines splits the group into one line per instance
  100 |     lines = pft.eval_lines('(v910^b)', rec)
  101 |     check('eval_lines yields 3 lines', lines == ['INV1', 'INV2', 'INV3'])
  102 | 
  103 | 
  104 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  105 | # 3. IF / THEN / ELSE / FI + 920 branching.
```

```py
  106 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  107 | def if_checks():
  108 |     print('-- if/then/else/fi')
  109 |     rec = _book()                              # 920 = PAZK
  110 |     check("if v920='J' else branch",
  111 |           pft_eval("if v920='J' then 'журнал' else 'книга' fi", rec) == 'книга')
  112 |     jrec = dict(rec, **{'920': 'J'})
  113 |     check("if v920='J' then branch",
  114 |           pft_eval("if v920='J' then 'журнал' else 'книга' fi", jrec) == 'журнал')
  115 |     # ':' contains operator (case-insensitive)
  116 |     njrec = dict(rec, **{'920': 'NJ31'})
  117 |     check("if v920:'NJ' contains",
  118 |           pft_eval("if v920:'NJ' then 'периодика' else 'моно' fi", njrec) == 'периодика')
  119 |     check("contains is false for PAZK",
  120 |           pft_eval("if v920:'NJ' then 'периодика' else 'моно' fi", rec) == 'моно')
  121 |     # nested IF with its own FI
  122 |     nested = "if v920:'NJ' then if v101='rus' then 'РУС' else 'X' fi else 'NO' fi"
  123 |     check('nested if (NJ + rus)', pft_eval(nested, njrec) == 'РУС')
  124 |     # missing ELSE: no output when condition false
  125 |     check('if without else, false -> empty',
  126 |           pft_eval("if v920='J' then 'журнал' fi", rec) == '')
  127 |     # predicate p()/a() in condition
  128 |     check('p(v200^a) true branch',
  129 |           pft_eval("if p(v200^a) then 'есть' else 'нет' fi", rec) == 'есть')
  130 |     check('a(v700) (absent) true branch',
  131 |           pft_eval("if a(v700) then 'нет автора' else 'есть' fi", rec) == 'нет автора')
  132 |     # NOT / AND / OR
  133 |     check('AND both true',
  134 |           pft_eval("if p(v200^a) and v101='rus' then 'ok' fi", rec) == 'ok')
  135 |     check('OR one true',
  136 |           pft_eval("if v920='J' or p(v200^a) then 'ok' fi", rec) == 'ok')
  137 |     check('NOT inverts',
  138 |           pft_eval("if not v920='J' then 'не журнал' fi", rec) == 'не журнал')
  139 | 
  140 | 
  141 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #

  152 |     # unconditional literal always emits; conditional only with adjacent field

  158 |     check('conditional prefix literal on absent field suppressed',
```

```py
  161 | 
  162 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  163 | # 5. UNIFOR codes implemented.
  164 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  165 | def unifor_checks():
  166 |     print('-- unifor (implemented)')
  167 |     rec = _book()
  168 |     # 'C' — ISBN/ISSN checksum control: '0' ok, '1' error. The code ФЛК reuses.
  169 |     check("&unifor('C' valid ISBN-10) -> 0",
  170 |           pft_eval("&unifor('C'v10^a)", rec) == '0')
  171 |     bad = dict(rec, **{'10': {'a': '5-7654-0001-0'}})   # flipped check digit
  172 |     check("&unifor('C' bad ISBN) -> 1",
  173 |           pft_eval("&unifor('C'v10^a)", bad) == '1')
  174 |     issn = dict(rec, **{'11': {'a': '0378-5955'}})       # textbook-valid ISSN
  175 |     check("&unifor('C' valid ISSN) -> 0",
  176 |           pft_eval("&unifor('C'v11^a)", issn) == '0')
  177 |     # direct API check of the shared algorithm
  178 |     check('isbn_issn_ok valid ISBN-13', pft.isbn_issn_ok('978-0-306-40615-7'))
  179 |     check('isbn_issn_ok rejects bad', not pft.isbn_issn_ok('978-0-306-40615-0'))
  180 | 
  181 |     # '3' — date today (via ctx['now'] for determinism).
  182 |     fixed = datetime.datetime(2026, 6, 21, 9, 30, 0)
  183 |     check("&unifor('3') -> YYYYMMDD",
  184 |           pft_eval("&unifor('3')", rec, {'now': fixed}) == '20260621')
  185 |     check("&unifor('30') -> year",
  186 |           pft_eval("&unifor('30')", rec, {'now': fixed}) == '2026')
  187 |     check("&unifor('32') -> day",
  188 |           pft_eval("&unifor('32')", rec, {'now': fixed}) == '21')
  189 | 
  190 |     # 'A' — Nth field occurrence: &unifor('Av910^b#2') -> 2nd instance.
  191 |     check("&unifor('Av910^b#2') -> 2nd inv",
  192 |           pft_eval("&unifor('Av910^b#2')", rec) == 'INV2')
  193 |     check("&unifor('Av920#1') -> first 920 (brief.pft idiom)",
  194 |           pft_eval("&unifor('Av920#1')", rec) == 'PAZK')
  195 | 
  196 |     # 'Q' lowercase, '9' strip quotes.
  197 |     check("&unifor('Q' value) lowercases",
  198 |           pft_eval("&unifor('Q'v920)", rec) == 'pazk')
  199 |     quoted = dict(rec, **{'300': '"кавычки"'})
  200 |     check("&unifor('9' value) strips quotes",
  201 |           pft_eval("&unifor('9'v300)", quoted) == 'кавычки')
  202 | 
  203 |     # '&uf' short form is equivalent to &unifor.
  204 |     check("&uf('C' …) short form works",
  205 |           pft_eval("&uf('C'v10^a)", rec) == '0')
  206 | 
  207 | 
  208 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  209 | # 5b. UNIFOR — newly-deepened batch (date 36/37/38/3A/3B/3C, E/F/G, T, L, 0,
  210 | #     +6, +3E/+3D). Grounded in PFT_LANGUAGE.md §9.1 + IRBIS64_UNIFOR_decompiled.c.
  211 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  212 | def unifor_date_checks():
  213 |     print('-- unifor date extensions (36/37/38/3A/3B/3C)')
  214 |     rec = _book()
  215 |     fixed = datetime.datetime(2026, 6, 21, 9, 30, 0)   # June 21st 2026, day 172
```

```py
  216 |     # 36MM/37MM/38MM — month name nominative / genitive / English.
  217 |     check("&unifor('3606') -> июнь (nominative)",
  218 |           pft_eval("&unifor('3606')", rec, {'now': fixed}) == 'июнь')
  219 |     check("&unifor('3706') -> июня (genitive)",
  220 |           pft_eval("&unifor('3706')", rec, {'now': fixed}) == 'июня')
  221 |     check("&unifor('3806') -> June (English)",
  222 |           pft_eval("&unifor('3806')", rec, {'now': fixed}) == 'June')
  223 |     # month name fed from another date code: &unifor('36',&unifor('31')).
  224 |     check("&unifor('36'&unifor('31')) composes month name from today",
  225 |           pft_eval("&unifor('36'&unifor('31'))", rec, {'now': fixed}) == 'июнь')

  243 | def unifor_string_checks():

  245 |     rec = {'200': [{'a': 'Hello World Foo Bar'}],

  257 |     check("&unifor('F0') -> empty",

  259 |     # G — substring up to / from a marker char (# = digit, $ = letter).

  267 |     check("&unifor('G1#') from-first-digit -> '123def'",

  269 |     check("&unifor('G0Z') marker absent (mode 0) -> whole string",
```

```py
  314 | def degrade_checks():
  315 |     print('-- graceful degradation')
  316 |     rec = _book()
  317 |     # ref(l(...)) needs the inverted index — not in this slice; must NOT crash,
  318 |     # and the surrounding text must still render.
  319 |     out = pft_eval("v200^a, ref(l('I='v933), v200^a), ' КОНЕЦ'", rec)

  321 |           out == 'Основы каталогизации КОНЕЦ')
  322 |     # unknown UNIFOR code -> '' (Format error 99 parity), no crash.
  323 |     check("unknown unifor code -> empty",
  324 |           pft_eval("'A'&unifor('ZZZ')'B'", rec) == 'AB')
  325 |     # malformed IF (no FI) degrades rather than raising, in non-strict mode.
```

### Файл: `irbis-web/backend/tests/test_platform.py`  · строк: 528

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """Platform suite: tenant PROVISIONING + BILLING skeleton (issue #207/#209, epic
    3 | #223 — MVP Phase 2).
    4 | 
    5 | Three layers, mirroring the rest of the Access suite's structure:
    6 | 
    7 |   * PURE (always run, no DB): the billing plan catalogue — plan_modules / plan_limits,
    8 |     and check_limit allowing under / blocking over a ceiling. Plus the provisioning
    9 |     flow on the sqlite dev path (single 'public' store): provision a tenant → its
   10 |     roles/vocabs/admin account exist and the admin AUTHENTICATES; re-provision is
   11 |     idempotent.
   12 |   * ROUTES (always run, over the real ``core.Api.route()`` dispatcher, sqlite, no
   13 |     live ИРБИС): GET /api/admin/tenants, POST /api/admin/tenant, GET /api/admin/billing,
   14 |     POST /api/admin/billing/plan, POST /api/admin/billing/module — admitted for a
   15 |     super-admin (admin.db@admin) and 403 for non-admin staff / reader / guest; a
   16 |     module toggle reflects in the enabled-module list.
   17 |   * PG (run when postgres is reachable; skipped cleanly otherwise): provision a real
   18 |     isolated ``t_<slug>`` schema → its vocabularies + named admin exist in THAT schema,
   19 |     the admin authenticates against it, and the plan drives ``control.tenant_module``
   20 |     so exactly the plan's modules are enabled (and a non-plan module is disabled).
   21 |     Idempotent re-provision. Deprovision drops the schema.
   22 | 
   23 | Wired into the test_access.py runner (its module list) via ``module_checks`` — the
```

```py
   24 | runner calls every ``*_checks()`` defined here and folds the PASS/FAIL tally in, so
   25 | the CI sqlite + postgres legs both exercise it.
   26 | 
   27 | Standalone:  py -3.12 tests/test_platform.py
   28 | """
   29 | import os
   30 | import sys
   31 | 
   32 | sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))))

   34 | from access import billing

   39 | 
   40 | PASS = [0]
   41 | FAIL = [0]
   42 | 
   43 | 
   44 | def check(name, cond):
   45 |     if cond:
   46 |         PASS[0] += 1
   47 |         print('  ok  ', name)
   48 |     else:
   49 |         FAIL[0] += 1
   50 |         print('  FAIL', name)
   51 | 
   52 | 
   53 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   54 | # 1. Billing plan catalogue + limit enforcement — pure, no DB.
   55 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   56 | def billing_plan_checks():
   57 |     print('-- billing: plan catalogue (modules + limits)')
   58 |     # Every advertised plan is a known plan with sorted modules + full limit set.
   59 |     for name in ('free', 'standard', 'pro'):
   60 |         check('plan %r is known' % name, billing.is_plan(name))
   61 |         mods = billing.plan_modules(name)
   62 |         check('plan %r modules sorted+nonempty' % name,
   63 |               mods == sorted(mods) and len(mods) >= 1)
   64 |         lims = billing.plan_limits(name)
   65 |         check('plan %r limits cover every resource' % name,
   66 |               set(lims) == set(billing.LIMIT_RESOURCES))
   67 |         # Each module a plan licenses is a real product module.
   68 |         check('plan %r modules subset of ALL_MODULES' % name,
   69 |               all(m in billing.ALL_MODULES for m in mods))
   70 | 
   71 |     # free is a strict subset of standard is a strict subset of pro (monotone tiers).
   72 |     free, std, pro = (set(billing.plan_modules(p)) for p in ('free', 'standard', 'pro'))
   73 |     check('free strict-subset standard', free < std)
   74 |     check('standard strict-subset pro', std < pro)
   75 |     check('pro = ALL_MODULES', pro == set(billing.ALL_MODULES))
   76 | 
   77 |     # cataloging is paid (not in free), acquisition is pro-only.
   78 |     check('free has no cataloging', 'cataloging' not in free)
```

```py
  227 | # Real staff, but NO admin.* grant.

  231 | ]
```

```py
  355 | def enforcement_route_checks():
  356 |     print('-- enforcement: save_record max_records soft check (fail-open in dev)')
  357 |     api, _core = _api()
  358 |     # A cataloger session that may write IBIS.
  359 |     tok, _ = api._new_session('staff', 'cat', [
  360 |         {'function': 'record.write', 'db': '*', 'level': 'write'},
  361 |         {'function': 'search', 'db': '*', 'level': 'read'}], tenant='public')
  362 |     H = {'authorization': 'Bearer ' + tok}
  363 | 
  364 |     # Stub the live server so a CREATE returns mfn=43; max_mfn high to exercise the
  365 |     # count path. On the dev 'public' tenant the plan is DEFAULT (standard, 100k cap)
  366 |     # so a create well under it is ALLOWED — proving the wiring fails open / doesn't
  367 |     # break normal writes.
  368 |     api.irbis.max_mfn = lambda db: 42
  369 |     api.irbis.update_record = lambda db, lines, lock=0, actualize=1: _FakeUpdateResult(43)
  370 |     st, p = api.route('POST', '/api/record/IBIS/0', {},
  371 |                       {'fields': [{'tag': '200', 'value': '^aТест'}]}, H)
  372 |     check('create under plan limit allowed (200)', st == 200)
  373 |     check('create returns the assigned mfn', st == 200 and p['data']['mfn'] == 43)
  374 |     check('create flagged created', st == 200 and p['data']['created'] is True)
  375 | 
  376 |     # If the count blows past a (hypothetical) tiny cap, check_limit blocks → 402.
  377 |     # We force it by pinning the plan via a direct check: the route uses the tenant's
  378 |     # plan, which on dev is standard; so we assert the helper itself blocks the same
  379 |     # input under 'free' (the route would 402 identically once a free plan is set).
  380 |     over = billing.plan_limit('free', 'max_records') + 1
  381 |     check('over free cap would block (helper)',
  382 |           not billing.check_limit(api.access, 'somelib', 'max_records', over, plan='free'))
  383 |
```

```py
  421 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  422 | # 4. PG leg — real schema-per-tenant provisioning + entitlement-driven billing.
  423 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  424 | def _pg_reachable(dsn):
  425 |     try:
  426 |         import psycopg
  427 |     except Exception:
  428 |         return False
  429 |     try:
  430 |         conn = psycopg.connect(dsn, connect_timeout=2)
  431 |         conn.close()
  432 |         return True
  433 |     except Exception:
  434 |         return False
  435 | 
  436 | 
  437 | def run_pg():
  438 |     """Provision a real tenant on PG: isolated schema + vocabs + admin + plan-driven
  439 |     entitlements. Skips cleanly when postgres/psycopg unavailable."""
  440 |     want = os.environ.get('ACCESS_BACKEND', '').lower() in ('postgres', 'pg') \
  441 |         or os.environ.get('ACCESS_TEST_PG') == '1'
  442 |     if not want:
  443 |         return
  444 |     from access import pgstore
  445 |     dsn = pgstore.default_pg_dsn()
  446 |     if not _pg_reachable(dsn):
  447 |         print('-- platform PG SKIPPED (postgres unreachable at %s)' % dsn.rsplit('@', 1)[-1])
  448 |         return
  449 |     print('-- platform: postgres', dsn.rsplit('@', 1)[-1])
  450 |     os.environ['ACCESS_BACKEND'] = 'postgres'
  451 |     slug = 'plat_test'
  452 |     # Clean slate.
  453 |     provision.deprovision_tenant(slug, dsn=dsn)
  454 |     try:
  455 |         public = pgstore.PgAccessStore(dsn)
  456 |         report = provision.provision_tenant(
  457 |             public, slug, 'Platform Test Lib', 'padmin',
  458 |             admin_password='pw-padmin', plan='standard', dsn=dsn)
  459 |         check('[pg] provision postgres flag True', report['postgres'] is True)
  460 | 
  461 |         # Isolated tenant schema: vocabs + admin live in t_<slug>.
  462 |         tstore = pgstore.make_tenant_store(slug, dsn)
  463 |         vocabs = {v['name'] for v in tstore.list_vocabularies()}
```

```py
  467 |         acc = tstore.get_account('padmin')
```

### Файл: `irbis-web/backend/tests/test_portal222.py`  · строк: 390

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """Reader-portal #222 BACKEND tests — holds + queue, notification inbox, shelves.
    3 | 
    4 | Exercises the real endpoints on a constructed ``core.Api`` (the constructor does
    5 | NOT connect to ИРБИС), with the title-resolution seam stubbed so no live server is
    6 | touched. All state is persisted in OUR access store / notification queue, reader-
    7 | scoped by ticket — nothing is written to the live ИРБИС server.
    8 | 
    9 | Covered (per the #222 contract):
   10 |   * HOLDS: place -> ``ready`` when a copy is free, ``queued``+position when taken; a
   11 |     second reader gets position 2; idempotent re-hold; list; cancel.
   12 |   * INBOX: a circulation-dispatched notice (real ``CirculationEngine._emit`` ->
   13 |     persistent queue) appears in the addressed reader's inbox; unread count drops
   14 |     after marking read (one + all); reader-scoped (one reader can't see another's).
   15 |   * SHELVES: the two system lists seed lazily on first GET; add / remove / dedup;
   16 |     create a custom list.
   17 |   * AUTH: a guest session is refused (401/403) on every reader-portal route.
   18 | 
   19 | Wired into the test_access.py runner (module list) so the CI sqlite + postgres
   20 | legs both run it. Standalone-runnable in the house style:
   21 |   py -3.12 tests/test_portal222.py  -> ok ... + "N passed, M failed" + exit code.
   22 | """
   23 | import os
   24 | import sys
   25 | 
   26 | sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))))
   27 | 
   28 | from access.store import AccessStore
   29 | from access import notifications as nt
   30 | from access import circulation as circ
   31 | from access.holds import HoldService
   32 | from access.shelves import ShelfService, SYSTEM_IDS
   33 | from access.authz import READER_GRANTS, GUEST_GRANTS
   34 | 
   35 | PASS = [0]
   36 | FAIL = [0]
   37 | 
   38 | 
   39 | def check(name, cond):
   40 |     if cond:
   41 |         PASS[0] += 1
   42 |         print('  ok  ', name)
   43 |     else:
   44 |         FAIL[0] += 1
   45 |         print('  FAIL', name)
```

<!-- ─── страница 437 ─── -->

```py
   46 | 
   47 | 
   48 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   49 | # A constructed Api with NO live ИРБИС: stub the brief-read seam, and rebuild the
   50 | # reader-portal services over a fresh in-memory access store + queue so each test
   51 | # group is isolated and fast.
   52 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   53 | def _api(brief=None):
   54 |     os.environ['JWT_SECRET'] = 'portal222-test-secret'
   55 |     import importlib
   56 |     import core as _core
   57 |     importlib.reload(_core)
   58 |     api = _core.Api()
   59 |     # Fresh, isolated, in-memory persistence (no live server, no temp files).
   60 |     api.access = AccessStore(':memory:')
   61 |     api.notifications = nt.NotificationQueue(':memory:')
   62 |     api.circulation = circ.CirculationEngine(
   63 |         store=circ.CirculationStore(':memory:'), notifications=api.notifications)
   64 |     seam = brief or (lambda db, mfn: {'title': 'Заглавие %d' % mfn})
   65 |     api.holds = HoldService(api.access, catalog=None, brief_read=seam)
   66 |     api.shelves = ShelfService(api.access, brief_read=seam)
   67 |     return api, _core
   68 | 
   69 | 
   70 | def _reader(api, ticket='111'):
   71 |     _tok, sess = api._new_session('reader', 'RI=%s' % ticket, READER_GRANTS,
   72 |                                   tenant='public', rdr_mfn=1)
   73 |     return sess
   74 | 
   75 | 
   76 | def _guest(api):
   77 |     _tok, sess = api._new_session('guest', 'guest', GUEST_GRANTS, tenant='public')
   78 |     return sess
   79 | 
   80 | 
   81 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   82 | # 1. Holds — ready / queued / position / idempotency / list / cancel.
   83 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   84 | def holds_checks():
   85 |     print('-- holds: place (ready/queued), position, cancel')
   86 |     api, _core = _api()
   87 |     r1 = _reader(api, '111')
   88 | 
   89 |     # First hold on a free item (no catalog opinion -> first-in-line surfaced ready).
   90 |     st, payload = api.place_hold(r1, {'db': 'IBIS', 'mfn': 42})
   91 |     check('place hold returns 200', st == 200)
   92 |     d = payload['data']
   93 |     check('first hold is ready', d['status'] == 'ready')
   94 |     check('first hold position 1', d['position'] == 1)
   95 |     hold1 = d['holdId']
   96 | 
   97 |     # Idempotent re-hold: same reader, same item -> existing hold returned.
   98 |     st, payload = api.place_hold(r1, {'db': 'IBIS', 'mfn': 42})
   99 |     check('re-hold idempotent (same holdId)', payload['data']['holdId'] == hold1)
  100 |     check('re-hold keeps position 1', payload['data']['position'] == 1)
```

<!-- ─── страница 438 ─── -->

```py
  101 | 
  102 |     # Second reader on the SAME item -> queued at position 2.
  103 |     r2 = _reader(api, '222')
  104 |     st, payload = api.place_hold(r2, {'db': 'IBIS', 'mfn': 42})
  105 |     check('second reader queued', payload['data']['status'] == 'queued')
  106 |     check('second reader position 2', payload['data']['position'] == 2)
  107 | 
  108 |     # GET /api/holds for r1: one live hold, title resolved, position present.
  109 |     st, payload = api.list_holds(r1)
  110 |     items = payload['data']['items']
  111 |     check('list_holds returns the reader hold', len(items) == 1)
  112 |     check('hold card carries resolved title', items[0]['title'] == 'Заглавие 42')
  113 |     check('hold card carries db/mfn', items[0]['db'] == 'IBIS' and items[0]['mfn'] == 42)
  114 |     check('hold card position 1', items[0]['position'] == 1)
  115 | 
  116 |     # r2 only sees its own hold (reader-scoped).
  117 |     st, payload = api.list_holds(r2)
  118 |     check('holds reader-scoped (r2 sees only its own)',
  119 |           len(payload['data']['items']) == 1 and
  120 |           payload['data']['items'][0]['holdId'] != hold1)
  121 | 
  122 |     # Cancel r1's hold -> cancelled; then r2 (still queued) is now alone.
  123 |     st, payload = api.cancel_hold(r1, {'holdId': hold1})
  124 |     check('cancel returns 200', st == 200)
  125 |     check('cancel reports cancelled', payload['data']['status'] == 'cancelled')
  126 |     st, payload = api.list_holds(r1)

  139 | 
  140 | def holds_availability_checks():
  141 |     print('-- holds: catalog availability drives ready vs queued')
  142 | 
  143 |     class _Cat:
  144 |         """Minimal catalog seam: item is issued (not free)."""
  145 |         def find_exemplar(self, db, item):
  146 |             return (1, 0, {'b': item})
  147 | 
  148 |         def is_available(self, db, item):
  149 |             return False    # taken -> first holder must be QUEUED, not ready
```

```py
  164 | def inbox_checks():

  169 | 
  170 |     # Drive a REAL circulation event addressed to the reader ticket: a return that
  171 |     # frees a queued item enqueues hold_ready to the queue-head reader (= ticket).
  172 |     cstore = api.circulation.store
  173 |     cstore.add_reader('owner', category='В01')
  174 |     cstore.add_reader(ticket, category='В01')      # the reader, keyed by ticket
  175 |     DAY = circ.SECONDS_PER_DAY
  176 |     T0 = 1_700_000_000
  177 |     loan = api.circulation.checkout('owner', 'BK-1', T0).computed['loan']
  178 |     api.circulation.place_hold(ticket, 'BK-1', T0 + DAY)   # reader queues
  179 |     api.circulation.return_item(loan['id'], T0 + 2 * DAY)  # -> hold_ready to ticket
  180 | 
  181 |     # The dispatched notice is now in the persistent queue, addressed to ticket.
  182 |     st, payload = api.notifications_inbox(r1, False)
  183 |     check('inbox returns 200', st == 200)
  184 |     items = payload['data']['items']
  185 |     check('inbox lists the circulation-dispatched notice', len(items) == 1)
  186 |     check('notice is hold_ready', items[0]['event'] == 'hold_ready')
  187 |     check('notice rendered (non-empty subject)', bool(items[0]['subject']))
  188 |     check('notice starts unread', items[0]['read'] is False)
  189 |     check('inbox unread count is 1', payload['data']['unread'] == 1)
  190 |     notif_id = items[0]['id']
  191 | 
  192 |     # unread filter shows it; reader-scoped (a different ticket sees nothing).

  195 |     other = _reader(api, '999')
```

```py
  276 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  277 | # 4. Auth — guest is refused (401/403) on every reader-portal route.
  278 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  279 | def auth_checks():
  280 |     print('-- auth: guest 401/403 on all reader-portal routes')
  281 |     api, _core = _api()
  282 |     guest = _guest(api)
  283 | 
  284 |     routes = [
  285 |         ('POST', '/api/hold', {'db': 'IBIS', 'mfn': 1}),
  286 |         ('GET', '/api/holds', None),
  287 |         ('POST', '/api/hold/cancel', {'holdId': 1}),
  288 |         ('GET', '/api/notifications', None),
  289 |         ('POST', '/api/notifications/read', {'all': True}),
  290 |         ('GET', '/api/shelves', None),
  291 |         ('POST', '/api/shelves', {'name': 'x'}),
  292 |         ('POST', '/api/shelves/item', {'listId': 'want', 'db': 'IBIS', 'mfn': 1}),
  293 |         ('POST', '/api/shelves/item/remove', {'listId': 'want', 'db': 'IBIS', 'mfn': 1}),
  294 |     ]
  295 |     # Direct handler calls raise Denied(403) for a guest (reader session required).
  296 |     handlers = [
  297 |         lambda: api.place_hold(guest, {'db': 'IBIS', 'mfn': 1}),
  298 |         lambda: api.list_holds(guest),
  299 |         lambda: api.cancel_hold(guest, {'holdId': 1}),
  300 |         lambda: api.notifications_inbox(guest, False),
  301 |         lambda: api.notifications_read(guest, {'all': True}),
  302 |         lambda: api.shelves_list(guest),
  303 |         lambda: api.shelf_create(guest, {'name': 'x'}),
  304 |         lambda: api.shelf_add_item(guest, {'listId': 'want', 'db': 'IBIS', 'mfn': 1}),
  305 |         lambda: api.shelf_remove_item(guest, {'listId': 'want', 'db': 'IBIS', 'mfn': 1}),
  306 |     ]
  307 |     for (m, p, _b), fn in zip(routes, handlers):
  308 |         denied = False
  309 |         try:
  310 |             fn()
  311 |         except _core.Denied as d:
  312 |             denied = d.status in (401, 403)
  313 |         check('guest denied on %s %s (403)' % (m, p), denied)
  314 | 
  315 |     # And through the real dispatcher: a guest token -> 401/403 status, never 200.
  316 |     headers = {}   # no bearer at all -> no session
  317 |     for m, p, b in routes:
  318 |         st, _payload = api.route(m, p, {}, b, headers)
  319 |         check('route %s %s with no session -> 401/403' % (m, p), st in (401, 403))
  320 |
```

```py
  327 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  328 | # 5. Postgres parity — the reader_hold / reader_shelf store CRUD on real PG.
  329 | # Exercises the %s-style SQL + ON CONFLICT against PostgreSQL when reachable;
  330 | # skips cleanly (not a failure) on the sqlite dev box / when PG/psycopg is absent.
  331 | # This is what proves the schema_postgres.sql DDL + PgAccessStore methods work on
  332 | # the CI postgres leg, alongside the sqlite coverage above.
  333 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  334 | def pg_parity_checks():
  335 |     want = os.environ.get('ACCESS_BACKEND', '').lower() in ('postgres', 'pg') \
  336 |         or os.environ.get('ACCESS_TEST_PG') == '1'
  337 |     if not want:
  338 |         return
  339 |     try:
  340 |         from access.pgstore import PgAccessStore, default_pg_dsn
  341 |         st = PgAccessStore(os.environ.get('ACCESS_PG_DSN', default_pg_dsn()))
  342 |         st._conn().execute('TRUNCATE reader_hold, reader_shelf, reader_shelf_item '
  343 |                            'RESTART IDENTITY')
  344 |     except Exception as e:
  345 |         print('-- portal222 pg parity SKIPPED (%s: %s)'
  346 |               % (type(e).__name__, str(e).splitlines()[0]))
  347 |         return
  348 |     print('-- portal222: postgres parity (reader_hold / reader_shelf CRUD)')
  349 | 
  350 |     # Holds CRUD on PG via the HoldService (no catalog → first-in-line ready).
  351 |     holds = HoldService(st, catalog=None, brief_read=lambda db, mfn: {'title': 'PG%d' % mfn})
  352 |     r1 = holds.place('pg-1', 'IBIS', 5)
  353 |     check('[pg] first hold ready position 1', r1['status'] == 'ready' and r1['position'] == 1)
  354 |     check('[pg] re-hold idempotent', holds.place('pg-1', 'IBIS', 5)['holdId'] == r1['holdId'])
  355 |     r2 = holds.place('pg-2', 'IBIS', 5)
  356 |     check('[pg] second reader queued position 2',
  357 |           r2['status'] == 'queued' and r2['position'] == 2)
  358 |     check('[pg] list_for is reader-scoped', len(holds.list_for('pg-1')) == 1)
  359 |     check('[pg] cancel works', holds.cancel('pg-1', r1['holdId'])['status'] == 'cancelled')
  360 |     check('[pg] cancelled hold gone from list', holds.list_for('pg-1') == [])
  361 | 
  362 |     # Shelves CRUD on PG: lazy system seed + add/dedup/remove/create.
  363 |     shelves = ShelfService(st, brief_read=lambda db, mfn: {'title': 'PG%d' % mfn})
  364 |     lists = shelves.lists('pg-1')
  365 |     check('[pg] system lists seeded', SYSTEM_IDS <= {l['id'] for l in lists})
  366 |     shelves.add_item('pg-1', 'want', 'IBIS', 10)
  367 |     shelves.add_item('pg-1', 'want', 'IBIS', 10)   # dup
  368 |     want = {l['id']: l for l in shelves.lists('pg-1')}['want']
  369 |     check('[pg] add deduped', len(want['items']) == 1)
  370 |     cust = shelves.create('pg-1', 'PG-список')
  371 |     check('[pg] custom list created', cust['id'] not in SYSTEM_IDS)
  372 |     check('[pg] add to unknown list -> None', shelves.add_item('pg-1', 'nope', 'IBIS', 1) is None)
  373 |     shelves.remove_item('pg-1', 'want', 'IBIS', 10)
  374 |     want = {l['id']: l for l in shelves.lists('pg-1')}['want']
  375 |     check('[pg] item removed', want['items'] == [])
```

### Файл: `irbis-web/backend/tests/test_resilience.py`  · строк: 525

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """Backend hardening tests (issue: harden ИРБИС connection + public-only OPAC).
    3 | 
    4 | Two independent concerns, both fully sqlite-green and requiring NO live ИРБИС:
    5 | 
    6 |   A. SELF-HEAL — ``core.ResilientIrbis`` wraps the SessionManager so a STALE
    7 |      registered session (after a server Стоп/Старт, or the demo "max 3 clients"
    8 |      cap evicting us) RE-REGISTERS and retries instead of failing every request
    9 |      until a manual process restart. We assert: a first-call connection error
   10 |      re-registers and succeeds on retry; a stale-session IRBIS return code
   11 |      (-3337/-3338/-33xx) self-heals the same way; a DATA-level code (-140 missing
   12 |      MFN) propagates unchanged (it's a real answer, not a dead session); retries
   13 |      are bounded (exhaustion raises); health()/ping() reflect reachability.
   14 | 
   15 |   B. PUBLIC-ONLY OPAC — a guest/reader session may only touch the configured
   16 |      PUBLIC bibliographic bases (Config.public_dbs, default {IBIS}); a non-public
   17 |      db (RDR readers, LICH/PAY/RIGHT ПДн, RQST/CMPL service, LOG*) is refused 403
   18 |      on search/record/render/terms/showcase/rubricator/facets/cover/
   19 |      example-queries, and is hidden from /api/databases. STAFF keep access per
   20 |      their grants (the public confinement does not apply to them).
   21 | 
   22 | House style mirrors tests/test_discovery.py — a constructed ``Api`` (whose
   23 | constructor does NOT connect to ИРБИС) with stubs injected over ``api.irbis``.
   24 | Invoked by the tests/test_access.py runner via the generic ``module_checks``
   25 | loop, so the existing CI step that runs test_access.py also runs this suite with
   26 | no .github/ workflow change.
   27 | 
   28 | Standalone too::
   29 | 
   30 |     py -3.12 tests/test_resilience.py   ->  'ok …' lines + 'N passed, M failed'
   31 | """
   32 | import os
   33 | import sys
   34 | 
   35 | sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))))
   36 | 
   37 | import importlib
   38 | 
   39 | PASS = [0]
   40 | FAIL = [0]
```

```py
   51 | 
   52 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   53 | # Stubs.
   54 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   55 | class _FakeClient:
   56 |     """The bits of irbis.client.IrbisClient that ResilientIrbis._reset_session
   57 |     touches (so we can observe the client_id rotation on re-register)."""
   58 | 
   59 |     def __init__(self):
   60 |         self.connected = True
   61 |         self.client_id = 123456
   62 | 
   63 | 
   64 | class FlakySession:
   65 |     """Stand-in for irbis.SessionManager.
   66 | 
   67 |     ``fail_plan`` maps a method name -> a list of exceptions to raise on the first
   68 |     N calls (one per entry), after which the call succeeds and returns
   69 |     ``results[name]``. ``register_count`` counts how often the wrapper forced a
   70 |     re-register (via _reset_session marking us disconnected, the way a real
   71 |     re-register is observed)."""
   72 | 
   73 |     def __init__(self, fail_plan=None, results=None):
   74 |         self._fail_plan = {k: list(v) for k, v in (fail_plan or {}).items()}
   75 |         self._results = results or {}
   76 |         self._calls = {}
   77 |         self.connected = True
   78 |         self._client = _FakeClient()
   79 |         self.reset_count = 0
   80 |         self.closed = False
   81 | 
   82 |     def _dispatch(self, name):
   83 |         self._calls[name] = self._calls.get(name, 0) + 1
   84 |         plan = self._fail_plan.get(name)
   85 |         if plan:
   86 |             raise plan.pop(0)
   87 |         return self._results.get(name, ('OK', name))
   88 | 
   89 |     # SessionManager surface that ResilientIrbis proxies / touches
   90 |     def max_mfn(self, db):
   91 |         return self._dispatch('max_mfn')
   92 | 
   93 |     def search(self, db, expr, first=1, maxn=0):
   94 |         return self._dispatch('search')
   95 |
```

```py
  121 | def _irbis_error(code):
  122 |     from irbis.client import IrbisError

  125 | 
  126 | def _resilient(fail_plan=None, results=None, retries=2, backoff=0.0):
  127 |     """Build a ResilientIrbis over a FlakySession (backoff 0 keeps tests fast)."""
  128 |     import core as _core
  129 |     importlib.reload(_core)
  130 |     sm = FlakySession(fail_plan=fail_plan, results=results)
  131 |     wrapper = _core.ResilientIrbis(sm, retries=retries, backoff=backoff)
  132 |     return wrapper, sm, _core
  133 | 
  134 | 
  135 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  136 | # A. ResilientIrbis self-heal.
  137 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  138 | def reconnect_on_socket_error_checks():
  139 |     print('-- ResilientIrbis: socket error -> re-register + retry')
  140 |     # First search() raises a ConnectionError (dropped TCP / server restart),
  141 |     # the retry succeeds.
  142 |     w, sm, _ = _resilient(fail_plan={'search': [ConnectionError('reset by peer')]},
  143 |                           results={'search': (7, [1, 2, 3])})
  144 |     cid_before = sm._client.client_id
  145 |     out = w.search('IBIS', '"K=x"')
  146 |     check('search retried after socket error -> success', out == (7, [1, 2, 3]))
  147 |     check('search was attempted twice (fail + retry)', sm.calls('search') == 2)
  148 |     check('re-register rotated client_id (new server session)',
  149 |           sm._client.client_id != cid_before)
  150 |     check('reset marked session disconnected before retry',
```

```py
  155 | 
  156 | def reconnect_on_stale_session_code_checks():
  157 |     print('-- ResilientIrbis: stale-session IRBIS code -> re-register + retry')
  158 |     # A -3338 (CLIENT_NOT_ALLOWED, also seen when the server forgot our client_id
  159 |     # after a restart) self-heals: drop the session, re-register, retry.
  160 |     for code in (-3337, -3338, -3310):
  161 |         w, sm, _ = _resilient(fail_plan={'read_record': [_irbis_error(code)]},
  162 |                               results={'read_record': {'mfn': 5}})
  163 |         out = w.read_record('IBIS', 5)
  164 |         check('stale code %d self-heals on retry' % code, out == {'mfn': 5})
  165 |         check('stale code %d retried once' % code, sm.calls('read_record') == 2)
  166 |

  168 | def data_level_code_propagates_checks():
  169 |     print('-- ResilientIrbis: data-level IRBIS code propagates (NOT a dead session)')
  170 |     from irbis.client import IrbisError
  171 |     # -140 (no such MFN) and -603 (deleted) are real answers — must NOT be retried
  172 |     # or swallowed; they propagate unchanged for the endpoint to handle.
  173 |     for code in (-140, -603):
  174 |         w, sm, _ = _resilient(fail_plan={'read_record': [_irbis_error(code),
  175 |                                                           _irbis_error(code)]})
  176 |         raised = None
  177 |         try:
  178 |             w.read_record('IBIS', 999)
  179 |         except IrbisError as e:
  180 |             raised = e.code
  181 |         check('data code %d propagates unchanged' % code, raised == code)
  182 |         check('data code %d NOT retried (single attempt)' % code,
  183 |               sm.calls('read_record') == 1)
  184 | 
  185 | 
  186 | def retries_are_bounded_checks():
  187 |     print('-- ResilientIrbis: bounded retries (exhaustion raises)')
  188 |     from irbis.client import IrbisError
  189 |     # Every attempt fails with a connection error -> after retries exhausted, raise.
  190 |     w, sm, _ = _resilient(
  191 |         fail_plan={'max_mfn': [ConnectionError('down')] * 9}, retries=2)
  192 |     raised = False
  193 |     try:
  194 |         w.max_mfn('IBIS')
  195 |     except IrbisError:
  196 |         raised = True
  197 |     check('exhausted reconnect raises IrbisError', raised)
  198 |     check('attempted exactly retries+1 (3) times', sm.calls('max_mfn') == 3)
  199 |     check('last_error retained after exhaustion', bool(w.last_error))
  200 | 
  201 |     # Stale IRBIS code that never clears also exhausts and re-raises that error.
  202 |     w2, sm2, _ = _resilient(
  203 |         fail_plan={'search': [_irbis_error(-3338)] * 9}, retries=1)
  204 |     raised2 = None
  205 |     try:
```

```py
  227 |     check('ping() False when server unreachable (no raise)', w2.ping() is False)

  250 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  251 | # B. Public-only OPAC enforcement (constructed Api + discovery-style FakeIrbis).
  252 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  253 | def _fld(tag, value, subfields=None):
  254 |     return {'tag': tag, 'value': value, 'text': value, 'subfields': subfields or {}}
  255 | 
  256 | 
  257 | class OpacFake:
  258 |     """Minimal ИРБИС stub for the Api OPAC endpoints (records/terms/menu/maxmfn).
  259 |     The public-DB guard must reject a non-public db BEFORE any of these run, so a
  260 |     403 path never reaches the stub — but a public db must flow through to it."""
```

```py
  262 |     def __init__(self, dbmenu=''):

  264 |         self.touched = []          # dbs that actually reached IRBIS (public path)

  266 |     def max_mfn(self, db):
  267 |         self.touched.append(db)
  268 |         return 6

  270 |     def read_record(self, db, mfn):

  272 |         return {'mfn': mfn, 'status': '0', 'version': '1', 'guid': None,

  278 | 
  279 |     def read_terms(self, db, start, count=20):

  283 |     def read_file(self, spec):
  284 |         return self._dbmenu
```

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """Per-tenant vocabulary seeding tests (gap A5, epic #188).
    3 | 
    4 | Covers the seeding engine layered on the merged tenancy (#100) + Identity (#101):
    5 | 
    6 |   1. Parsers (pure, always run): ``parse_mnu`` round-trips incl. CP1251 bytes and
    7 |      the ``*****`` terminator (recon #VOC-03 empty final pair filtered);
    8 |      ``parse_tre`` recovers depth + parent from leading dots.
    9 |   2. sqlite dev seed (pure, always run): seeding the single-tenant ``public`` store
   10 |      populates SYSTEM vocabs with values and creates INSTITUTION vocabs EMPTY;
```

```py
   11 |      re-seed is idempotent.
   12 |   3. PostgreSQL (skipped cleanly when PG unavailable): provision a tenant ->
   13 |      system vocabs present & populated, institution vocabs present & empty;
   14 |      per-tenant isolation (tenant A's vocab edit invisible to B); re-provision
   15 |      idempotent.
   16 | 
   17 | Invoked by the test_access.py runner (seeding_checks) so the CI postgres step

   55 |     # CP1251 BYTES decode -> UTF-8 (the on-disk ИРБИS encoding).

   57 |     pairs_cp = _mnu.parse_mnu(raw)

   60 | 
   61 |     # Empty final pair before ***** is dropped (recon #VOC-03).
   62 |     artefact = 'a\nAlpha\nb\nBeta\n\n\n*****\n'
```

```py
   66 |     # Stops at ***** — nothing after the terminator leaks in.

   68 |     check('parse_mnu stops at terminator', _mnu.parse_mnu(after) == [('a', 'Alpha')])

   70 |     # Real seed data round-trips through a serialize->parse cycle (ste.mnu, 14).
   71 |     ste = seed_data.SYSTEM_VOCABS['ste.mnu']['values']
   72 |     serialized = ''.join('%s\n%s\n' % (c, l) for c, l in ste) + '*****\n'

   75 | 
   76 |     # .tre: leading dots -> depth; parent reconstructed by the level stack.
   77 |     tre = 'Root\n.Child A\n..Leaf A1\n.Child B\n'
   78 |     nodes = _mnu.parse_tre(tre)
   79 |     check('parse_tre node count', len(nodes) == 4)

   81 |     check('parse_tre root has no parent', nodes[0]['parent'] is None)
   82 |     check('parse_tre child parent is root', nodes[1]['parent'] == 0)

   86 |     # live extraction is a documented stub, must raise (not silently no-op)
```

```py
  154 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  155 | # 3. PostgreSQL — provision -> seeded; isolation; idempotent re-provision.

  157 | A_SLUG, B_SLUG = 'seed_a', 'seed_b'

  159 | 
  160 | def pg_reachable(dsn):
  161 |     want = os.environ.get('ACCESS_BACKEND', '').lower() in ('postgres', 'pg') \
  162 |         or os.environ.get('ACCESS_TEST_PG') == '1'
  163 |     if not want:
  164 |         return False
  165 |     try:
  166 |         from access import pgstore
  167 |         conn = pgstore._admin_conn(dsn)
  168 |         conn.close()
  169 |         return True
  170 |     except Exception as e:
  171 |         print('-- seeding PG suite SKIPPED (%s: %s)'
  172 |               % (type(e).__name__, str(e).splitlines()[0]))
  173 |         return False
  174 | 
  175 |
```

```py
  176 | def pg_checks(dsn):
  177 |     print('-- seeding: postgres', dsn.rsplit('@', 1)[-1])
  178 |     from access import pgstore
  179 | 
  180 |     # clean slate
  181 |     pgstore.deprovision_tenant(A_SLUG, dsn)
  182 |     pgstore.deprovision_tenant(B_SLUG, dsn)
  183 | 
  184 |     # provision_tenant runs seed_vocabularies internally
  185 |     sa = pgstore.provision_tenant(A_SLUG, 'Seed A', 'публичная', dsn)
  186 |     sb = pgstore.provision_tenant(B_SLUG, 'Seed B', 'школьная', dsn)
  187 | 
  188 |     # --- system vocabs present & populated in the tenant schema ---
  189 |     vd = sa.get_vocabulary('vd.mnu')
  190 |     check('PG: system vd.mnu seeded', vd is not None and vd['kind'] == 'system')
  191 |     check('PG: vd.mnu has seed_version', vd['seed_version'] == seed_data.SEED_VERSION)
  192 |     check('PG: vd.mnu populated from catalog',
  193 |           len(sa.vocabulary_values('vd.mnu')) == len(seed_data.SYSTEM_VOCABS['vd.mnu']['values']))
  194 |     check('PG: ste.mnu full (14)', len(sa.vocabulary_values('ste.mnu')) == 14)
  195 |     check('PG: reservstatus.mnu full (5)', len(sa.vocabulary_values('reservstatus.mnu')) == 5)
  196 | 
  197 |     # --- institution vocabs present & EMPTY ---
  198 |     kv = sa.get_vocabulary('kv.mnu')
  199 |     check('PG: institution kv.mnu present', kv is not None and kv['kind'] == 'institution')
  200 |     check('PG: institution kv.mnu empty', sa.vocabulary_values('kv.mnu') == []
  201 |           and kv['seed_version'] is None)
  202 | 
  203 |     # --- per-tenant ISOLATION: edit A's vocab, B never sees it ---
  204 |     sa.upsert_vocabulary_value('kv.mnu', 'AB', 'Абонемент (A only)', 0, 'custom')
  205 |     a_kv = sa.vocabulary_values('kv.mnu')
  206 |     b_kv = sb.vocabulary_values('kv.mnu')
  207 |     check('PG: A sees its own kv edit', any(v['code'] == 'AB' for v in a_kv))
  208 |     check('PG: B does NOT see A kv edit (isolation)', b_kv == [])
  209 |     # And a system-vocab edit in A is invisible to B too
  210 |     sa.upsert_vocabulary_value('vd.mnu', 'KN', 'KN-renamed-in-A', 0, 'custom')
  211 |     b_kn = [v for v in sb.vocabulary_values('vd.mnu') if v['code'] == 'KN']
  212 |     check('PG: B keeps original KN label (isolation)',
  213 |           b_kn and b_kn[0]['label'] == 'Книги в целом')
  214 | 
  215 |     # --- re-provision is idempotent: counts stable, custom edit preserved ---
  216 |     before = len(sa.vocabulary_values('vd.mnu'))
  217 |     sa2 = pgstore.provision_tenant(A_SLUG, 'Seed A', 'публичная', dsn)
  218 |     after = len(sa2.vocabulary_values('vd.mnu'))
  219 |     check('PG: re-provision stable vd.mnu count', before == after)
  220 |     a_kv2 = sa2.vocabulary_values('kv.mnu')
  221 |     check('PG: re-provision preserves custom kv value',
  222 |           len(a_kv2) == 1 and a_kv2[0]['origin'] == 'custom')
  223 | 
  224 |     # cleanup
  225 |     pgstore.deprovision_tenant(A_SLUG, dsn)
  226 |     pgstore.deprovision_tenant(B_SLUG, dsn)
  227 | 
  228 | 
  229 | def run_pg():
  230 |     from access import pgstore
```

### Файл: `irbis-web/backend/tests/test_social.py`  · строк: 467

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """Reader-portal v2 social BACKEND tests (#134 engagement / #133 discovery).
    3 | 
    4 | Exercises the real endpoints on a constructed ``core.Api`` (the constructor does
    5 | NOT connect to ИРБИС). A tiny in-memory fake ИРБИС backs the recommendation seams
    6 | (606/700/610 reads + index search) and reader-name resolution, so no live server
    7 | is touched and the "similar"/"for you" ranking is deterministic. All social state
    8 | is persisted in OUR access store, reader-scoped by ticket — nothing is written to
    9 | the live ИРБИС server (the seams only READ it).
   10 | 
   11 | Covered (per the v2 contract):
   12 |   * REVIEWS: post -> {id}; list aggregates avg/count + cards + ``mine``; one
   13 |     editable review per (reader,db,mfn) (upsert replaces rating/text); delete own;
   14 |     403 deleting someone else's; rating 1..5 validation.
   15 |   * RECOMMENDATIONS: "similar" ranks candidates by shared-subject/author overlap,
   16 |     excludes the seed, attaches a human reason; "for you" derives from the reader's
   17 |     history subjects and is empty with no history.
   18 |   * HISTORY: the record-open path auto-logs for a reader (not a guest); deduped by
   19 |     (db,mfn) keeping the latest; capped/newest-first.
   20 |   * SAVED SEARCHES: CRUD (list/add/delete), reader-scoped, delete-own.
   21 |   * AUTH: guests READ reviews/recommendations but are 401/403 on every write +
   22 |     history + saved-search + foryou route.
   23 |   * PG parity: the social-store CRUD on a real PostgreSQL when reachable (skips
   24 |     cleanly otherwise) — proves schema_postgres.sql + PgAccessStore methods.
   25 | 
   26 | Wired into the test_access.py runner (module list) so the CI sqlite + postgres
   27 | legs both run it. Standalone:  py -3.12 tests/test_social.py
   28 | """
   29 | import os
   30 | import sys
   31 | 
   32 | sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))))
   33 | 
   34 | from access.store import AccessStore
   35 | from access.social import SocialService
   36 | from access.authz import READER_GRANTS, GUEST_GRANTS
   37 | 
   38 | PASS = [0]
   39 | FAIL = [0]
```

```py
   40 | 
   41 | 
   42 | def check(name, cond):
   43 |     if cond:
   44 |         PASS[0] += 1
   45 |         print('  ok  ', name)
   46 |     else:
   47 |         FAIL[0] += 1
   48 |         print('  FAIL', name)
   49 | 
   50 | 
   51 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   52 | # Fake ИРБИС: a small fixed catalogue used by the recommendation seams. Each
   53 | # record carries 606 (subjects), 700 (authors), 610 (collectives). The fake
   54 | # exposes the SAME read_record / search surface core's seams call, so the real
   55 | # _social_terms / _social_search run unchanged over it (no live server).
   56 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   57 | #   mfn 1 (seed): subjects {Базы данных, Программирование}, author Иванов, coll НИИ
   58 | #   mfn 2: subjects {Базы данных, Программирование} -> overlap 2 (top similar)
   59 | #   mfn 3: subjects {Базы данных}, author Иванов -> overlap 2 (subject+author)
   60 | #   mfn 4: subjects {Сети} -> no overlap with the seed (must NOT appear)
   61 | #   mfn 5: author Иванов only -> overlap 1
   62 | _CATALOG = {
   63 |     1: {'subjects': ['Базы данных', 'Программирование'], 'authors': ['Иванов И.И.'],
   64 |         'collectives': ['НИИ Систем'], 'title': 'Введение в БД'},
   65 |     2: {'subjects': ['Базы данных', 'Программирование'], 'authors': ['Петров П.П.'],
   66 |         'collectives': [], 'title': 'БД и программирование'},
   67 |     3: {'subjects': ['Базы данных'], 'authors': ['Иванов И.И.'],
   68 |         'collectives': [], 'title': 'Реляционные БД'},
   69 |     4: {'subjects': ['Сети'], 'authors': ['Сидоров С.С.'],
   70 |         'collectives': [], 'title': 'Компьютерные сети'},
   71 |     5: {'subjects': ['Алгоритмы'], 'authors': ['Иванов И.И.'],
   72 |         'collectives': [], 'title': 'Алгоритмы'},
   73 | }
   74 | 
   75 | 
   76 | def _make_record(mfn):
   77 |     """Build the parsed-record shape core's seams expect from a _CATALOG row."""
   78 |     rec = {'mfn': mfn, 'fields': []}
   79 |     row = _CATALOG[mfn]
   80 |     for s in row['subjects']:
   81 |         rec['fields'].append({'tag': '606', 'value': '^A' + s, 'text': '',
   82 |                               'subfields': {'A': s}})
   83 |     for a in row['authors']:
   84 |         rec['fields'].append({'tag': '700', 'value': '^A' + a, 'text': '',
   85 |                               'subfields': {'A': a}})
   86 |     for c in row['collectives']:
   87 |         rec['fields'].append({'tag': '610', 'value': c, 'text': c, 'subfields': {}})
   88 |     rec['fields'].append({'tag': '200', 'value': '^A' + row['title'], 'text': '',
   89 |                           'subfields': {'A': row['title']}})
   90 |     return rec
   91 | 
   92 | 
   93 | class FakeIrbis:
   94 |     """Minimal stand-in for the ResilientIrbis handle core's seams call."""
```

<!-- ─── страница 459 ─── -->

```py
   95 | 
   96 |     def read_record(self, db, mfn):
   97 |         if mfn not in _CATALOG:
   98 |             from irbis.client import IrbisError
   99 |             raise IrbisError(-140, 'no such mfn')
  100 |         return _make_record(mfn)
  101 | 
  102 |     def format_record(self, db, mfn, pft='@brief'):
  103 |         return _CATALOG.get(mfn, {}).get('title', '') if db != 'RDR' else ''
  104 | 
  105 |     def search(self, db, expr):
  106 |         """Honour '"PREFIX=term"' for S=/A=/K= and RDR RI= (reader name)."""
  107 |         e = expr.strip().strip('"')
  108 |         if e.startswith('RI='):
  109 |             return (1, [77])      # any reader resolves to a fixed RDR mfn
  110 |         if '=' not in e:
  111 |             return (0, [])
  112 |         prefix, term = e.split('=', 1)
  113 |         kind = {'S': 'subjects', 'A': 'authors', 'K': 'collectives'}.get(prefix)
  114 |         if kind is None:
  115 |             return (0, [])
  116 |         mfns = [m for m, row in sorted(_CATALOG.items()) if term in row[kind]]
  117 |         return (len(mfns), mfns)
  118 | 
  119 | 
  120 | class FakeRdrIrbis(FakeIrbis):
  121 |     """As FakeIrbis but read_record('RDR', 77) yields a reader name (field 10)."""
  122 | 
  123 |     def read_record(self, db, mfn):
  124 |         if db == 'RDR':
  125 |             return {'mfn': mfn, 'fields': [
  126 |                 {'tag': '10', 'value': 'Читателев Ч.Ч.', 'text': 'Читателев Ч.Ч.',
  127 |                  'subfields': {}}]}
  128 |         return super().read_record(db, mfn)
  129 | 
  130 | 
  131 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  132 | # A constructed Api with NO live ИРБИС: a fresh in-memory access store + the fake
  133 | # ИРБИС wired so the real social seams run deterministically.
  134 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  135 | def _api(irbis=None):
  136 |     os.environ['JWT_SECRET'] = 'social-test-secret'
  137 |     import importlib
  138 |     import core as _core
  139 |     importlib.reload(_core)
  140 |     api = _core.Api()
  141 |     api.access = AccessStore(':memory:')
  142 |     api.irbis = irbis or FakeRdrIrbis()
  143 |     # Rebuild social over the fresh store with the (reloaded) instance seams.
  144 |     api.social = SocialService(
  145 |         api.access, read_terms=api._social_terms, search=api._social_search,
  146 |         brief_read=api._hold_brief, reader_name=api._social_reader_name)
  147 |     return api, _core
  148 | 
  149 | 
```

```py
  233 | def recommendations_checks():

  243 |     check('non-overlapping record (mfn 4) excluded', 4 not in mfns)
  244 |     check('overlapping records surfaced (2 and 3)', 2 in mfns and 3 in mfns)
  245 |     # mfn 2 shares two subjects; mfn 3 shares one subject + one author => both
  246 |     # overlap 2; mfn 5 shares one author => overlap 1. Top-overlap come first.
  247 |     check('mfn 5 (overlap 1) ranks below the overlap-2 records',
  248 |           mfns.index(5) > mfns.index(2) and mfns.index(5) > mfns.index(3))
```

```py
  420 |     except Exception as e:

  422 |               % (type(e).__name__, str(e).splitlines()[0]))
  423 |         return
  424 |     print('-- social: postgres parity (reader_review / reader_history / saved_search)')
```

### Файл: `irbis-web/backend/tests/test_stabilize.py`  · строк: 317

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """Backend stabilization suite (post-MVP rough edges from the pilot smoke, epic #223).
    3 | 
    4 | Two rough edges found during the deployed-container smoke:
    5 | 
    6 |   1. **Post-provision propagation** (the real bug). After
    7 |      ``provision_tenant('public', admin=…)`` stood the tenant + admin up on the PG
    8 |      stack, the very next ``POST /api/auth/staff {admin}`` transiently returned "no
    9 |      session" — the freshly-created account was not visible to the next request.
   10 |      Root cause: provisioning writes a tenant's accounts into its OWN schema
   11 |      ``t_<slug>`` (the default tenant included → ``t_public``), but the auth read
   12 |      short-circuited the default tenant to the un-scoped public store (search_path =
```

<!-- ─── страница 466 ─── -->

```py
   13 |      PG ``public``), looking in the wrong schema. The fix routes the default tenant's
   14 |      auth read to its provisioned ``t_public`` schema when that schema exists, so a
   15 |      provisioned admin is authable on the very next request.
   16 | 
   17 |      This leg is PG-only: it provisions ``t_public`` and asserts the next-request
   18 |      auth succeeds (it would 401 before the fix). It is GATED on a reachable
   19 |      postgres (ACCESS_BACKEND=postgres / ACCESS_TEST_PG=1) and SKIPS cleanly
   20 |      otherwise. On the sqlite single-tenant dev box there are no per-tenant schemas,
   21 |      so the bug cannot occur — a documented no-op (a sanity pass that the default
   22 |      store authenticates a freshly-seeded admin).
   23 | 
   24 |   2. **Metrics / health deepening** (pilot observability). ``GET /api/metrics`` —
   25 |      a plain-JSON, unauthenticated-safe operational snapshot: version marker, uptime,
   26 |      request/error counters, the ИРБИС reachability block, and a per-tenant count
   27 |      (bare count unauthenticated; per-tenant detail only for a super-admin). The
   28 |      checks assert the documented shape, that it needs no auth, that error counting
   29 |      works, that the admin-only detail is withheld from anon/non-admin, and that no
   30 |      secret/PII (JWT secret, password, ticket) leaks into the payload. All pure
   31 |      (sqlite, no live ИРБИС) — always run.
   32 | 
   33 | Wired into the test_access.py runner (its module list) via ``module_checks`` — the
   34 | runner calls every ``*_checks()`` here and folds the PASS/FAIL tally in, so the CI
   35 | sqlite + postgres legs both exercise it.
   36 | 
   37 | Standalone:  py -3.12 tests/test_stabilize.py
   38 | """
   39 | import json
   40 | import os
   41 | import sys
   42 | 
   43 | sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))))
   44 | 
   45 | PASS = [0]
   46 | FAIL = [0]
   47 | 
   48 | # A minimal DB-menu + max_mfn stub so a constructed Api needs no live ИРБИС.
   49 | _DB_MENU = 'IBIS\nЭлектронный каталог\nRDR\nЧитатели\n*****\n'
   50 | 
   51 | SUPER_ADMIN_GRANTS = [
   52 |     {'function': 'admin.db', 'db': '*', 'level': 'admin'},
   53 |     {'function': 'admin.users', 'db': '*', 'level': 'admin'},
   54 |     {'function': 'search', 'db': '*', 'level': 'read'},
   55 | ]
   56 | NONADMIN_STAFF_GRANTS = [
   57 |     {'function': 'record.write', 'db': '*', 'level': 'write'},
   58 |     {'function': 'search', 'db': '*', 'level': 'read'},
   59 | ]
   60 | 
   61 |

   63 |     if cond:
   64 |         PASS[0] += 1
   65 |         print('  ok  ', name)
   66 |     else:
   67 |         FAIL[0] += 1
```

```py
  181 |     provision.provision_tenant(api.access, 'public', 'Dev', 'devadmin',

  188 | 
  189 | def _pg_reachable(dsn):
  190 |     # Gate on the PG intent. NB: a sibling suite (test_platform / the sqlite checks
  191 |     # in THIS module) mutates os.environ['ACCESS_BACKEND'] to 'sqlite' before this
  192 |     # leg runs in the test_access.py runner, so we must NOT rely on ACCESS_BACKEND
  193 |     # alone — we also honour an explicit ACCESS_PG_DSN (set by the CI postgres job)
  194 |     # and ACCESS_TEST_PG, mirroring test_compliance.pg_parity_checks, so the
  195 |     # regression actually runs on CI's postgres backend.
  196 |     want = os.environ.get('ACCESS_BACKEND', '').lower() in ('postgres', 'pg') \
  197 |         or os.environ.get('ACCESS_TEST_PG') == '1' \
  198 |         or bool(os.environ.get('ACCESS_PG_DSN'))
  199 |     if not want:
  200 |         return False
  201 |     try:
  202 |         from access import pgstore
  203 |         conn = pgstore._admin_conn(dsn)
  204 |         conn.close()
  205 |         return True
  206 |     except Exception as e:
  207 |         print('-- stabilize PG SKIPPED (%s: %s)'
  208 |               % (type(e).__name__, str(e).splitlines()[0]))
  209 |         return False
  210 | 
  211 | 
  212 | def pg_regression_checks():
  213 |     """THE regression for the container-smoke bug: provision the DEFAULT tenant on
  214 |     PG (admin lands in t_public) and assert the very next POST /api/auth/staff
  215 |     succeeds. This 401'd before the _store_for fix (it read the bare public schema)
  216 |     and 200s after. Skips cleanly when postgres/psycopg unavailable.
  217 | 
  218 |     Named ``*_checks`` (not ``run_pg``) so the test_access.py runner's
  219 |     ``module_checks`` — which invokes every ``*_checks()`` — actually exercises
  220 |     this PG leg on CI's postgres backend (a plain ``run_pg`` would be skipped by
  221 |     the runner and only run standalone). Gated on a reachable PG; restores
  222 |     ACCESS_BACKEND so it never leaks env into a later module."""
  223 |     from access import pgstore
  224 |     dsn = pgstore.default_pg_dsn()
  225 |     if not _pg_reachable(dsn):
  226 |         return
  227 |     print('-- stabilize: postgres', dsn.rsplit('@', 1)[-1])
  228 |     os.environ['JWT_SECRET'] = 'stabilize-pg-secret'
  229 |     os.environ['ACCESS_BACKEND'] = 'postgres'
  230 |     from access import provision
  231 | 
  232 |     # ---- (a) DEFAULT tenant ('public') — the exact smoke scenario ----
```

### Файл: `irbis-web/backend/tests/test_tenancy.py`  · строк: 178

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """Tenant isolation tests (issue #100, I1): schema-per-tenant on PostgreSQL.
    3 | 
    4 | The hard gate for multi-tenancy. Provisions two tenants in separate schemas
    5 | (t_<slug>) and asserts that a tenant-scoped Access store sees ONLY its own
    6 | tenant's accounts / grants / audit — never the other tenant's rows, in either
    7 | direction — plus a direct negative SQL probe that tenant A's scope cannot read
    8 | tenant B's schema. Also checks provisioning idempotency and deprovision teardown.
    9 | 
   10 | Backends:
   11 |   * postgres only — schema-per-tenant is a PG feature. Runs when
   12 |     ACCESS_BACKEND=postgres (or ACCESS_TEST_PG=1) AND a PG is reachable at
   13 |     ACCESS_PG_DSN; otherwise the whole suite is SKIPPED cleanly (exit 0), so it
   14 |     never red-flags the sqlite-only dev box. CI runs it against the postgres
   15 |     service container (see .github/workflows/ci.yml).
   16 | 
   17 | Run (PG):  (set ACCESS_BACKEND=postgres) py -3.12 irbis-web/backend/tests/test_tenancy.py
   18 | """
   19 | import os
   20 | import sys
   21 | 
   22 | sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))))
   23 | from access import pgstore
   24 | from access.authz import authorize
   25 | 
```

<!-- ─── страница 472 ─── -->

```py
   26 | PASS = [0]
   27 | FAIL = [0]
   28 | 
   29 | # Two tenants in their own schemas. Slugs are deliberately disjoint.
   30 | A_SLUG, B_SLUG = 'tnt_a', 'tnt_b'
   31 | 
   32 | 
   33 | def check(name, cond):
   34 |     if cond:
   35 |         PASS[0] += 1
   36 |         print('  ok  ', name)
   37 |     else:
   38 |         FAIL[0] += 1
   39 |         print('  FAIL', name)
   40 | 
   41 | 
   42 | def pg_reachable(dsn):
   43 |     """True iff the postgres backend is requested AND a server is reachable."""
   44 |     want = os.environ.get('ACCESS_BACKEND', '').lower() in ('postgres', 'pg') \
   45 |         or os.environ.get('ACCESS_TEST_PG') == '1'
   46 |     if not want:
   47 |         return False
   48 |     try:
   49 |         conn = pgstore._admin_conn(dsn)
   50 |         conn.close()
   51 |         return True
   52 |     except Exception as e:                       # PG down / psycopg missing -> skip cleanly
   53 |         print('-- tenancy suite SKIPPED (%s: %s)'
   54 |               % (type(e).__name__, str(e).splitlines()[0]))
   55 |         return False
   56 | 
   57 | 
   58 | def _count_in_schema(dsn, schema, table):
   59 |     """Direct row count in a fully-qualified schema.table (control-plane probe)."""
   60 |     from psycopg import sql
   61 |     conn = pgstore._admin_conn(dsn)
   62 |     try:
   63 |         return conn.execute(sql.SQL('SELECT count(*) AS n FROM {}.{}').format(
   64 |             sql.Identifier(schema), sql.Identifier(table))).fetchone()['n']
   65 |     finally:
   66 |         conn.close()
   67 | 
   68 | 
   69 | def run(dsn):
   70 |     # Clean slate so the run is deterministic.
   71 |     pgstore.deprovision_tenant(A_SLUG, dsn)
   72 |     pgstore.deprovision_tenant(B_SLUG, dsn)
   73 | 
   74 |     # ---- provision two tenants, each seeded (admin/admin, librarian/librarian) ----
   75 |     sa = pgstore.provision_tenant(A_SLUG, 'Library A', 'публичная', dsn)
   76 |     sb = pgstore.provision_tenant(B_SLUG, 'Library B', 'школьная', dsn)
   77 |     check('two distinct schemas', sa.tenant_schema != sb.tenant_schema
   78 |           and sa.tenant_schema == 't_tnt_a' and sb.tenant_schema == 't_tnt_b')
   79 | 
   80 |     # control catalog has both rows with the right kind
```

```py
   81 |     tenants = {t['slug']: t for t in pgstore.list_tenants(dsn)}
   82 |     check('control row A (kind)', tenants.get('tnt_a', {}).get('kind') == 'публичная')
   83 |     check('control row B (kind)', tenants.get('tnt_b', {}).get('kind') == 'школьная')
   84 | 
   85 |     # ---- a DISTINCT account in each tenant ----
   86 |     sa.create_account('alice', 'pw_a', 'Alice (A only)')
   87 |     sb.create_account('bob', 'pw_b', 'Bob (B only)')
   88 | 
   89 |     # ---- authenticate: each tenant sees only its own account ----
   90 |     check('A authenticates alice', sa.authenticate('alice', 'pw_a') is not None)
   91 |     check('B authenticates bob', sb.authenticate('bob', 'pw_b') is not None)
   92 |     # cross-tenant invisibility: A never sees bob; B never sees alice
   93 |     check('A cannot see bob (authenticate)', sa.authenticate('bob', 'pw_b') is None)
   94 |     check('A cannot see bob (get_account)', sa.get_account('bob') is None)
   95 |     check('B cannot see alice (authenticate)', sb.authenticate('alice', 'pw_a') is None)
   96 |     check('B cannot see alice (get_account)', sb.get_account('alice') is None)
   97 | 
   98 |     # seeded admin is independent per tenant (same login, different schema/row)
   99 |     admin_a = sa.authenticate('admin', 'admin')
  100 |     admin_b = sb.authenticate('admin', 'admin')
  101 |     check('both tenants have own admin', admin_a is not None and admin_b is not None)
  102 | 
  103 |     # ---- effective_grants are per-tenant (seeded admin has admin.users) ----
  104 |     ega = sa.effective_grants(admin_a['id'])
  105 |     egb = sb.effective_grants(admin_b['id'])
  106 |     check('A admin effective grants', authorize(ega, 'admin.users', 'IBIS', 'admin'))
  107 |     check('B admin effective grants', authorize(egb, 'admin.users', 'IBIS', 'admin'))
  108 | 
  109 |     # ---- audit is per-tenant: write in A only ----
  110 |     sa.audit('alice', 'record.write', 'IBIS', 7, 'ok', {'note': 'A-only'})
  111 |     aud_a = sa.recent_audit()
  112 |     aud_b = sb.recent_audit()
  113 |     check('A audit has its row', any(r['actor'] == 'alice' for r in aud_a))
  114 |     check('B audit never sees A row', not any(r['actor'] == 'alice' for r in aud_b))
  115 | 
  116 |     # ---- NEGATIVE probe: tenant A's scope cannot read tenant B's schema rows ----
  117 |     # Run a query through A's pinned connection (search_path = t_tnt_a, public)
  118 |     # using the UNQUALIFIED table name: it must resolve to A's schema, so bob
  119 |     # (who only exists in B) is invisible. Reaching bob would prove a leak.
  120 |     leaked = sa._conn().execute(
  121 |         'SELECT count(*) AS n FROM staff_account WHERE login=%s', ('bob',)
  122 |     ).fetchone()['n']
  123 |     check('A search_path cannot reach B rows', leaked == 0)
  124 |     # And symmetric: B's scope cannot reach alice.
  125 |     leaked_b = sb._conn().execute(
  126 |         'SELECT count(*) AS n FROM staff_account WHERE login=%s', ('alice',)
  127 |     ).fetchone()['n']
  128 |     check('B search_path cannot reach A rows', leaked_b == 0)
  129 | 
  130 |     # Ground truth via the control plane: each row physically lives in its schema.
  131 |     check('alice physically in t_tnt_a only',
  132 |           _count_in_schema(dsn, 't_tnt_a', 'staff_account') >= 1
  133 |           and _count_in_schema(dsn, 't_tnt_b', 'staff_account') >= 1)
  134 | 
  135 |     # ---- provisioning is idempotent: re-provision must not duplicate or raise ----
```

### Файл: `irbis-web/backend/tools/irbis_mst.py`  · строк: 450

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """Офлайн-адаптер прямого чтения мастер-файлов ИРБИС64 (`.mst` + `.xrf`).
    3 | 
    4 | Локальный источник для миграции (GitHub #225, эпик #223): читает базу данных
    5 | ИРБИС64 **прямо с диска**, без запущенного сервера, разбирая физический формат
    6 | мастер-файла (`<db>.mst`) и таблицы перекрёстных ссылок (`<db>.xrf`). Это
    7 | альтернатива сетевому пути (`irbis.SessionManager`): мигратор и интроспекция
    8 | получают записи через тот же утиный интерфейс ``max_mfn`` / ``read_record``,
    9 | поэтому источник «сеть» и источник «файл» взаимозаменяемы.
   10 | 
```

<!-- ─── страница 475 ─── -->

```py
   11 | Зачем офлайн-путь. Чтобы перенести библиотеку с устаревшей САБ, не нужен живой
   12 | сервер ИРБИС64 — достаточно скопированной папки DataPath. Адаптер читает байты
   13 | сам и отдаёт каноническую запись в той же форме ``{поле: [{подполе: значение}]}``,
   14 | что и ``access.catalog.CatalogStore`` / ``tools.migrate_irbis.map_catalog_record``.
   15 | 
   16 | Формат (выверен по реальным `C:\\IRBIS64\\Datai\\IBIS\\ibis.MST/.XRF` и `RDR`,
   17 | подтверждён реверсом — см. docs/recon/deep/reference/protocol/DECOMPILATION_FINDINGS.md:
   18 | числовые поля ИРБИС64 — big-endian, явные ``htonl``/``ntohl`` в движке)
   19 | 
   20 | `.xrf` — таблица MFN → смещение в `.mst`
   21 | ----------------------------------------
   22 | Массив 12-байтовых записей, по одной на MFN (1-based: запись с индексом ``i``
   23 | соответствует ``MFN = i + 1``). Поля big-endian. Запись хранит абсолютное
   24 | файловое смещение записи в `.mst`, упакованное парой 32-битных слов
   25 | ``(XRF_LOW, XRF_HIGH)`` (старший бит ``XRF_LOW`` — флаг логического удаления,
   26 | как в классическом ISIS/ИРБИС). Полное смещение восстанавливается из младших и
   27 | старших разрядов; в наблюдаемых файлах оно умещается в первом слове, но мы
   28 | читаем 64-битно, чтобы поддержать `.mst` > 2 ГБ.
   29 | 
   30 |     смещение  размер  поле        значение
   31 |     +0x00     4       XRF_LOW     младшие разряды смещения; бит 0x80000000 = удалён
   32 |     +0x04     4       XRF_HIGH    старшие разряды смещения (обычно 0)
   33 |     +0x08     4       (резерв)    в наблюдаемых файлах = 0
   34 | 
   35 | `.mst` — мастер-файл записей
   36 | ----------------------------
   37 | Первая запись файла (смещение 0) — **управляющая** (control record), 36 байт BE:
   38 | 
   39 |     +0x00     4       CTLMFN      (служебное, обычно 0)
   40 |     +0x04     4       NXTMFN      следующий свободный MFN  => max_mfn = NXTMFN - 1
   41 |     +0x08     4       NXTBLK_LOW  младшие разряды смещения хвоста (куда дописывать)
   42 |     +0x0c     4       NXTBLK_HIGH старшие разряды
   43 |     +0x10..   …       (резерв / флаги БД)
   44 | 
   45 | Каждая запись (по смещению из `.xrf`) — лидер 32 байта BE + директорий полей +
   46 | переменная часть:
   47 | 
   48 |     ЛИДЕР (32 байта, big-endian int32):
   49 |     +0x00     4       MFN         номер записи
   50 |     +0x04     4       MFRL        полная длина записи в байтах (лидер+дир.+данные)
   51 |     +0x08     4       PREV_LOW    backlink на пред. версию (младшие разряды)
   52 |     +0x0c     4       PREV_HIGH   backlink на пред. версию (старшие разряды)
   53 |     +0x10     4       BASE        смещение начала переменной части от начала записи
   54 |     +0x14     4       NVF         число полей (записей директория)
   55 |     +0x18     4       VERSION     версия записи
   56 |     +0x1c     4       STATUS      статус: бит 0x01 = ЛОГИЧЕСКИ УДАЛЕНА,
   57 |                                   бит 0x02 = длинная (несколько блоков),
   58 |                                   бит 0x20 = последняя/актуализированная версия
   59 | 
   60 |     ДИРЕКТОРИЙ (NVF × 12 байт, сразу за лидером, big-endian int32):
   61 |     +0x00     4       TAG         метка поля
   62 |     +0x04     4       POS         смещение значения от BASE
   63 |     +0x08     4       LEN         длина значения в байтах
   64 |     (инвариант: BASE == 32 + NVF*12)
   65 | 
```

<!-- ─── страница 476 ─── -->

```py
   66 |     ПЕРЕМЕННАЯ ЧАСТЬ: для поля i значение лежит по
   67 |         запись_offset + BASE + POS[i], длиной LEN[i], кодировка CP1251.
   68 |     Разделитель подполей — '^': '^aЗначение^bДругое' => {'a': ..., 'b': ...}.
   69 |     Текст до первого '^' — «голова» поля (поля без подполей — голый текст).
   70 | 
   71 | Системная метка ``2147483647`` (MaxInt32) — GUID записи, не библиографическое
   72 | поле: в каноническую запись не попадает (как и в ``irbis.parser``).
   73 | 
   74 | Защита. Битый/короткий `.xrf`/`.mst`, метка/смещение/длина вне файла, исключение
   75 | декодирования — запись пропускается со счётчиком предупреждений, без падения.
   76 | """
   77 | import os
   78 | import struct
   79 | 
   80 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   81 | # Константы формата.
   82 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   83 | XRF_ENTRY_SIZE = 12          # байт на запись таблицы перекрёстных ссылок
   84 | MST_LEADER_SIZE = 32         # байт лидера записи мастер-файла
   85 | MST_DIR_ENTRY_SIZE = 12      # байт на запись директория (tag/pos/len)
   86 | MST_CONTROL_SIZE = 36        # байт управляющей записи (первая в .mst)
   87 | 
   88 | STATUS_DELETED = 0x01        # бит «логически удалена»
   89 | STATUS_ABSENT = 0x80         # бит «отсутствует/заблокирована»
   90 | STATUS_DELETED_BITS = STATUS_DELETED | STATUS_ABSENT
   91 | 
   92 | XRF_DELETED_FLAG = 0x80000000  # старший бит XRF_LOW — признак удаления в .xrf
   93 | 
   94 | SUBFIELD_DELIM = '^'
   95 | SYSTEM_GUID_TAG = 2147483647   # MaxInt32 — системное поле GUID, не библиографическое
   96 | 
   97 | # Кодировка значений полей. Классический ИРБИС64 хранит записи в CP1251 (правило
   98 | # проекта, см. docs/recon/.../databases/*), НО встречаются UTF-8-сборки/базы
   99 | # (выверено на реальном C:\IRBIS64\Datai\IBIS: все поля — валидный UTF-8 с
  100 | # многобайтовой кириллицей). Поэтому декодирование адаптивное: если байты поля —
  101 | # валидный UTF-8 и содержат не-ASCII, читаем как UTF-8; иначе — как CP1251.
  102 | # Чисто-ASCII декодируется одинаково в обеих кодировках.
  103 | ENCODING = 'cp1251'            # документированная базовая кодировка формата
  104 | 
  105 | _MST_SUFFIX = '.mst'
  106 | _XRF_SUFFIX = '.xrf'
  107 | 
  108 | 
  109 | class IrbisMstError(Exception):
  110 |     """Ошибка структуры мастер-файла (отсутствует файл БД и т. п.)."""
  111 | 
  112 | 
  113 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  114 | # Поиск файлов БД (на Windows расширения бывают .MST/.XRF в верхнем регистре).
  115 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  116 | def _find_file(directory, db, suffix):
  117 |     """Путь к ``<db><suffix>`` в ``directory`` без учёта регистра, либо None."""
  118 |     target = (db + suffix).lower()
  119 |     try:
  120 |         names = os.listdir(directory)
```

```py
  122 |         return None
  123 |     for name in names:
  124 |         if name.lower() == target:
  125 |             return os.path.join(directory, name)
  126 |     return None

  128 | 
  129 | def _db_paths(data_path, db):
  130 |     """(mst_path, xrf_path) для БД. Подкаталог ``<data_path>/<db>/`` или сам
  131 |     ``data_path`` (некоторые DataPath держат файлы прямо в корне БД)."""

  134 |         mst = _find_file(directory, db, _MST_SUFFIX)
  135 |         if mst:
  136 |             xrf = _find_file(directory, db, _XRF_SUFFIX)

  139 | 
  140 | 
  141 | def list_databases(data_path):
  142 |     """Список кодов БД под DataPath ИРБИС64.
  143 | 
  144 |     БД — это подкаталог, содержащий ``<имя_подкаталога>.mst`` (без учёта
  145 |     регистра). Возвращает отсортированный список кодов в верхнем регистре."""
  146 |     found = []
  147 |     try:
  148 |         entries = os.listdir(data_path)
  149 |     except OSError as e:
  150 |         raise IrbisMstError('DataPath недоступен: %s (%s)' % (data_path, e))
  151 |     for entry in entries:
  152 |         sub = os.path.join(data_path, entry)
  153 |         if not os.path.isdir(sub):
  154 |             continue
  155 |         if _find_file(sub, entry, _MST_SUFFIX):
  156 |             found.append(entry.upper())
  157 |     return sorted(found)
```
